subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, which of the following is the greatest?
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 13:13, Paul states, 'And now these three remain: faith, hope and love. But the greatest of these is love.' Therefore, according to Paul, love is the greatest.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''... Abstain from what has been sacrificed to idols and from blood and from what is strangled and from unchastity...'' To which issue was the above a response?
explanation: The quote is from Acts 15:29, which is part of the letter sent by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem to Gentile believers in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia. The letter was a response to the controversy over whether Gentile converts needed to be circumcised and follow the law of Moses.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul and Barnabas were referred to as 'gods' in Lystra because they
explanation: In Acts 14:8-18, Paul and Barnabas were mistaken for gods in Lystra after they healed a man who was lame from birth. The people of Lystra called Barnabas Zeus and Paul Hermes.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Saul escaped from Damascus to Jerusalem after his conversion, the disciples in Jerusalem
explanation: In Acts 9:26-27, when Saul arrived in Jerusalem after his conversion, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him, not believing that he really was a disciple.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Saul's encounter with the Risen Christ on the road to Damascus convinced him that
options: the faith of his fathers was right
he should have gone to Damascus with more armed escort
explanation: In Acts 9:3-20, Saul's encounter with the Risen Christ on the road to Damascus led to his conversion. He was convinced that Jesus is the Son of God, and he began to preach this truth in the synagogues.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The apostles created a new office in the ministry besides prayer and preaching, in order to address the problem of
explanation: The apostles created a new office in the ministry to address the problem of injustice. This is evident in the book of Acts 6:1-4 where the apostles appointed seven men to oversee the daily distribution of food to widows to ensure fairness.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''...You have not lied to men but to God''. The immediate effect of this statement of Peter on the liar was that he
explanation: The statement 'You have not lied to men but to God' was made by Peter to Ananias who had lied about the proceeds of a piece of property he sold. The immediate effect of this statement was that Ananias fell down dead. This is recorded in Acts 5:1-5.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''....Let all the house of Israel therefore know assuredly that God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified ...As a reaction to this declaration, the Jews
options: decided to kill Peter and the apostles
went back to their houses in anger
were deeply troubled and asked what they should do
explanation: Peter's declaration that 'God has made him both Lord and Christ, this Jesus whom you crucified' deeply troubled the Jews and they asked what they should do. This is recorded in Acts 2:36-37.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Very early in the morning, on the first day of the week, three women went to the tomb of Jesus to
options: see whether the soldiers had carried the body away
explanation: On the first day of the week, three women went to the tomb of Jesus to anoint his body. This is recorded in Mark 16:1-2.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What is was the first statement of Jesus on the cross according to Luke's account of the gospel?
options: 'Today you shall be with me in paradise'
'Father forgive them for they do not know what they do'
explanation: The first statement of Jesus on the cross according to Luke's account of the gospel was 'Father forgive them for they do not know what they do'. This is recorded in Luke 23:34.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''....Do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children...'' These words were addressed by Jesus to the
explanation: The words 'Do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children' were addressed by Jesus to the 'daughters of Jerusalem' as he was being led to be crucified. This is recorded in Luke 23:28.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the following questions did the high priest ask Jesus during His trial?
options: 'Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?
'Are you the King of the Jews?
explanation: During Jesus' trial, the high priest asked him, 'Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?' This is recorded in Mark 14:61.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''...My house shall be a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of robbers''. Just before making this statement, the speaker
options: drove out those who sold in the temple
explanation: Before making the statement 'My house shall be a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of robbers', Jesus drove out those who sold in the temple. This is recorded in Luke 19:45-46.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''This is my beloved Son, listen to him''. This was said on the occasion of Jesus
explanation: The statement 'This is my beloved Son, listen to him' was made on the occasion of Jesus' transfiguration. This is recorded in Mark 9:7.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The appearance of Moses and Elijah at the transfiguration underscores the importance in the ministry of Jesus of
explanation: The appearance of Moses and Elijah at the transfiguration underscores the importance in the ministry of Jesus of 'the law and the prophets'. Moses represents the law and Elijah represents the prophets. This is a way of saying that Jesus' ministry fulfills both the law and the prophets.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TRANSFIGURATION TO THE DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After their mission, the seventy disciples reported to Jesus that
options: demons were subject to them in His name
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Luke 10:17, the seventy-two disciples returned with joy and said, 'Lord, even the demons submit to us in your name.' This indicates that the disciples were amazed that even demons were subject to them in the name of Jesus.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew's account of the gospel the most important divine requirement for entering the kingdom of heaven is
explanation: In Matthew 7:21, Jesus says, 'Not everyone who says to me, 'Lord, Lord,' will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.' This indicates that doing the will of God is the most important divine requirement for entering the kingdom of heaven.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable of the prodigal son emphasizes that
options: it is not good for the son and the father to quarrel
the father loves the second son more than the first
explanation: The parable of the prodigal son, found in Luke 15:11-32, emphasizes the themes of repentance and reconciliation. The younger son, after squandering his inheritance, repents and returns home, where he is warmly welcomed by his father. This shows the importance of repentance and reconciliation.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The story of the healing of blind Bartimaeus teaches that
options: there is need to persistently shout for attention
explanation: The story of the healing of blind Bartimaeus, found in Mark 10:46-52, emphasizes the importance of faith for healing. Bartimaeus had faith in Jesus' ability to heal him, and Jesus said to him, 'Go, your faith has healed you.' This shows that faith is a prerequisite for healing.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''...I adjure you by God, do not torment me...'' what is the significance of this statement ?
options: the demons did not like Jesus and so they cursed Him
the demons wanted to reach an agreement with Jesus
it is a recognition of the fact that demons are subject to Jesus
explanation: The statement '...I adjure you by God, do not torment me...' is found in Mark 5:7, where a man possessed by an impure spirit sees Jesus and cries out. This statement signifies the recognition of the fact that demons are subject to Jesus, as they plead with Him not to torment them.

topics.title: MIRACLES : EXORCISM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Luke, the healing of the paralytic was intended to show that
options: the Holy Spirit makes intercession for sinners
explanation: In the Gospel of Luke, Jesus heals a paralytic man to demonstrate his authority to forgive sins. When Jesus saw the faith of the men who brought the paralytic, he said to the paralytic, 'Friend, your sins are forgiven.' The Pharisees and teachers of the law began thinking to themselves, 'Who is this fellow who speaks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins but God alone?' Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, 'Why are you thinking these things in your hearts? Which is easier: to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'? But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.' So he said to the paralyzed man, 'I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.' (Luke 5:17-24)

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which miracle story was Jesus mistaken for a ghost by his disciples?
explanation: In the Gospel of Matthew (14:22-33), Mark (6:45-52) and John (6:16-21), there is a story where Jesus walks on water towards his disciples' boat during a storm. The disciples, seeing him walking on the sea, were terrified and said, 'It is a ghost!' and cried out in fear. But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, 'Take heart; it is I. Do not be afraid.'

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A would-be disciple, who puts his hand to the plough and looks back lacks
explanation: In Luke 9:62, Jesus says, 'No one who puts a hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service in the kingdom of God.' This implies that a would-be disciple who looks back after putting his hand to the plough lacks total commitment to the cause of the kingdom of God.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Christ say should be the qualification of whoever was to be first or great among the apostles?
options: drinking from the same cup as Jesus
explanation: In Mark 10:43-44, Jesus says, 'Whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant, and whoever wants to be first must be slave of all.' This implies that the qualification for greatness among the apostles is being a servant or a slave to all.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The second temptation of Jesus according to Luke's account of the gospel was
options: giving the kingdoms of the world to Jesus if He would worship the devil
a command for a stone to become bread
jumping down from the pinnacle of the temple
denying the name of God, the Father
explanation: In Luke's account of the temptation of Jesus (Luke 4:5-8), the second temptation involves the devil leading Jesus up to a high place, showing him in an instant all the kingdoms of the world, and saying to him, 'I will give you all their authority and splendor; it has been given to me, and I can give it to anyone I want to. If you worship me, it will all be yours.' Jesus answered, 'It is written: 'Worship the Lord your God and serve him only.''

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?...''This was said by John to the
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 3:7, John the Baptist refers to the Pharisees and Sadducees as a 'brood of vipers' when they came to where he was baptizing.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : JOHN, THE FORERUNNER OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Lord, now lettest thy servant depart in peace, according to thy word, for mine eyes have seen thy salvation....'' This was said at the dedication of Jesus by
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Luke 2:29-32, Simeon, a righteous and devout man who was waiting for the consolation of Israel, said these words when he saw the baby Jesus during his presentation at the temple.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the birth and infancy narratives in St. Matthew, the angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream three times. In the second appearance, he instructed Joseph to
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 2:13-15, the angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream for the second time and instructed him to take Mary and Jesus and flee to Egypt because Herod was seeking to kill the child. This is considered as going into exile.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was upon the face of the deep on the first day of creation?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Genesis 1:2, it is stated that darkness was upon the face of the deep before God said, 'Let there be light'.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Let there be lights in the firmament of the heavens to separate the day from night....''This statement was made by God on the
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Genesis 1:14-19, God made the statement about creating lights in the firmament of the heavens to separate the day from the night on the fourth day of creation.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The story of the Fall teaches that
options: man was made in the Image of God
it is a sin to disobey the God
explanation: The story of the Fall in the Bible teaches that disobedience to God is a sin. Adam and Eve were commanded not to eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, but they disobeyed this command, which led to their expulsion from the Garden of Eden.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''On that day all the fountains of the great deep burst forth and the windows of the heavens were opened'' The result of this was a
explanation: The quote is from the story of Noah's Ark in the Bible. It describes the beginning of the Great Flood, which was caused by heavy rainfall that lasted for forty days and forty nights.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would suckle children?....'' .The above question was asked by
explanation: The question was asked by Sarah in the Bible. She was expressing her disbelief and joy at having a child in her old age.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The quotation, ''Let me not look upon the death of the child'' comes from the story about
options: Moses when he was placed on the bank of the Nile
explanation: The quote, 'Let me not look upon the death of the child' comes from the story about the expulsion of Hagar and Ishmael in the Bible. Hagar said this when she thought her son Ishmael was about to die of thirst in the desert.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joseph's dream about his future leadership of his brothers was fulfilled when he became
explanation: Joseph's dream about his future leadership of his brothers was fulfilled when he became the second in command to Pharaoh. In this position, he was able to save his family from famine by bringing them to Egypt.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The children of Israel cried out to God for help in Egypt because
explanation: The children of Israel cried out to God for help in Egypt because they were in bondage. This is a reference to the biblical account of the Israelites' enslavement in Egypt, where they were forced to work as slaves for the Egyptians. The harsh conditions and brutal treatment led them to cry out to God for deliverance.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : DISOBEDIENCE AND CONSEQUENCES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?'' The above statement was made by
explanation: The statement 'What is this we have done, that we have let Israel go from serving us?' was made by Pharaoh and his servants. This is a reference to the biblical account of the Exodus, where Pharaoh regretted his decision to let the Israelites leave Egypt, as they were a significant source of labor.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The manna which the Isrealites left till the morning in disobedience to God
explanation: The manna which the Israelites left till the morning in disobedience to God bred worms and became foul. This is a reference to the biblical account of the Israelites in the wilderness, where God provided manna for them to eat. God commanded them not to keep any of it until morning, but some disobeyed, and the leftover manna bred worms and became foul.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God gave the ten commandment to the people of Israel because He
options: frowned at those who took His Name in vain
explanation: God gave the ten commandments to the people of Israel because He redeemed them from slavery in Egypt. The Ten Commandments were given as part of the covenant between God and the Israelites following their liberation from Egypt. They served as a guide for moral and religious conduct.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''....choose this day whom you will serve,... as for me and my house, we will serve the LORD.'' ''The Israel'' response to the above statement is''... we
options: also will serve the LORD, for He is our God''
cannot serve the LORD, for He is a holy God'
will serve the LORD though He is a jealous God'
cannot put away the foreign gods among us because our fathers served them
explanation: The Israelites' response to the statement '....choose this day whom you will serve,... as for me and my house, we will serve the LORD.' is '... we also will serve the LORD, for He is our God''. This is a reference to the biblical account where Joshua, the leader of the Israelites after Moses, challenged the people to choose whom they would serve. The people responded by affirming their commitment to serve the Lord.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSHUA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God's anger was kindled against Eli and his children because Eli did not
options: train his children in the way of the LORD
apply appropriate sanctions on his children when due
explanation: In the Bible, Eli's sons were wicked and did not respect the offerings brought by the people. Eli was aware of their actions but did not adequately reprimand or punish them. Therefore, God's anger was kindled against Eli and his children because Eli did not apply appropriate sanctions on his children when due.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prophets whom Saul met at Gibeath-elohim as a confirmation that the LORD had anointed him to rule over God's people, were
explanation: In the Bible, Saul met a group of prophets at Gibeath-elohim who were prophesying, and the Spirit of God came upon him, and he prophesied among them. These prophets were known as ecstatic prophets because they were often overcome with religious emotion and would prophesy in a state of ecstasy.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: While David was being anointed king, he received
explanation: When David was anointed king by Samuel, the Spirit of the LORD came mightily upon him from that day forward. This is recorded in 1 Samuel 16:13.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Against whom were the Israelites fighting when King David committed adultery with Uriah's wife?
explanation: King David committed adultery with Bathsheba, Uriah's wife, while the Israelites were fighting against the Ammonites. This is recorded in 2 Samuel 11.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Psalm 51, the sacrifice acceptable to God is
explanation: According to Psalm 51:17, the sacrifices of God are a broken spirit; a broken and contrite heart, O God, you will not despise. However, in the context of the entire Psalm 51, it is clear that a clean heart is also a sacrifice acceptable to God (Psalm 51:10). Therefore, the correct answer is a clean heart.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Solomon could devote his attention to the building of the house of the LORD because
options: of the supplies from the Qeen of Sheba
of David's purchase of the site for the building
explanation: Solomon was able to devote his attention to the building of the house of the LORD because there was neither adversary nor misfortune. This means that there was peace and stability during his reign which allowed him to focus on the construction of the temple.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The immediate cause of the division of the kingdom was
explanation: The immediate cause of the division of the kingdom was Rehoboam's foolishness. Rehoboam, Solomon's son, rejected the advice of the elders to lighten the harsh labor and heavy taxes his father had imposed on the Israelites. His decision led to the division of the kingdom.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Arise, go to Zarephath, which belongs to Sidon and dwell there, Behold, I have commanded a widow there to feed you''. What exactly necessitated this event?
options: Ahab's evil in the sight of the LORD
explanation: The event was necessitated by the drought caused by Elijah's curse. Elijah, a prophet of God, had declared there would be no rain or dew in Israel except at his word. The drought led to severe famine, and God directed Elijah to Zarephath where a widow would provide for him.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ... I am Pharisees, a son of Pharisee, with respect to the hope and resurrect of the dead I am on trial. This was part of the address of Paul to
explanation: This was part of the address of Paul to the Sanhedrin. The Sanhedrin was the supreme council and tribunal of the Jews during postexilic times headed by a High Priest and having religious, civil, and criminal jurisdiction.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Moses was keeping flock for his father-in-law on Mount _____.
explanation: Moses was keeping flock for his father-in-law on Mount Horeb. It was here that he encountered the burning bush through which God spoke to him.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF MOSES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the creation account of Genesis 1, God charged man to
explanation: In the creation account of Genesis 1, God gave man dominion over all the earth. This is stated in Genesis 1:28, where God blessed them and said to them, 'Be fruitful and increase in number; fill the earth and subdue it. Rule over the fish in the sea and the birds in the sky and over every living creature that moves on the ground.'

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed; for God made man in his own image'' . This statement explains the
explanation: The statement 'Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed; for God made man in his own image' is found in Genesis 9:6. It is part of the covenant God made with Noah after the flood, and it establishes the principle of capital punishment, also known as the prohibition of blood revenge.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When God told Abraham that Sarah would have a child, Abraham laughed because
options: they were too old to have a child
he already had a child called Ishmeal
explanation: In Genesis 17:17, Abraham laughed when God told him that Sarah would have a child because he and Sarah were too old to have a child. Abraham was 100 years old and Sarah was 90 years old at the time.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After Jacob's dream at Bethel, he promised that he would
options: give a tenth of all he would have to God
return immediately and settle there with his family
go to his father's house in peace
explanation: After Jacob's dream at Bethel, he made a vow to God that if God would be with him and watch over him on his journey, and give him food to eat and clothes to wear, and if he returned safely to his father's house, then the Lord would be his God and he would give a tenth of all he had to God. This is recorded in Genesis 28:20-22.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why did Judah advise his brothers against shedding the blood of Joseph?
options: he wanted to raise some money by selling Joseph
explanation: In Genesis 37:26-27, Judah advised his brothers against shedding Joseph's blood because he was their brother and he wanted to save him.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why did God redeem the Israelites from the hands of Pharaoh?
options: God remembered His convenant with Abraham, Isaac and Jacob
the yoke of the Israelites was heavy
God wanted to show His power to Pharaoh
He wanted to show that Moses was powerful
explanation: God redeemed the Israelites from the hands of Pharaoh because He remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. This covenant was a promise God made to these patriarchs that He would make their descendants a great nation and deliver them from bondage.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When the people of Israel saw the great work of the LORD in drowning the Egyptians, they
explanation: When the Israelites saw the great work of the LORD in drowning the Egyptians, they believed in Moses. This event demonstrated God's power and His support for Moses, which led the Israelites to trust in Moses' leadership.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During the giving of the law at Mount Sinai, the Israelites wanted Moses to speak to them instead of God because
explanation: During the giving of the law at Mount Sinai, the Israelites wanted Moses to speak to them instead of God because they were afraid and trembled. The presence of God was overwhelming and terrifying to them, so they preferred to hear from Moses.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The golden calf which Aaron made for the people of Israel signified
options: a return to the gods of Egypt
the final break with the God that brought them out of Egypt
the end of Moses' leadership of the people of Israel
explanation: The golden calf which Aaron made for the people of Israel signified a return to the gods of Egypt. This act was a form of idolatry, as they were reverting to the worship of the Egyptian deities they had left behind.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where did Joshua gather all the tribes of Israel for presentation before God?
explanation: Joshua gathered all the tribes of Israel for presentation before God at Shechem. This was a significant location in the history of Israel, where important events such as covenant renewals took place.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The valley of Eshcol is symbolic to Israel because it was the place where
options: the men of Israel cut down the cluster of grapes
the spies set out to surve Canaan
the Israelites challenged the leadership of Moses and Aaron
Moses and his people had a resounding victory over the Amalekites
explanation: The valley of Eshcol is symbolic to Israel because it was the place where the men of Israel cut down the cluster of grapes. This event is recorded in Numbers 13:23-24, where the spies sent by Moses to explore the land of Canaan came back with a branch bearing a single cluster of grapes so large that it took two men to carry it on a pole.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The report brought by the men who followed Caleb to spy the land of Canaan showed that the land
explanation: The report brought by the men who followed Caleb to spy the land of Canaan showed that the land flowed with milk and honey. This phrase is often used in the Bible to describe a land of abundance and fertility. However, despite the positive report about the land's fertility, the spies also reported that the inhabitants of the land were strong and their cities fortified, which caused fear among the Israelites (Numbers 13:27-29).

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Shema emphasizes the
options: importance of Israel to the world
explanation: The Shema emphasizes the unity of God. The Shema is a declaration of faith in Judaism, a pledge of allegiance to God. It is found in Deuteronomy 6:4, and it begins with the phrase: 'Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is one.' This statement emphasizes the monotheistic nature of God in Judaism.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''The LORD give you children by this woman for the loan which she lent to the LORD....'' What was this loan?
explanation: The loan referred to in this question is Samuel. In 1 Samuel 1:27-28, Hannah, Samuel's mother, dedicated Samuel to the Lord, saying, 'For this child I prayed, and the Lord has granted me my petition that I made to him. Therefore I have lent him to the Lord. As long as he lives, he is lent to the Lord.' This is why Samuel is referred to as the 'loan' which she lent to the Lord.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A good example of parental upbringing is manifested in the son of
explanation: A good example of parental upbringing is manifested in the son of Asa. Asa was a king of Judah who did what was good and right in the eyes of the Lord his God (2 Chronicles 14:2). He removed the foreign altars and high places, smashed the sacred stones and cut down the Asherah poles (2 Chronicles 14:3). His actions showed his commitment to the Lord, and this would have been a good example for his children.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ASA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The leadership potential of David was demonstrated publicly for the first time when he
options: spared King Saul's life in the cave
explanation: David's leadership potential was publicly demonstrated for the first time when he killed Goliath, the Philistine giant. This event showed his courage, faith, and ability to lead, which were key qualities of a leader.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the sin committed by Saul that cost him his kingdom?
explanation: The sin committed by Saul that cost him his kingdom was disobedience. Saul disobeyed God's command through Prophet Samuel to completely destroy the Amalekites and all their possessions. Instead, he spared King Agag and the best of the livestock. This act of disobedience led to God rejecting Saul as king.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Although David refused to kill Saul because he was the LORD'S anointed, he still believed that
options: Saul would be killed by the Philistines
Saul's soldiers would cause him to die in battle
explanation: David refused to kill Saul because he was the LORD'S anointed, but he still believed that the LORD would eventually smite Saul. David had the opportunity to kill Saul in the cave but he didn't because he respected the fact that Saul was anointed by God. However, he believed that God would judge Saul for his actions.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Blessed be you, my son David! You will do many things and will succeed in them'' . The above statement was made when David
explanation: The statement 'Blessed be you, my son David! You will do many things and will succeed in them' was made when David spared Saul's life. This was a recognition of David's mercy and righteousness in not taking Saul's life when he had the opportunity to do so.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Solomon asked God for wisdom to enable him
explanation: Solomon asked God for wisdom to enable him discern what was right. When God appeared to Solomon in a dream and asked what he wanted, Solomon asked for wisdom and a discerning heart to govern the people of Israel and to distinguish between right and wrong. This request pleased God who granted him wisdom and also added wealth and long life.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The consequence of Rehoboam's unwise decision at the time the Israelites came to him at Shechem eventually resulted in
explanation: Rehoboam's unwise decision to increase the burden of the Israelites led to a rebellion. The northern tribes of Israel rejected Rehoboam's authority and chose Jeroboam as their king, thus splitting the kingdom into two - Israel in the north and Judah in the south.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Luke, John the Baptist instructed the multitudes that came to be baptized to
explanation: In Luke 3:8, John the Baptist instructed the multitudes that came to be baptized to 'bear fruits in keeping with repentance'. This means that their actions should reflect a change of heart and a commitment to turn away from sin.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Go and search diligently for the child, and when intention of the speaker in this context was to
options: kill all the children in the area
explanation: The speaker in this context is King Herod. His intention was to kill the child Jesus, whom he saw as a threat to his throne. He disguised his intention by telling the Wise Men to inform him where the child was so that he could also go and worship him.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus was tempted in the wilderness in order to
options: prove that He was the Son of God
explanation: Jesus was tempted in the wilderness to prepare Him for His earthly ministry. The temptations were a way of testing His obedience and faithfulness to God. It was after overcoming these temptations that Jesus began His public ministry.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One important fact about Mary brought out in Jesus' miracle of changing water to wine was that she
options: must intercede for a miracle to occur
had a deep insight of her son and His abllity
explanation: In the miracle of changing water to wine at the wedding in Cana, Mary demonstrated a deep insight of her son Jesus and His ability. She told the servants to do whatever Jesus instructed them to do, showing her faith in His ability to handle the situation.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The raising of Lazarus illustrates that Jesus
explanation: The raising of Lazarus from the dead by Jesus is a demonstration of His power over life and death, thus illustrating that He is the giver of life. This miracle is a testament to Jesus' divine authority and His role as the resurrection and the life.

topics.title: MIRACLES : MIRACLES OF RESUSCITATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Lord, do not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy to have you come under my roof;...''The continuation of this message by the centurion indicates that
options: he had faith in the power of Jesus' word
as a Gentile, he did not like the Jews
as an army officer, he wanted to avoid contact with Jesus
explanation: The continuation of the centurion's message indicates his faith in the power of Jesus' word. He believed that Jesus did not need to be physically present to heal his servant, but could do so with His word alone. This shows a deep faith in Jesus' authority and power.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The epileptic boy could not be healed by the disciples because
explanation: Jesus' response, which was directed to the people (including the scribes), suggests that the inability of his disciples to bring about a cure appears to have been taken as a validation for their unbelief. He addressed them as a faithless or unbelieving and crooked (not upright) generation, asking how much longer he would have to remain with them and to put up with them. Jesus then requested that the boy be brought to him. (Matthew 17:17; Mark 9:19; Luke 9:41)

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The poor in the spirit are promised?
explanation: In the Beatitudes, Jesus promises the kingdom of God to the poor in spirit. Being 'poor in spirit' refers to those who recognize their spiritual poverty and their need for God. Jesus assures such individuals of their place in the kingdom of God.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Those that are hated and reviled shall
explanation: In the Beatitudes, Jesus promises that those who are hated and reviled for His sake will have a great reward in heaven. This is a reassurance for those who face persecution and hardship for their faith in Christ.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus' objective for sending out the twelve was
options: for them to teach the Gentiles about the God of the Jews
to train the disciples in His ministry
to divert the attention of Herod from Himself
to demonstrate that He was about to be crucified
explanation: Jesus sent out the twelve disciples to train them in His ministry. This was a part of their preparation for the time when they would have to carry on His work after His departure. The disciples were sent out to preach the kingdom of God, heal the sick, and cast out demons, which were all part of Jesus' ministry.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who appeared to talk to Jesus during the transfiguration?
explanation: During the transfiguration of Jesus, Moses and Elijah appeared to talk to Him. This event is recorded in the Gospels of Matthew (17:1-9), Mark (9:2-8), and Luke (9:28-36). Moses and Elijah represent the Law and the Prophets, respectively, which testify about Jesus.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Grant us to sit, one at your right hand one at your left, In your glory ...''Why was this request by the sons of Zebedee not granted?
options: they were not qualified for the privilege
they were unable to drink the cup
it was not the duty of Jesus to grant their request
explanation: The request by the sons of Zebedee was not granted because it was not Jesus' place to grant such a request. Jesus responded to them by saying, 'To sit at my right or left is not for me to grant. These places belong to those for whom they have been prepared by my Father' (Matthew 20:23).

topics.title: THE LAST SUPPER : THE STORY OF THE LAST SUPPER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where did Jesus lodge after he cleansed the temple?
explanation: After Jesus cleansed the temple, He lodged in Bethany. This is recorded in Mark 11:11, 'Then Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple; and when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.'

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At the trial of Jesus, Pilate was perplexed because
options: the Jews had no genuine case against Him
Jesus gave no answer to the charges
the Jews asked for the release of Barabbas, a notorious criminal
he knew that Jesus had performed many miracles among the Jews
explanation: At the trial of Jesus, Pilate was perplexed because Jesus gave no answer to the charges. This is recorded in Mark 15:4-5, 'So again Pilate asked him, 'Aren't you going to answer? See how many things they are accusing you of.' But Jesus still made no reply, and Pilate was amazed.'

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE PILATE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why did the accusers of Jesus stay behind while He was led to the Praetorium?
options: it was forbidden to follow the accused
they did not want to be defiled
explanation: The accusers of Jesus stayed behind while He was led to the Praetorium because they did not want to be defiled. According to Jewish customs, entering the Praetorium would make them ceremonially unclean and thus unable to participate in the Passover feast.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was forced to carry the cross behind Jesus?
explanation: Simon of Cyrene was forced to carry the cross behind Jesus. This is recorded in all the Synoptic Gospels (Matthew 27:32, Mark 15:21, Luke 23:26).

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise.'' The statement above was made to
explanation: The statement 'Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise' was made to the repentant robber. This is recorded in Luke 23:43, when one of the criminals crucified with Jesus repented and asked Jesus to remember him when He comes into His kingdom.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Luke, the people who first got to Jesus' tomb were
options: Mary the mother of Jesus ,Peter and John
explanation: According to Luke, the people who first got to Jesus' tomb were Mary Magdalene and the other women. This is recorded in Luke 24:1-10, where it is stated that the women who had come with Jesus from Galilee went to the tomb, found it empty and were told by two men in dazzling apparel that Jesus had risen.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus said to her, '' I am the resurrection and the life; he who believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live, and whoever lives and believes in me shall never die....'' On what occasion was this statement made?
options: When Jesus demonstrated the fact of His resurrection to Thomas
On the day Jesus restored Lazarus back to life
When Jairus daughter was raised from the dead
explanation: The statement 'I am the resurrection and the life; he who believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live, and whoever lives and believes in me shall never die' was made on the day Jesus restored Lazarus back to life. This is recorded in John 11:25-26, just before Jesus raises Lazarus from the dead.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus Christ, the Good Shepherd was loved by the Father because He
options: laid down His life for the sheep
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in John 10:11, Jesus is referred to as the Good Shepherd who lays down his life for his sheep. This is a metaphor for his sacrifice on the cross for the salvation of mankind. Therefore, the correct answer is 'laid down His life for the sheep'.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, if we give away all that we have and we deliver our bodies to be burnt, but have not love we
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 13:3, Paul states that if we give away all we have, and if we deliver up our bodies to be burned, but have not love, we gain nothing. This emphasizes the importance of love in all our actions. Therefore, the correct answer is 'gain nothing'.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Now the company of those who believed were of one heart and soul ...''This reveals that the early Christians
options: were always filled with the Holy spirit
explanation: The quote is from Acts 4:32, which describes the early Christian community as being of one heart and soul, and having everything in common. This means they shared their possessions and resources with each other. Therefore, the correct answer is 'had everything in common'.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The suggestion to choose the seven deacons was made by
explanation: The suggestion to choose seven deacons was made by the twelve apostles. This is recorded in Acts 6:2-3, where the twelve gathered all the disciples together and suggested that seven men full of the Spirit and wisdom should be chosen to oversee the daily distribution of food. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the twelve'.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''And in the last days it shall be, God declares, that I will pour out my Spirit upon all flesh, and your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, and your young men shall see visions,...'' This prophesy was fulfilled
explanation: The prophecy quoted is from Joel 2:28-29 and it was fulfilled at Pentecost, as recorded in Acts 2:17-18. During Pentecost, the Holy Spirit was poured out on the disciples, and they began to speak in other tongues and prophesy. Therefore, the correct answer is 'at pentecost'.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''But you shall receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you; and you shall be my witnesses in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria and to the end of the earth''. This statement commissions the apostles to
options: preach to the Gentiles an and outside Palestine
explanation: The statement is a part of the Great Commission given by Jesus to his apostles before his ascension. It instructs them to spread the gospel to all parts of the world, not just Jewish cities or to the Jews alone. Therefore, the correct answer is 'carry the gospel to all continents'.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was sent by God to restore Saul's sight after his encounter with Jesus on the road to Damascus?
explanation: In the book of Acts, it is recorded that Saul (later known as Paul) was blinded on the road to Damascus after an encounter with Jesus. Ananias, a disciple in Damascus, was instructed by God in a vision to go to Saul and restore his sight. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Ananias'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them'' . This statement was made by the
explanation: The statement 'Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them' was made by the Holy Spirit. This is recorded in the book of Acts where the Holy Spirit instructs the church at Antioch to set apart Barnabas and Saul (Paul) for the work of spreading the gospel. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Holy Spirit'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Following his encounter with the apostles, the ruler of Cyprus
options: requested that they choose another time to preach the gospel
explanation: In the book of Acts, it is recorded that the proconsul of Cyprus, Sergius Paulus, believed the gospel after witnessing the encounter between Paul and a sorcerer named Elymas. Therefore, the correct answer is 'believed the gospel'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF CORNELIUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The new covenant which God was to make with the house of Israel was that it would be
explanation: The new covenant that God promised to make with the house of Israel, as prophesied in the book of Jeremiah, was that He would put His law in their minds and write it on their hearts. Therefore, the correct answer is 'written upon their hearts'.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Elijah escaped to the Brook Cherith, he was fed by
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically 1 Kings 17:4-6, when Elijah escaped to the Brook Cherith, he was fed by ravens. God commanded the ravens to feed him there. So, he drank from the brook, and the ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning and bread and meat in the evening.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The contest on Mount Carmel was to prove that
explanation: The contest on Mount Carmel, as described in 1 Kings 18, was to prove that God was supreme to Baal. Elijah, the prophet of God, challenged the prophets of Baal to call on their god to light a fire on their altar. When they failed, Elijah called on God, who immediately sent fire from heaven. This demonstrated God's supremacy over Baal.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Naboth did not agree to release his vineyard to Abab because
options: the amount offered for it was too small
it was his major source of income
it was the inhertance of his fathers
explanation: Since he inherited the land from his ancestors, Naboth refused to sell it to Ahab. According to the Mosaic law, the law forbade the permanent selling of land.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Gehazi deceitfully receive from Naaman?
options: two talents of silver and two festal garments
two talents silver and two shekels of gold
two festal garments and two talents of gold
two thousand shekels and two festal garments
explanation: Elisha's servant, Gehazi, followed Naaman and deceitfully asked for a gift in Elisha's name. Naaman gave him "two talents of silver in two bags, with two changes of clothing" (2 Kings 5:23).

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The greatest achievement of King Josiah of Judah was his
options: sweeping reforms of the religious life of his people
training a worth successor before his death
explanation: King Josiah of Judah is best known for his religious reforms. He sought to purify the religious practices of his kingdom by removing all forms of idolatry and restoring the worship of Yahweh, the God of Israel. This is documented in the Bible in 2 Kings 22-23 and 2 Chronicles 34-35.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jerusalem fell in 586 B.C to the Babylonian King called
explanation: Jerusalem fell to the Babylonians in 586 B.C. The Babylonian king who led this conquest was Nebuchadnezzar II. This event marked the end of the kingdom of Judah and the beginning of the Babylonian exile, a significant period in Jewish history.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''And the King (Artaxerxes) said to me, ''why is your face sad, seeing you are not sick? This is nothing else buy sadness of the heart''. Why was the addressee sad?
options: enemies of Jerusalem were laughing at him
explanation: In the book of Nehemiah in the Bible, Nehemiah was sad because the walls of Jerusalem were broken down and its gates were destroyed by fire. This made the condition of Jerusalem deplorable. Nehemiah was a cupbearer to King Artaxerxes and when the king noticed his sadness, he asked him why he was sad. Nehemiah then explained the reason for his sadness.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Although Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego were cast into the fiery furnace, King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished to see four men in the furnace. He said of the LORD
explanation: "I see four men loose, walking in the midst of the fire; and they are not hurt, and the form of the fourth is like the Son of God."

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the belly of the fish, Jonah prayed to God to
explanation: Jonah 2 . From inside the fish Jonah prayed to the LORD his God. He said: "In my distress I called to the LORD, and he answered me. From the depths of the grave I called for help, and you listened to my cry.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sins of Nineveh notwithstanding, God spared the inhabitants because
options: Jonah was reluctant to deliver the message to them
the people responded positively to God's message
Nineveh was made up of a large population which did not know its right from its left
Nineveh was a great and prosperous city with one hundred and twenty thousand inhabitants
explanation: God spared the inhabitants of Nineveh because the people responded positively to God's message. This is recorded in the book of Jonah 3:5-10. When Jonah finally went to Nineveh and delivered God's message, the people of Nineveh believed God, proclaimed a fast, and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them to the least of them. When God saw what they did and how they turned from their evil ways, He relented and did not bring on them the destruction He had threatened.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Daniel was distinguished above all other satraps and presidents because he
explanation: In the book of Daniel, it is mentioned that Daniel was distinguished above all other satraps and presidents because he had an excellent spirit in him. This is found in Daniel 6:3 which says, 'Then this Daniel was preferred above the presidents and princes, because an excellent spirit was in him; and the king thought to set him over the whole realm.'

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Daniel could not be harmed by the lions because
options: an angel shut the mouths of the lions
a pillar of cloud separated Daniel from the lions
explanation: My God sent his angel, and he shut the mouths of the lions. They have not hurt me, because I was found innocent in his sight. Nor have I ever done any wrong before you, O king."

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Israelites did evil in the eyes of the LORD in the days of the Judges by
explanation: They took their daughters in marriage and gave their own daughters to their sons, and served their gods. The Israelites did evil in the eyes of the LORD; they forgot the LORD their God and served the Baals and the Asherahs.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Selling the needy for a pair of shoes in Amos means
explanation: The line about "buying the poor for silver and the needy for a pair of sandals" intrigued me, and I wanted to understand what was meant in the context of Amos' time. As I discovered it was the practice of buying people as slaves who had fallen into debt, hence buying people who couldn't even afford their sandals.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Thus says the LORD For three transgressions of Israel and for four, I will not revoke the punishment....'' In the latter part of this prophecy, Amos condemned Israel for
options: rejecting the laws and statutes of the LORD
explanation: In the latter part of the prophecy by Amos, he condemns Israel for their social injustices such as selling the righteous for silver and the needy for a pair of sandals. This is evident in Amos 2:6-8.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: From Hosea's teaching, we learn that God
options: will pour His Spirit on all flesh
will create a new heart in His people
explanation: Hosea's teaching emphasizes that God prefers steadfast love to sacrifice. This is evident in Hosea 6:6 where it is written, 'For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings'.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : BENEFITS OF FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The central theme of Isaiah's teachings is that
options: the vilest sinner will not be redeemed
explanation: The central theme of Isaiah's teachings is that no sinner is beyond redemption. This is based on the belief that God's mercy and forgiveness are infinite, and that anyone who sincerely repents and turns away from their sins can be saved. This is a fundamental concept in many religions, including Christianity.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Whom shall I send, and who will go for us?'' Then I said, ''Here am I! send me .'' Why did Isaiah offer himself to be God's messager?
options: because the kinghad died and he needed another job
having been cleansed of his sins, he saw the need to warn others
because he had seen the beauty of heaven and wanted to work for God
explanation: Isaiah offered himself to be God's messenger because, having been cleansed of his sins, he saw the need to warn others. This is evident in the book of Isaiah, where after his vision of God and his cleansing, he hears God's call for a messenger and volunteers himself. This shows his understanding of the gravity of sin and the importance of repentance, and his desire to help others achieve the same.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who according to Ezekiel, did the exiles blame for their plight?
explanation: According to the book of Ezekiel, the exiles blamed their fathers for their plight. They believed that their fathers' sins and disobedience to God's commandments were the cause of their suffering and exile.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God told Jeremiah that He would give Jerusalem into the hands of the Chaldeans because the Israelites
options: did not prepare adequately for war
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah, God told Jeremiah that He would give Jerusalem into the hands of the Chaldeans because the Israelites had provoked Him to anger. They had turned away from God and worshipped other gods, which was against God's commandments.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : DISOBEDIENCE AND CONSEQUENCES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The expression 'dry bones' in Ezekiel refers to
explanation: In the book of Ezekiel, the expression 'dry bones' is used as a metaphor for the whole house of Israel. Ezekiel's vision of the valley of dry bones (Ezekiel 37:1-14) is a prophetic vision where the dry bones represent the whole house of Israel in exile. God promises to breathe life into the bones (which represents Israel) and return them to the land of Israel.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The function of the law to believers before the coming of Christ was that of a
explanation: In the New Testament, specifically in Galatians 3:24, Apostle Paul refers to the law as a 'custodian' or 'schoolmaster' that was put in charge to lead us to Christ. The law was given to guide the believers in their relationship with God and others until the coming of Christ, who fulfilled the law.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Justification by grace shows that work is
explanation: Justification by grace is a Christian doctrine that emphasizes that salvation is solely through the grace of God, and not by any human works or efforts. Therefore, work is not required for salvation.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: All Christians who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized
explanation: According to the New Testament of the Bible, specifically Romans 6:3, all Christians who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death. This signifies that they have died to their old sinful nature and have been raised to a new life in Christ.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In what way did God condemn sin in the flesh?
options: repentance was made possible for the forgiveness of sin
resurrection of the flesh rendered sin ineffective
He sent His own Son in the likeness of man
explanation: "For what the law could not do, in that it was weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh" (Rom. 8:3).

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''But you are not in darkness brethren, for that day to surprise you like a thief''. This day refers to the
explanation: The phrase 'But you are not in darkness brethren, for that day to surprise you like a thief' is from 1 Thessalonians 5:4 in the Bible. This 'day' refers to the second coming of the Lord. The context of this verse is the Day of the Lord, a future time when God will intervene dramatically in world affairs. The Apostle Paul is encouraging believers that they are not in darkness, meaning they are spiritually enlightened, and therefore the Day of the Lord will not catch them by surprise like a thief in the night.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The main aim of the teaching of Christ's humility in Paul's Letter to the Philippians is that every Christian should
options: regard himself as equal to Jesus because He came in human form
confess that Jesus Chirst is LORD to the glory of God the Father
work out his own salvation with fear and trembling
look into the interest of others and do nothing from selfishness
explanation: In Paul's Letter to the Philippians, he emphasizes the humility of Christ and encourages Christians to emulate this humility. This includes looking into the interest of others and doing nothing from selfishness. This is a key teaching in Christianity, promoting selflessness and love for others.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul's Letter to the Philippians, God exalted Jesus and gave Him a name which is above all names because He
explanation: In Paul's Letter to the Philippians, it is stated that God exalted Jesus and gave Him a name which is above all names because He was humble and obedient unto death. This is a central theme in Christianity, emphasizing the importance of humility and obedience to God's will.

topics.title: HUMILITY : REWARDS OF HUMILITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Letter to the Galatians,Christians are encouraged to bear one another's burdens in order to
explanation: In the Letter to the Galatians, specifically Galatians 6:2, it is written 'Carry each other's burdens, and in this way you will fulfill the law of Christ.' Therefore, Christians are encouraged to bear one another's burdens in order to fulfill the law of Christ.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AMONG CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Be angry but do not sin,......''means that anger
explanation: The phrase 'Be angry but do not sin' is from Ephesians 4:26. It means that while it is natural to feel anger, one should not let it lead to sinful actions. Therefore, anger could be an occasion for sin if not controlled.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIAN ATTITUDE TO PERSECUTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: There varieties of gifts and so different people have different gifts. Who gives all these gifts?
explanation: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but the same Spirit is the source of them all. There are different kinds of service

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The overriding principle in the use of spiritual gifts is
explanation: The overriding principle in the use of spiritual gifts is decency and order. This is based on the teachings of the Bible, specifically in 1 Corinthians 14:40 where it is stated that everything should be done in a fitting and orderly way. This principle is applied to the use of spiritual gifts to ensure that they are used in a way that promotes peace and unity within the Christian community.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE BENEFITS OF SPIRITUAL GIFTS TO THE INDIVIDUAL AND THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: For the administration of the churches, Paul directed that Christians should
options: surrender all they have to the church
explanation: In the New Testament, Apostle Paul directed Christians to regularly contribute to the church for its administration. This is seen in 1 Corinthians 16:2 where Paul instructs the church in Corinth to set aside a sum of money in keeping with their income, saving it up, so that collections will not have to be made when he comes.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Contribution of relief materials for the needy in the church according to Paul, must be
explanation: In 2 Corinthians 9:7, Paul states that each person should give what they have decided in their heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver. This implies that the contribution of relief materials for the needy in the church must be done with cheerfulness, not out of obligation or pressure.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul teaches that we should obey governing authorities in order to avoid
explanation: Let everyone be subject to the governing authorities, for there is no authority except that which God has established. The authorities that exist have been established by God.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Paul says ''If anyone will not work, let him not eat'', he is saying that
options: the lazy person must eat only a little
every person should toil and labour with his hands
explanation: In 2 Thessalonians 3:10, Apostle Paul says 'For even when we were with you, we would give you this command: If anyone is not willing to work, let him not eat.' This statement is a call to personal responsibility and the importance of labor. It is not a condemnation of those who are unable to work, but rather a condemnation of idleness and a call for everyone to contribute to the best of their ability. Therefore, the correct answer is 'every person should toil and labour with his hands'.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : BENEFITS OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul worked harder than any of the apostles because
options: he had the strength to do so
of the grace of God that was with him
Jesus personally revealed Himself to him
he was formerly a persecutor of the church
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 15:10, Paul states that he worked harder than any of the apostles, not because of his own strength, but because of the grace of God that was with him. This grace is the unmerited favor of God, and it was this that empowered Paul to work harder than his contemporaries.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians wives are to be subject to their husbands
options: since their husbands take care of them
because it is fitting in the LORD
because it is the nature thing to do
since women are not expected to be heard
explanation: In Colossians 3:18, Paul instructs Christian wives to submit to their husbands, as it is fitting in the Lord. This means that the submission is not because of any inferiority or because the husbands take care of them, but because it is a part of God's design for marriage, which reflects the relationship between Christ and the Church.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : RELATIONSHIP IN THE CHRISTIAN FAMILY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: On being revealed at the coming of Christ, the lawless will be slain by the
explanation: then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will consume with the breath of His mouth and will annul by the appearing of His coming

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: An important feature of the second coming of Jesus as taught by Paul is the
options: establishment of a world government under Christ
explanation: In 1 Thessalonians 4:16, Paul teaches that the Lord himself will come down from heaven, with a loud command, with the voice of the archangel and with the trumpet call of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first. Therefore, an important feature of the second coming of Jesus as taught by Paul is the 'resurrection of the dead'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''What you sow does not come to life unless it does'' This statement refers to the
options: good works which are expected of the Christian
explanation: The statement 'What you sow does not come to life unless it does' is a reference to the resurrection of the dead. This concept is often used in the Bible to illustrate the idea that just as a seed must die before it can grow into a new plant, so too must we die before we can be resurrected to eternal life.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul's experience as recorded in his second Letter to the Corinthians, teaches that the attitude of Christians to sufferings should be that of
explanation: In his second letter to the Corinthians, Paul talks about his own sufferings and how he persevered through them. He encourages Christians to have the same attitude towards suffering, emphasizing that it is not something to be rejected or rebelled against, but rather something to be endured with patience and perseverance.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Letter to the Hebrews, it is found that despite the victory through faith in God of the men of old they did not receive what was promised. What was this promise?
explanation: The promise referred to in the Letter to the Hebrews is the coming of Jesus Christ. The men of old, despite their faith and victories, did not live to see the fulfillment of this promise. This is a central theme in the New Testament, emphasizing the long-awaited arrival of the Messiah, Jesus Christ.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Abraham's faith and works were better illustrated when he
options: offered his son, Isaac, on the altar
explanation: Abraham's faith and works were best illustrated when he was willing to offer his son, Isaac, on the altar as a sacrifice to God. This event, often referred to as the Binding of Isaac, is seen as a test of Abraham's faith, and his willingness to make the ultimate sacrifice is seen as a demonstration of his unwavering faith in God.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for,...For by it the men of old received divine approval.'' Who are the men of old mentioned as examples of faith in the Letter to the Hebrews?
explanation: The men of old mentioned as examples of faith in the Letter to the Hebrews are Abel, Abraham and Rahab. This is found in Hebrews 11, which is often referred to as the 'Hall of Faith'. Here, the author of Hebrews provides an overview of the Old Testament characters who lived by faith, and these three are among those mentioned.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter, Christians living among non -Christians should
explanation: According to Peter, Christians living among non-Christians should maintain good conduct. This is found in 1 Peter 2:12, where Peter encourages Christians to live such good lives among the pagans that, though they accuse them of doing wrong, they may see their good deeds and glorify God on the day he visits us.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, effective prayer is that which is offered
explanation: James 5:15 ' And the prayer offered in faith will restore the one who is sick. The Lord will raise him up. If he has sinned, he will be forgiven.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter enjoins Christians to be good citizens by
options: using their freedom to fight for their right
explanation: In the Bible, 1 Peter 2:13-17, Peter instructs Christians to submit themselves for the Lord's sake to every human authority, whether to the emperor, as the supreme authority, or to governors. This is because it is God's will that by doing good, they should silence the ignorant talk of foolish people. Therefore, Peter enjoins Christians to be good citizens by submitting to civil authorities.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD CITIZENSHIP.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul in his Letter to the Galatians, one is justified by
explanation: The major theological point Paul makes in his letter to the Galatians is that a person is justified through faith in Christ's death, not by works of the law. If the law could justify a person, then Jesus died for no reason.
