topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, the law and the prophets bear witness to the
explanation: Romans 3:21, NIV: But now apart from the law the righteousness of God has been made known, to which the Law and the Prophets testify.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "let us make man in our own image....... "In the statement above, 'man' refers to?
explanation: In the context of the Bible, when God said 'let us make man in our own image', 'man' refers to both male and female. This is evident in Genesis 1:27, where it is written that 'God created man in His own image, in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.' Therefore, the term 'man' here is used to represent mankind or humanity, which includes both males and females.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the covenant with Noah, God equates blood with?
explanation: In the covenant with Noah, God equates blood with life. This is evident in Genesis 9:4-6, where God tells Noah that 'But you must not eat meat that has its lifeblood still in it. Whoever sheds human blood, by humans shall their blood be shed; for in the image of God has God made mankind.' Therefore, in this context, blood is seen as the life force of a creature, and thus is equated with life.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The covenant of Abraham stipulates that an uncircumcised male shall?
explanation: Genesis 17:14, NIV: Any uncircumcised male, who has not been circumcised in the flesh, will be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.'

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joseph was hated the more by his brothers because?
options: he was the first son of Rebecca
his father bought him a coat of many colours
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Genesis 37, Joseph's brothers were jealous of him because of his dreams that suggested that he would rule over them. While it's true that Joseph was his father's favorite and that he was given a coat of many colors, the text specifically states that his brothers hated him even more after he shared his dreams with them.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Pharaoh set Joseph over the land of Egypt because he was?
explanation: In the Bible, Genesis 41:39, Pharaoh set Joseph over the land of Egypt because he was discreet and wise. Pharaoh recognized that Joseph was filled with the spirit of God and had the wisdom to interpret dreams and make wise decisions.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the burning bush, God revealed Himself to Moses as the God of?
explanation: In the burning bush, God revealed Himself to Moses as the God of deliverance. This is found in Exodus 3:7-8 where God spoke to Moses about delivering the Israelites from the hand of the Egyptians and bringing them to a land flowing with milk and honey.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF MOSES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When the Hebrews left Egypt, God did not lead them by the way of the land of the philistines because?
options: He did not want them to see war and return to Egypt
the Hebrews went out of Egypt not requiped for war
explanation: When Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them on the road through the Philistine country, though that was shorter. For God said, "If they face war, they might change their minds and return to Egypt."

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Samuel's reaction during his first encounter with God showed that he was?
explanation: In 1 Samuel 3, when God first called Samuel, he did not recognize God's voice and thought it was Eli who was calling him. This shows that Samuel was inexperienced in dealing with God at that time. He had to be told by Eli that it was God who was calling him. Therefore, Samuel's reaction during his first encounter with God showed that he was inexperienced in dealing with God.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: From the accounts in the First Book of Samuel, Eli's attitude to his children showed parental?
explanation: In the First Book of Samuel, Eli's attitude towards his children showed parental weakness. Eli was aware of the sins his sons were committing but failed to restrain them, which indicates a lack of control or weakness.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David demonstrated his submission to the will of God when he?
options: lamented the death of Saul and Jonathan
explanation: David demonstrated his submission to the will of God when he prevented the killing of Saul. Despite Saul's attempts to kill him, David spared Saul's life twice, showing his respect for Saul's anointed position and his submission to God's will.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When David repented of his adultery with Bathsheba, he said?
options: The man who has done this deserve to die"
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 2 Samuel 12:13, after Prophet Nathan confronted David about his adultery with Bathsheba, David confessed, 'I have sinned against the Lord.' This shows his acknowledgement of his sin and his repentance.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
The Lord has set the sun in the heavens, but he said that he will dwell in thick darkness ...."
This statement was made in the context of the?
explanation: The statement 'The Lord has set the sun in the heavens, but he said that he will dwell in thick darkness' is found in 1 Kings 8:12. This was said by Solomon during the dedication of the temple he built for the Lord. Therefore, the context of this statement is the dedication of Solomon's temple.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the first three chapters of the Book of Hosea, his main teaching is that?
options: one should keep the commandments of God
explanation: Three major themes are reflected in the book of Hosea: First, that God suffers exceedingly because of human rebellion against him; second, that God loves unconditionally; and third, that God forgives completely.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The valley of Achor in the Book of Hosea is a door of?
explanation: The valley of Achor runs through the life of the world. Trouble is not young. · Sanctified trouble is the door of hope, the herald of victory and rest.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Come let us return to the Lord; for he has torn, that he may heal us; he has stricken, and he will bind us up."This call is to encourage?
options: response to the need of the nation
explanation: The verse is from Hosea 6:1. It is a call to return to God, acknowledging that despite the pain and suffering (he has torn, he has stricken), God is also the healer and restorer (he may heal us; he will bind us up). This is a response to God's love, acknowledging His discipline but also His healing and restoration.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: With reference to Prophet Isaiah's vision, which of the following attributes must a minister of God possess?
explanation: In Isaiah's vision (Isaiah 6), Isaiah saw the Lord in His holiness ('Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of hosts; the whole earth is full of his glory!'). The vision led to Isaiah's confession of his own sinfulness and the sinfulness of his people, and his cleansing by God. This shows that a minister of God must possess holiness, being cleansed from sin and set apart for God's service.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did God give to Ezekiel to eat in his vision?
explanation: So I opened my mouth, and he gave me the scroll to eat. Then he said to me, "Son of man, eat this scroll I am giving you and fill your stomach with it." So I ate it, and it tasted as sweet as honey in my mouth. He then said to me: "Son of man, go now to the house of Israel and speak my words to them.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus was baptized by John the Baptist?
options: so that the heavens could open up blessings for all believers
so that the Holy Spirit could descend upon Him
explanation: In Matthew 3:13-15, Jesus was baptized by John the Baptist to 'fulfil all righteousness'. This was a part of God's plan for Jesus' life and ministry. It was not specifically about prophecy, blessings for believers, or the descent of the Holy Spirit, although the Holy Spirit did descend on Jesus after His baptism as a sign of God's approval.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Thou art my beloved Son; with thee i am well pleased ." This statement was made during the?
explanation: The statement "Thou art my beloved Son; with thee I am well pleased" was made during the baptism of Jesus. This is recorded in the Gospels of Matthew (3:17), Mark (1:11), and Luke (3:22). During this event, the heavens opened, the Spirit of God descended like a dove and a voice from heaven said this statement, affirming Jesus as the Son of God and expressing divine pleasure in him.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the temptation narratives in the gospels according to Mathew and Luke, Jesus overcame Satan because He?
explanation: We can see in Matthew 4 that Jesus could defeat Satan because He knew the word of God better than the tempter.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?''. On what occasion was this question asked?
options: when Jesus ate in the house of Zecchaeus
following the invitation of Jesus to a meal by a Pharisee
explanation: The question 'Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?' was asked following the call of Matthew. Matthew, also known as Levi, was a tax collector. When Jesus called him to be a disciple, Matthew invited Jesus to a meal at his house. The Pharisees and scribes questioned Jesus' disciples about why Jesus was eating with tax collectors and sinners. This event is recorded in the Gospels of Matthew (9:9-13), Mark (2:13-17), and Luke (5:27-32).

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Follow me and leave the dead to bury their own dead.'' By this statement, Jesus means that
options: disciples should leave their families in God's care
explanation: The statement 'Follow me and leave the dead to bury their own dead' means that following Jesus comes before anything else. This statement was made by Jesus in response to a disciple who asked to go and bury his father before following Him. Jesus was emphasizing the urgency and importance of spreading the Gospel, which is a higher duty than even familial responsibilities. This event is recorded in the Gospel of Matthew (8:21-22).

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The disciples became terrified when they saw Jesus walking on the sea because
options: there was a great storm in the sea
explanation: In the biblical account (Matthew 14:26), the disciples were terrified when they saw Jesus walking on the sea because they thought he was a ghost. The concept of walking on water was beyond their understanding, hence they thought it was a supernatural being, a ghost.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: By the miracle of raising Lazarus to life, Jesus wanted to
options: reward Mary and Martha for their charity
convince the people that He was the LORD of life
explanation: In the biblical account (John 11:25), Jesus said 'I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die'. By raising Lazarus from the dead, Jesus was demonstrating that He has power over life and death, thus convincing the people that He was the Lord of life.

topics.title: MIRACLES : MIRACLES OF RESUSCITATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the temptation narratives in the gospels according to Matthew and Luke, Jesus overcome Satan because He
explanation: According to the temptation narratives in the gospels according to Matthew and Luke, Jesus overcame Satan by depending on the word of God. In each of the three temptations, Jesus responded to Satan's temptations by quoting scripture (Matthew 4:1-11, Luke 4:1-13). This shows the importance of the word of God in overcoming temptation.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Jews sought all the more to kill Jesus after healing the man at the pool of Bethzatha because they thought that He
options: claimed to have authority to forgive sins
did not agree with the law of Moses
said that He was born before Abraham
explanation: Therefore the Jewssought all the more to kill Him, because He not only broke the Sabbath, but also said that God was His Father,making Himself equal with God.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the gospel according to Matthew, the disciples could not heal the epileptic boy because
options: the boy's father had no faith in them
explanation: In the Gospel according to Matthew 17:14-21, Jesus explains to his disciples that they could not heal the epileptic boy because of their little faith. He further explains that if they had faith as small as a mustard seed, they could move mountains.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In parable of the sower, the seed which fell on rocky ground refers to people whose conversion is
explanation: In the parable of the sower in Matthew 13:1-23, the seed which fell on rocky ground refers to people who hear the word and at once receive it with joy. But since they have no root, they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away. This is a superficial conversion, not deep or lasting.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable of the lost sheep teaches much about God's
explanation: The parable of the lost sheep is a story told by Jesus to illustrate the love and compassion God has for those who are lost. In the parable, the shepherd leaves his flock of 99 sheep to find the one that is lost, demonstrating God's love for each individual and His desire to bring those who are lost back to Him.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew's account of the gospel, Jesus charged the twelve disciples to give without pay because they themselves
explanation: In Matthew 10:8, Jesus tells His disciples, 'Freely you have received; freely give.' This charge was given to the disciples as they were sent out to heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those who have leprosy, and drive out demons. The disciples were to give without pay because they had received their power and authority from Jesus without cost.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which disciples were with Jesus during the transfiguration?
explanation: Jesus Christ took three of his disciples, Peter, James, and John, up on a mountain, where Moses and Elijah appeared and Jesus was transfigured, his face and clothes becoming dazzlingly bright (Mark 9:2–13; Matthew 17:1–13; Luke 9:28–36).

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Gospel according to Luke, the multitude that followed Jesus during the triumphal entry rejoiced and praised God with a loud voice because
options: they realized that Jesus was a prophet from Nazareth
of the might works that they had seen done by Jesus
Jesus was for the first time riding on a colt
they believerd that Jesus would deliver them from the hands of their enemies
explanation: In the Gospel according to Luke, specifically in Luke 19:37, it is mentioned that the multitude that followed Jesus during the triumphal entry rejoiced and praised God with a loud voice because of the mighty works they had seen done by Jesus. The triumphal entry is the event where Jesus entered Jerusalem on a colt and was greeted by crowds who laid their cloaks and palm branches on the road before him, praising him as the coming king.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus did not appeal to His Father for legions of angels to fight for Him because
options: it was not proper angels to physically fight human beings
He could; on His own, avoid the cross
He wanted the scriptures to be fulfilled
explanation: Jesus did not appeal to His Father for legions of angels to fight for Him because He wanted the scriptures to be fulfilled. This is a reference to the biblical account of Jesus' arrest in the Garden of Gethsemane. When Peter tried to defend Jesus by cutting off the ear of the high priest's servant, Jesus rebuked him and said that he could call on his Father, and he would provide more than twelve legions of angels. But then, how would the Scriptures be fulfilled that say it must happen this way? (Matthew 26:53-54). This shows that Jesus was committed to fulfilling the prophecies about his death, rather than avoiding it.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus wept over Jerusalem because
options: the people did not believe in Him
the city did not know the things that made for peace
people were buying and selling in the temple
the Pharisees and the priests had misled the people
explanation: Jesus wept over the city of Jerusalem because they weren't ready when the General came to town. They were not prepared and missed out on worshipping and following Him. God in flesh was standing right before their eyes, and they missed it! Because they missed the General and weren't ready, a future judgment would come.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE:THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Pilate achieve by sending Jesus to Herod?
options: He escaped the anger of the Jews
Jesus was not killed in his hands
he was able to pacify the Jews
explanation: This is the correct answer as Pilate didnt want to be responsible for the death.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE HEROD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of Jesus' words on the cross led a bystander to give him vinegar?
options: ''Father, into thy hands I commit my spirit
''Father forgive them, for they know not what they do
''My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me
explanation: In the Bible, specifically Matthew 27:46 and Mark 15:34, Jesus cried out in a loud voice, saying, ''My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?'' This was misunderstood by some of the bystanders as a call for Elijah. One of them ran and filled a sponge with sour wine (vinegar), put it on a reed, and gave it to him to drink.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Jesus said that Lazarus would rise again Martha thought that
options: Jesus would rise her brother from the dead
her brother would rise again at the last day
Jesus was talking of His own resurrection
explanation: I am the resurrection and the life. Jesus said to her, "Your brother will rise again." Martha said to Him, "I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day." Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in Me, though he may die, he shall live

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What kind of a man was elected to replace Judas Iscariot?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically Acts 1:21-26, the apostles decided to replace Judas Iscariot with someone who had been with them from the beginning of Jesus' ministry and could be a witness to Jesus' resurrection. Therefore, the man elected to replace Judas Iscariot was a co-witness of the resurrection.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The event of the day of Pentecost was prophesied by
explanation: Over 800 years before that day, the Prophet Joel had prophesied that the Holy Spirit would be poured out, and the phenomena would be that both sons and daughters would prophesy

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the second martyr of the early church?
explanation: The second martyr of the early church was James, the brother of John. This is recorded in Acts 12:1-2, where King Herod arrested some who belonged to the church, including James, the brother of John, whom he put to death with the sword.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE MARTYRDOM OF STEPHEN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Christians concept of justification is that
options: through faith in Jesus, the sinner is now regarded by God as guitless
by one's efforts in keeping the law, man is now perfect before God
through his own knowledge, man can now do what is pleasing to God
by his natural wisdom, a sinner could be reconciled to God
explanation: Justification in Christian theology is the process by which a person is made or declared 'Righteous'. According to the Bible, particularly in the book of Romans, justification is by faith in Jesus Christ. Faith in Jesus Christ brings about a legal declaration that is God's pronouncement that the sinner is now righteous in His sight.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, those who rely on the works of the law are under a curse since
options: the law is made for the weak
the works of the law are of the devil
explanation: According to Apostle Paul in the book of Galatians 3:10, those who rely on the works of the law are under a curse because no one can fully keep the law. The law demands perfect obedience, which is impossible for humans to achieve, hence, the curse. This is why Paul emphasizes faith in Jesus Christ as the means of justification.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Letter of Paul to the Romans, sin reigned in death, while grace reigned through righteousness to
explanation: "Just as sin reigned in death, so also grace might reign through righteousness to bring eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord"

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : FRUITS OF JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians believe that they are children of God because
options: they keep the laws and covenants of Abraham as commanded by God
they are descendants of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob
through faith, they were baptized into Christ and put on Christ
explanation: According to Galatians 3:26-27, 'For you are all children of God through faith in Christ Jesus. And all who have been united with Christ in baptism have put on Christ, like putting on new clothes.' Therefore, Christians believe they are children of God because through faith, they were baptized into Christ and put on Christ.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians ought to be humble because
options: only the humble will inherit eternal life
explanation: Christians are encouraged to be humble because Jesus Christ, who is their model, demonstrated humility throughout his life. This is a key teaching in Christianity, as seen in Philippians 2:5-8, where Paul encourages believers to have the same mindset as Christ Jesus, who, being in very nature God, did not consider equality with God something to be used to his own advantage; rather, he made himself nothing by taking the very nature of a servant, being made in human likeness. And being found in appearance as a man, he humbled himself by becoming obedient to death—even death on a cross!

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, spiritual gifts, if properly employed help in
explanation: According to Apostle Paul, spiritual gifts are given for the edification of the church. This is seen in 1 Corinthians 14:12, where Paul states 'So it is with you. Since you are eager for gifts of the Spirit, try to excel in those that build up the church.' The purpose of spiritual gifts is to strengthen the church, to help it grow and mature in faith, not for personal gain or recognition.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE BENEFITS OF SPIRITUAL GIFTS TO THE INDIVIDUAL AND THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul advised Christians in Thessalonica to keep away from any brother who is
explanation: Paul calls on the church at Thessalonica to avoid any brother who makes idleness habitual. He reminds his readers that he and his associates were not idle when they were in Thessalonica.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The order of events at the second coming of Christ will be that
options: those who are alive will be caught up first before the dead will rise
the dead will rise first before the living are caught up
the dead and the living will be caught up together
only the dead will rise leaving behind those who are alive
explanation: At that time the dead in Christ will be raised and living Christians will be caught up to meet the Lord in the air and be with Him forever.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the following commandments is described by James as the royal law?
options: You shall have no other gods before me
You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart
You shall love your neighbour as yourself
You shall not covet your neighbour's property
explanation: In the Epistle of James, ch. 2: 8, the Levitical law, "Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself", is called the "royal law".

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''If your enemy is hungry, feed him.'' This statement implies
explanation: The statement 'If your enemy is hungry, feed him' implies kindness. This is a teaching from the Bible found in Romans 12:20, which encourages believers to show kindness to their enemies. The act of feeding a hungry enemy is a demonstration of kindness, as it goes beyond the normal expectation of simply not harming one's enemy. It is an act of love and compassion, showing that the believer is willing to put aside personal feelings of animosity and instead show kindness.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : BENEFITS OF FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In extolling the sovereignty of God, Amos contrasted Him with the Nile which he described as
options: the source of the waters of the sea
explanation: In Amos 8:8, Amos contrasted God with the Nile and rivers of Egypt when he says "The whole land will rise like the Nile ...and then sink like the rivers of Egypt.".

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: By keeping God's covenant, the people of Israel would become
options: a nation where God would manifest Himself
explanation: In Exodus 19:5-6, God makes a covenant with the Israelites. He promises that if they obey His voice and keep His covenant, they would be His treasured possession among all peoples, and they would be a kingdom of priests and a holy nation. Therefore, by keeping God's covenant, the people of Israel would become a kingdom of priests of God.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jeremiah, the vital element in the new covenant God would make the Jews was that
options: He would bless all nations through the Jews
man would be enabled to keep God's law
salvation would come through a virgin's seed
the individual would be accountable before God
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah, the prophet speaks of a new covenant that God would make with the Jews. The vital element in this new covenant was that man would be enabled to keep God's law. This is because the new covenant would involve God writing His laws in the hearts of the people, thus enabling them to keep His laws. This is different from the old covenant where the laws were written on tablets of stone and people found it difficult to keep them.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joseph saw his rise to leadership position in Egypt in relation to his brothers as
options: a proof of his superiority over them
an opportunity to avenge their evil deeds
explanation: Joseph saw his rise to leadership position in Egypt in relation to his brothers as a means of preserving their lives. This is evident in Genesis 45:5-7 where Joseph reveals his identity to his brothers and tells them not to be distressed or angry with themselves because God sent him ahead of them to preserve life. He saw his position as part of God's plan to ensure the survival of his family during the severe famine.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''If you will go with me, I will go'; but if you will not go with me, I will not go''. In the statement above, 'you' refers to
explanation: The statement 'If you will go with me, I will go; but if you will not go with me, I will not go' was made by Barak to Deborah in the Bible (Judges 4:8). Therefore, 'you' in the statement refers to Deborah.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The name Ichabod means
explanation: It was a name given by the wife of Phinehas, the son of Eli, when she heard that the Ark of God had been captured by the Philistines and that her husband and father-in-law were dead (1 Samuel 4:21).

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did David perceive that his child from Bathsheba was dead?
options: Bathsheba lamented the loss of her child
he received a message to that effect from Joab
God revealed it to him through Nathan
explanation: David noticed that his servants were whispering among themselves so he perceived that the child was dead.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''As one falls before the wicked you have fallen'' This is a part of David's lamentation over
explanation: The phrase ''As one falls before the wicked you have fallen'' is a part of David's lamentation over Abner. This can be found in 2 Samuel 3:34 where David laments over the death of Abner, who was killed by Joab. David considered Abner's death as a great loss and mourned for him.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The mother of the living child asked Solomon to give the child to the other woman because
options: killing the child would serve no useful purpose
she was confident that the king would ensure justice
she believed the child would recognize her at maturity
explanation: In the biblical story, the real mother of the living child asked King Solomon to give the child to the other woman because she would rather see her child alive and in the care of another woman than see her child killed. This was a test set by King Solomon to determine the real mother, as the real mother's love would not allow harm to come to her child. Therefore, the correct answer is 'killing the child would serve no useful purpose'.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Hosea's matrimonial experience symbolizes God's intention to
explanation: Hosea's marriage to Gomer, a prostitute, was a symbolic act commanded by God to illustrate Israel's unfaithfulness to Him. Despite Gomer's repeated infidelity, Hosea was commanded to take her back, symbolizing God's enduring love and willingness to forgive Israel despite her unfaithfulness. Therefore, the correct answer is 'redeem Israel from her unfaithfulness'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The harlotry of Gomer is compared to Israel's
explanation: The harlotry of Gomer is compared to Israel's unfaithfulness to God. In the book of Hosea, Gomer, the wife of Hosea, is used as a metaphor for the unfaithfulness of the Israelites to God. Just as Gomer was unfaithful to her husband, so were the Israelites unfaithful to God by worshipping other gods and breaking the covenant they had with Him.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts; the whole earth is full of his glory'', This statement was made in the year
explanation: The statement 'Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of hosts; the whole earth is full of his glory' was made in the year Uzziah of Judah died. This statement is found in Isaiah 6:3. The context of this chapter is the vision that Isaiah had of the Lord in the year that King Uzziah died. In this vision, seraphim were praising God with these words.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Hear the word of the LORD, you rulers of Sodom! Give ear to the teaching of our God, you people of Gomorrah!''The nation referred to as Sodom and Gomorrah in the statement above is
explanation: In the book of Isaiah 1:10, the prophet Isaiah uses the names Sodom and Gomorrah metaphorically to refer to the nation of Israel. He does this to express God's displeasure with Israel's sins and their disobedience to His laws. Sodom and Gomorrah were cities known for their wickedness and were destroyed by God as a result. By comparing Israel to these cities, Isaiah was highlighting the severity of Israel's sins.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jeremiah was not anxious to accept God's call to become a prophet because
options: he knew that the people would not listen to him
explanation: He felt that he was too young/was inexperienced as a prophet. He did not have the message to the people. He did not know how to speak/not a good speaker. The task was too difficult overwhelming for him.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The devil's principal purpose for tempting Jesus Christ was to
options: prove that afterall, Jesus is only human
explanation: This is the correct answer. It shows nobody is above temptation.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man has nowhere to lay his head,'' This statement above implies that
options: the Son of man needs no house
explanation: The statement ''Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man has nowhere to lay his head,'' implies that discipleship costs much. Jesus was explaining to a would-be follower that following Him might mean giving up physical comforts and securities. This is a cost that one must be willing to pay to be a disciple of Jesus.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Luke, following Jesus involves
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke, following Jesus involves self-denial. This is based on Luke 9:23 where Jesus says, 'If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me.' Self-denial in this context means giving up one's own desires or interests for the sake of others or for a higher purpose.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the healing ministry of Jesus Christ which of the following played a very vital role?
explanation: In the healing ministry of Jesus Christ, faith played a very vital role. Many instances in the Bible, particularly in the Gospels, show that Jesus often healed people based on their faith. For example, in Matthew 9:22, Jesus heals a woman who had been suffering from a bleeding disorder for twelve years, saying 'your faith has made you well.' Therefore, faith is the correct answer.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus healed the only son of the widow of Nain because
options: He wanted to make people believe in Him
explanation: In the Bible, Luke 7:11-17, Jesus healed the only son of the widow of Nain out of compassion. When Jesus saw the widow, his heart went out to her and he said, 'Don't cry.' Then he went up and touched the bier they were carrying him on, and the bearers stood still. He said, 'Young man, I say to you, get up!' The dead man sat up and began to talk, and Jesus gave him back to his mother.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The healing of the paralytic brought by his four friends demonstrates
options: the power of Jesus to heal all types of diseases
that the friends had faith in the healing power of Jesus
the compassion of Jesus on the sick man
that Jesus has power to forgive sin
explanation: In the Bible, Mark 2:1-12, the healing of the paralytic brought by his four friends demonstrates that Jesus has power to forgive sin. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.' Some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves, 'Why does this fellow talk like that? He's blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?' Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, 'Why are you thinking these things? Which is easier: to say to this paralyzed man, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up, take your mat and walk'? But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.' So he said to the man, 'I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.' He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, 'We have never seen anything like this!'

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Mark and Luke, the response of blind Bartimaeus to his healing was to
options: run to show himself to the priests
explanation: According to the Gospel of Mark (10:52) and Luke (18:43), after Jesus healed blind Bartimaeus, he followed Jesus immediately. This shows his gratitude and faith in Jesus.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable which teaches about God's judgement is that of the
explanation: The parable of the weeds (also known as the parable of the tares) found in Matthew 13:24-30, teaches about God's judgement. In this parable, Jesus explains that at the end of the age, God will separate the 'weeds' (evil doers) from the 'wheat' (righteous), which is a metaphor for the final judgement.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable of the good Samaritan Implies that
options: priests and Levites hardly show love
neighbourliness is expressed in love and concern
a good neighbour must show love to his enemies
explanation: The parable of the good Samaritan is a story told by Jesus in the Bible, found in Luke 10:25–37. It is about a traveler who is stripped of clothing, beaten, and left half dead alongside the road. A priest and a Levite pass by, but a Samaritan stops to help him. The story is a lesson about compassion and love towards all individuals, regardless of their race or religion. Therefore, the correct answer is that 'neighbourliness is expressed in love and concern'.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Luke ends the Sermon on the Plain with an analogy of a building to emphasize the importance of
explanation: The analogy of a building at the end of the Sermon on the Plain in Luke 6:46-49 is used by Jesus to emphasize the importance of not just hearing His words, but also acting on them. He compares the person who comes to Him, hears His words, and acts on them to a man building a house, who dug deep and laid the foundation on rock. When a flood arose, the river burst against that house but could not shake it, because it had been well built. Therefore, the correct answer is 'hearing and obeying the word'.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus implies in His teaching on adultery, prayer, alms and and fasting that these are more of matters of
explanation: In His teachings on adultery, prayer, alms, and fasting, Jesus emphasizes the importance of the heart's condition. He teaches that these are not just external actions, but internal matters of the heart. For instance, in the case of adultery, He says that even looking at a woman lustfully is equivalent to committing adultery in one's heart. Similarly, prayer, alms, and fasting are not to be done for show, but with a sincere heart.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus injunction not to lay up treasures on earth for ourselves implies that the christian should
explanation: Jesus' teaching about not laying up treasures on earth implies that Christians should focus on being 'rich in good works'. This means that instead of focusing on accumulating earthly wealth, Christians should focus on doing good works that have eternal value. This is because earthly treasures are temporary and can be destroyed or stolen, but treasures in heaven are eternal and cannot be destroyed or stolen.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The seventy disciples who returned from their missionary work were told by Jesus to rejoice because
explanation: Then at the right moment Jesus said, "Rejoice that your names are recorded in heaven!" Jesus' comment reveals what scripture has already recorded.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew, Jesus entered Jerusalem riding
explanation: According to the Gospel of Matthew, specifically Matthew 21:1-7, Jesus entered Jerusalem riding on an ass and a colt. This fulfilled a prophecy from Zechariah 9:9 that the Messiah would enter Jerusalem in this manner. The ass and the colt are the same animal, the colt is a young, untrained ass. The other options are incorrect because they mention a horse, which is not mentioned in the biblical account.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prayer of Jesus before His arrest showed that
options: His disciples had not understood Him thoroughly
He was not prepared to die on the cross
He wanted His disciples to be prayerful
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 26:38, Jesus expressed his sorrow and trouble before his arrest, saying 'My soul is overwhelmed with sorrow to the point of death.' This shows that he was indeed sorrowful and troubled.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Are you the king of the Jews?'' Jesus reply to the question above by Pilate was
options: My kingship is not of this word''
''Who has made you a prince and a judge over us?''
''Why put me to the test, you hypocrites?''
explanation: In John 18:37, Jesus answered Pilate's question by saying, 'You have said so.'

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE PILATE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew, when the woman went to see the sepulchre, they
options: saw an angel sitting upon the stone
found that the disciples had been there
found the guards rolling back the stone
explanation: According to the Gospel of Matthew, specifically Matthew 28:2, when the women went to see the sepulchre, they saw an angel of the Lord descending from heaven, coming and rolling back the stone and sitting upon it. The angel's appearance was like lightning, and his clothing was white as snow. The guards were so afraid of him that they shook and became like dead men.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus used the illustration of the vine to teach that a christian that abides in Him will surely
explanation: In John 15:5, Jesus used the illustration of the vine to teach about the relationship between Him and His followers. He said, 'I am the vine, you are the branches. He who abides in Me, and I in him, bears much fruit; for without Me you can do nothing.' This means that a Christian who remains in Jesus, and in whom Jesus remains, will bear much fruit, symbolizing the good works and virtues that should characterize a Christian's life.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE TRUE VINE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Two of the seven disciples chosen to serve tables in the early church were
explanation: They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch. The congregation presented these men to the apostles. When the apostles had prayed, they laid their hands on the chosen men." (Acts 6:5-6).

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Give me also this power, that any one whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit''. Peter rebuked the speaker for being
explanation: The quote is from Acts 8:18-20, where Simon the sorcerer, seeing that the Holy Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles' hands, offered them money to buy this power. Peter rebuked him for thinking that the gift of God could be purchased with money, indicating that Simon was money conscious.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At the Beautiful Gate,Peter and John healed a man who was
explanation: True enough, it was what happened when Peter and John healed the lame man. According to Acts 3:9 (ESV), "all the people saw him walking and praising God." They "recognized him as the one who sat at the Beautiful Gate of the temple, asking for alms."

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The people referred to as pillars of the Jerusalem church, who gave Barnabas and Paul the right hand of fellowship were
explanation:
Galatians 2:9
James, Cephas and John, those esteemed as pillars, gave me and Barnabas the right hand of fellowship when they recognized the grace given to me.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The condition for justification according to Paul is
explanation: According to Apostle Paul in the New Testament, the condition for justification is faith in Christ crucified. Justification is a theological concept that means being declared righteous by God. Paul emphasizes that this is achieved not through human efforts or adherence to the law, but through faith in Jesus Christ and his sacrificial death on the cross.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: On which of the following grounds will faith be null and promise void?
options: faith is a necessary condition for justification
salvation comes through adherence to the law
explanation: According to Paul, faith would be null and promise void if salvation comes through adherence to the law. This is because Paul taught that salvation is by grace through faith in Jesus Christ, not by adherence to the law. If salvation could be achieved through the law, then faith in Christ would be unnecessary and the promise of salvation through faith would be void.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Letter of Paul to the Romans, Christians are to yield themselves to God as men who
options: have been raised from death to life
no longer live by the works of law
have listened to the gospel of Christ
explanation: In the Letter of Paul to the Romans, specifically in Romans 6:13, Paul urges Christians to yield themselves to God as those who have been raised from death to life. This is a metaphorical way of saying that Christians, through faith and baptism, have died to their old sinful selves and have been raised to a new life in Christ. This concept is often referred to as 'spiritual resurrection' or 'regeneration'.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God sent His Son , Jesus Christ, into the world in the likeness of sinful flesh in order
options: that the law might be fulfilled
that man may walk in the flesh
explanation: According to Romans 8:3-4, God sent His Son, Jesus Christ, in the likeness of sinful flesh so that the righteous requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us. This means that Jesus, by living a sinless life and dying on the cross, fulfilled the law's requirement for perfect righteousness. This righteousness is then imputed to those who trust in Christ for their salvation. This is a key concept in Christian theology known as 'justification by faith'.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''......at the name of Jesus every knee should bow ....every tongues confess that Jesus Christ is Lord .....'' The statement above demonstrates the Lordship of Jesus over
explanation: The statement is a quote from Philippians 2:10-11 in the Bible. It emphasizes the universal lordship of Jesus Christ over all creation, not just over religious persons, Christians, or non-Christians. Therefore, the correct answer is 'all creation'.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul told the Corinthians that if any one has caused pain to the church he should be
explanation: But if any one has caused pain, he has caused it not to me, but in some measure --not to put it too severely -- to you all. [6] For such a one this punishment by the majority is enough; [7] so you should rather turn to forgive and comfort him, or he may be overwhelmed by excessive sorrow.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The greatest spiritual gift to seek for is
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Corinthians 13:13, Apostle Paul states that 'And now these three remain: faith, hope and love. But the greatest of these is love.' This implies that love is the greatest spiritual gift to seek for.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the civic responsibilities Christians are commanded to carry out is
explanation: Romans 13:1-2 in the Bible instructs Christians to be obedient to lawful authority. It states 'Let everyone be subject to the governing authorities, for there is no authority except that which God has established. The authorities that exist have been established by God. Consequently, whoever rebels against the authority is rebelling against what God has instituted, and those who do so will bring judgment on themselves.' This implies that one of the civic responsibilities Christians are commanded to carry out is obedience to lawful authority.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the course of his ministry, Paul worked night and day so that
options: he might not be a burden to any one
he could raise money for the work of the gospel
he could pay the debt he was owing the brethren
explanation: Surely you remember, brothers and sisters, our toil and hardship; we worked night and day in order not to be a burden to anyone while we preached the gospel of God to you.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : BENEFITS OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Those who have fallen asleep before the coming of the Lord are those
explanation: Therefore, those who are "asleep" in 1Thess 4 are those Christians who have died. By the context, we see Paul was referring to believers who die prior to Christ's coming for the Church at the Rapture.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : PREPARATION FOR HIS COMING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: James enjoins Christians to remain impartial by
explanation: In the book of James, Christians are encouraged to show impartiality by showing love to all without distinction. This is based on the principle of the 'royal law' as stated in James 2:8, which is to love your neighbor as yourself. However, the emphasis in James is on showing love without distinction, which means not showing favoritism or bias, and treating everyone with the same love and respect, regardless of their status or background.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, the man who prays to God and at the same time doubts is
explanation: But let him ask in faith, with no doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter admonishes Christians that suffering for Christ's sake brings God's
explanation: In the Bible, Peter encourages Christians to endure suffering for Christ's sake, stating that it brings blessings from God. This is based on the belief that suffering for the sake of Christ is a form of sacrifice that God rewards with blessings.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIAN ATTITUDE TO PERSECUTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew, Jesus entered Jerusalem riding
explanation: According to the Gospel of Matthew (Matthew 21:1-11), Jesus entered Jerusalem riding on an ass and a colt. This fulfilled a prophecy from the Old Testament (Zechariah 9:9) and symbolized Jesus' humility and peace.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God made two great lights to
options: demarcate between the day and the night
explanation: God made the two great lights—the greater light (the sun) to rule the day, and the lesser light (the moon) to rule the night

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God commanded the earth to bring forth living creatures according to their kinds on the
explanation: According to the creation account in the book of Genesis, God commanded the earth to bring forth living creatures according to their kinds on the 6th day. Therefore, the correct answer is '6th day'.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When God heard the groaning of the people of Israel in Egypt, He
explanation: In the book of Exodus, it is mentioned that when God heard the groaning of the people of Israel in Egypt, He remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. This means He remembered the promises He made to these patriarchs, which included making their descendants a great nation and delivering them from bondage.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The golden calf which Aaron made for Israel was an evidence of
explanation: The golden calf which Aaron made for Israel was an evidence of lack of faith in God. When Moses was on Mount Sinai receiving the Ten Commandments, the people of Israel grew impatient and asked Aaron to make gods for them. This shows their lack of faith in God who had brought them out of Egypt.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
''Is it because there are no graves in Egypt that you have taken us away to die in the wilderness?
This question was asked by the Israelites when
options: there was no water for them to drink
the spies brought evil reports about Canaan
explanation: The Israelites asked this question when the Egyptians marched after them. This happened after Pharaoh had let them go, then changed his mind and pursued them with his army. The Israelites, seeing the Egyptians marching after them, were very afraid and complained to Moses, asking if it was because there were no graves in Egypt that he had taken them away to die in the wilderness.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God promised to send bread from heaven to the people of Israel, so as to see if they would
explanation: God announces to Moses the coming of bread from heaven. Then the LORD said to Moses, "Behold, I will rain bread from heaven for you. And the people shall go out and gather a certain quota every day, that I may test them, whether they will walk in My law or not.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Behold, I am about to do a thing in Israel, at which these were the words of God to
explanation: The words 'Behold, I am about to do a thing in Israel, at which both ears of everyone who hears it will tingle' were spoken by God to Samuel the prophet. This was a prophecy about the punishment that God was going to bring upon Eli's house because his sons, Hophni and Phinehas, were blaspheming God and he did not restrain them.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why did David spare the life of king Saul at Ziph?
options: He did not want to shed blood
explanation: David spared the life of King Saul at Ziph because Saul was anointed by God. Despite Saul's pursuit to kill him, David respected the fact that Saul was God's anointed king and did not want to harm him. This shows David's respect for God's authority and his understanding that it was not his place to take revenge.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nathan told David that although God has put away his sin, he would still be
explanation: But because by doing this you have shown utter contempt for the LORD, the son born to you will die."

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The ark of the covenant brought before Israel and king Solomon contained
explanation: The Ark of the Covenant, according to the Bible, contained the two stone tablets of the Ten Commandments, also known as the Law of Moses. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the law of Moses'.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Hosea accused Israel of harlotry because
explanation: In the book of Hosea, the prophet Hosea accused Israel of harlotry because the people worshipped other gods. This is often referred to as spiritual adultery or idolatry, where the people of Israel turned away from God and worshipped idols, breaking their covenant with God.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who among the following was consecrated and appointed a prophet before he was born?
explanation: "Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, And before you were born I consecrated you; I have appointed you a prophet to the nations.".

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Son of man, stand upon your feet, and I will speak with you''. The statement above was made during the call of
explanation: The statement ''Son of man, stand upon your feet, and I will speak with you'' was made during the call of Ezekiel. This can be found in the book of Ezekiel 2:1 in the Bible.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Satan promised to give Jesus all the kingdoms of the world if He would
options: throw Himself down from the pinnacle of the temple
explanation: During the temptation of Jesus in the wilderness, Satan promised to give Jesus all the kingdoms of the world if He would bow down and worship him. This can be found in the book of Matthew 4:8-9 in the Bible.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The would-be disciple who wanted to go and bury his father before following Jesus was told that he
options: was not fit for the kingdom of God
should go and proclaim the kingdom of God
should go and build a house first
explanation:
Luke 9:60
And Jesus said to him, "Leave the dead to bury their own dead. But as for you, go and proclaim the kingdom of God."

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew, the first two disciples to be called by Jesus were
explanation: As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, called Peter and his brother Andrew. They were casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen. "Come, follow me," Jesus said, "and I will make you fishers of men." At once they left their nets and followed him.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus had compassion for the five thousand people fed by Him because they were
options: hungry but had no time to eat
strangers from the country and villages far away
poor and had no money to buy food
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Mark 6:34, it is stated that when Jesus saw the large crowd, he had compassion on them because they were like sheep without a shepherd. This is why he decided to feed the five thousand people.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF: THE BREAD OF LIFE AND THE LIVING WATER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Jesus' response when the message of Lazarus' illness reached Him?
options: He asked Mary and Martha to take care of him
He said that the Son of God may be glorified by it
He hurried to Bethany to see him
He instructed that he should be brought to Him
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of John 11:4, when Jesus heard that Lazarus was sick, he said, 'This sickness will not end in death. No, it is for God's glory so that God's Son may be glorified through it.' Therefore, Jesus' response to the message of Lazarus' illness was that the Son of God may be glorified by it.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At the country of the Gerasenes, Jesus
options: raised the widow's son from the dead
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Mark 5:1-20 and Luke 8:26-39, it is recorded that Jesus healed a man who had demons when he visited the country of the Gerasenes. The man was possessed by a legion of demons, but Jesus cast them out into a herd of pigs.

topics.title: MIRACLES : EXORCISM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable of the wheat and the tares illustrates that
options: the kingdom of God is already established on earth
both good and evil live in the world
the wheat cannot grow where there are weeds
explanation: The parable of the wheat and the tares (weeds) is found in Matthew 13:24-30. The parable illustrates that both good (wheat) and evil (tares) live in the world. The wheat represents the children of the kingdom, while the tares represent the children of the wicked one. The parable teaches that both will grow together until the end of the world (harvest time), at which point they will be separated.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus' teaching on swearing is that Christians should
explanation: Jesus' teaching leads us to conclude that it is better not to make a vow than to swear an oath that we have no intention of keeping.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What blessing has Jesus promised the pure at heart?
explanation: In the Beatitudes, part of the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus said, 'Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.' (Matthew 5:8). This means that those who are pure in heart, those who are sincere and genuine in their faith and actions, will be blessed with the vision of God.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''The harvest is plentiful, but the labourers are few;.... Jesus made the statement above during the
explanation: The statement 'The harvest is plentiful, but the labourers are few' was made by Jesus during the commissioning of the seventy others. In the Bible, specifically in Luke 10:2, Jesus made this statement to illustrate the great need for more people to work in spreading the message of the kingdom of God.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
''....Hosanna to the Son of David!....''
The statement above indicates Jesus'
explanation: The statement 'Hosanna to the Son of David!' indicates Jesus' kingship. In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 21:9, the crowd shouted this phrase as Jesus entered Jerusalem. 'Hosanna' is an expression of adoration, praise, or joy, and 'Son of David' is a messianic title. Therefore, the crowd was acknowledging Jesus as their expected Messiah and King.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: With regard to the triumphal entry, which of the following descriptions is peculiar to Luke?
options: The disciples were sent to bring the animal
People spread their garments on the road
The disciples set Jesus on the animal
explanation: In Luke's account of the triumphal entry, it is specifically mentioned that the disciples set Jesus on the animal. This detail is not found in the other gospels.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew, when Christ was arrested He was led to
explanation: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The conviction of Jesus by the high priest and the elders was on the ground that He
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 26:65, it is stated that the high priest accused Jesus of blasphemy. Blasphemy is the act of speaking sacrilegiously about God or sacred things. The high priest and the elders convicted Jesus on the ground that He claimed to be the Son of God, which they considered as blasphemy.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the account of John about the resurrection, Mary was first asked why she was weeping by
explanation: According to John 20:11-13, Mary Magdalene was weeping outside the tomb and as she wept, she stooped to look into the tomb. And she saw two angels in white, sitting where the body of Jesus had lain, one at the head and one at the feet. They said to her, 'Woman, why are you weeping?' So, the first ones to ask Mary why she was weeping were two angels.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Lord, who knowest the hearts of all men, show which one of these two thou has chosen to take the place... from which Judas turned aside....'' Who were the two men referred to in the statement above ?
explanation: The two men referred to in the statement are Justus and Matthias. After Judas Iscariot's betrayal and subsequent death, the apostles decided to replace him to maintain their number at twelve. Two men were proposed, Joseph called Barsabbas (also known as Justus) and Matthias. Therefore, the correct answer is Justus and Matthias.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The criteria for the choice of the seven deacons were
options: good repute and being full of the spirit
explanation: According to the narrative in Acts, they were identified and selected by the community of believers on the basis of their reputation and wisdom, being 'full of the Holy Spirit', and their appointment was confirmed by the Apostles.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At Pentecost, Peter explained that God has promised to pour out His Spirit on all
explanation: In the book of Acts 2:17, Peter quoted the prophecy of Joel 2:28 during his sermon at Pentecost, explaining that God has promised to pour out His Spirit on all flesh, not just on a specific group of people. Therefore, the correct answer is 'flesh'.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The resolution of the Jerusalem council was that the Gentiles should abstain from food offered to idols,
options: blood, what is strangled and stong drinks
explanation: It is my judgment, therefore, that we should not make it difficult for the Gentiles who are turning to God. Instead we should write to them, telling them to abstain from food polluted by idols, from sexual immorality, from the meat of strangled animals and from blood.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders............. to choose men from among them and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas. The men chosen were
explanation: In the book of Acts 15:22, it is stated that the apostles and elders, along with the whole church, decided to choose some of their own men and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Judas (called Barsabbas) and Silas, men who were leaders among the believers.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter and John were arrested and imprisoned because they
explanation: These leaders were very disturbed that Peter and John were teaching the people that through Jesus there is a resurrection of the dead.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE ARREST AND IMPRISONMENT OF PETER AND JOHN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, what bear witness to the righteousness of God are
explanation: According to Apostle Paul in the book of Romans 3:21, the righteousness of God is witnessed by the Law and the Prophets. This means that the teachings and prophecies in the Law (the first five books of the Old Testament) and the books of the Prophets bear witness to God's righteousness.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The consequence of setting one's mind on the things of the flesh is
explanation: In Romans 8:5-6, Paul states that those who live according to the flesh have their minds set on what the flesh desires; but those who live in accordance with the Spirit have their minds set on what the Spirit desires. The mind governed by the flesh is death, but the mind governed by the Spirit is life and peace. Therefore, setting one's mind on the things of the flesh leads to death.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul admonished the Romans to live a new life in Christ by yielding themselves to God as men who
options: will receive judgement in the last days
have suffered for the sake of the gospel
explanation: In Romans 6:13, Paul admonishes the Romans to yield themselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life. This means that they should live a new life in Christ, having been redeemed from the power of sin and death. The concept here is the Christian belief in spiritual rebirth, where a believer is said to move from a state of spiritual death (separation from God due to sin) to spiritual life (reconciliation with God through faith in Christ).

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian...'' Custodian here means the
explanation: The 'custodian' in Galatians 3:25 refers to the law. Before the coming of faith, the law was our custodian, meaning it guided and protected us. But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian, which means we are no longer under the law but under grace. The concept here is the Christian belief in the transition from the Old Covenant (Law) to the New Covenant (Grace) through the coming of Christ.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul, writing to the Philippians, said that God highly exalted Christ and bestowed on Him the name which is above every name because He was
explanation: In Philippians 2:5-11, Paul explains that Christ, though He was in the form of God, did not count equality with God a thing to be grasped, but emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant, being born in the likeness of men. And being found in human form, He humbled Himself by becoming obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross. Therefore, God has highly exalted Him and bestowed on Him the name that is above every name. So, the reason for Christ's exaltation and the bestowing of the name above every name was His obedience and humility.

topics.title: HUMILITY : REWARDS OF HUMILITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, Christians should forgive one another so that
options: Satan may not gain advantage over them
explanation: In 2 Corinthians 2:10-11, Paul advises the Corinthians to forgive one another so that Satan may not gain advantage over them, for they are not ignorant of his designs. Therefore, the reason Christians should forgive one another is so that Satan may not gain advantage over them.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Spiritual gifts, according to Paul, should be used for the
explanation: In the New Testament, Apostle Paul emphasized that spiritual gifts are given for the edification of the church. This means that they are meant to build up, strengthen, and improve the church as a whole, rather than for the upliftment of individuals or eradication of enmity among Christians. This concept is found in 1 Corinthians 14:12, where Paul says, 'So with yourselves, since you are eager for manifestations of the Spirit, strive to excel in building up the church.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'edification of the church'.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE BENEFITS OF SPIRITUAL GIFTS TO THE INDIVIDUAL AND THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul considered the Philippians' gifts to him as
options: an exhibition of Christian maturity and generosity
a fragrant offering and a sacrifice acceptable to God
explanation: In Philippians 4:18, Paul refers to the gifts he received from the Philippians as 'a fragrant offering, a sacrifice acceptable and pleasing to God.' He did not see it as a duty they owed him or indispensable to his life, nor did he specifically link it to an exhibition of Christian maturity and generosity.
