Therefore, the correct answer is 'a fragrant offering and a sacrifice acceptable to God'.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'For rulers are not terrors to good conduct, but to bed'. Christians, therefore, must do what is good and they would
options: be left alone to preach the gospel
explanation: The quote is from Romans 13:3 in the Bible. It implies that if Christians do what is good, they will receive approval from their rulers. This is because rulers are a terror not to good conduct, but to bad. When Christians do good, they align themselves with the purpose of the rulers, which is to punish bad conduct and approve good conduct.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Now we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you keep away from any brother who is living in idleness....'' The statement above was made to teach
explanation: The quote is from 2 Thessalonians 3:6 in the Bible. It is a command to Christians to keep away from any brother who is living in idleness, which means not working or being lazy. This teaches the dignity of labour, that every Christian should work and not live in idleness. It does not primarily teach about Christian charity, justice, or humility.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : CONCEPT OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The second coming of Jesus Christ according to Paul, will be preceded by the coming of
explanation: According to Paul in 2 Thessalonians 2:3-4, the second coming of Jesus Christ will be preceded by the coming of the man of lawlessness. This man of lawlessness, also known as the Antichrist, will oppose and exalt himself above all that is called God or that is worshipped, so that he sits as God in the temple of God, showing himself that he is God.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Is it not the rich who oppress you, is it not they who drag you into court?'' James asked this question in his teachings on
explanation: James 2:6-7 addresses the issue of partiality and favoritism towards the rich, and oppression by the rich. James asked this question to illustrate the irony of favoring those who oppress the believers and drag them into court. Therefore, the correct answer is impartiality.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The wisdom of God is necessary for salvation, hence James teaches that Christians should
explanation: "If any of you is lacking in wisdom, ask God, who gives to all generously and ungrudgingly, and it will be given you" (James 1:5).

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The importance of love in the teachings of Peter is that it
options: brings together all believers in the community
explanation: "Most important of all, continue to show deep love for each other, for love covers a multitude of sins." (1 Peter 4:8, NLT).

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter, when the chief shepherd is manifested,Christians will
explanation: In 1 Peter 5:4, Peter states that when the Chief Shepherd (Jesus Christ) appears, Christians will receive the crown of glory that will never fade away. This crown is a metaphor for the eternal life and glory that believers will receive in heaven.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : THE BENEFITS OF BEING JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, what bear witness to the righteousness of God are
explanation: But now the righteousness of God has been manifested apart from the law, although the Law and the Prophets bear witness to it

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Joseph narrated his second dream, he was rebuked by Jacob because the dream would
options: make the brothers all the more jealous
send the family to Egypt as slaves
explanation: In the Bible, Genesis 37:10, when Joseph shared his second dream with his father Jacob and his brothers, the dream suggested that his parents and his brothers would bow down to him. This implies that they would be subject to his authority. Jacob rebuked him because he was surprised and probably upset that Joseph was suggesting that he and his mother would bow down to him.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Jacob woke from sleep on his way to Haran, he was afraid and named the place Bethel because he believed that the
options: place had a ladder that led to heaven
explanation:
When Jacob awoke from his sleep, he thought, 'Surely the LORD is in this place, and I was not aware of it.' 17 He was afraid and said, 'How awesome is this place! This is none other than the house of God; this is the gate of heaven.'
18 Early the next morning Jacob took the stone he had placed under his head and set it up as a pillar and poured oil on top of it. 19 He called that place Bethel, though the city used to be called Luz.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Deborah was a judge when Israel was under the yoke of
explanation: Deborah was a judge in Israel during the period of Canaanite oppression. The Canaanites were led by their king Jabin, and the commander of his army was Sisera. This is recorded in the Book of Judges, Chapter 4 in the Bible.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
''Behold, I have given you every plant yielding seed which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree with seed in its fruit....
In the statement above, God made all provision for man to
explanation: The statement is a quote from Genesis 1:29 in the Bible. In this verse, God is giving man every plant that yields seed and every tree that has fruit with seed for food. Therefore, the provision made by God in this context is for man to have enough food.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Lord appeared to Abraham and promised his descendants the land of the Canaanites at
explanation:
God made this promise to Abraham when he was in Oak of Moreh, in Shechem.

Genesis 12:6-7 "6. Abram traveled through the land as far as the site of the great tree of Moreh at Shechem. At that time the Canaanites were in the land. 7. The LORD appeared to Abram and said, "To your offspring I will give this land." So he built an altar there to the LORD, who had appeared to him."

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did God promise the Israelites after their rescue from Babylon to make them fear Him for ever?
options: a new convenant and a new spirit
a new heart and a new life
explanation: In Jeremiah 32:39, God promised the Israelites 'one heart and one way' after their rescue from Babylon. This was to ensure that they and their descendants would fear Him forever for their own good and the good of their descendants. Therefore, the correct answer is 'one heart and one way'.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God told Noah that ''Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed;...''Because
explanation: "Whoever sheds human blood, by a human his own blood is to be shed; because God made human beings in his own image

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The division of David's kingdom was direct consequence of
explanation: The division of David's kingdom was a direct consequence of Rehoboam's unwise decision. After Solomon's death, his son Rehoboam became king. The people, led by Jeroboam, asked him to lighten the harsh labor and heavy taxes imposed by Solomon. Rehoboam refused and instead threatened to increase the burden. This led to the northern tribes revolting and appointing Jeroboam as their king, thus dividing the kingdom.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Abner sent messagers to David at Hebron because he wanted to
options: spy on Hebron and capture it later
explanation: Then Abner sent messengers on his behalf to say to David, "Whose land is it? Make an agreement with me, and I will help you bring all Israel over to you."

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The final consequence of Saul's disobedience to God was his
explanation: Disobedience leads to suffering and condemnation from God. Saul suffered greatly because God allowed evil spirit to torment him. He lost his life at the end.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where did the Lord appear to Solomon in a dream?
explanation: The king went to Gibeon to offer sacrifices, for that was the most important high place, and Solomon offered a thousand burnt offerings on that altar. At Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon during the night in a dream, and God said, "Ask for whatever you want me to give you."

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God forgive David when he took Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah because
options: the child he had with her died
David fasted and laid upon the ground
David was a man after God's heart
explanation: God forgave David when he took Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah because David accepted his guilt. This is recorded in 2 Samuel 12:13 where David says to Nathan, 'I have sinned against the Lord.' And Nathan said to David, 'The Lord also has put away your sin; you shall not die.'

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God rejected the sons of Eli from being priests over His people because they were
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Samuel 2:12-17, Eli's sons were described as wicked men who had no regard for the Lord or for the duties of the priests to the people. They were blasphemous in their disrespect for the offerings and sacrifices made to God, and they were unrestrained in their sinful behavior, including sleeping with the women who served at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. Therefore, the correct answer is 'blasphemous and unrestrained'.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The reforms of King Josiah were made possible by the
options: discovery of the book of the law
explanation: The book of the law was read to him and when he heard the content he rent his clothes as a sign of repentance because they knew that they had transgressed. This encouraged him to carry out more reforms.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Amos told the Israelites that they have turned justice into
explanation: The Israelites turn justice into bitterness; they throw what is fair and right to the ground. ... Ah, you that turn justice to wormwood

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Naaman's leprosy was transferred to Gehazi as a
options: the bath Gahazi took in the River Jordan
the gifts is giving to Gahazi in the River Jordan
explanation: In the Bible, Gehazi's leprosy was transferred to him as a result of his disobedience to God. Gehazi, the servant of Elisha, had lied to Naaman and taken gifts from him after Elisha had healed Naaman of his leprosy. When Elisha found out, he cursed Gehazi, saying that Naaman's leprosy would cling to Gehazi and his descendants forever.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prayer of Elijah in the name of God of Abraham., Isaac and Jacob brought down fire from heaven which
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Kings 18:36-38, it is recorded that Elijah prayed to God and fire came down from heaven to consume the burnt offering. This was during a contest between Elijah and the prophets of Baal to determine who the true God was. The fire from heaven was a sign that God had accepted Elijah's sacrifice.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: As Nehemiah embarked on reconstruction of Jerusalem, he faced threats and opposition from
explanation: But it so happened, when Sanballat heard that we were rebuilding the wall, that he was furious and very indignant, and mocked the Jews.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did God convince Jeremiah of his divine call?
options: He spoke to him directly from heaven
He sent a prophet to inform him
explanation: According to the Bible, in Jeremiah 1:9, God touched Jeremiah's mouth to convince him of his divine call.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''....even though you make many prayers, I will not listen;...'' Why will God the Holy One of Israel not listen?
options: because they sacrifice with incense to Him
because God detests their burnt offerings of rams
because their hands are full of blood
because their new moon gifts are a burden to Him
explanation: And when ye spread forth your hands, I will hide mine eyes from you; yea, when ye make many prayers, I will not hear: your hands are full of blood.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The vision of the dry bones of Ezekiel portrays
explanation: Again, the valley of dry bones describes the restoration of God's people, but it reminds us of His promise to raise all who die in Jesus Christ.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The attribute of God which Isaiah emphasized most is His
explanation: God's Holiness Defines Himself. In Isaiah 6 the seraphim are saying God is holy, holy, holy - in other words, He is the absolutely holiest.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Hosea compared Judah's love for the Lord to the morning cloud because is
options: goes up to God like a smoke
explanation: "Israel and Judah, what am I going to do with you? Your love for me disappears as quickly as morning mist; it is like dew

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus commissioned the twelve disciples charging them to
options: go to all the world and preach
go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel
receive hospitality and wages for their labour
explanation: In the Gospel of Matthew 10:5-6, Jesus commissioned the twelve disciples and instructed them not to go among the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans, but rather go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel. Therefore, the correct answer is 'go to the lost sheep of the house of Israel'.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable of the lost sheep portrays the shepherd as being
explanation: The parable of the lost sheep portrays the shepherd as being loving. In the parable, the shepherd leaves the 99 sheep to go and find the one that is lost, showing his love and care for each individual sheep.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given..'' This statement was made after Jesus had told the parable of the
explanation: The statement ''To you it has been given to know the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given..'' was made after Jesus had told the parable of the sower. In this parable, Jesus explains that the secrets of the kingdom of heaven are given to those who are open to receive them (like the good soil in the parable), but not to those who are not (like the path, rocky ground, and thorns in the parable).

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
''Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth,...''
This statement shows that Jesus
options: does not want Christians to be rich
wants Christians to seek first the kingdom of God
regards poverty as a condition for the kingdom of God
explanation: The statement 'Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth,...' is a part of Jesus' teachings in the Sermon on the Mount. It is followed by '...but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven...'. The essence of this teaching is not that Jesus forbids wealth or worldly possessions, but rather that He encourages His followers to prioritize seeking the kingdom of God over earthly wealth. This is further emphasized in the following verse, '...seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things shall be added to you.' Therefore, the correct answer is that Jesus wants Christians to seek first the kingdom of God.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLE ABOUT WEALTH: THE RICH FOOL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When the man with an unclean spirit in the region of the Gerasenes saw Jesus, he
options: acknowledged Him as the Son of the Most High God
pleaded that a miracle be performed to cleanse him
requested to follow Him as a disciple
pleaded to be cast into the abyss
explanation: When he saw Jesus, he cried out, fell down before Him, and with a loud voice said, "What have I to do with You, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg You, do not torment me!

topics.title: MIRACLES : EXORCISM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the feeding of the five thousand, according to Matthew, the first thing that Jesus did was to
options: order the loaves and fish brought to Him
break the loaves and fish brought to Him
look up to heaven and pray to God
give thanks to God and have the fish and loaves shared
explanation: According to the account in Matthew 14:19, the first thing Jesus did was to look up to heaven and pray to God. This is a common practice in many religious traditions where one looks up to heaven (symbolizing a connection with the divine) and prays before performing a significant act or making an important decision.

topics.title: MIRACLES : MIRACLES OF RESUSCITATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Peter's reaction when he saw the great shoal of fish he and his partners caught?
explanation: In Luke 5:8, when Peter saw the great shoal of fish he and his partners caught, he fell at Jesus' knees and said, 'Go away from me, Lord; I am a sinful man!' This shows that Peter was overwhelmed by the miracle and recognized his unworthiness in the presence of Jesus, hence he confessed being a sinner.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to John, the six stone jars of water which Jesus turned into the wine were kept there for the
explanation: Turning water into wine was the first of John's seven signs (miracles) proving Jesus' divinity. The water jars were used for ritual purification

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE LIGHT OF THE WORLD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
''.....For he was numbered among us, and was allotted his share in this ministry...''
What happened to the person referred to in the statement above?
explanation: The statement is a reference to Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve apostles of Jesus. After betraying Jesus, Judas felt remorse and committed suicide. Therefore, the correct answer is that he committed suicide.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What were the major issues of controversy that led to the appearance of Paul and Barnabas before the Council in Jerusalem?
options: the Law of Moses and pollutions by idols
explanation: After Paul's first mission, he and Barnabas traveled to Jerusalem to speak on an issue that had been plaguing their converts. Would Gentiles, those who had not been converts to Judaism, have to be circumcised and uphold the Law of Moses be Christians?

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The disciples in the early church chose the seven deacons in order to
options: solve the problem of murmuring by the Hellenists
take proper care of the neglected widows
devote themselves to prayer and teaching the word
explanation: The disciples in the early church chose the seven deacons in order to devote themselves to prayer and teaching the word. This is recorded in Acts 6:1-4. The Hellenistic Jews among them complained against the Hebraic Jews because their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution of food. So the Twelve gathered all the disciples together and said, 'It would not be right for us to neglect the ministry of the word of God in order to wait on tables. Brothers and sisters, choose seven men from among you who are known to be full of the Spirit and wisdom. We will turn this responsibility over to them and will give our attention to prayer and the ministry of the word.'

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to John, the distinctive quality of the disciples of Jesus Christ is
explanation: In the Gospel of John, Jesus is quoted as saying, 'A new command I give you: Love one another. As I have loved you, so you must love one another. By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.' (John 13:34-35). Therefore, the distinctive quality of the disciples of Jesus Christ, according to John, is showing love to one another.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AMONG CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Mark, after Jesus has triumphantly entered Jerusalem, He
options: wept over the city and prohesied its destruction
addressed the Pharisees who had asked Him to rebuke His disciples
explanation: According to the Gospel of Mark, after Jesus triumphantly entered Jerusalem, He went to Bethany with the twelve. This is recorded in Mark 11:11, 'Then Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple courts. He looked around at everything, but since it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the Twelve.'

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which disciple asked Jesus, ''Lord, we do not know where you are going; how can we know the way?
explanation: Thomas said to Him, 'Lord, we do not know where You are going, how do we know the way? ' Jesus said to him, 'I am the way, and the truth, and the life; no one comes to the Father but through Me.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE WAY, THE TRUTH AND THE LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew, the inscription over Jesus when he was crucified was
options: '' Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews''
''This is Jesus the King of the Jews"
explanation: According to Matthew 27:37, the inscription over Jesus when he was crucified was 'This is Jesus the King of the Jews'. Therefore, the correct answer is 'This is Jesus the King of the Jews'.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sound which came from heaven on the day of Pentecost is likened to that of
explanation: And suddenly from heaven there came a sound like the rush of a violent wind, and it filled the entire house where they were sitting.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul advises Christians to obey authorities because
explanation: In Romans 13:1-2, Paul advises Christians to obey authorities because they are instituted by God, hence they have a divine mandate. Therefore, the correct answer is 'they have divine mandate'.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Speaking in tongues according to Paul, is meaningful where
options: there is one with the gift of interpretation
the Holy Spirit is present during worship
explanation: According to Apostle Paul in the Bible, speaking in tongues is meaningful where there is one with the gift of interpretation. This is because the person with the gift of interpretation can interpret the tongues (unknown language) so that everyone present can understand the message being conveyed. This is found in 1 Corinthians 14:27-28.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul says that anyone who does acts of mercy should do so with
explanation: In Romans 12:8, Apostle Paul says that anyone who does acts of mercy should do so cheerfully. This means that acts of mercy should be done with a joyful and willing heart, not out of compulsion or reluctance.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Galatians, what makes all believers sons of God is
explanation: According to Galatians 3:26, 'For you are all sons of God through faith in Christ Jesus.' This means that faith in Christ Jesus is what makes all believers sons of God. The other options do not align with the teachings in the book of Galatians.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God has extended the promise He made to Abraham to all those who share his faith based on
explanation: In Romans 4:13, it is stated that 'For the promise to Abraham or to his descendants that he would be heir of the world was not through the Law, but through the righteousness of faith.' This means that God extended the promise He made to Abraham to all those who share his faith based on righteousness of faith. The other options do not align with the teachings in the book of Romans.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter says that Christians would put to silence the ignorance of foolish men by
explanation: In the Bible, 1 Peter 2:15 states 'For so is the will of God, that with well doing ye may put to silence the ignorance of foolish men'. Therefore, Christians would put to silence the ignorance of foolish men by doing right.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIAN ATTITUDE TO PERSECUTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: James in his Epistle urged the brethren to count their trials all joy because the testing of their faith produces
explanation: "Count it all joy, my brothers, when you meet trials of various kinds, for you know that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness. And let steadfastness have its full effect, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing" (James 1:2–4, ESV).

q: ''And the dead in Christ will rise; then we who are alive, who are left, shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air...'' From the statement above, Paul's belief was that he would
options: live to witness the second coming of Christ
have died before the second coming of Christ
resurrect at the second coming of Christ
have been in paradise before the second coming
explanation: In the statement, Paul mentions 'we who are alive, who are left', which indicates that he believed he would be alive to witness the second coming of Christ. The concept here is the belief in the second coming of Christ, which is a fundamental doctrine in Christianity that refers to the future return of Jesus to Earth after his 'first coming' and ascension to heaven about two thousand years ago.

Therefore, the correct answer is 'Joel'.

explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of John 11:1-6, when Jesus heard that Lazarus was sick, He stayed two more days in the place where He was. He later explained to His disciples that Lazarus' sickness was for the glory of God, that the Son of God may be glorified through it. This indicates that Jesus delayed going to Lazarus to manifest His power over death and to strengthen the faith of His disciples in Him.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Let us make man in our image, after our likeness..."
In the statement above, in our image implies man
options: being a representative of God and fellow men
explanation: The phrase 'Let us make man in our image, after our likeness' is from the Bible, specifically Genesis 1:26. In this context, 'in our image' means that man was created to be a representative of God on earth, not to become God or to be exactly like God. This is because humans, while made in the image of God, are not divine and do not possess all the attributes of God. Therefore, the correct answer is 'being a representative of God and fellow men'.

options: inability to get to the promise land
explanation: The murmuring of the Israelites in the wilderness against Moses and Aaron, as described in the Bible, was a sign of their lack of faith in them. They doubted Moses and Aaron's ability to lead them to the promised land and often complained about their circumstances, despite the miracles they had witnessed. Therefore, the correct answer is 'lack of faith in them'.

explanation: The disciples celebrated the passover with Jesus because they wanted to keep the passover feast with Him. The Passover is a Jewish holiday that commemorates the liberation of the Israelites from Egyptian slavery, as told in the Book of Exodus. Jesus, being a Jew, observed this holiday with his disciples.

options: incessant fighting of wars against his enemies

explanation: King Solomon's reign was marked by peace and prosperity, but one of his policies that was considered unwise was the use of forced labour to build the house of God. This policy led to discontent among the people and eventually contributed to the division of the kingdom after his death. Therefore, the correct answer is the use of forced labour to build the house of God.

explanation: In the biblical account of creation in Genesis 2:18-20, after God said, "It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper fit for him", the first thing He did was to form all the beasts of the field and the birds of the air and brought them to the man to see what he would call them. Therefore, the correct answer is option 1: 'form beasts and bird and bring them to the man'.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After healing the man at the pool of Bethsaida, the Jews sought all the more to kill Jesus because
options: they hated the man that was healed
they were unhappy that He healed the man
He claimed that God his father was still working
explanation: After healing the man at the pool of Bethsaida, the Jews sought all the more to kill Jesus because He claimed that God his father was still working. This was considered blasphemy by the Jews as it was equating Himself with God. The Sabbath law was very important to the Jews, and they believed that God rested on the seventh day. So, when Jesus said that His Father is always at His work to this very day, and He too is working, it was a direct challenge to their belief system.

explanation: The glory of the LORD was manifested when God provided the Israelites with bread from heaven, also known as manna. This event is recorded in Exodus 16:4-15. In this passage, the Israelites were grumbling about their lack of food in the wilderness, and God responded by promising to rain down bread from heaven for them. The next morning, when the dew lifted, there was a fine flaky substance on the ground, which was the bread that God had provided. This miraculous provision of food demonstrated God's glory and care for His people.

explanation: The baptism of Jesus by John in River Jordan was to serve as an example. This is because Jesus, being sinless, did not need baptism for the remission of sins. This act of Jesus symbolizes the believer's identification with Christ in His death, burial, and resurrection.
