q: Immediately after the contest on Mount Carmel, Elijah prayed and God answered him with
explanation:
Bible Passage: 1st Kings 18: 41 - 46
And Elijah said unto Ahab, Get thee up, eat and drink; for there is a sound of abundance of rain.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Prophet Amos told Israel to hate evil, love good and establish justice in the gate so that God would
explanation:
Bible Passage: Amos 5: 15
Hate the evil, and love the good, and establish judgement in the gate:it may be that the LORD God of hosts will be gracious unto the remnant of Joseph.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In addition to the declaration of freedom to the exiles in Babylon, King Cyrus ordered for
options: freewill offering for the house of God in Jerusalem
the release of Jewish slaves in the empire
security check on the Jews before departure
explanation:
Bible Passage: Ezra 1: 2 - 4
Thus saith Cyprus king of Persia, The LORD God of heaven hath given me all the kingdom,and put it also in writing,saying.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Am I God, to kill and to make alive, that this man sends words to me to cure a man of his leprosy..."
Prophet Elisha responded to the king's reaction in the statement above by
options: directing the nation of Israel to pray and fast
requesting the leper to come to his house
directing the leper to offer a sacrifice
explanation:
Bible Passage: 2nd Kings 5: 6 - 8
And he brought the letter to the king of Israel, saying, Now when this letter is come unto thee, Behold, i have my servant to thee, that thou mayest recover him of his leprosy.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Prophet Jeremiah, one of the promises of God was that He would give the Israelites
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah, God promises to give the Israelites 'shepherds after His own heart' who will guide them with knowledge and understanding (Jeremiah 3:15). Therefore, the correct answer is 'shepherds after His own heart'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Prophet Hosea named his second child Not Pitied because God would no more pity the
explanation:
Bible Passage: Hosea 1: 6
And she conceived again, and bare a daughter. And God said unto him, call her name Lo-ruhamah: for i will no more have mercy upon the house of Israel; but i will utterly take them away.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Test your servants for ten days; let us be given vegetables to eat and water to drink..."
In the statement above, Daniel was talking to
explanation:
Bible Passage: Daniel 1: 11 - 13
Prove thy servants, i beseech thee, ten days; and let them give us pulse to eat, and water to drink.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was God's purpose in creating man?
options: To help Him look after the garden
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically Genesis 1:26-28, God created man in His own image and gave him dominion over all other creatures. This implies that man was created to be master of all creation.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Isaac's prayer to God for his barren wife showed that he
explanation: Isaac's prayer to God for his barren wife, as recorded in Genesis 25:21, is an act of faith. He believed in God's power to intervene in human situations, including barrenness. Therefore, this act showed his faith in God.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In God's covenant with Israel, the central theme was
explanation: In the Bible, God's covenant with Israel was centered around obedience. God promised to bless Israel if they obeyed His laws and commandments. This is seen in several books of the Bible, including Exodus, Leviticus, and Deuteronomy. Therefore, the central theme of God's covenant with Israel was obedience.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh..."
When Moses resisted God's call with the statement above, it portrayed his
explanation: When Moses said, 'Who am I that I should go to Pharaoh...', he was expressing his feelings of inadequacy and lack of eloquence. He felt he was not a good speaker and would not be able to convince Pharaoh to let the Israelites go. This is seen in Exodus 4:10 where Moses said to the Lord, 'Pardon your servant, Lord. I have never been eloquent, neither in the past nor since you have spoken to your servant. I am slow of speech and tongue.' Therefore, this statement portrayed his lack of eloquence.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF MOSES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Samson's major achievement as Israel's leader?
options: He saved the Israelites from the Amorites
explanation: Samson's major achievement as Israel's leader was that he suppressed the Philistines. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Judges chapters 13-16. Samson was a judge of Israel and he is known for his superhuman strength, which he used to fight against the Philistines, who were enemies of Israel at that time.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God guided the Israelites on their way from Egypt to the promised land by
options: a pillar of cloud and a pillar of fire
miracles that gave them direction on the way
explanation: According to the Bible, in the book of Exodus, God guided the Israelites on their way from Egypt to the promised land by a pillar of cloud during the day and a pillar of fire during the night. These served as guides for the Israelites, showing them the direction they should go.

topics.title: DIVINE PROVIDENCE, GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION :GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Deal gently for my sake with the young man Absalom."
David's statement above exhibited his
explanation: David's statement 'Deal gently for my sake with the young man Absalom' shows his indulgent love for his son Absalom. Despite Absalom's rebellion against him, David still cared for his son and did not want him to be harmed. This is a clear indication of his indulgent love for his son, not his willingness to forgive his competitors, desire to punish Absalom himself, or high level of parental control.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did king Asa prove to be a godly king?
options: He killed all the prophets of Baal at Mount Carmel
He saved Judah from the Philistine menace
He instituted spiritual reforms that removed all idols and prostitution in the land
He made the worship of Yahweh mandatory for every Jew and sojourner
explanation: King Asa proved to be a godly king by instituting spiritual reforms that removed all idols and prostitution in the land. He was known for his religious reforms and efforts to rid his kingdom of idolatry and pagan practices. This is not about killing prophets of Baal at Mount Carmel, saving Judah from the Philistine menace, or making the worship of Yahweh mandatory for every Jew and sojourner.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Hebrew midwives who obeyed God rather than Pharaoh were blessed with
explanation: In the book of Exodus 1:15-21, the Hebrew midwives, Shiphrah and Puah, feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the male children alive. Therefore, God dealt well with the midwives and the people multiplied and grew very mighty. And because the midwives feared God, He provided households for them. So, the correct answer is 'families'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Saul disobeyed God's command, Samuel rebuked him by saying that
options: the sword would never depart from his house
he should fear the LORD and obey the people's voice
though he was little, yet he was the head
explanation: In 1 Samuel 15:22, after Saul disobeyed God's command to completely destroy the Amalekites and their livestock, Samuel rebuked him by saying, 'Has the LORD as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the LORD? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen than the fat of rams.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'to obey was better than sacrifice'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David's behaviour after Prophet Nathan accused him of murder and adultery showed his
explanation: David's behavior after Prophet Nathan accused him of murder and adultery showed his repentance. In the Bible, after Nathan confronted David about his sins, David confessed and repented. This is a demonstration of remorse and a desire to make amends, which is what repentance is about.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did King Solomon demonstrate his wisdom in setting the dispute over the ownership of a baby by the two prostitutes?
options: He interrogated them for seven days until the truth was discovered.
He interrogated them for only three days and the truth was revealed.
He asked them to divide the child into two and the mother was known.
He threatened to give the child to a neutral woman and the mother was known
explanation: King Solomon demonstrated his wisdom in settling the dispute over the ownership of a baby by the two prostitutes by suggesting to divide the child into two. This was a test to reveal the true mother who would rather lose the dispute than see her child killed. This is a demonstration of Solomon's wisdom in understanding human nature and using it to reveal the truth.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prophet who made known the imminent division of the kingdom of Israel during Solomon's reign was
explanation: The prophet who made known the imminent division of the kingdom of Israel during Solomon's reign was Ahijah. This is recorded in 1 Kings 11:29-31, where Ahijah the Shilonite prophesied to Jeroboam that he would become king over ten tribes of Israel.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Naaman's leprosy was transferred to Gehazi because Gehazi was
explanation: Naaman's leprosy was transferred to Gehazi because Gehazi was covetous. This is recorded in 2 Kings 5:20-27, where Gehazi, the servant of Elisha, lied to Naaman to get gifts that Elisha had refused. When Elisha found out, he cursed Gehazi and his descendants with Naaman's leprosy.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Obadiah who was in charge of Ahab's household demonstrated reverence for the LORD by
options: giving Elijah the bull for the sacrifice on Mount Carmel
rebuking Ahab and Jezebel for their evil deeds
hiding a hundred prophets of God from Jezebel's wrath
helping Elijah to kill the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel
explanation: Obadiah demonstrated reverence for the LORD by hiding a hundred prophets of God from Jezebel's wrath. Jezebel was a queen who was known for her worship of Baal and her attempts to kill the prophets of God. Obadiah, however, feared the LORD greatly and when Jezebel cut off the prophets of the LORD, Obadiah took a hundred prophets and hid them by fifties in a cave, and fed them with bread and water (1 Kings 18:4).

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Although Josiah removed the vessels of Baal and Asherah from the Temple, God did not turn from the fierceness of His wrath because
options: the people did not remove them completely
explanation: God did not turn from the fierceness of His wrath because Manasseh had provoked Him. Manasseh was a king of Judah who did evil in the sight of the LORD. He rebuilt the high places which his father Hezekiah had destroyed; he raised up altars for Baal, made a wooden image, as Ahab king of Israel had done, and worshipped all the host of heaven and served them (2 Kings 21:1-6). Despite Josiah's efforts to remove the vessels of Baal and Asherah from the Temple, the sins of Manasseh had provoked God's wrath against Judah.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Nehemiah's initial reaction to the news about the ruins of Jerusalem?
options: He tasked the people on what to contribute.
He fasted and prayed for forgiveness of sins
He asked for permission to travel home
explanation: In the book of Nehemiah, when Nehemiah heard about the state of Jerusalem, his initial reaction was to fast and pray for the forgiveness of sins. He was deeply saddened by the news and turned to God in prayer, confessing the sins of the people of Israel and asking for God's mercy and help. This is recorded in Nehemiah 1:4-11.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After the dedication of the Temple, Ezra studied the law of the LORD in order to
options: teach His status and ordinances in Israel
explanation: Ezra was a scribe and a priest who was deeply devoted to the law of the LORD. After the dedication of the Temple, Ezra studied the law not for personal gain or status, but to teach His statutes and ordinances in Israel. His aim was to educate the people about God's laws and to ensure that they were being followed. This is recorded in Ezra 7:10.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Daniel became distinguished above all other presidents and satraps because
options: the spirit of Darius was in him
explanation: In the book of Daniel 6:3, it is stated that Daniel became distinguished above all other presidents and satraps because an excellent spirit was in him. This means that Daniel had a unique character and wisdom that set him apart from others.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did the Ninevites react to Jonah's message?
options: They ordered him out of their city
explanation: In the book of Jonah 3:5-10, it is stated that the Ninevites believed Jonah's message and repented of their sins. They declared a fast, and all of them, from the greatest to the least, put on sackcloth to show their repentance.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Prophet Hosea's analysis, harlotry is equivalent to Israel's
explanation: In the book of Hosea, the prophet Hosea uses the metaphor of harlotry to describe Israel's idolatrous practices. Harlotry in this context is symbolic of Israel's unfaithfulness to God through idol worship.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"...But let justice roll down like waters and righteousness like an overflowing stream."
Amos' statement above suggests that the society was terribly
explanation: The statement by Amos is a call for justice and righteousness, suggesting that the society was oppressive. The imagery of justice rolling down like waters and righteousness like an overflowing stream indicates a desire for a significant and overwhelming change in the society.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Isaiah emphasized holiness above all things because God
options: knew their hearts were right with him
explanation: Isaiah emphasized holiness above all things because God who called them is holy. This is based on the biblical principle that God is holy and those who serve Him must also be holy. The concept of holiness in this context refers to being set apart for God's service, living a life that is pleasing to God, and avoiding sin.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God told Ezekiel that the house of Israel would not listen to him because they
explanation: God told Ezekiel that the house of Israel would not listen to him because they were of a stubborn heart. This is based on the biblical account where God describes the Israelites as a rebellious and stubborn people who have turned away from His commands. The concept of a 'stubborn heart' in this context refers to a state of being resistant to God's word and unwilling to obey His commands.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Isaiah, God sent him to
explanation: According to the book of Isaiah, specifically Isaiah 61:1, God sent Isaiah to 'proclaim liberty to the captives'. This was part of Isaiah's mission to bring good news to the poor and bind up the brokenhearted.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus was tempted by the devil so as to
options: show His power over forces of nature
show that no one is above temptation
explanation: The temptation of Jesus by the devil as recorded in Matthew 4:1-11 was not to fulfill all righteousness, show His power over forces of nature, or test the devil's power. Rather, it was to show that Jesus, though being divine, was also human and subject to temptation, yet without sin. This demonstrates that no one, not even the Son of God, is above temptation.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord."
This statement was uttered by Peter at the
explanation: The statement "Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord." was uttered by Peter at the call of the disciples. This happened when Jesus performed a miracle by catching a large number of fish after Peter and his partners had toiled all night and caught nothing. Peter was astonished and overwhelmed by the miracle, and he recognized his own sinfulness in the presence of Jesus, hence the statement.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The impact of Jesus' miracle of turning water into wine was that the
explanation: The impact of Jesus' miracle of turning water into wine was that the disciples believed more in Him. This miracle, which is recorded in John 2:1-11, was the first of Jesus' signs through which He revealed His glory, and His disciples believed in Him. The miracle demonstrated Jesus' divine authority and power, confirming their faith in Him as the Messiah.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After the healing of the demoniac in the country of the Gerasenes, the people begged Jesus to
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Luke 8:37, after Jesus healed the demon-possessed man in the region of the Gerasenes, the people were overcome with fear and asked Jesus to leave their region. Therefore, the correct answer is 'go away from them'.

topics.title: MIRACLES : EXORCISM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus told the parable of the lost sheep in order to illustrate that
options: every believer ought to rejoice with those who rejoice
there is joy in heaven over sinner who repents
explanation: The parable of the lost sheep, found in Luke 15:1-7, is a story told by Jesus to illustrate the joy in heaven when a sinner repents and turns back to God. Therefore, the correct answer is 'there is joy in heaven over a sinner who repents'.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jesus, the seventy disciples should rejoice because
options: of the submission of evil spirits to them
of the fall of Satan from heaven like lightening
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Luke 10:20, Jesus told the seventy disciples to rejoice because their names were written in heaven. This was after they returned from their mission, excited that even demons submitted to them in Jesus' name. Jesus acknowledged their success but pointed out that the greater cause for joy was their secure relationship with God.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Mark, after the transfiguration, Jesus charged His disciples to
options: tell no one what they had seen
explanation: In Mark 9:9, after the transfiguration, Jesus gave His disciples orders not to tell anyone what they had seen until the Son of Man had risen from the dead. The transfiguration was a significant event where Jesus' divine glory was revealed, but He wanted to keep it a secret until after His resurrection to fully understand the event in the light of His death and resurrection.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Luke, the multitude that followed Jesus during the triumphal entry rejoiced and praised God with a loud voice because
options: they realized that Jesus was a prophet
of the mighty works He had done
He was riding on a colt in humility
explanation: In the book of Luke 19:37, it is stated that the multitude praised God joyfully with a loud voice for all the mighty works they had seen. This indicates that their praise was a result of the mighty works Jesus had done, not necessarily because they realized He was a prophet, He was riding on a colt in humility, or they believed He was their Messiah.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise."
Jesus made the statement above to one of the criminals because he
explanation: In Luke 23:39-43, one of the criminals hanging beside Jesus rebuked the other criminal and acknowledged his own sinfulness, showing repentance. He then asked Jesus to remember him when He comes into His kingdom. In response to this, Jesus told him, "Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise." This shows that the criminal was repentant, not merely because he respected, praised Jesus or rebuked his partner.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The message of the risen Christ to the women who came early to the tomb was to be relayed to the disciples and
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 28:7, the risen Christ instructed the women who came early to the tomb to go and tell his disciples that he has risen from the dead and that they will see him in Galilee. Among the disciples, Peter was specifically mentioned, hence the correct answer is Peter.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Gospel of John, Jesus is the true vine while the vine dresser is
explanation: In the Gospel of John, specifically in John 15:1, Jesus refers to himself as the true vine and his Father as the gardener or the vine dresser. This metaphor is used to illustrate the relationship between God the Father (the vine dresser), Jesus (the vine), and believers (the branches). The vine dresser takes care of the vine and prunes the branches to make them bear more fruit, just as God takes care of believers and sometimes disciplines them to make them more fruitful.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE TRUE VINE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"...Why has Satan filled your heart to lie to the Holy Spirit..."
Peter rebuked Ananias in the statement above for his sin of
explanation: The sin of Ananias, as described in the book of Acts in the Bible, was greed. Ananias and his wife Sapphira sold a piece of property and kept back part of the proceeds for themselves, while pretending to give all of it to the church. Peter rebuked them for lying to the Holy Spirit, which was a manifestation of their greed.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul informed the Galatians that the gospel he preached came through
explanation: In Galatians 1:11-12, Paul states that the gospel he preached was not of human origin. He did not receive it from any man, nor was he taught it; rather, he received it by revelation from Jesus Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is 'a revelation of Jesus Christ'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"You son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, full of all deceit and villainy..."
The statement of Paul was addressed to
explanation: In the book of Acts 13:6-12, Paul and Barnabas encountered a Jewish sorcerer and false prophet named Bar-Jesus, also known as Elymas. When Elymas tried to turn the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, away from the faith, Paul rebuked him and called him 'You son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness, full of all deceit and villainy.' Therefore, the statement was addressed to Elymas, the magician.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"...Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved."
The statement above by the men from Judea lead to the
explanation: The statement 'Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved' was made by certain men from Judea who came down to Antioch. This statement caused a great dispute and debate among the believers, leading to the convening of the Jerusalem Council to discuss this matter. This event is recorded in Acts 15:1-2.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Galatians, the Law was added because of
explanation: According to Galatians 3:19, the Law was added because of transgressions. The Law was given to make people aware of sin and its consequences. It served as a guide to show people their sins and to lead them to Christ.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PLACE OF THE LAW AMONG THE JEWS.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Galatians, the death of Christ has eliminated all forms of
explanation: According to Galatians 3:28, the death of Christ has eliminated all forms of difference. In Christ, there is no Jew or Greek, slave or free, male or female. All are one in Christ Jesus. This means that the death of Christ has broken down the barriers and differences that separate people.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter in his teaching on humility, admonishes Christians should re-affirm their love for those who
explanation: In his teachings, Peter emphasized the importance of humility and gentleness in spirit. He encouraged Christians to show love and kindness to everyone, regardless of their actions or attitudes. This is in line with the Christian teaching of 'love your neighbor as yourself'.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Corinthians advised that Christians should re-affirm their love for those who
explanation: In Corinthians, Paul advised Christians to reaffirm their love for those who wrong them. This is in line with the Christian teaching of forgiveness and love. Christians are encouraged to forgive those who wrong them and to show them love, as a reflection of God's love for humanity.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Corinthians, variety of gifts are inspired by
explanation: According to Corinthians 12:4-6, there are different kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit distributes them. Therefore, the variety of gifts are inspired by the same Spirit, not by fervent prayers, angels of God, or constant fasting.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul in Corinthians, generosity produces
explanation: In 2 Corinthians 9:11-12, Paul states that generosity results in thanksgiving to God. This is because the generosity of believers not only meets the needs of the saints but also overflows in many thanksgivings to God. Therefore, according to Paul, generosity produces thanksgiving to God, not righteousness, salvation, or the grace of God.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Busy bodies and those living in idleness in the church at Thessalonica were admonished to
options: pray for those in authority for peace
do their work in quietness and earn a living
appeal to those in authority to create jobs
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 2 Thessalonians 3:11-12, Paul admonishes those who are idle and disruptive and not busy at work but busybodies. He commands them to settle down and earn the food they eat. Therefore, the correct answer is 'do their work in quietness and earn a living'.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : BENEFITS OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Thessalonians told believers that the coming of the Lord would be accompanied by
explanation: In 1 Thessalonians 4:16, Paul describes the coming of the Lord, saying 'For the Lord himself will come down from heaven, with a loud command, with the voice of the archangel and with the trumpet call of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'the sound of the trumpet'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: James stated in his epistle that when believers met various trails, their faith produces
explanation: In the book of James, specifically James 1:2-4, it is stated that when believers face various trials, the testing of their faith produces steadfastness (or perseverance). This means that going through trials helps believers to become more resilient and steadfast in their faith.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter in his epistle enjoined servants to be submissive to their masters with all
explanation: In 1 Peter 2:18, Peter instructs servants (or slaves) to be submissive to their masters with all respect. This means that servants should show proper respect and obedience to their masters, regardless of whether they are good and gentle or harsh.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God blessed and hallowed the seventh day because
options: it was the day He rested from creation
it was the day He created man
He wanted it to be a day of worship
He saw that all He had created was good
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically Genesis 2:2-3, God blessed the seventh day and made it holy because on it He rested from all the work of creating that He had done. Therefore, the correct answer is that God blessed and hallowed the seventh day because it was the day He rested from creation.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Noah's ark was sustained during the flood by its
explanation: The Bible, specifically Genesis 7:17-18, states that the ark was lifted high above the earth as the waters rose and increased greatly on the earth, and the ark floated on the surface of the water. Therefore, Noah's ark was sustained during the flood by its floating on the water.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God visited the children of Isreal on Mount Sinai during the giving of the commandments through
explanation: In the book of Exodus, it is recorded that when God gave the Ten Commandments to the Israelites at Mount Sinai, His presence was manifested through smoke and fire. This is found in Exodus 19:18 which says, 'Mount Sinai was covered with smoke, because the Lord descended on it in fire. The smoke billowed up from it like smoke from a furnace, and the whole mountain trembled violently.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'smoke and fire'.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When asked to interpret Pharaoh's dream, Joseph's first response was
options: "...God has revealed to Pharaoh what he is about to do."
"It is not in me; God will give Pharaoh a favourable answer. "
"... God will shortly bring it to pass."
"...let Pharaoh select a man discrete and wise."
explanation: In Genesis 41:16, when Pharaoh asked Joseph to interpret his dream, Joseph's first response was, 'I cannot do it,' Joseph replied to Pharaoh, 'but God will give Pharaoh the answer he desires.' This is similar to the option 'It is not in me; God will give Pharaoh a favourable answer.' Therefore, the correct answer is this option.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Joshua fell upon his face during Israel's defeat to Ai, God told him to rise and
explanation: In the Bible, after the defeat of Israel by Ai, Joshua fell on his face in despair. God told him to rise and sanctify the people (Joshua 7:13). Sanctify in this context means to purify or make holy. The Israelites had sinned by taking the accursed things from Jericho, which was against God's command. Therefore, sanctification was necessary for them to be able to stand before their enemies.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSHUA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God guided Abraham's steward in choosing a wife for Isaac by
options: teaching him how to select a good woman
leading a lady to fulfil his prayer request
explanation: In Genesis 24, Abraham's servant prayed to God for guidance in choosing a wife for Isaac. He asked that the woman who would offer him and his camels water to drink would be the one chosen for Isaac. Rebecca fulfilled this prayer request, thus God guided Abraham's servant by leading a lady to fulfil his prayer request.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why was God angry with Eli?
options: Eli's sons dishonoured God and he treated them with levity
Eli's sons were appointed as priests against God's will
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Samuel 2:12-17, 22-25, Eli's sons, Hophni and Phinehas, were described as wicked men who had no regard for the Lord or for the duties of the priests to the people. They were taking portions of the sacrifices that were not theirs to take and sleeping with the women who served at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. When Eli heard about their behavior, he rebuked them but did not take any further action to stop them. God was angry with Eli because he honored his sons more than he honored God. Eli knew about their sins but failed to restrain them. This is why God's judgment came upon Eli's house (1 Samuel 2:27-36).

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Laxity in disciplining children is dangerous to both the family and society as exemplified by
explanation: Absalom, the third son of David, is an example of the danger of laxity in disciplining children. Absalom killed his half-brother Amnon for raping their sister Tamar (2 Samuel 13). Later, he conspired against his father, David, and temporarily overthrew him (2 Samuel 15-18). David, out of love for Absalom, failed to discipline him appropriately for his actions. This lack of discipline led to Absalom developing a rebellious and vengeful attitude, which eventually led to his downfall and death. This story serves as a warning of the potential societal and familial dangers of failing to discipline children.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God promised Abraham that nations of the earth shall be blessed through him because
explanation: God promised Abraham that nations of the earth shall be blessed through him because he had obeyed Him. This is a biblical concept found in Genesis 22:18, where God says 'In your seed all the nations of the earth shall be blessed, because you have obeyed My voice.' Therefore, the correct answer is option 1.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The punishment for Adam and Eve's sin was that they
explanation: The punishment for Adam and Eve's sin was that they were driven out of the Garden of Eden. This is a biblical concept found in Genesis 3:23-24, where God sends them out of the garden to prevent them from eating from the tree of life. Therefore, the correct answer is option 1.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Do not destroy him; for who can stretch out his hand on the LORD'S anointed and be guiltless?" '
In the statement above, David was acknowledging God's role in
explanation: The statement was made by David when he had the opportunity to kill Saul but he didn't. He acknowledged that Saul was anointed by God and it was not his place to harm him. This shows his recognition of God's role in Saul's leadership.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Saul's decision to consult a medium displayed his lack of
explanation: Saul's decision to consult a medium, which is against God's commandments, shows his lack of faith in God. If he had faith in God, he would have sought God's guidance instead of resorting to a medium.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : RELIANCE ON A MEDIUM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Solomon made the most unwise decision by engaging in
explanation: Solomon's unwise decision was his marriages to foreign women. According to the Bible, Solomon married many foreign women (besides the daughter of Pharaoh), including Moabites, Ammonites, Edomites, Sidonians, and Hittites. They were from nations about which the LORD had told the Israelites, 'You must not intermarry with them, because they will surely turn your hearts after their gods.' Nevertheless, Solomon held fast to them in love (1 Kings 11:1-2). This led him to stray from God in his later years.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The man of God whose word was ignored by Kings Ahab and Jehoshaphat was
explanation: The man of God whose word was ignored by Kings Ahab and Jehoshaphat was Micaiah. In 1 Kings 22, both kings ignored the prophecy of Micaiah who predicted a defeat in the battle at Ramoth-Gilead. Instead, they listened to the false prophets who predicted victory. This led to Ahab's death in the battle, just as Micaiah had prophesied.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Baal did not answer his worshippers at the contest on Mount Carmel because he
explanation: In the biblical account of the contest on Mount Carmel (1 Kings 18), the prophet Elijah challenged the prophets of Baal to call on their god to light a fire for a sacrifice. Despite their loud cries and rituals, Baal did not answer because he does not exist. Therefore, the correct answer is that Baal did not hear his worshippers.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The workmen who repaired the house of the Lord during Josiah's religious reforms were not made to account for the money given them because they
explanation: In the biblical account of King Josiah's religious reforms (2 Kings 22), it is stated that the workmen who repaired the house of the Lord were not required to account for the money given to them because they were considered honest. Therefore, the correct answer is that they were honest workmen.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During Jehoakim's reign, Judah was destroyed as punishment for Manasseh's sin of
explanation: During Jehoakim's reign, Judah was destroyed as punishment for Manasseh's sin of shedding innocent blood. Manasseh was one of the kings of Judah who did evil in the sight of the Lord. He shed so much innocent blood that he filled Jerusalem from one end to another, besides his sin with which he made Judah sin, in doing what was evil in the sight of the Lord.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nehemiah responded to the opposition of the enemies of Israel by
options: telling the people not to say a word
inviting them to dialogue in the King's court
asking God to return their taunt back to them
explanation: Nehemiah responded to the opposition of the enemies of Israel by asking God to return their taunt back to them. When Sanballat, Tobiah, the Arabs, the Ammonites, and the Ashdodites heard that the repair of the walls of Jerusalem was going forward and that the gaps were beginning to be closed, they were very angry. They all plotted together to come and fight against Jerusalem and to cause confusion in it. So Nehemiah prayed to God and set a guard as a protection against them day and night.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nebuchadnezzar ordered his subjects to worship the image he set up whenever they
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Daniel 3:1-7, King Nebuchadnezzar made an image of gold and ordered that as soon as the people heard the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipe and all kinds of music, they must fall down and worship the image he had set up. Therefore, the correct answer is 'heard the sound of the horn'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"What is this that you have done!"
The people in the ship asked Jonah the question above because
options: he was fast asleep in the ship
they knew he was fleeing from the LORD
he lied to them about his presence in the ship
they realised that he had made attempts to commit suicide
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Jonah 1:7-10, when the ship was caught in a storm, the sailors cast lots to find out who was responsible for the calamity. The lot fell on Jonah and they asked him about his identity and why this had happened. Jonah confessed that he was fleeing from the Lord, which is why they asked him 'What is this that you have done!'. Therefore, the correct answer is 'they knew he was fleeing from the LORD'.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"What shall I do with you, O Ephraim? What shall I do with you, O Judah? Your love is like a morning cloud..."
In the statement above the love of Israel is compared to a morning cloud because it
explanation: In the biblical context, the love of Israel is compared to a morning cloud because it disappears fast. Morning clouds often disappear as the day warms up, symbolizing the fleeting and inconsistent love of the Israelites.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Amos, God desires knowledge of Him rather than
explanation: According to the prophet Amos in the Bible, God desires knowledge of Him rather than burnt offerings. This is a critique of the Israelites' religious practices at the time, which focused more on ritual sacrifices (burnt offerings) than on truly knowing and following God's will.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In his vision, Isaiah's guilt was taken away and his sin forgiven after
options: the angel of the LORD appeared to him
his mouth was touched with a burning coal
explanation: In the book of Isaiah 6:6-7, it is written that one of the seraphim flew to Isaiah with a live coal in his hand, which he had taken with tongs from the altar. With it he touched Isaiah's mouth and said, 'See, this has touched your lips; your guilt is taken away and your sin atoned for.' Therefore, Isaiah's guilt was taken away and his sin forgiven after his mouth was touched with a burning coal.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prophet who was ordained from the womb for God's service was
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah 1:5, God said to Jeremiah, 'Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, before you were born I set you apart; I appointed you as a prophet to the nations.' Therefore, Jeremiah was the prophet who was ordained from the womb for God's service.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jeremiah, the anger of God towards Israel would only be reduced by
explanation: According to the book of Jeremiah, it is God's mercy that would reduce His anger towards Israel. This is based on the understanding that God's mercy is His willingness to forgive and not punish as much as the sin deserves.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Zechariah saw the angel of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of incense, he
explanation: In the book of Luke 1:12, when Zechariah saw the angel of the Lord standing on the right side of the altar of incense, he was troubled and fear fell upon him. This was a common reaction in the Bible when humans encountered angels.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : JOHN, THE FORERUNNER OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Follow me and leave the dead to bury their own dead"
By this statement, Jesus means that
options: disciples shall leave their families in God's care
explanation: The statement "Follow me and leave the dead to bury their own dead" by Jesus means that following Him comes before anything else. In this context, 'the dead' refers to those who are spiritually dead or those who do not follow the teachings of Jesus. Therefore, Jesus is emphasizing the importance of prioritizing spiritual matters over worldly matters.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The disciples became terrified when they saw Jesus walking on the sea because
explanation: The disciples became terrified when they saw Jesus walking on the sea because they thought He was a ghost. In the biblical account, when the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they were frightened because they thought they were seeing a ghost. Their fear was not due to a lack of faith or the presence of a storm, but rather their misunderstanding of what they were witnessing.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Jews sought to kill Jesus after He healed the man at the pool of Bethzatha because they thought that He
options: claimed to have authority to forgive sins
did not agree with the law of Moses
said that He was born before Abraham
explanation: In John 5:18, it is stated that the Jews sought to kill Jesus because He not only broke the Sabbath, but also was calling God His own Father, making Himself equal with God.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE LIGHT OF THE WORLD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the parable of the sower, the seed that fell among the thorns symbolized the one who hears the word but
options: the cares of the world choke it
explanation: In the parable of the sower, as explained in Matthew 13:22, the seed that fell among the thorns represents the one who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, making it unfruitful.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus encouraged His followers to rejoice when they were persecuted because
options: great would be their reward in heaven
explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus said, 'Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Rejoice and be glad, because great is your reward in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.' (Matthew 5:10-12). This means that Jesus encouraged His followers to rejoice when they were persecuted because great would be their reward in heaven.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIAN ATTITUDE TO PERSECUTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jona! For flesh and blood has not revealed this to you..."
As exemplified by the statement above, for believers to be able to identify with Jesus, they must
explanation: The statement was made by Jesus to Peter after Peter confessed that Jesus was the Christ, the Son of the living God. Jesus told Peter that this revelation was not from flesh and blood, but from His Father in heaven. This implies that for believers to truly identify with Jesus, they must be led by the Spirit of God, as it is the Spirit that reveals the truth about Jesus.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus drove the money changers and sellers of doves out of the Temple because
options: the people defiled it with dubious business
there was no need for animal sacrifice anymore
the people wee disobedient to the Law
animals and birds were not allowed in it
explanation: Jesus drove the money changers and sellers of doves out of the Temple because they were defiling it with their business activities. This is recorded in the Bible in Matthew 21:12-13, where Jesus says, 'My house will be called a house of prayer, but you are making it a den of robbers.' This indicates that the Temple, which was supposed to be a place of worship, had been turned into a place of commerce, hence defiling it.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The tearing of the curtain of the Temple into two at the death of Jesus signifies
explanation: The tearing of the curtain of the Temple into two at the death of Jesus signifies direct access to God through Christ. This is recorded in the Bible in Matthew 27:51. Before Jesus' death, the curtain in the Temple separated the Holy of Holies, where God's presence was believed to be, from the rest of the Temple, and only the high priest could enter it once a year. When Jesus died, the curtain was torn, symbolizing that through Jesus' sacrifice, everyone could now have direct access to God, not just the high priest.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Tell people, His disciples came by night and stole him away while we were asleep..." '
The intention of the speaker in the statement above was to
explanation: The speaker in the statement is trying to deny the resurrection story. The resurrection story is a fundamental belief in Christianity that Jesus Christ was raised from the dead three days after his crucifixion. By saying that the disciples stole Jesus' body while the guards were asleep, the speaker is trying to provide an alternative explanation to the resurrection, thus denying it.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus charged His followers to walk in the light while they had the light so that they may
explanation: Jesus' statement about walking in the light is a metaphor for following his teachings and living a righteous life. When he says 'so that they may have the light of life', he means that by following his teachings (walking in the light), his followers will gain spiritual enlightenment and guidance (the light of life). This is a common theme in Christian teachings, where light is often used as a symbol for goodness, knowledge, and divine presence.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE LIGHT OF THE WORLD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the early Church, believers devoted themselves to the
explanation: In the early Church, believers devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and fellowship. This is recorded in the book of Acts 2:42, where it is stated that they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and to fellowship, to the breaking of bread and to prayer.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The disciple that went to preach in Samaria after the great persecution in Jerusalem was
explanation: After the great persecution in Jerusalem, it was Philip who went to preach in Samaria. This is recorded in Acts 8:5, where it is stated that Philip went down to a city in Samaria and proclaimed the Messiah there.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Saul was converted so that he might
explanation: Saul, who later became known as Paul, was converted on the road to Damascus. After his conversion, he became one of the most fervent apostles of Christianity, spreading the word to the Gentiles. This is recorded in the book of Acts in the Bible.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What effect did the decision of the Council of Jerusalem have on the early church?
options: The Jews became leaders of the community
Gentiles were free to be members of the Church
explanation: The Council of Jerusalem, as described in Acts 15, was a meeting of the early Christian community to resolve the issue of whether Gentiles who converted to Christianity needed to follow Jewish customs and laws. The decision was that they did not, which helped to unify the early church as it meant that Gentiles and Jews could worship together without the need for Gentiles to adopt Jewish customs. This decision helped to unify the members of the early church.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Romans stated that the righteousness of God has been manifested through
explanation: In the book of Romans, specifically Romans 3:21-22, Paul states that the righteousness of God has been manifested through faith in Jesus Christ for all who believe. Therefore, the correct answer is 'faith in Jesus'.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Romans, all who were also baptized into His
explanation: According to Romans 6:3, Paul states that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death. Therefore, the correct answer is 'death'.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul in Philippians, humility means giving consideration to other people's
explanation: In Philippians 2:4, Paul instructs, 'Let each of you look not only to his own interests, but also to the interests of others.' This verse implies that humility involves considering the interests of others, not just one's own.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Corinthians, believers are taught to forgive one another in order to
options: prevent the wrong doer from losing his salvation
discourage the offender from committing more sin
explanation: In 2 Corinthians 2:10-11, Paul writes, 'Anyone you forgive, I also forgive. And what I have forgiven—if there was anything to forgive—I have forgiven in the sight of Christ for your sake, in order that Satan might not outwit us. For we are not unaware of his schemes.' This suggests that forgiveness is necessary to prevent Satan from gaining an advantage over believers.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Corinthians, Paul stated that by one Spirit we were
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 12:13, Paul stated that 'For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body'. This means that through the Holy Spirit, all believers become part of the body of Christ, which is the church. Therefore, the correct answer is 'baptized into one body'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul commended the churches in Macedonia for giving out of their freewill to the
explanation: In 2 Corinthians 8:1-5, Paul commended the churches in Macedonia for their generosity in giving out of their own free will, despite their own poverty, to the saints in Jerusalem. Therefore, the correct answer is 'saints'.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Pay all of them their dues, taxes to whom taxes are due, revenue to whom revenue is due, respect to whom respect is due, honour to whom honour is due."nl*Paul made the statement above to the Church at
explanation: The statement "Pay all of them their dues, taxes to whom taxes are due, revenue to whom revenue is due, respect to whom respect is due, honour to whom honour is due." was made by Paul in his letter to the Romans. This can be found in Romans 13:7 of the Bible.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At the second coming of Christ, those who are alive shall be
explanation: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:17 in the Bible, at the second coming of Christ, those who are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. Therefore, the correct answer is 'caught up together with the lord'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : PREPARATION FOR HIS COMING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, treatment of brethren at fellowship should not be based on
explanation: In the book of James, it is stated that believers should not show favoritism based on appearance. James 2:1-4 warns against favoritism, stating that if a man with gold rings and fine clothes comes into your assembly, and a poor man in shabby clothing also comes in, and you pay attention to the one who wears the fine clothing and say, 'You sit here in a good place,' while you say to the poor man, 'You stand over there,' or, 'Sit down at my feet,' have you not then made distinctions among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts? This clearly indicates that treatment of brethren at fellowship should not be based on appearance.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter in his epistle, charged his fellow elders to
explanation: In 1 Peter 5:1-2, Peter charges his fellow elders to 'tend the flock of God that is in your charge, exercising the oversight, not under compulsion but willingly, as God would have you do it—not for sordid gain but eagerly.' This clearly indicates that Peter charged his fellow elders to tend the flock of God.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : PASTORAL CARE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which subject is given to you?
explanation: The subject is Christian Religious Knowledge. This is the accurate answer.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sign of God's covenant with Abraham was
explanation:
Bible Passage: Genesis chapter 17:4
As for me, Behold, my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many nations.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Ezekiel, the new covenant would involve
options: washing the people with the blood of an animal
explanation:
Bible Passage: Ezekiel 36: 25
Then will i sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will i cleanse you.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The commander of Jabin's army was
explanation:
Bible Passage: Judges 4: 2
And the LORD sold them into the hand of Jabin king of cannaan, that reigned in Hazor; the captain of whose host was sisera, which dwealt in Haroshelt of the Gentiles.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The new covenant between God and the Israelites is centered on
explanation: The new covenant between God and the Israelites is centered on individual responsibility. This is evident in the Bible where God holds each person accountable for their actions. Collective responsibility, religious doctrines, and intensive teaching are not the central themes of the new covenant.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The ark that God commanded Noah to build was made of
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically Genesis 6:14, God commanded Noah to build the ark using gopher wood. Therefore, the correct answer is 'gopher wood'.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : MOSES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During Moses' leadership in Israel, he exhibited most importantly, the talent of a
explanation: Moses is known in the Bible for his role as a mediator between God and the people of Israel. He interceded on behalf of the Israelites many times, most notably when he received the Ten Commandments from God on Mount Sinai. Therefore, the correct answer is 'mediator'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Moses ran away from Egypt after killing an Egyptian because he felt that he might be
explanation: In the book of Exodus in the Bible, it is recorded that Moses killed an Egyptian who was beating a Hebrew slave. When Pharaoh heard of this, he sought to kill Moses. Therefore, Moses fled from Pharaoh and stayed in the land of Midian. So, the correct answer is that Moses ran away from Egypt after killing an Egyptian because he felt that he might be killed by Pharaoh of Egypt.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did God guide Eleazar in choosing a wife for Isaac?
options: He caused His angel to go ahead of him.
He revealed to Rebekah before Eleazar's arrival
He caused Rebekah to give him free accommodation
He made Rebekah's parent willing to release her
explanation: In the book of Genesis in the Bible, it is recorded that Abraham sent his servant Eleazar to find a wife for his son Isaac from his own people. Abraham instructed Eleazar that God would send his angel ahead of him to make his journey successful. Therefore, the correct answer is that God guided Eleazar in choosing a wife for Isaac by causing His angel to go ahead of him.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David's immediate reaction to the news that the hearts of the Israelites have gone after Absalom was to
explanation: In the Bible, 2 Samuel 15:13-14, when David heard that the hearts of the people of Israel have gone after Absalom, his immediate reaction was to flee to the wilderness. This was because Absalom had conspired against him and had turned the hearts of the people of Israel against David. Fearing for his life and his kingdom, David chose to flee rather than fight.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David's inaction to Amnon's incest with Tamar showed his
explanation: David's inaction to Amnon's incest with Tamar showed his lack of control over his children. This is evident in 2 Samuel 13, where Amnon, David's son, raped his half-sister Tamar and David, although angry, did not punish Amnon. This lack of action can be interpreted as a lack of control over his children.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The young man of Egypt took David to the band of the Amalekites because
options: the Spirit of God was working through him
he sought revenge against his former master
explanation: In the Bible, specifically 1 Samuel 30:15, the young man of Egypt led David to the band of the Amalekites because he was afraid for his life. David had found him in the field, sick and abandoned, and after David had given him food and drink, the young man asked David to swear that he would neither kill him nor hand him over to his master before he would agree to lead David to the Amalekites.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Because David sought God's will to pursue the Amalekites who invaded Ziklag, he was rewarded with victory and
explanation: In 1 Samuel 30:8, David inquired of the Lord, asking if he should pursue the Amalekites who had invaded Ziklag. God told him to pursue them and assured him of victory. After the victory, David recovered everything the Amalekites had taken, and he also took the spoils, as stated in 1 Samuel 30:19-20. Therefore, the correct answer is 'spoils'.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"...Why then have you not kept watch over your lord the king?..."
The question above by David was addressed to
explanation: The question by David was addressed to Abner. This is based on the biblical account in 2 Samuel 3:31-39, where David questioned Abner's loyalty after the death of King Saul.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Solomon's wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the East and all wisdom of Egypt because he
explanation: Solomon's wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the East and all wisdom of Egypt because he got his wisdom from God. This is based on the biblical account in 1 Kings 3:5-12, where Solomon asked God for wisdom in a dream, and God granted his request.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Saul relied on a medium because God did not answer him through
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Samuel 28:6, it is stated that Saul sought answers from God through dreams, Urim, and prophets but God did not answer him. The Urim was a method of divination in ancient Israel where questions were posed to the high priest who wore the Urim and Thummim in a pouch on his breastplate. When God did not answer Saul through these means, he turned to a medium, also known as a witch or a necromancer, to summon the spirit of the deceased prophet Samuel.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : RELIANCE ON A MEDIUM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
When Naboth refused to exchange his vineyard with King Ahab, Ahab reacted
by
options: going to his house vexed and sullen
explanation: In 1 Kings 21:4, it is recorded that after Naboth refused to give his vineyard to King Ahab, Ahab went to his house vexed and sullen. He was upset because Naboth had said, 'The Lord forbid that I should give you the inheritance of my fathers.' Ahab's wife, Jezebel, later orchestrated the killing of Naboth but this was not Ahab's initial reaction.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sins of Israel during the reign of Ahab consisted of
explanation: During the reign of Ahab, the Israelites were led into the worship of Baal, a Canaanite idol. This was largely due to the influence of Jezebel, Ahab's wife, who was a staunch worshipper of Baal. This is documented in the Bible in 1 Kings 16:31-33.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
The book of the law was found in the house of the LORD during the reign of
King
explanation: The book of the law was found in the house of the LORD during the reign of King Josiah. This is recorded in the Bible in 2 Kings 22:8. The book of the law refers to the Torah, the first five books of the Bible. Its discovery led to a religious reform in Judah.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: King Josiah was killed by Pharaoh Neco in a battle at
explanation: King Josiah was killed by Pharaoh Neco in a battle at Megiddo. This is a historical event recorded in the Bible, specifically in 2 Kings 23:29-30. Megiddo is an ancient city located in northern Israel, known for its strategic location on a major trade route of the ancient world.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The major reason God allowed the Babylonian attack on the city of Jerusalem was to
options:
restore the people of Judah and their leaders to the true worship of
God
punish the people of Judah and their leaders for their sins
demonstrate the ability of God to deliver His people from captivity
set a trap for the Babylonians and destroy them
explanation: The major reason God allowed the Babylonian attack on the city of Jerusalem was to punish the people of Judah and their leaders for their sins. This is a theological interpretation based on the biblical narrative, particularly in the books of Jeremiah and 2 Kings. The people of Judah and their leaders had turned away from God and were worshipping idols, which was against God's commandments. Therefore, God allowed the Babylonians to attack Jerusalem as a form of punishment for their disobedience.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When the three Hebrew men refused to worship the King's image, he commanded that they be
explanation: In the book of Daniel 3:19-23, King Nebuchadnezzar ordered Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego to be thrown into a fiery furnace because they refused to bow down to his golden image. Therefore, the correct answer is 'thrown into a furnace'.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jonah's explanation to the LORD for resisting His first call teaches us that GOD is
explanation: In the book of Jonah 4:2, Jonah explains that he fled to Tarshish because he knew that God is a merciful and compassionate God, slow to anger and abundant in love, a God who relents from sending calamity. Therefore, the correct answer is 'repentant of evil'.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Before his call, Amos was a
explanation: Before his call, Amos was a herdsman. This is stated in the Bible in Amos 7:14 where Amos himself said, 'I was no prophet, nor a prophet's son, but I was a herdsman, and a dresser of sycamore figs.' Therefore, the correct answer is option 3, 'herdsman'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Amos' prophecies which were considered as a conspiracy against the northern kingdom were reported to the King by
explanation: Amos' prophecies, which were considered as a conspiracy against the northern kingdom, were reported to the King by Amaziah. This is stated in the Bible in Amos 7:10-13 where Amaziah, the priest of Bethel, sent to Jeroboam king of Israel, saying, 'Amos hath conspired against thee in the midst of the house of Israel: the land is not able to bear all his words.' Therefore, the correct answer is option 4, 'Amaziah'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did God help Jeremiah overcome his inability to speak as prophet?
options: He gave him a cake of honey to eat
He touched his mouth with a burning coal
He put out His hands and touched his mouth
He sent the Seraphim to speak to him
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah 1:9, it is written that 'Then the LORD put out his hand and touched my mouth. And the LORD said to me, "Behold, I have put my words in your mouth."' This indicates that God helped Jeremiah overcome his inability to speak as a prophet by putting out His hands and touching his mouth.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Prophet Ezekiel was set upon his feet to hear the LORD speak when
options: he was made to touch the ephod
an angel of the LORD lifted him
a voice from heaven commanded him to do so.
explanation: In the book of Ezekiel 2:2, it is written that 'And as he spoke to me, the Spirit entered into me and set me on my feet, and I heard him speaking to me.' This indicates that the spirit entered into Ezekiel and set him upon his feet to hear the LORD speak.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jeremiah, the people of Israel were in exile because of their
explanation: According to the book of Jeremiah, the people of Israel were in exile because of their unfaithfulness and faithlessness. They had turned away from God and worshipped idols, breaking the covenant they had with God. This led to their exile as a form of punishment.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"...and many will rejoice at his birth; for he will be great before the Lord..."
In the statement above, the angel made this pronouncement because of
explanation: The angel made this pronouncement to Zachariah, the father of John the Baptist. The angel was announcing the birth of John the Baptist, who would be great before the Lord. This was in response to Zachariah's fervent prayers for a child, as his wife Elizabeth was barren.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : JOHN, THE FORERUNNER OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One quality of a good disciple that was exhibited by the first followers of Jesus was
explanation: The first followers of Jesus were known for their faith. They believed in his teachings and followed him without question. This is a key quality of a good disciple. Diligence, resourcefulness, and piousness are also important qualities, but faith is the most significant quality that was exhibited by the first followers of Jesus.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"..Lord, if you will, you can make me clean.."
The statement above was made by the
explanation: The statement 'Lord, if you will, you can make me clean' was made by the leper in the Bible (Matthew 8:2). The leper showed faith in Jesus' ability to heal him. The centurion servant, epileptic boy, and paralytic are other individuals who had encounters with Jesus, but they did not make this statement.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The disciples of Jesus could not heal the boy with epilepsy because they
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 17:20, Jesus explains that the disciples could not heal the boy with epilepsy because of their little faith. He said to them, 'Because of your little faith. For truly, I say to you, if you have faith like a grain of mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move, and nothing will be impossible for you.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'had little faith'.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The seeds that fell among the thorns in the parable of the sower refer to people that heard the word of God but
options: the cares of the world choked it
explanation: In the parable of the sower in the Bible (Matthew 13:22), the seeds that fell among the thorns refer to those who hear the word, but the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word, and it proves unfruitful. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the cares of the world choked it'.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The promise to those who are persecuted for righteousness sake is that they will
explanation: The promise to those who are persecuted for righteousness sake is that they will inherit the kingdom of heaven. This is based on the Beatitudes in the Bible, specifically Matthew 5:10, which states 'Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.'

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter during the great confession, people believed that Jesus was
explanation: According to Peter during the great confession, people believed that Jesus was one of the prophets. This is based on Matthew 16:14, where the disciples tell Jesus that people believe he is John the Baptist, Elijah, Jeremiah, or one of the other prophets.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"...Behold, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on an ass,..."
The prophecy in the statement above was fulfilled during Jesus'
