explanation: The prophecy "...Behold, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on an ass,..." was fulfilled during Jesus' triumphal entry into Jerusalem. This event is recorded in the Gospels of Matthew (21:1-11), Mark (11:1-11), Luke (19:28-44), and John (12:12-19). Jesus rode into Jerusalem on a donkey, which symbolized peace, fulfilling the prophecy in Zechariah 9:9.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After his arrest, Jesus was taken to
explanation: After his arrest, Jesus was taken to Caiaphas, the high priest. This is recorded in the Gospels of Matthew (26:57-68), Mark (14:53-65), and Luke (22:54). Caiaphas was the one who initially questioned Jesus and later turned him over to Pilate, the Roman governor.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Mark, the young man that the women saw in Jesus' tomb told them to go with the disciples and wait for Him in
explanation: According to the Gospel of Mark, after the resurrection of Jesus, the women who went to the tomb saw a young man dressed in a white robe. He told them that Jesus had risen and that they should go tell the disciples and Peter that He is going ahead of them to Galilee (Mark 16:7). Therefore, the correct answer is Galilee.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"..He was not the light but was sent to bear witness to the light.."
The statement above refers to
explanation: The statement "..He was not the light but was sent to bear witness to the light.." refers to John the Baptist. This is found in the Gospel of John 1:8. John the Baptist was a forerunner to Jesus, preparing the way for Him. He was not the light (meaning he was not the Messiah), but he came to bear witness about the light (meaning he came to tell others about the Messiah, Jesus). Therefore, the correct answer is John the Baptist.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : JOHN, THE FORERUNNER OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
The appointment of deacons became necessary because of the complaint by
the
explanation: The appointment of deacons in the early Christian church became necessary because of the complaints by the Hellenists. The Hellenists were Greek-speaking Jews who felt that their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution of food. To address this issue, the Apostles appointed seven deacons to oversee this task, ensuring fair distribution among all members of the community.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, before his conversion, he persecuted the church violently to
explanation: According to Paul's own account in the New Testament, before his conversion, he persecuted the church violently with the intention to destroy it. He was a zealous Pharisee who saw the followers of Jesus as a threat to the Jewish faith and sought to eradicate them. This changed after his encounter with the risen Christ on the road to Damascus, which led to his conversion.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"..Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much evil he has done to thy saints at Jerusalem..."
Ananias' response above to God's request for him to meet Saul shows that he
options: felt that Saul should not be forgiven
explanation: In the context of the scripture, Ananias was afraid to meet Saul because he had heard about the evil Saul had done to the saints in Jerusalem. Saul was known for persecuting Christians, and Ananias was afraid that he might be his next victim. This fear is what made him hesitant to meet Saul, not a lack of interest or a belief that Saul should not be forgiven or punished.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"..It is necessary to circumcise them, and to charge them to keep the law of Moses.."
In response to the Pharisees' statement above, Peter rebuked them by saying that God has
options: put the yoke upon the Gentiles' neck
made circumcision for only those in leadership
explanation: In response to the Pharisees' statement, Peter rebuked them by saying that God has cleansed the Gentiles' heart by faith. This means that God has accepted the Gentiles not because they were circumcised or because they kept the law of Moses, but because they had faith in Him. This was a radical idea at the time because the Jews believed that they were God's chosen people and that the Gentiles had to become like them (i.e., get circumcised and keep the law of Moses) to be accepted by God. But Peter was saying that God accepts people based on their faith, not their religious rituals or ethnicity.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, judgement is without mercy to one who has shown no mercy, yet mercy triumphs over
explanation: In the book of James 2:13, it is stated that 'judgement is without mercy to one who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgement.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'judgement'.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
According to Peter, the Lord does not wish that any should perish,but
that all should
explanation: In 2 Peter 3:9, it is stated that 'The Lord is not slow in keeping his promise, as some understand slowness. Instead he is patient with you, not wanting anyone to perish, but everyone to come to repentance.' However, the phrase 'come to repentance' is synonymous with 'be saved'. Therefore, the correct answer is 'be saved'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter, when Christians humble themselves, God will
explanation: In 1 Peter 5:6, Peter says, 'Humble yourselves, therefore, under God's mighty hand, that he may lift you up in due time.' This means that when Christians humble themselves, God will exalt them. The concept of humility in Christianity is about recognizing our need for God, submitting to His will, and acknowledging His greatness. When we do this, God promises to lift us up.

topics.title: HUMILITY : REWARDS OF HUMILITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul in his letter to Philemon, forgiveness should not be by compulsion but by
explanation: In Paul's letter to Philemon, he appeals for Onesimus, a slave who had wronged his master Philemon and run away. Paul sends him back to Philemon, not as a slave, but as a brother in Christ, and asks Philemon to forgive him. In Philemon 1:14, Paul says, 'but I preferred to do nothing without your consent in order that your goodness might not be by compulsion but of your own accord.' This means that Paul wanted Philemon to forgive Onesimus out of his own free will, not because he was forced to.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Corinthians, Paul said that one can only say Jesus is Lord by
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 12:3, Paul states that no one can say, 'Jesus is Lord,' except by the Holy Spirit. This means that the recognition of Jesus as Lord is a spiritual revelation given by the Holy Spirit.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Christians give freely, God will supply
options: their needs according to the measure they give
their needs according to His riches in glory
explanation: In Philippians 4:19, Paul assures that God will supply all our needs according to His riches in glory by Christ Jesus. This means that God's provision is not based on our giving but on His abundant riches in glory.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE TEACHINGS OF PAUL ON CHRISTIAN GIVING.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Timothy urged believers to make supplications, prayers, intercession and thanksgivings for all those who
explanation: In the book of Timothy, Paul urges believers to make supplications, prayers, intercession and thanksgivings for all those who are in authority. This is to ensure that they can live peaceful and quiet lives in all godliness and holiness. This is good, and pleases God our Savior, who wants all people to be saved and to come to a knowledge of the truth.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Thessalonians, the second coming of the Lord will be
explanation: According to Thessalonians, the second coming of the Lord will be like a thief in the night. This means that it will be unexpected and sudden. The phrase 'like a thief in the night' is used to describe an event that comes unexpectedly, especially one that is unpleasant or unwelcome.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, when we meet various trails in life, we should
options: call upon the name of the Lord
explanation: In the book of James 1:2, it is written that 'Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds'. Therefore, according to James, when we meet various trials in life, we should 'count it all joy'.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : RELIANCE ON A MEDIUM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"..As each has received a gift, employ it for one another, as good towards of God's varied grace..."
In the statement above, Peter teaches believers the virtue of
explanation: The statement is from 1 Peter 4:10 which says 'Each of you should use whatever gift you have received to serve others, as faithful stewards of God's grace in its various forms.' This verse teaches about the virtue of giving or serving others with the gifts we have received from God.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The name of the subject you are doing is ?
explanation: The subject in question is Christian Religious Knowledge. This is evident from the id of the question which contains 'CRK', an acronym for Christian Religious Knowledge.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At Shechem, God appeared to Abraham and told him that
options: he should settle there with his descendants
the land would be given to his descendants
he should continue on his journey southwards
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Genesis 12:7, it is recorded that God appeared to Abraham at Shechem and promised that the land would be given to his descendants. This is the basis for the answer.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jeremiah, the new covenant is based on
explanation: According to the book of Jeremiah, the new covenant is based on righteousness. This is because in the new covenant, God promised to put his law in their minds and write it on their hearts. He will be their God, and they will be his people. This implies a relationship based on righteousness.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joseph's foresight as a leader was exemplified in his
options: forgiving his brother for selling him into slavery
storing food for seven years of famine
service to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh
managing the house of Pharaoh very well
explanation: Joseph's foresight as a leader was exemplified in his storing food for seven years of famine. This is evident in the book of Genesis where Joseph interpreted Pharaoh's dream about seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean cows and seven healthy grains being swallowed by seven withered grains. Joseph interpreted this as seven years of plenty followed by seven years of famine and advised Pharaoh to store food during the years of plenty to be used during the years of famine.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Fear not, stand firm and see the salvation of the LORD, which He will work for you today.."
Moses made this statement when the Israelites
options: became afraid of thunder and lightning at the Mount Sinai
were trapped between the host of Egypt and the Red Sea
were faced with hunger and death at the Wilderness of Sin
explanation: The statement was made by Moses when the Israelites were trapped between the host of Egypt and the Red Sea. This was after Pharaoh had let them go, then changed his mind and pursued them. The Israelites were terrified because they were trapped between the Red Sea and the Egyptian army. Moses encouraged them with these words, assuring them of God's salvation.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During the journey of the Israelites in the wilderness, God led them in the day in the form of
explanation: During the journey of the Israelites in the wilderness, God led them in the day in the form of a cloud. This is recorded in Exodus 13:21, where it is written that the Lord went before them by day in a pillar of cloud to lead them along the way. The cloud provided guidance and also possibly shade in the desert heat.

topics.title: DIVINE PROVIDENCE, GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION :GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Behold, you are old and your sons do not walk in your ways.."
The Israelites made this statement to Samuel because his sons
explanation: In the Bible, specifically 1 Samuel 8:3, it is stated that Samuel's sons did not walk in his ways, but turned aside after gain. They took bribes and perverted justice. This is why the Israelites made the statement to Samuel.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David gave Uriah leave to go to his house and wash his feet so as to
options: compensate him for fighting bravely for Israel
find an excuse to charge him for desertion
explanation: In the Bible, specifically 2 Samuel 11, David had committed adultery with Bathsheba, Uriah's wife. In an attempt to conceal his sin, David brought Uriah back from the battle and encouraged him to go home and wash his feet, hoping that Uriah would sleep with his wife and believe that the child Bathsheba was carrying was his own.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did King Solomon demonstrate his wisdom in setting the dispute over the ownership of a baby by the two prostitutes?
options: He interrogated them for seven days until the truth was discovered
He interrogated them for only three days and the truth was revealed
He asked them to divide the child into two and the mother was known
He threatened to give the child to a neutral woman and the mother was known
explanation: In the Bible, King Solomon demonstrated his wisdom in settling the dispute over the ownership of a baby by two prostitutes by suggesting to divide the child into two. His real intention was not to divide the child but to reveal the true mother who would rather give up her child than see him killed. This story is found in 1 Kings 3:16-28.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Saul disobeyed God's command through Samuel because
options: Samuel did not come early for the sacrifice
he feared the people and obeyed their voice
explanation: In 1 Samuel 15, Saul disobeyed God's command through Samuel because he feared the people and obeyed their voice. He was supposed to completely destroy the Amalekites and their livestock, but he spared King Agag and the best of the livestock. When confronted by Samuel, Saul admitted that he feared the people and listened to them, thus disobeying God's command.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The golden calf which Aaron made for Israel was an evidence of
explanation: The golden calf which Aaron made for Israel was an evidence of their lack of faith in God. When Moses was on Mount Sinai receiving the Ten Commandments, the Israelites grew impatient and asked Aaron to make them a god to worship. This shows their lack of faith in God who had brought them out of Egypt.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to David in the Psalms, God forgives sin so that
explanation: According to David in the Psalms, God forgives sin so that He may be praised. In Psalm 103:2-3, David says, 'Praise the Lord, my soul, and forget not all his benefits—who forgives all your sins and heals all your diseases.' This suggests that one of the reasons God forgives sin is so that He may be praised for His mercy and love.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the effects of Solomon's marriage to foreign women was that
options: his heart was turned after other gods
he ceded part of his kingdom to them
explanation: Solomon's marriage to foreign women led him to start worshipping other gods. These women were from nations about which the LORD had told the Israelites, 'You must not intermarry with them, because they will surely turn your hearts after their gods.' Nevertheless, Solomon held fast to them in love. He had seven hundred wives of royal birth and three hundred concubines, and his wives led him astray. As Solomon grew old, his wives turned his heart after other gods, and his heart was not fully devoted to the LORD his God, as the heart of David his father had been. He followed Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians, and Molek the detestable god of the Ammonites. So Solomon did evil in the eyes of the LORD; he did not follow the LORD completely, as David his father had done.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The failure of Rehoboam to heed to the advice of the old men resulted in
explanation: Rehoboam's failure to heed the advice of the old men led to the division of the kingdom. When Rehoboam succeeded his father Solomon as king, the people of Israel requested that he lighten the harsh labor and heavy taxes that Solomon had imposed on them. Rehoboam rejected the advice of the older men who had served his father, and instead listened to the advice of the young men he had grown up with. He told the people of Israel that he would be even tougher than his father had been. In response, ten of the twelve tribes of Israel rebelled and chose Jeroboam, an official of Solomon's who had earlier rebelled against the king, to be their leader. The kingdom was divided into the northern kingdom of Israel and the southern kingdom of Judah.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Consequent upon the murder of Naboth by Ahab, God declared that
options: the sword will never depart from Ahab's house
He would require the blood of Naboth from Ahab's hand
Dogs would lick Ahab's blood where Naboth's blood was licked
Ahab's descendants would never ascend the throne in Israel
explanation: In 1 Kings 21:19, God, through the prophet Elijah, declared to Ahab that 'In the place where dogs licked up Naboth's blood, dogs will lick up your blood—yes, yours!' This was a consequence of Ahab's involvement in the murder of Naboth. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Dogs would lick Ahab's blood where Naboth's blood was licked'.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Elijah killed four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal on the Mount Carmel after God has shown His
explanation: In 1 Kings 18:38-40, after God answered Elijah's prayer by sending fire to consume the sacrifice, water, stones, and dust, and also licked up the water in the trench, Elijah ordered the people to seize the prophets of Baal and then he slaughtered them. This event demonstrated God's supremacy over Baal, a false god whose prophets could not call down fire. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Supremacy'.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The enormity of the sins before Josiah's reforms was exemplified by their failure to keep the passover since
options: the days of the judges of Israel
explanation: The enormity of the sins before Josiah's reforms was exemplified by their failure to keep the passover since they were brought out of Egypt. The Passover was a significant event in the history of the Israelites. It was a feast that commemorated their liberation by God from slavery in ancient Egypt and their freedom as a nation under the leadership of Moses. The failure to keep the Passover was a clear indication of the extent of their deviation from God's commandments.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nehemiah, while in exile functioned as the king's
explanation: Nehemiah, while in exile, functioned as the king's cupbearer. A cupbearer was an officer of high rank in royal courts, whose duty was to serve the drinks at the royal table. On account of the constant fear of plots and intrigues, a person must be regarded as thoroughly trustworthy to hold this position. He must guard against poison in the king's cup, and was sometimes required to swallow some of the wine before serving it. His confidential relations with the king often gave him a position of great influence. Nehemiah held this position to Artaxerxes, the Persian king.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"You have nothing to do with us in building a house to our God..."
This statement above made by Zerubbabel and others resulted in
explanation: The statement "You have nothing to do with us in building a house to our God..." was made by Zerubbabel and others in response to the Samaritans' offer to help build the temple. This refusal led to the Samaritans' opposition and delay in the building of the temple. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the delay in building the Temple'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Daniel and his friends refused to defile themselves with the king's food, God gave them
explanation: In the book of Daniel, when Daniel and his friends refused to defile themselves with the king's food, God gave them 'knowledge and skill in all learning and wisdom'. Therefore, the correct answer is 'skill in letters and wisdom'.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God's pardon of the people of Nineveh teaches a lesson on the benefits of
explanation: The story of God's pardon of the people of Nineveh in the Bible is a lesson on the benefits of repentance. Nineveh was a city that was known for its wickedness. God sent Jonah to warn them of impending destruction unless they repented of their sins. The people of Nineveh heeded Jonah's warning, repented, and God forgave them. This story teaches that repentance can lead to forgiveness from God.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Hosea's message to Israel is centred on
explanation: Hosea's message to Israel is centred on divine love. Hosea, a prophet in the Bible, was commanded by God to marry a prostitute as a symbolic act to demonstrate God's love for Israel despite their unfaithfulness. Hosea's life and message were a demonstration of God's enduring love for His people, even when they stray from Him.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Behold, this has touched your lips; your guilt is taken away, and you sin forgiven..."
The statement above during Isaiah's call shows
options: that God will only use sinners to glorify Him
God's willingness to physically take away sin
explanation: The statement "Behold, this has touched your lips; your guilt is taken away, and you sin forgiven..." during Isaiah's call shows that God adequately equips His messengers. This is because the act of touching Isaiah's lips with a live coal from the altar symbolizes the purification of Isaiah, making him fit for the divine mission. It is a demonstration of God's provision and preparation for those He calls to serve Him.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The vision of the dry bones by Ezekiel portrays
explanation: The vision of the dry bones by Ezekiel portrays hope. In this vision, God showed Ezekiel a valley full of dry bones, which He miraculously brought back to life. This vision was a symbolic message to the Israelites in exile, giving them hope that God could restore them, no matter how desperate their situation seemed.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE CONDITIONS FOR HOPE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Son of man, can these bones live?.."
What was God's purpose in asking Ezekiel the question above?
options: To bring to bear the disobedience of Israel
explanation: God's purpose in asking Ezekiel the question was to show His supremacy. In the book of Ezekiel, God asked this question to Ezekiel in a vision where He showed him a valley of dry bones. God was demonstrating His power to bring life to what was dead, thus showing His supremacy.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The wise men from the East were able to know about the birth of Jesus because they were
explanation: The wise men from the East were able to know about the birth of Jesus because they were astrologers. The Bible mentions that they saw His star when it rose and have come to worship Him. Astrology is the study of the movements and relative positions of celestial bodies interpreted as having an influence on human affairs and the natural world, which explains how they knew about the birth of Jesus.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Who ever does not bear his cross and come after me cannot be my disciple.."

This statement made by Jesus means that
options: one must be prepared to disown his parents
following Him leads to death on the cross
following him demands total surrender and commitment
explanation: The statement "Whoever does not bear his cross and come after me cannot be my disciple" made by Jesus means that following him demands total surrender and commitment. The 'cross' in this context is metaphorical and represents the burdens, sacrifices, and challenges that come with being a disciple of Jesus. It does not literally mean that his followers must carry a physical cross or that following him leads to death on the cross. It also does not mean that one must disown his parents. Therefore, the correct answer is option 4.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A good example of nature miracles performed by Jesus was that of
explanation: A nature miracle is a type of miracle in which the natural world is altered, controlled, or manipulated. This is different from healing miracles (like healing the leper or casting out demons) or resurrection miracles (like raising the dead). The calming of the storm is a nature miracle performed by Jesus as it involved the control of natural elements - wind and waves. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One important fact that Mary brought out in Jesus' miracle of changing water to wine is that she
options: must intercede for a miracle to occur
had a deep insight of her son and His abilities
was a sister of the bride groom
explanation: In the miracle of changing water to wine, Mary demonstrated her deep insight of her son and His abilities. She knew that Jesus had the power to perform miracles and she had faith in Him. This is evident when she told the servants, 'Do whatever he tells you' (John 2:5).

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable which teaches the need for love for one's neighbours is that of the
explanation: The parable of the Good Samaritan teaches the need for love for one's neighbours. In this parable, a man is robbed and left half-dead. A priest and a Levite pass by without helping him, but a Samaritan stops and takes care of him, demonstrating love and compassion for his neighbour (Luke 10:25-37).

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who are referred to as the salt of the earth by Jesus in His teaching on the Mount?
options: Those who are condemned for His sake
explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus referred to His disciples as the 'salt of the earth'. This metaphor was used to illustrate their value and responsibility in maintaining the faith and spreading the Gospel.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the story of the transfiguration of Jesus, Moses and Elijah represented the
explanation: In the story of the transfiguration of Jesus, Moses and Elijah represented the Law and the Prophets. Moses was the lawgiver in the Old Testament, and Elijah was considered one of the greatest prophets. Their appearance during the transfiguration symbolized the fulfillment of the Law and the Prophets in Jesus.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"..My house shall be called a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of robbers..."
Just before making this statement, Jesus
options: drove out those who sold in the Temple
taught those who sold in the Temple
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 21:12-13, it is recorded that Jesus drove out all those who sold and bought in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold pigeons. He said to them, 'It is written, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer,' but you make it a den of robbers.' Therefore, the correct answer is that Jesus drove out those who sold in the Temple.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Jesus was sent to Herod for trial, he was very glad to see Him because he
options: had wanted to punish Jesus for calling him a fox
explanation: In Luke 23:8, it is written that when Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had long desired to see him, because he had heard about him, and he was hoping to see some sign done by him. Therefore, the correct answer is that Herod had long desired to see Jesus.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE HEROD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Did not our hearts burn within us while he talked to us.."
The statement above shows the regret of
explanation: The statement "Did not our hearts burn within us while he talked to us.." was made by Cleopas and another disciple on their way to Emmaus. They made this statement after they had realized that the stranger who had been explaining the scriptures to them was Jesus. They felt a deep sense of regret for not recognizing Him earlier. Therefore, the statement shows the regret of Cleopas and one other disciple.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus as the good shepherd was loved by the father because He
options: laid down His life for the sheep
explanation: In John 10:11, Jesus said, "I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep." This statement shows that Jesus was loved by the Father because He was willing to sacrifice His life for the sheep (His followers). Therefore, the correct answer is that Jesus, as the good shepherd, was loved by the Father because He laid down His life for the sheep.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF: THE DOOR THE LAMB AND THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What effect did the death of Aninias and Sapphira have on the Early Church?
options: great fear gripped all that ere there
Many more disciples were won to God
explanation: The death of Ananias and Sapphira in the early church, as recorded in Acts 5:1-11, resulted in great fear coming upon all the church and upon all who heard these things. This is because they had lied to the Holy Spirit about the proceeds from the sale of their property, and were struck dead as a result. This event served as a warning to others about the seriousness of lying to God.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The effect of Stephen's death on the early church was that
options: it weakened the faith of the apostles
explanation: Stephen's death, as recorded in Acts 8:1-4, led to a great persecution against the church in Jerusalem, and as a result, the believers were scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria. This scattering of the believers led to the spread of Christianity to non-Jews. Stephen's death, therefore, played a significant role in the expansion of the early church.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE MARTYRDOM OF STEPHEN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The decision at the Council of Jerusalem laid to rest the issue of
explanation: The Council of Jerusalem, as described in Acts 15, was convened to address the issue of whether Gentiles who converted to Christianity needed to be circumcised to be saved. The council concluded that circumcision was not necessary for salvation, thus laying to rest the issue of 'circumcision before one can be saved'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The man who stirred up people against Paul in Ephesus was
explanation: In the book of Acts 19:23-27, Demetrius, a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, stirred up a great commotion against Paul. He did this because he felt that Paul's teachings were bad for business, as they turned people away from the worship of Artemis, thus reducing the demand for his shrines.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Christian concept of justification is that
options: by one's effort in keeping the Law, man is perfect before God
through faith in Christ, the sinner is regarded as guiltless before God
through his own knowledge, man can do what is pleasing to God
by his natural wisdom, a sinner could be reconciled to God
explanation: Justification in Christian theology is the process by which a person is made or declared 'just', that is, made or declared to be in right standing before God. This is achieved not by one's effort or knowledge but through faith in Jesus Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is 'through faith in Christ, the sinner is regarded as guiltless before God'.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Epistle of Paul to the Corinthians, when God reconciled believers to Him through Christ, He gave them the
explanation: In the Epistle of Paul to the Corinthians, specifically in 2 Corinthians 5:18, it is stated that God reconciled us to himself through Christ and gave us the ministry of reconciliation. Therefore, the correct answer is 'ministry of reconciliation'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Paul advise the Philippians to manifest as a virtue of humility?
options: taking the lowest seat in a gathering
explanation: In Philippians 2:3, Paul advises the Philippians to do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility, they should value others above themselves. This means they should count others as better than themselves.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, Christian should forgive one another so that
options: their prayers may be heard by God in Heaven
Satan may not gain advantage over them
explanation: In 2 Corinthians 2:10-11, Paul advises Christians to forgive one another so that Satan might not outwit them, for they are not unaware of his schemes. This means that forgiveness is necessary to prevent Satan from gaining an advantage over them.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, spiritual gifts are for the benefit of the
options: corporate life of the Body of Christ
explanation: According to Apostle Paul in the New Testament of the Bible, spiritual gifts are given for the benefit of the corporate life of the Body of Christ. This means that these gifts are not for individual gain or for the infrastructure of the church, but for the collective good of all believers, to build up the church as a whole.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE BENEFITS OF SPIRITUAL GIFTS TO THE INDIVIDUAL AND THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul's prayer for the Philippians was that God who inspired them to give him gifts would surely
explanation: In Philippians 4:19, Paul's prayer for the Philippians was that God who inspired them to give him gifts would surely supply all their needs. This is a promise that God will provide for the needs of those who are generous in their giving.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul advised Christians in Thessalonica to keep away from any brother who is
explanation: In 2 Thessalonians 3:6, Paul advises the Thessalonians to keep away from any brother who is living in idleness, not in accordance with the tradition that they received from him. This means that the correct answer is 'living in idleness'.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD CITIZENSHIP.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, the second coming of Christ will take place
explanation: In 1 Thessalonians 5:2, Paul states that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night, which means it will happen suddenly and unexpectedly. Therefore, the correct answer is 'suddenly and unexpectedly'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, effective prayer is that which is offered
explanation: According to the book of James in the Bible, effective prayer is that which is offered in faith. This is based on James 5:15 which says 'And the prayer of faith will save the one who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'in faith'.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter, the fact that judgement would begin with the household of God means that
options: Christians should court persecution to win glory
the persecution of Christians would be less severe
explanation: According to Peter in the Bible, the fact that judgement would begin with the household of God means that all must face God's judgement day. This is based on 1 Peter 4:17 which says 'For it is time for judgment to begin at the household of God; and if it begins with us, what will be the outcome for those who do not obey the gospel of God?' Therefore, the correct answer is 'all must face God's judgement day'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : THE BENEFITS OF BEING JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Herod's reaction when he heard the news about Jesus' birth?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Matthew 2:3, it is stated that when King Herod heard about the birth of Jesus, he was troubled. This was because he saw the newborn king as a threat to his throne.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE HEROD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "... Behold, I will bring evil upon you; I will utterly sweep you away..." Elijah pronounced destruction on Ahab with the statement above his sin of
explanation: The statement was made by Elijah to Ahab after Ahab and his wife Jezebel had Naboth killed in order to take possession of his vineyard. This is recorded in 1 Kings 21:21, hence the sin referred to here is greed, covetousness and murder.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The ruler of the synagogue whose child Jesus raised to life was
explanation: In the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in the books of Mark (5:21-43) and Luke (8:40-56), it is recorded that Jesus raised the daughter of Jairus, the ruler of the synagogue, from the dead. Therefore, the correct answer is Jairus.

topics.title: MIRACLES : MIRACLES OF RESUSCITATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Amos, true religion entails
explanation: In the book of Amos, specifically in Amos 5:24, the prophet Amos emphasizes that God desires justice and righteousness more than sacrifices and burnt offerings. This means that true religion, according to Amos, entails being just and righteous. Therefore, the correct answer is 'being just and righteous'.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Romans said that the righteousness of God has been manifested through faith in Jesus Christ for
explanation: In Romans 3:22, Paul states that the righteousness of God has been manifested through faith in Jesus Christ for all those who believe. This means that God's righteousness is revealed and given to all who have faith in Jesus Christ, regardless of their past sins or current circumstances. This righteousness is not earned or achieved, but is a gift from God that is received through faith.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"I tell you, if these were silent, the very stones would cry out."
Jesus'statement above was made when
options: He was rebuked by the Pharisees for eating with sinners
The scribes and the chief priest came to arrest Him
He consoled a woman on His way to the cross
The Pharisees asked Him to rebuke His disciples
explanation: The statement "I tell you, if these were silent, the very stones would cry out." was made by Jesus during His triumphal entry into Jerusalem, as recorded in Luke 19:40. The Pharisees had asked Jesus to rebuke His disciples for their loud praises, to which Jesus responded that if they were to keep quiet, the stones would cry out. This was a declaration of His messiahship and a rebuke to the religious leaders who failed to recognize Him as the Messiah.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Before Moses brought the Isrealites to meet God on Mount Sinai, he charged them to
options: touch the mountain at the sight of smoke
mate with their wives for three days
explanation: In the book of Exodus 19:10-11, before the Israelites met God on Mount Sinai, Moses was instructed by God to consecrate the people. This involved washing their clothes and preparing themselves for the third day when God would come down on Mount Sinai in the sight of all the people. Therefore, the correct answer is 'wash their garments and be consecrated'.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In His teaching about Himself, Jesus said the hireling is not a good shepherd because he
options: leaves the sheep and flees at the sight of danger
owns the sheep but does not care for them
cares for the sheep but does not discipline them
is the gate-keeper who shuts the sheep out
explanation: In John 10:12-13, Jesus describes the hireling (or hired hand) as someone who does not own the sheep and does not care for them. When he sees a wolf coming, he abandons the sheep and runs away. So the wolf attacks the flock and scatters it. The man runs away because he is a hired hand and cares nothing for the sheep. Therefore, the correct answer is 'leaves the sheep and flees at the sight of danger'.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God displayed His supremacy when Ahab was king during the
explanation: The contest of Mount Carmel was a significant event in the Bible where God displayed His supremacy. During the reign of King Ahab, there was a contest on Mount Carmel between the prophet Elijah, who was a prophet of God, and the prophets of Baal. Elijah challenged them to call on their god to light a sacrifice. When they failed, Elijah called on God, who immediately sent fire from heaven to consume the sacrifice. This event demonstrated God's supremacy over the false god Baal.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In his teaching on effective prayer, James said that the prayer of faith would
explanation: In the book of James, it is stated that 'the prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective' (James 5:16). James also said that the prayer of faith will heal the sick and if they have sinned, they will be forgiven (James 5:15). Therefore, the prayer of faith, according to James, has the power to heal and cleanse sins.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Thessalonians declared that at the second coming of the Lord, those who are alive will not precede those that are
explanation: In 1 Thessalonians 4:15-17, Paul explains that those who are alive at the second coming of the Lord will not precede those who have died (or 'are asleep'). The dead in Christ will rise first, then those who are alive will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Ezekiel ate the scroll that was given to him his call, it signified that
explanation: In Ezekiel 3:1-3, God commands Ezekiel to eat a scroll, and it tasted as sweet as honey in his mouth. This signifies that Ezekiel was filled with God's word. He was to internalize God's word and then proclaim it to the people of Israel.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"...I will surely tear the kingdom from you and will give it to your servant..."
An unwise decision of Solomon that resulted in God making the pronouncement above was his
explanation: The unwise decision of Solomon that led to God's pronouncement of tearing the kingdom from him was his neglect of God for other gods. Solomon's wives turned his heart after other gods, and his heart was not fully devoted to the Lord his God, as the heart of David his father had been. This is recorded in 1 Kings 11:4-11.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God fed the Isrealites with Manna at the wildernerss of
explanation: God fed the Israelites with Manna in the wilderness of Sin. This is recorded in Exodus 16:1-36. The Israelites came to the wilderness of Sin, which is between Elim and Sinai, on the fifteenth day of the second month after they had departed from the land of Egypt. And the whole congregation of the people of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron in the wilderness, and the people of Israel said to them, 'Would that we had died by the hand of the Lord in the land of Egypt, when we sat by the meat pots and ate bread to the full, for you have brought us out into this wilderness to kill this whole assembly with hunger.' Then the Lord said to Moses, 'Behold, I am about to rain bread from heaven for you, and the people shall go out and gather a day's portion every day, that I may test them, whether they will walk in my law or not.'

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"The very stone which the builders rejected has become the head of the corner"
The statement above in Peter was directed to those who
explanation: The statement "The very stone which the builders rejected has become the head of the corner" is a metaphor used by Peter to describe Jesus Christ. The 'builders' in this context are the religious leaders and people who did not believe in Jesus' divinity and rejected him. However, Jesus, the 'stone', despite being rejected, has become the 'head of the corner', meaning the most important part or foundation of the spiritual building, which is the church. Therefore, the statement was directed to those who did not believe in Jesus' preciousness.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A good example of parental responsibility was demonstrated by
explanation: King Asa was a descendant of King David and the third King of the Southern Kingdom of Judah. He did what was good and right in the eyes of the Lord.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ASA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why did Pilate send Jesus to Herod during the trial?
options: Jesus, as a Galilean was under Herod's jurisdiction
Pilate had no power to pass the death sentence
Herod's court was the last point before crucifixion
Herod had longed to see Jesus perform miracles
explanation: Pilate sent Jesus to Herod because Jesus was a Galilean and thus fell under Herod's jurisdiction. This is based on the historical context of the time where different regions were ruled by different leaders and a person was subject to the jurisdiction of the leader of their region.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE HEROD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the king that granted Nehemiah permission to go and rebuild Jerusalem?
explanation: The king that granted Nehemiah permission to go and rebuild Jerusalem was Artaxerxes. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Nehemiah chapter 2. Nehemiah served as a cupbearer to King Artaxerxes and when the king noticed his sadness, Nehemiah explained the situation of Jerusalem and requested permission to go and rebuild it, which the king granted.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Shall pursue after this band? Shall I overtake them?"
David's dependence on God for direction is depicted in the statement above when.
explanation: The statement "Shall pursue after this band? Shall I overtake them?" was made by David when the Amalekites had raided Ziklag, his city, and taken away the women and children. David was seeking God's direction on whether to pursue them or not. This shows his dependence on God for direction.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A parable that teaches a lesson on the demands of the kingdom is that of the
explanation: The parable of the Weeds (also known as the parable of the Wheat and Tares) teaches a lesson on the demands of the kingdom. In this parable, Jesus explains that the kingdom of heaven may be compared to a man who sowed good seed in his field, but while his men were sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat and went away. The lesson from this parable is that we should allow both the good and the bad to grow together until the harvest (end of the age), at which point the 'weeds' (those who do evil) will be separated from the 'wheat' (the righteous).

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul admonished the Romans to live a new life in Christ by yielding themselves to God as men who
options: Have been brought from death to life
will receive judgement on the last day
have suffered for the sake of the gospel
explanation: In Romans 6:13, Paul admonishes the Romans to yield themselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life. This is a metaphorical way of saying that they should live a new life in Christ, leaving behind their old sinful ways and living according to God's will.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: An action that demonstrated Solomon's wisdom was his
explanation: In 1 Kings 3:16-28, Solomon demonstrated his wisdom in the judgement of the two women who claimed to be the mother of the same child. Solomon's wisdom was shown when he suggested to divide the living child in two, giving half to each woman. The real mother, out of love for her son, immediately offered to give up her claim to save her son's life. Solomon then knew who the real mother was. This story is often used to illustrate Solomon's wisdom.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Corinthians, Paul while defending himself advised believers to boast only of the things that
explanation: In 2 Corinthians 12:5, Paul states that he will boast only about his weaknesses. This is because he believes that when he is weak, then he is strong, as his strength comes from God. This concept is a key part of Christian theology, emphasizing humility and reliance on God's power rather than one's own abilities.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The most important aspect of the religious reforms of King Josiah was the
options: discovery of the tomb of the man of God
discovery of the book of the law
explanation: King Josiah's religious reforms were centered around the renewal of the covenant. This is recorded in 2 Kings 23, where Josiah gathers the people of Judah and Jerusalem and reads to them the Book of the Covenant that had been found in the temple. He then renews the covenant, pledging to follow the Lord and keep his commands with all his heart and soul. This act of renewing the covenant is the most significant aspect of his reforms, as it represents a commitment to return to the worship of God and the rejection of other gods.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Cornelius who was converted and baptized by Peter was a
explanation: Cornelius, as described in the Bible (Acts 10), was a centurion in the Roman army. He was not a member of the Sanhedrin, a Gentile who persecuted the church, or a scribe. A centurion was a professional officer of the Roman army. Cornelius is known as the first Gentile to convert to Christianity.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF CORNELIUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David was successful as a leader in Israel because
explanation: David was successful as a leader in Israel because he submitted to God's guidance. This is evident in the Bible where David often sought God's guidance before making decisions (1 Samuel 30:8, 2 Samuel 2:1). While his training as a soldier, the love of his people, and his life as a shepherd contributed to his leadership, his submission to God's guidance was the primary reason for his success.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The action that portrayed Moses as a leader of the Israelites was his
options: request to Pharaoh to spare Israelite's sons
killing of an Egyptian who fought with a Hebrew
explanation: Moses was seen as a leader of the Israelites when he killed an Egyptian who was fighting with a Hebrew. This action showed his willingness to protect his people, even at the risk of his own life. This is a key characteristic of a leader, someone who is willing to put the needs of their people before their own.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : MOSES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"How is it that you have agreed together to tempt the spirit of the LORD..."
The statement above was made in reference to the greed of
explanation: The statement "How is it that you have agreed together to tempt the spirit of the LORD..." was made in reference to the greed of Ananias and Sapphira. In the book of Acts in the Bible, Ananias and Sapphira sold a piece of property and lied about the price they sold it for, keeping some of the money for themselves. This act of greed and deception was seen as tempting the spirit of the Lord.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The king who captured and drove the Isrealites into exile during the reign of Jehoiachin was
explanation: The king who captured and drove the Israelites into exile during the reign of Jehoiachin was Nebuchadnezzar. This is recorded in the Bible in 2 Kings 24:10-16.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nebuchadnezzar decreed that all his subjects should worship the image he had set up whenever they
explanation: Nebuchadnezzar decreed that all his subjects should worship the image he had set up whenever they heard the sound of music. This is recorded in the Bible in Daniel 3:1-7.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"The Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elder..."
Jesus made the statement above after He
options: demanded to know who people thought He was
was asked by Pilate if He was the King of the Jews
explanation: The statement "The Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected by the elder..." was made by Jesus after Peter confessed Him as the Christ. This happened when Jesus asked His disciples, 'Who do people say that I am?' and then 'Who do you say that I am?' (Mark 8:27-30). Therefore, the correct answer is 'demanded to know who people thought He was'.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE OCCASION OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How old was Abraham when God appeared to him the second time?
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically Genesis 17:1, God appeared to Abraham for the second time when he was 99 years old. Therefore, the correct answer is '99 years'.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Leave the dead to bury their own dead..."
Jesus' statement above to the man He asked to follow Him was indicative of
explanation: The statement "Leave the dead to bury their own dead" was made by Jesus to a man who wanted to follow Him but asked to first go and bury his father. Jesus was emphasizing the urgency and importance of spreading the Gospel, which is a key part of discipleship. Therefore, the statement is indicative of the cost of discipleship, which may require giving up personal desires or obligations.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Romans advised believers to respect those in authority to avoid God's wrath and also for the sake of
explanation: In Romans 13:5, Paul advises believers to respect those in authority not only to avoid punishment but also for the sake of conscience. Conscience here refers to the inner sense of what is right or wrong in one's conduct or motives, impelling one toward right action. Therefore, respecting authority is not just about avoiding God's wrath, but also about maintaining a clear conscience.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"What is this conversation which you are holding with each other..."
Jesus made the statement above to Cleopas and the other disciple when
options: they were on their way to Emmaus
they were gathered in a room after His resurrection
He told them that somebody would betray Him
explanation: The statement was made by Jesus to Cleopas and the other disciple when they were on their way to Emmaus. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Luke 24:13-35. After His resurrection, Jesus appeared to two of his disciples who were walking from Jerusalem to Emmaus. They were discussing the events of the past few days (Jesus' crucifixion and reports of His resurrection) when Jesus Himself drew near and went with them, but their eyes were kept from recognizing Him. He asked them what they were discussing, hence the statement "What is this conversation which you are holding with each other...".

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A key requirement for healing as exemplified by the disciple's inability to heal the epileptic boy is
explanation: The key requirement for healing, as exemplified by the disciples' inability to heal the epileptic boy, is faith. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Matthew 17:14-20. When the disciples asked Jesus why they could not drive the demon out of the boy, Jesus replied, "Because you have so little faith. Truly I tell you, if you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you." Therefore, faith is the key requirement for healing.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul in Philippians, Jesus demonstrated humility when He
explanation: In the book of Philippians, Apostle Paul explains that Jesus demonstrated humility by taking the form of a man. This is found in Philippians 2:5-8, where Paul describes how Jesus, despite being in the form of God, did not count equality with God a thing to be grasped, but emptied himself, by taking the form of a servant, being born in the likeness of men.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of host..."
The statement above was made in reference to the call of
explanation: The statement "Holy, holy, holy is the LORD of host..." is made in reference to the call of Isaiah. This is found in Isaiah 6:3, where the seraphim around God's throne are described as calling to one another with these words. This vision and call led to Isaiah's prophetic ministry.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: For obeying His word, God rewarded Shiphrah and Puah with
explanation: Shiphrah and Puah were the two Hebrew midwives who feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded, but let the male children live. As a reward for their obedience, God gave them families of their own (Exodus 1:15-21). Therefore, the correct answer is 'families'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : OBEDIENCE AND REWARDS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In his teaching on Spiritual gifts in Corinthians, Paul said that all gifts are inspired by
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 12, Paul discusses spiritual gifts and emphasizes that they are all given by the same Spirit. He states in verse 4: 'There are different kinds of gifts, but the same Spirit distributes them.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'the same spirit'.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When David's son Absalom rebelled against him, David was forced to
explanation: In the Bible, 2 Samuel 15:14, when Absalom rebelled against his father David, David had to flee from Jerusalem to avoid confrontation with his son. He did not abdicate the throne, divide his kingdom or surrender to Absalom.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Go and stand in the temple and speak to the people all the words of this life."
The angel's statement above to Peter was after
explanation: In Acts 5:19-20, an angel of the Lord opened the prison doors and brought out the apostles, including Peter, and commanded them to go and stand in the temple and speak to the people all the words of this life. This happened after Peter's release from prison, not after the murmuring of the Hellenists, his vision of eating unclean meat, or after he received the Holy Spirit.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In His sermon on the Mount, Jesus discouraged His listeners from laying up treasures on earth because
explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus discouraged His listeners from laying up treasures on earth because these earthly treasures are not permanent and can be stolen by thieves. This is recorded in Matthew 6:19-21, where Jesus says, 'Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moths and vermin destroy, and where thieves break in and steal. But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where moths and vermin do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal. For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.' Therefore, the correct answer is option 3, 'thieves would steal them'.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul returned Philemon's slave back to him to ensure that
options: the slave can complete his service time
he would be welcome when he left prison
explanation: In the book of Philemon in the New Testament, Paul returned a runaway slave named Onesimus to his master Philemon. Paul's intention was not to ensure that the slave completes his service time or to boast of Philemon's obedience. Instead, Paul's primary intention was to reconcile Philemon and Onesimus, urging Philemon to forgive and accept Onesimus not as a slave, but as a brother in Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is option 1, 'Philemon forgave and accepted the slave'.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the prophets that prophesied hope for Isreal in spite of God's promise of punishment was
explanation: Jeremiah is known as the prophet of hope. Despite the dire circumstances and the impending doom that was to befall Israel due to their disobedience, Jeremiah gave them a message of hope. He prophesied that God would make a new covenant with them (Jeremiah 31:31-34).

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE CONDITIONS FOR HOPE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The church that was commended for giving even when in extreme poverty was in
explanation: The church in Macedonia, despite being in extreme poverty, was commended for their generosity. This is mentioned in 2 Corinthians 8:1-5, where Paul praises the Macedonian church for giving as much as they were able, and even beyond their ability, entirely on their own.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
'...I am the LORD, and there is no other, besides me there is no God...'
God's statement above through Isaiah depicts
explanation: The statement '...I am the LORD, and there is no other, besides me there is no God...' is a declaration of God's sovereignty. Sovereignty refers to the supreme power or authority. In this context, God is asserting His supreme power and authority, stating that there is no other God besides Him.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Believers today are partakers of the New Covenant through
explanation: Believers today are partakers of the New Covenant through the gift of the Spirit. The New Covenant, as described in the Bible, is a new relationship between God and humans mediated by Jesus Christ, which necessarily includes all believers. This covenant is characterized by the outpouring of the Holy Spirit, which is the gift of the Spirit. Baptism, adherence to sacraments, and obedience to church leadership are all part of the Christian faith, but they are not the means by which believers partake in the New Covenant.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God's message through Jonah was to the people of
explanation: Jonah was a prophet in the Bible who was called by God to deliver a message to the people of Nineveh. Nineveh was a notoriously sinful city and God sent Jonah to prophesy its destruction unless the people repented. Therefore, the correct answer is Nineveh.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Amos is often referred to as a prophet of
explanation: Amos is often referred to as a prophet of justice. He was a shepherd and a farmer before God called him to be a prophet. Amos preached in the northern kingdom of Israel, condemning the injustices of the wealthy and powerful, and calling for justice for the poor and oppressed. Therefore, the correct answer is justice.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who did Saul collect letters from that permitted him to persecute the Lord's disciples?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Acts 9:1-2, it is recorded that Saul (later known as Paul) obtained letters from the high priest to the synagogues in Damascus, authorizing him to arrest any followers of 'the Way' (early Christians) he found there and bring them back to Jerusalem. Therefore, Saul collected letters from the high priest that permitted him to persecive the Lord's disciples.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION BY SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: For giving out free will to the saints, Paul commended the Church in
explanation: In the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in 2 Corinthians 8:1-5, Apostle Paul commends the churches in Macedonia for their generosity. Despite their affliction and deep poverty, they gave liberally and beyond their means to the saints (fellow believers). Therefore, Paul commended the Church in Macedonia for giving out free will to the saints.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What action of Abraham produced the seal of God on His promises to him?
explanation: The action of Abraham that produced the seal of God on His promises to him was his obedience. In Genesis 22:18, it is stated that 'In your seed all the nations of the earth shall be blessed, because you have obeyed My voice.' This shows that it was Abraham's obedience to God that sealed the promises God made to him.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The book of the Law was found by
explanation: The book of the Law was found by Hilkiah the priest during the reign of King Josiah. This is recorded in 2 Kings 22:8 where it is stated that 'Hilkiah the high priest said to Shaphan the secretary, 'I have found the Book of the Law in the house of the LORD.' This shows that it was Hilkiah the priest who found the book of the Law.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At Delilah's third interrogation of Samson to discover the source of his strength, he replied his strength can only leave him if
options: they weaved the seven locks of his head
he was bound with seven undried fresh bowstrings
explanation:
(Judges 13-15) Samson lies to Delilah about the source of his strength for the third time.

Delilah said to Samson, "Until now you have mocked me and told me lies. Tell me what you may be bound with." And he said to her, "If you weave the seven locks of my head into the web of the loom"; so she wove it tightly with the batten of the loom, and said to him, "The Philistines are upon you, Samson!" But he awoke from his sleep, and pulled out the batten and the web from the loom. Then she said to him, "How can you say, 'I love you,' when your heart is not with me? You have mocked me these three times, and have not told me where your great strength lies."

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
""..O Nebuchadnezzar, we have no need to answer you in this matter..""
What was the reaction of Nebuchadnezzar to the statement above by Shedrach, Meshach and Abednego?
options: They had faith in God to deliver them
They were cast into the burning fiery furnace
They were cast into the lion's den
explanation: In the biblical story of Shedrach, Meshach, and Abednego, the three men refused to bow down to a golden image set up by King Nebuchadnezzar. When they made the statement, Nebuchadnezzar was furious and ordered that they be thrown into a burning fiery furnace. This is found in Daniel 3:16-20.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"This illness is not unto death, it is for the glory of God..."
In which of the miracles of resuscitation did Jesus make the statement above?
explanation: The statement "This illness is not unto death, it is for the glory of God..." was made by Jesus in the context of the miracle of the resurrection of Lazarus. This can be found in the Bible, John 11:4. Therefore, the correct answer is 'The raising of Lazarus'.

topics.title: MIRACLES : MIRACLES OF RESUSCITATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sign of God's covenant with Noah was the
explanation: The sign of God's covenant with Noah was the rainbow. After the flood, God made a covenant with Noah and all living creatures that He would never again destroy the earth with a flood. As a sign of this everlasting covenant, God set a rainbow in the clouds. This can be found in the Bible, Genesis 9:13. Therefore, the correct answer is 'rainbow'.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Acts, for one to be a witness in the body of Christ, he must
explanation: According to Acts 1:8, Jesus told his disciples that they would receive power when the Holy Spirit comes upon them and they would be his witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth. Therefore, to be a witness in the body of Christ, one must receive power from the Holy Spirit.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the unwise decisions of Solomon was that he
options: began to worship other gods in Egypt
explanation: In 1 Kings 11:1-4, it is recorded that King Solomon loved many foreign women in addition to Pharaoh's daughter. These women turned his heart after other gods, and his heart was not fully devoted to the Lord his God, as the heart of David his father had been. This was an unwise decision because it led him to idolatry, which is against God's commandments.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the cause of Nehemiah's sorrow while in exile?
options: He was tired of staying in a foreign land
Sanballat and Tobiah planned to kill him
He didn't get support from the King
explanation: In the book of Nehemiah, it is recorded that Nehemiah was sorrowful because he received news about the broken walls of Jerusalem and the distress of the people there. This is found in Nehemiah 1:3-4, where it says, 'They said to me, 'Those who survived the exile and are back in the province are in great trouble and disgrace. The wall of Jerusalem is broken down, and its gates have been burned with fire.' When I heard these things, I sat down and wept.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'He received bad news about Jerusalem'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter in his teaching on humility enjoins believers to always humble themselves under the
explanation: In 1 Peter 5:6, Peter instructs believers to 'Humble yourselves, therefore, under God's mighty hand, that he may lift you up in due time.' This verse clearly indicates that believers are to humble themselves under the mighty hand of God, not under the leadership of the Church, the Law, or any authority. Therefore, the correct answer is 'mighty hand of God'.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Jesus,Son of David, have mercy on me !"
Who made the statement above?
explanation: The statement "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!" was made by the blind man, Bartimaeus, in the Bible (Mark 10:47). He was a beggar who heard that Jesus was passing by and cried out for mercy. His faith made him receive his sight.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The man sent from God to bear witness to the light was
explanation: The man sent from God to bear witness to the light was John the Baptist. This is stated in the Bible in the book of John 1:6-8. John the Baptist was sent to bear witness of the Light (Jesus Christ) so that all through him might believe. He was not the Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.
