topics.title: JESUS' TEACHING ABOUT HIMSELF: THE DOOR THE LAMB AND THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Hosea, God would punish the house of Jehu for the blood of
explanation: In the book of Hosea, God promised to punish the house of Jehu for the bloodshed at Jezreel. This is because Jehu had killed all the descendants of Ahab in Jezreel as a fulfillment of Elijah's prophecy. However, Jehu did not follow the law of God with all his heart, but became like the house of Jeroboam who led Israel to sin. Therefore, God promised to avenge the blood of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "This is the prophet Jesus from Nazareth of Galilee." The statement above during the Triumphant Entry was made by the
explanation: The statement "This is the prophet Jesus from Nazareth of Galilee." was made by the crowd during the Triumphant Entry of Jesus into Jerusalem. The crowd recognized Jesus as a prophet and acknowledged His place of origin, Nazareth of Galilee. This is recorded in the Gospel of Matthew 21:11.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jonah..."When did Jesus make the statement above?
options: During the feeding of the five thousand
explanation: Jesus made the statement "Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jonah..." when Peter revealed who He is. This event is recorded in Matthew 16:13-20. In this passage, Jesus asked His disciples who people say He is. After hearing their responses, He asked them who they think He is. Peter responded by saying that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of the living God. Jesus then blessed Peter, calling him Simon Bar-Jonah, and told him that this revelation was not from flesh and blood, but from His Father in heaven.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Is it you, you troubler of Israel?".
Why did Ahab refer to Elijah as the troubler of Israel in the statement above
options: Elijah had killed four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal
Elijah stopped the worship of Baal in Israel
Elijah had caused drought and famine for three years
Elijah pronounced evil against Ahab's wife, Jezebel
explanation: Ahab referred to Elijah as the troubler of Israel because Elijah had caused drought and famine for three years. This is recorded in 1 Kings 17:1, where Elijah, by the word of the Lord, declared to Ahab that there would be neither dew nor rain in the next few years except at his word. This caused a severe famine in Samaria (1 Kings 18:2). Ahab blamed Elijah for this trouble (1 Kings 18:17), hence referring to him as the troubler of Israel.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: All the calamity that befell Jerusalem after the death of King Josiah were as a result of
explanation: The calamities that befell Jerusalem after the death of King Josiah were as a result of the sins of Manasseh. Manasseh was a king of Judah who, according to the Hebrew Bible, committed such abominable acts as idolatry and witchcraft, and he led Judah into such severe sin that it provoked God to destroy the kingdom. His sins were said to have provoked God to anger, leading to the calamities that befell Jerusalem.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God compared Israel to a harlot because she
explanation: God compared Israel to a harlot because she followed after other gods. In the Bible, harlotry is often used metaphorically to describe idolatry or unfaithfulness to God. Israel, despite being chosen and loved by God, often turned to worship other gods, which is likened to a woman being unfaithful to her husband. Therefore, the comparison of Israel to a harlot is due to her idolatry.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What happened after Samuel anointed David as King?
options: His eyes were opened to see the task ahead
The spirit of God came mightily upon him
explanation: After Samuel anointed David as King, the spirit of God came mightily upon him. This is recorded in the Bible in 1 Samuel 16:13, where it says 'So Samuel took the horn of oil and anointed him in the presence of his brothers, and from that day on the Spirit of the LORD came powerfully upon David.' Therefore, the correct answer is option 2.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of man has nowhere to lay his head."
The statement above by Jesus implies that
options: we should prepare against the rainy day
believers should be as cunning as foxes
as believers we should have a house of our own
we should surrender all for the kingdom's sake
explanation: The statement 'Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of man has nowhere to lay his head.' by Jesus implies that we should surrender all for the kingdom's sake. This statement is found in Matthew 8:20 and Luke 9:58, and it is often interpreted as Jesus emphasizing the cost of discipleship. Jesus was saying that following Him might mean giving up physical comforts and security. Therefore, the correct answer is option 4.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During the time of Moses, the congregation of Israel planned to choose a captain and return to Egypt because
options: they were tired of eating the same kind of food
the host of Pharaoh was approaching their camp
explanation: In the book of Numbers 13:31-32, the spies that Moses sent to scout the land of Canaan returned with a discouraging report about the land. They reported that the people who dwell in the land were strong, and the cities were fortified and very large. This report made the congregation of Israel to lose faith and they planned to choose a captain and return to Egypt.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the reward of the Hebrew midwives when they refused to obey the instruction given to them by Pharaoh?
explanation: In Exodus 1:15-21, the Hebrew midwives, Shiphrah and Puah, feared God and did not do as the king of Egypt commanded them, but saved the male children alive. Therefore, God dealt well with the midwives and the people multiplied and grew very mighty. And because the midwives feared God, He gave them families.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter, the reward of the elders who tend the flock of God is
explanation: In 1 Peter 5:4, Peter states that when the Chief Shepherd appears, the elders who have tended the flock of God will receive the unfading crown of glory. This means that their reward is not a physical or material one, but a spiritual one that will never fade away.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : THE BENEFITS OF BEING JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Romans, Paul made it clear that believers were buried with christ so that when He is resurrected, they might
options: be partakers of the gifts of God
explanation: In Romans 6:4, Paul explains that believers were buried with Christ through baptism into death in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father, they too may live a new life. This means that believers are not just waiting for a future resurrection, but are called to live a new, transformed life right now.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Where is he who has been born King of the Jews.."
The statement above about Jesus' birth was made by
explanation: The statement "Where is he who has been born King of the Jews.." was made by the wise men. This is recorded in the Bible in Matthew 2:2. The wise men were guided by a star to the place where Jesus was born and they asked this question to Herod the king.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: On the occasion of Jesus' resurrection, the presence of the angel that rolled back the stone on His sepulchre caused
explanation: On the occasion of Jesus' resurrection, the presence of the angel that rolled back the stone on His sepulchre caused an earthquake. This is recorded in the Bible in Matthew 28:2. The earthquake occurred when the angel of the Lord descended from heaven, came and rolled back the stone and sat on it.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The plant which the Lord appointed as shade over Jonah was to
explanation: In the book of Jonah, the plant which the Lord appointed as shade over Jonah was to educate him. God used the plant to teach Jonah about compassion. After Jonah was upset about the plant's death, God pointed out that Jonah cared about the plant, which came up in a night and perished in a night. And should not God care about the great city of Nineveh, which had more than a hundred and twenty thousand people who could not tell their right hand from their left, and also many animals?

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God forgave the Israelites and asked Jeremiah to proclaim His words toward the
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah, God forgave the Israelites and asked Jeremiah to proclaim His words toward the north. This is because the northern kingdom of Israel had been taken into captivity by the Assyrians. God was directing His message to them, promising that He would bring them back to their land.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Romans, for a believer to be justified by faith,the Law and the Prophets have to
explanation: According to Romans 3:21, the Law and the Prophets bear witness to the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ. This means that the Law and the Prophets testify or give evidence to the fact that believers are justified by faith.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, one who speaks in tongues speaks to
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 14:2, Paul states that anyone who speaks in a tongue does not speak to men but to God. This is because in a tongue, one speaks mysteries with the Spirit, which are understood by God.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jehoshaphat did what was right in the sight of God but the people still sacrificed on the high places because he
options: did not take away the high places
did not destroy those who oppose him
love the people and allow them to do their will
explanation: Jehoshaphat was a king who did what was right in the sight of God. However, the people still sacrificed on the high places because he did not take away the high places. High places were locations where ancient Israelites worshipped, and they were often associated with idolatrous practices. Jehoshaphat's failure to remove these places allowed the people to continue their sacrifices.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the consequence of Gehazi's greed?
options: He was asked to cleanse the temple
explanation: The consequence of Gehazi's greed was that Naaman's leprosy cleaved to him. Gehazi was the servant of Elisha, and he lied to Naaman to get gifts that Elisha had refused. When Elisha found out, he cursed Gehazi, saying that Naaman's leprosy would cling to Gehazi and his descendants forever.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"I adjure you by the living God, tell us if you are the Christ, the son of God.".
Jesus was asked the question above during His trial before
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 26:63, it was the high priest who asked Jesus if He was the Christ, the Son of God during His trial. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the high priest'.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The two sons of Eli refused to listen to their father's instruction because
options: they were following the instructions of the gods
they were acting outside the will of God
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Samuel 2:12-25, Eli's sons, Hophni and Phinehas, were described as men who had no regard for the Lord or for the duties of the priests to the people. They were acting outside the will of God, which led to their downfall. Therefore, the correct answer is 'they were acting outside the will of God'.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the second coming of the Lord according to Thessalonians, Jesus will descend from heaven with
explanation: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:16, 'For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'a cry of command'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, he who doubts is like
explanation: According to James 1:6, 'But let him ask in faith, with no doubting, for the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea that is driven and tossed by the wind.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'waves of the sea'.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : RELIANCE ON A MEDIUM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "...I will blot out man whom I have created from the face of the ground. For I am sorry that I have made them. God made the statement above during Noah's time because
options: the people refused to heed Noah's warning
the sons of God were not recognised by the people as His representatives
the sons of God went into the daughters of men and they bore children
explanation: The statement was made by God during Noah's time because the sons of God went into the daughters of men and they bore children. This is a reference to Genesis 6:1-7, where it is stated that the sons of God saw that the daughters of men were attractive and they took as their wives any they chose. This led to a race of giants known as the Nephilim and it was one of the reasons God decided to destroy the earth with a flood.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Romans, every authority that exist is instituted by
explanation: According to Romans 13:1, every authority that exists is instituted by God. The verse states 'Let every person be subject to the governing authorities. For there is no authority except from God, and those that exist have been instituted by God.' This means that all authority comes from God and He is the one who establishes it.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The LORD led the Israelites round by the way of the wilderness and not through that of the Philistines which was near because
options: the Israelites preferred going through the wilderness
He didn't want them to encounter war and go back to Egypt
He wanted the journey to be long
He wanted to teach them patience and perseverance
explanation: In Exodus 13:17, it is stated that when Pharaoh let the people go, God did not lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, although that was near. For God said, 'Lest the people change their minds when they see war and return to Egypt.' Therefore, the reason was that God didn't want them to encounter war and go back to Egypt.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In His teaching on the parable of the sower, Jesus said that when anyone hears the word and does not understand it, it means that
options: he will fall away when tribulation and persecution arise
the cares of this world and the delighting riches choked it up
the evil one has come and snatched away what is sown in his heart
trials and temptations swept him off his feet
explanation: In Matthew 13:19, Jesus explains the parable of the sower and says 'When anyone hears the word of the kingdom and does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what has been sown in his heart.' Therefore, it means that the evil one has come and snatched away what is sown in his heart.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The remnant of the tribes that the children of Israel could not utterly destroy were made slaves by
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Kings 9:20-21, it was Solomon who made the remnant of the tribes that the children of Israel could not utterly destroy into slaves. These people were the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, who were not of the children of Israel. They were made to pay tribute and were not exterminated by the Israelites as they were supposed to do according to God's command.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the sermon of Jesus on the mount, the meek shall
explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, specifically in Matthew 5:5, Jesus said, 'Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.' The meek, according to Jesus, are those who are humble and gentle, not proud and aggressive. The promise that they will 'inherit the earth' is an assurance of God's ultimate blessing and provision.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Woe is me! For I am lost, for I am a man of unclean lips..."
What happened after Isaiah made the statement above?
options: The LORD put forth His hand and touched his mouth
The Seraphim touched his lips with a burning coal
He was given a scroll to eat
The Seraphim lifted him on their wings
explanation: In the book of Isaiah, after Isaiah made the statement "Woe is me! For I am lost, for I am a man of unclean lips...", a seraphim flew to him with a live coal in his hand, which he had taken with tongs from the altar. The seraphim touched Isaiah's mouth with it and said, "Behold, this has touched your lips; your guilt is taken away, and your sin atoned for." (Isaiah 6:5-7). Therefore, the correct answer is that the Seraphim touched his lips with a burning coal.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the book of Amos, God is described as one who builds His upper chambers in the heavens to depict His
explanation: In the book of Amos, God is described as one who builds His upper chambers in the heavens. This is a depiction of His sovereignty. Sovereignty refers to supreme power or authority. In this context, it means that God has ultimate control over the heavens and the earth. Therefore, the correct answer is sovereignty.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them."
According to the books of Acts, what work were the people mentioned above set apart for?
options: To heal the sick and raise the dead
To receive new converts to the Church
To preach the gospel to the Gentiles
explanation: In the book of Acts, specifically Acts 13:2, Barnabas and Saul (who is also known as Paul) were set apart by the Holy Spirit for the specific work of preaching the gospel to the Gentiles. This was a significant event as it marked the beginning of Paul's missionary journeys, where he traveled extensively to spread the gospel to non-Jewish people (Gentiles).

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in his letter to Philemon pleaded with him to accept Onesimus back as a
explanation: In the book of Philemon, Paul wrote a letter to Philemon, a fellow Christian, pleading with him to accept Onesimus back not as a slave, but as a 'useful brother' in Christ. Onesimus was a slave who had run away from Philemon, but during his time away, he had become a Christian under Paul's guidance. Paul's letter to Philemon is a plea for Christian brotherhood and forgiveness.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Son of man, stand upon your feet, and I will speak with you."
In the statement above, who was referred to as son of man?
explanation: The phrase 'Son of Man' is used in the Bible to refer to various individuals, depending on the context. In the context of the given statement, it is referring to the prophet Ezekiel. This is evident in the book of Ezekiel, where God often addresses Ezekiel as 'Son of Man'.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Tell me whether you sold the land for so much."
Peter's statement above to Sapphira was to
explanation: In the book of Acts, Peter asks Sapphira about the price of the land to test her sincerity. Sapphira and her husband Ananias had sold a piece of property and kept back part of the money for themselves, but claimed they had given all the money to the church. Peter's question was a test to see if Sapphira would tell the truth or continue the lie.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Jesus asked his disciples that 'who do men say that I am?', Peter said He was
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 16:15-16, when Jesus asked his disciples, 'Who do you say that I am?', Peter responded by saying 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'Christ'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : THE BENEFITS OF BEING JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul instructs that any Christian who is idle and lazy should be
explanation: In 2 Thessalonians 3:6-15, Paul instructs that any Christian who is idle and does not work should be ostracised. This means they should be avoided or excluded from the community until they change their ways. Therefore, the correct answer is 'ostracised'.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Obadiah, who was in charge of Ahab's household, ......
options: hid a hundred prophets of God from Jezebel's wrath
was in charge of the worship of Baal
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Kings 18:4, it is mentioned that Obadiah, who was in charge of Ahab's household, hid a hundred prophets of God in a cave when Jezebel was killing off the Lord's prophets. Therefore, the correct answer is that Obadiah 'hid a hundred prophets of God from Jezebel's wrath'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In apostle Paul's second epistle to the Thessalonians, what did he deem fit to thank God for on their behalf?
options: their salvation, growing faith and brotherly love
their salvation, growing faith, brotherly love and effective prayer life
explanation: In 2 Thessalonians 1:3, Apostle Paul expresses his gratitude to God for the Thessalonians' growing faith and increasing love for each other. Therefore, the correct answer is 'their salvation, growing faith and brotherly love'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : PREPARATION FOR HIS COMING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David submitted to the will of God when God killed the child he had with Bathsheba by
explanation: In the Bible, 2 Samuel 12:20, after God struck the child that Uriah's wife bore to David, David arose from the earth, washed and anointed himself, changed his clothes, and went into the house of the LORD and worshiped. Then he went to his own house, and when he asked, they set food before him, and he ate. This signifies the end of his fast.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
'But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus'.
'This statement was made by Paul to the
explanation: The statement 'But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus' is found in Philippians 4:19. Therefore, it was made by Paul to the Philippians.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE TEACHINGS OF PAUL ON CHRISTIAN GIVING.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul and Silas were sent on a mission by the
explanation: In the book of Acts 13:1-3, it was the church in Antioch that sent Paul and Silas on a mission. The Holy Spirit had directed the church to set apart Paul and Silas for the work to which He had called them. After fasting and praying, the church sent them off.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's letter to Philemon, he stated that forgiveness should be
explanation: In Paul's letter to Philemon, he appealed to Philemon to forgive Onesimus, his runaway slave who had wronged him. Paul did not command Philemon to forgive Onesimus but rather appealed to him to do so willingly out of love (Philemon 1:8-9). Therefore, forgiveness should be given willingly according to Paul's letter to Philemon.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Before the death and ascension of Jesus, He promised to give us another
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of John 14:16, Jesus promised to send another 'Comforter' (also known as the Holy Spirit) to his disciples after his ascension. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Comforter'.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Romans, those who commit acts of homosexuality are worthy of
options: being ostracised from the body of christ
explanation: According to Romans 1:26-27 and 1:32 in the Bible, those who commit acts of homosexuality are described as deserving death. This does not necessarily mean physical death, but can also refer to spiritual death or separation from God. Therefore, the correct answer is 'death'.

topics.title: SEXUAL IMMORALITY : HOMOSEXUALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The first martyr of Christ died
explanation: The first martyr of Christ was Stephen, who was stoned to death as recorded in Acts 7:54-60. Therefore, the correct answer is 'through stoning'.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE MARTYRDOM OF STEPHEN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The transfiguration took place on a high mountain in the presence of the following excluding
explanation: The transfiguration of Jesus took place on a high mountain in the presence of Peter, James, and John as recorded in Matthew 17:1-9. Andrew was not present at the transfiguration, making 'Andrew' the correct answer.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Herod sought Jesus to kill, his father hid Him in
explanation: In the Bible, it is recorded in Matthew 2:13-15 that an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and instructed him to take the child Jesus and his mother Mary and flee to Egypt. This was to escape King Herod who sought to kill Jesus. Therefore, the correct answer is Egypt.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was King Ahaseurus' chamberlain who kept his concubines?
explanation: In the Book of Esther in the Bible, King Ahasuerus' chamberlain who was in charge of his concubines was named Shaashgaz (Esther 2:14). Therefore, the correct answer is Shaashgaz.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Thessalonians told believers that the coming of the Lord would be accompanied by
explanation: In 1 Thessalonians 4:16, Paul describes the coming of the Lord as being accompanied by a shout, with the voice of the archangel and with the trumpet of God. Therefore, the correct answer is 'a shout'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Seeing that you reject it, behold, we turn to the Gentiles". The circumstance surrounding this statement by Paul and Barnabas was
options: The Jews contradicted the gospel, trying to mislead others
The Sadducees antagonised the teaching of resurrection
explanation: In Acts 13:45-46, Paul and Barnabas turned to the Gentiles after the Jews contradicted the gospel and tried to mislead others. Therefore, the correct answer is 'The Jews contradicted the gospel, trying to mislead others'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, effective prayer is that which is offered
explanation: According to the book of James in the Bible, effective prayer is that which is offered in faith. This means that when we pray, we should believe in what we are praying for and have faith that it will come to pass. This concept is based on the belief in the power of faith and prayer in Christianity.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The consequence of Hiel's building of Jericho was that he
options: was made a commander in Ahab's army
explanation: The consequence of Hiel's building of Jericho was that he lost two of his sons. This is according to the biblical account in 1 Kings 16:34, which states that Hiel of Bethel rebuilt Jericho and in the process, he laid its foundations at the cost of his firstborn son Abiram, and set up its gates at the cost of his youngest son Segub. This was in fulfillment of the curse pronounced by Joshua upon anyone who would rebuild the city of Jericho.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus' coming to earth in the form of a human showed His
explanation: Jesus' coming to earth in the form of a human showed His humility. This is because humility refers to the quality of being humble. In Christian theology, Jesus' incarnation (coming to earth in human form) is seen as an act of humility, as He, being God, chose to take on human nature and live among humans.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the sermon on the mount, Jesus said those who make peace should rejoice because
explanation: In the sermon on the mount, Jesus said those who make peace should rejoice because they will be called God's child. This is found in Matthew 5:9, where Jesus says, 'Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.' This means that those who strive to make peace among people are doing God's work and will be recognized as His children.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jonah was supposed to go to ...... where God sent him but he headed to ......
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically in the book of Jonah, Jonah was commanded by God to go to Nineveh to prophesy against it because of its wickedness. However, Jonah disobeyed God and instead headed to Tarshish. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Nineveh / Tarshish'.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christian women should be submissive to their husbands just as ...... did
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Peter 3:1-6, Christian women are encouraged to be submissive to their husbands, following the example of Sarah, who obeyed Abraham and called him her lord. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Sarah'.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these accounts didn't record the spread of tree branches during the Triumphant entry?
explanation: The account of the Triumphant entry of Jesus into Jerusalem is recorded in all four Gospels: Matthew 21:1-11, Mark 11:1-11, Luke 19:28-44, and John 12:12-19. However, the detail of the spread of tree branches is not mentioned in the Gospel of John. Therefore, John is the correct answer.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The devil promised to give Jesus all the kingdom of the world if He
explanation: In the temptation of Jesus as recorded in Matthew 4:1-11 and Luke 4:1-13, the devil promised to give Jesus all the kingdoms of the world if He would bow down and worship him (Matthew 4:8-9, Luke 4:5-7). Therefore, the correct answer is 'bowed down to him'.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, the kingdom of God is not in word but in
explanation: In the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in 1 Corinthians 4:20, Apostle Paul states that 'For the kingdom of God is not a matter of talk but of power.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'power'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Paul says 'If anyone will not work, let him not eat', he means
options: every person should toil and labour to earn a living
explanation: In 2 Thessalonians 3:10, Apostle Paul states 'For even when we were with you, we gave you this rule: The one who is unwilling to work shall not eat.' This means that everyone should work to earn their living. Therefore, the correct answer is 'every person should toil and labour to earn a living'.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : BENEFITS OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these was not part of the report Nehemiah got from Judah?
options: the remnant of the people were in great distress
the wall of Jerusalem had been broken down
explanation: In the book of Nehemiah, the report that Nehemiah got from Judah included that the remnant of the people were in great distress, the wall of Jerusalem had been broken down, and the gates were burnt. However, there was no mention of the people being idolatrous. Therefore, the statement 'the people were idolatrous' was not part of the report Nehemiah got from Judah.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Galatians, one of the benefits of being joint heirs with Christ is having His Spirit in
explanation: According to Galatians 4:6, 'Because you are his sons, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, the Spirit who calls out, "Abba, Father."' This means that one of the benefits of being joint heirs with Christ is having His Spirit in our hearts. Therefore, the correct answer is 'our hearts'.

explanation: Gehazi was the servant of Elisha. This is a biblical fact found in the Old Testament of the Bible, specifically in the book of 2 Kings. Gehazi served as Elisha's attendant and was involved in several of Elisha's miracles.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"No one, having put his hand to the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God".
By this, Jesus meant that
options: looking back while ploughing makes a person disqualified from the kingdom of God
anyone who is ploughing must be serious
the work of God permits no compromise
we should not plough if we want to enter God's kingdom
explanation: The statement "No one, having put his hand to the plough, and looking back, is fit for the kingdom of God" is a metaphor used by Jesus to illustrate the level of commitment required in the service of God. It means that once a person has decided to follow God, they should not be distracted or turn back. This implies that the work of God permits no compromise.

explanation: The son Abraham had with Hagar was named Ishmael. This is a biblical fact found in the book of Genesis 16:15.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: These were charges against Jesus except
options: saying that he himself is christ, a king
explanation: The charges against Jesus were perverting the nation, forbidding to give tribute to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king. However, prophesying about the kingdom of God was not a charge against him. This is a biblical fact found in the book of Luke 23:2.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE HEROD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
'This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased'.
These words were spoken
explanation: The phrase 'This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased' was spoken by God after Jesus' baptism. This event is recorded in the Gospels of Matthew (3:17), Mark (1:11), and Luke (3:22). After Jesus was baptized by John the Baptist, the heavens opened, and the Spirit of God descended like a dove and alighted on him. Then a voice from heaven said, 'This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'after Jesus' baptism'.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: John the baptist likened his relationship with Christ to that of
explanation: John the Baptist likened his relationship with Christ to that of a bridegroom and his friend. This is recorded in the Gospel of John (3:29), where John the Baptist says, 'The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend who attends the bridegroom waits and listens for him, and is full of joy when he hears the bridegroom's voice. That joy is mine, and it is now complete.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'a bridegroom and his friend'.

q: Prophet Hosea was to marry a prostitute to portray
options: that christians are permitted to marry prostitutes
anyone could be used by God irrespective of their religious status
Israel's unfaithfulness as opposed to God's love
explanation: In the book of Hosea, God commanded the prophet Hosea to marry a prostitute as a symbolic act. This was to portray the unfaithfulness of Israel to God, despite His steadfast love for them. The marriage between Hosea and the prostitute symbolizes the relationship between God (Hosea) and Israel (the prostitute), where Israel has been unfaithful by worshiping other gods, just like a prostitute is unfaithful to her spouse.

explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 17:1-9, it was Peter who suggested that three tabernacles be made for Jesus, Elijah, and Moses during the transfiguration of Jesus. Therefore, the correct answer is Peter.

explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 17:1-9, it is recorded that Moses and Elijah appeared to Jesus during the transfiguration. Therefore, the correct answer is Moses and Elijah.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The trials of Jesus took place before the following people except
explanation: The trials of Jesus took place before the High Priest, Pilate, and Herod. However, there is no biblical record of Jesus being tried before the Pharisees and Saducees. The Pharisees and Saducees were groups of influential religious leaders, but they did not individually try Jesus. They were part of the Sanhedrin, the Jewish ruling council, which collectively tried Jesus. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Pharisees and Saducees'.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

Are you the king of the Jews?
explanation: During Jesus' trial, the high priest asked Him, 'Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?' (Mark 14:61). The high priest was asking if Jesus was the Messiah, the anointed one who was prophesied to come and save the Jewish people. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Are you the Christ?'.

explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 16:13-16, when Jesus asked his disciples, 'Who do people say the Son of Man is?', they replied, 'Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets. Since men mentioned different prophets like John, Elijah and Jeremiah, the correct answer is 'One of the Prophets'.

explanation: The statement 'For all have sinned and come short of the glory of God but we are being justified' is followed by 'by grace' according to the Bible in Romans 3:23-24. Grace is the unmerited favor of God, and it is by this grace, not by our works or obedience to commandments, that we are justified or declared righteous before God. This is a central concept in Christian theology, emphasizing the love, mercy, and generosity of God.

Onesimus had wronged Philemon and ran away. He later met Paul during his imprisonment and became a believer of Christ. Paul, seeing the change in Onesimus, decided to send him back to Philemon with a letter pleading for his forgiveness and acceptance not just as a servant but as a brother in Christ.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Blessed are the peacemakers, for
options: they shall be called the sons of God
explanation: The phrase 'Blessed are the peacemakers, for' is a part of the Beatitudes, a section of the Sermon on the Mount in the Bible. The full verse, found in Matthew 5:9, is 'Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called the sons of God.' The Beatitudes are a series of blessings pronounced by Jesus on various groups of people who are in unfortunate circumstances, but display attitudes that should be emulated. In this case, peacemakers are those who strive for peace and reconciliation, and they are blessed because they will be called the sons of God.

After His resurrection, Jesus appeared to these two disciples who were traveling to Emmaus, a village about seven miles from Jerusalem. They were discussing the events of Jesus' crucifixion and resurrection, and Jesus joined them and explained the Scriptures to them.

This means that those who are humble, instead of being prideful, will be lifted up by God.

topics.title: HUMILITY : REWARDS OF HUMILITY

explanation: The phrase 'For all have sinned and come short of the glory of God but we are being justified' is from the Bible, specifically Romans 3:23-24. It means that all humans have sinned and therefore fall short of God's glory, but they are justified by His grace through the redemption that came by Christ Jesus. Therefore, the correct answer is 'by grace'.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Paul says 'If anyone will not work, let him not eat', he means
options: every person should toil and labour to earn a living
explanation: The phrase 'If anyone will not work, let him not eat' is from the Bible, specifically 2 Thessalonians 3:10. It means that those who are able to work should do so and not live off others. Therefore, the correct answer is 'every person should toil and labour to earn a living'.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : BENEFITS OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the book of James, the rich is condemned due to their acts of
explanation: In the book of James, the rich are condemned due to their acts of greed. James 5:1-6 criticizes the rich who hoard wealth, fail to pay proper wages, and live in self-indulgence.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the account of Matthew, the mission of the twelve was to
explanation: According to the account of Matthew, specifically Matthew 10:5-6, the mission of the twelve was to the lost sheep of Israel. Jesus instructed his disciples not to go among the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans, but rather go to the lost sheep of Israel.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Gehazi's greed made him take ...... and ...... from Naaman and he was punished with ......
explanation: In the Bible, specifically 2 Kings 5:20-27, Gehazi, the servant of Elisha the man of God, said to himself, 'My master was too easy on Naaman, this Aramean, by not accepting from him what he brought. As surely as the LORD lives, I will run after him and get something from him.' So Gehazi hurried after Naaman. When Naaman saw him running toward him, he got down from the chariot to meet him. Gehazi told him a lie that Elisha had sent him to collect two talents of silver and two sets of clothing. Naaman gave him two talents of silver in two bags, with two sets of clothing. But when he went in and stood before his master, Elisha asked him, 'Where have you been, Gehazi?' Gehazi answered, 'Your servant didn't go anywhere.' Then Elisha said to him, 'Was not my spirit with you when the man got down from his chariot to meet you? Is this the time to take money or to accept clothes—or olive groves and vineyards, or flocks and herds, or male and female slaves? Naaman's leprosy will cling to you and to your descendants forever.' Then Gehazi went from Elisha's presence and his skin was leprous—it had become as white as snow.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The beatitudes provide that those who mourn shall
explanation: In the Bible, specifically Matthew 5:4, it is stated in the Beatitudes that 'Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.' This is a promise from Jesus during His Sermon on the Mount. The comfort that Jesus speaks about here is a unique comfort that only God can give to those who mourn in a godly way.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: As Christians, we are enjoined to be good citizens by
explanation: As Christians, we are enjoined to be good citizens by submitting to civil authorities. This is based on the biblical teaching in Romans 13:1-2 which instructs Christians to be subject to governing authorities because they have been instituted by God. Avoiding political activities, questioning the government always, or instigating the people against the government do not align with this teaching.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'Silver and gold have I none, but such as I have, give I thee'. These words were spoken by
explanation: The statement 'Silver and gold have I none, but such as I have, give I thee' was spoken by Peter in the Bible, specifically in Acts 3:6. In this context, Peter was addressing a lame man who was begging for alms. Instead of giving him money, Peter healed him in the name of Jesus Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is Peter.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE ARREST AND IMPRISONMENT OF PETER AND JOHN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After the transfiguration of Jesus, He confirmed that Elijah has returned to earth in the form of
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 17:10-13, Jesus confirmed that Elijah has returned to earth in the form of John the Baptist. This was after His transfiguration. Therefore, the correct answer is 'John the Baptist'.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TRANSFIGURATION TO THE DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these is not a lesson of the story of prophet Jonah?
explanation: The story of Jonah in the Bible teaches several lessons including the terrible consequences of disobedience to God, the mercy of God, and the fact that God's thoughts are not like men's thoughts. However, the name 'Jehovah-Jireh', which means 'The Lord Will Provide', is not a lesson from the story of Jonah. Therefore, the correct answer is 'God is Jehovah-Jireh'.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The multitude that followed Jesus during the triumphant entry rejoiced because
explanation: In the Bible, it is recorded that the multitude that followed Jesus during the triumphant entry rejoiced because they believed He was their Messiah. They had seen His miracles and heard His teachings, and they believed that He was the promised one who would deliver them.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in Thessalonians told believers that the coming of the Lord would be accompanied by
explanation: In 1 Thessalonians 4:16, Paul describes the coming of the Lord saying, 'For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God.' Therefore, the coming of the Lord would be accompanied by a shout.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these is not a fruit of the spirit?
explanation: The fruits of the Spirit are listed in Galatians 5:22-23 as love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control. Righteousness is not listed as a fruit of the Spirit in this passage.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : FRUITS OF JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: John the baptist referred to Jesus as the Lamb of God
options: who takes away the sin of the world
who has been endowed with great power
explanation: In John 1:29, John the Baptist refers to Jesus as the 'Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world.' This is a reference to the sacrificial system of the Old Testament, where a lamb without blemish was sacrificed for the sins of the people. Jesus, as the Lamb of God, was the ultimate sacrifice for the sins of the world.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHING ABOUT HIMSELF: THE DOOR THE LAMB AND THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The tearing of the curtain of the Temple into two at the death of Jesus signifies
options: destruction of the earth of His death
the division of the church in later days
explanation: The tearing of the curtain of the Temple into two at the death of Jesus signifies direct access to God through Christ. In the Old Testament, the curtain in the temple was a physical barrier to the Holy of Holies - the most sacred place where God's presence dwelt. Only the high priest could enter, and only once a year on the Day of Atonement. When Jesus died, the curtain was torn, symbolizing that the barrier between God and humanity was removed. Now, through Jesus, everyone has direct access to God.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's letter to Philemon, he stated that forgiveness should be
explanation: In Paul's letter to Philemon, he stated that forgiveness should be willingly. Paul was writing to Philemon on behalf of Onesimus, a slave who had wronged Philemon and run away. Paul had led Onesimus to Christ and was sending him back to Philemon, not as a slave, but as a brother in Christ. Paul urged Philemon to forgive Onesimus, not out of compulsion, but willingly, as a reflection of the forgiveness we have received in Christ.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Even in a state of great trial affliction and poverty, the churches of ...... still engaged in christian giving
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 2 Corinthians 8:1-2, Apostle Paul commends the churches of Macedonia for their generosity even in a time of severe trial and extreme poverty. Therefore, the correct answer is Macedonia.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: James stated in his epistle that when believes are afflicted they should
explanation: In the book of James 5:13, it is stated that when believers are afflicted, they should pray. Therefore, the correct answer is 'pray'.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The life of Asa and his son Jehoshapat demonstrates
options: unrighteousness in the sight of the Lord
explanation: Asa and his son Jehoshaphat were kings of Judah who were known for their faith in God. They led the people of Judah in worshiping God and obeying His commands. This is evident in the Bible, particularly in the books of 1 Kings and 2 Chronicles.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ASA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The walls of Jerusalem were rebuilt in
explanation: The walls of Jerusalem were rebuilt in 52 days. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Nehemiah chapter 6 verse 15 which states, 'So the wall was completed on the twenty-fifth of Elul, in fifty-two days.'

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The question that arose in the church in Jerusalem was settled with the conclusion that
options: it is not necessary for gentiles to be circumcised
circumcision is a law that must be adhered to
salvation is gotten from obeying the law
explanation: The question that arose in the church in Jerusalem was about whether Gentiles who converted to Christianity needed to be circumcised according to Jewish law. The conclusion was that it was not necessary for Gentiles to be circumcised. This decision was made at the Council of Jerusalem, as recorded in Acts 15.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "A prophet is not without honour, except in his own country". This statement was made by
options: Jeremiah, following his despiteful usage in Judah
explanation: The statement "A prophet is not without honour, except in his own country" was made by Jesus in the New Testament of the Bible. This happened when Jesus visited his hometown of Nazareth and was rejected by the people there because they saw him as just a carpenter's son. This is recorded in the Gospel of Mark 6:4 and Matthew 13:57.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE LIGHT OF THE WORLD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God didn't allow David to build Him a house because
options: he didn't effectively perform his parental responsibility
he slew men at war and had blood on his hands
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Chronicles 22:8, it is stated that God didn't allow David to build Him a house because he had shed much blood and fought many wars. Therefore, the correct answer is 'he slew men at war and had blood on his hands'.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'As I live saith the Lord God, ye shall not have occasion any more to use this proverb in Isreal'. What proverb is referred to here
options: I will have mercy upon whom I will have mercy
The fathers have eaten sour grapes and the children's teeth are set on edge
A great nation that will be counted as the sand of the sea shall be counted will i make thee
We are the chosen generation brought forth to show forth the Lord's excellence
explanation: The proverb referred to in this question is found in Ezekiel 18:2-3. The Israelites used to say, 'The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge'. God was telling them through Ezekiel that they would no longer use this proverb in Israel, meaning that everyone would be responsible for their own sins, not the sins of their fathers. Therefore, the correct answer is 'The fathers have eaten sour grapes and the children's teeth are set on edge'.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE CONDITIONS FOR HOPE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the time of Amos, God promised to bring famine upon Isreal. Famine here means
explanation:
Amos 8: 11
Behold, the days come , saith the Lord God that i will send a famine in the land, not a famine of bread,nor a thirst for water, but of hearing the words of Lord.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians should lay up their treasures in Heaven rather than Earth becase
options: rot and moth destroy not things on Earth
it is impossible to reach Heaven while on Earth
Earth and Heaven are the abode of man
explanation: In the Bible, particularly in Matthew 6:19-20, Jesus advises his followers to store up treasures in heaven rather than on earth. The reason for this is that treasures on earth can be destroyed or stolen, but treasures in heaven are eternal and cannot be destroyed or stolen. This teaches Christians to value spiritual rewards over worldly possessions.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, judgement is without mercy to one who has shown no mercy, yet mercy triumphs over
explanation: In the book of James 2:13, it is stated that 'judgement is without mercy to one who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgement.' This means that if a person does not show mercy to others, they will not receive mercy in judgement. However, mercy is superior to judgement because it can pardon even the guilty.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : BENEFITS OF FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which disciples did Jesus take along with Him to the mount where He was transfigured?
explanation: In the New Testament, specifically in Matthew 17:1-2, Mark 9:2-3, and Luke 9:28-29, it is recorded that Jesus took Peter, James, and John to a high mountain where He was transfigured before them. Therefore, the correct disciples are James, John, and Peter.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David submitted to the will of God when God killed the child he had with Bathsheba by
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 2 Samuel 12:20, it is mentioned that after the death of David's child with Bathsheba, David ended his fast. He had been fasting in the hope that God would be gracious and the child would live. However, when the child died, David accepted God's will, ended his fast, cleaned himself up, and went to the house of the Lord to worship.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When the Amalekites invaded Zilag and David and his men had mourned their captured relation, the first thing David did was that he
options: commanded that the ephod be brought to him
explanation: In 1 Samuel 30:6, it is mentioned that when David and his men found out that their wives and children had been taken captive by the Amalekites, David was greatly distressed. However, he found strength in the Lord his God. This means he encouraged himself in God before doing anything else. This is a crucial step as it shows David's faith and reliance on God in times of distress.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At the second coming of Christ, those who are still alive shall be
options: left to wait for another three years
bound and taken up with the Lord
explanation: According to Christian eschatology, at the second coming of Christ, those who are still alive and believe in Him will be caught up together with Him in the clouds. This is based on 1 Thessalonians 4:17 which says, 'Then we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'caught up together with the Lord'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At the second coming of Christ
options: the dead in christ shall rise first
those who are alive will have to die first
everyone on earth shall meet the Lord in the air
explanation: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:16, 'For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first.' This means that at the second coming of Christ, the dead who believed in Christ will be the first to rise. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the dead in christ shall rise first'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of this places did apostle Paul not vist in his first missionary journey?
explanation: In Apostle Paul's first missionary journey, he visited places like Paphos, Pisidian Antioch, Iconium, Lystra, and Derbe. However, he did not visit Samothracia. Samothracia was actually a place visited by Paul during his second missionary journey, as recorded in Acts 16:11.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where did Paul heal a man who was born cripple?
explanation: In Acts 14:8-10, it is recorded that Paul healed a man who was born crippled in the city of Lystra. The man had faith to be healed and Paul, seeing this, commanded him to stand up straight on his feet, and the man leaped and walked.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the first Christian martyr?
explanation: Stephen is considered the first Christian martyr. He was stoned to death for blasphemy against the Jewish Temple, according to the Acts of the Apostles in the New Testament of the Bible.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE MARTYRDOM OF STEPHEN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'It is necessary to circumcise them and to charge them to keep the law of Moses". Peter's response to the statement above was that
options: through the grace of Christ, both Gentiles and Jews are saved
there is a difference between us and them
though Gentiles believe, they can'r be like the Jews
It is only circumcision that can make gentiles to be saved
explanation: In the context of the early Christian church, there was a debate about whether Gentile converts needed to follow Jewish customs, such as circumcision, to be saved. Peter, one of the apostles, argued that both Jews and Gentiles are saved through the grace of Jesus Christ, not by following the law of Moses. This is recorded in Acts 15:11.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nehemiah had access to ask for permission to visit Jerusalem because
options: he was one of the king's advisers
explanation: Nehemiah was able to ask for permission to visit Jerusalem because he was the king's cup bearer. In the Bible, Nehemiah 1:11, Nehemiah is described as the king's cup bearer. This position gave him close access to the king, allowing him to ask for permission to visit Jerusalem.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The king who granted Nehemiah permission to go and rebuild Jerusalem was
explanation: The king who granted Nehemiah permission to go and rebuild Jerusalem was Artaxerxes. According to the Bible, in the book of Nehemiah 2:1, it was King Artaxerxes who granted Nehemiah the permission to go and rebuild Jerusalem.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christian women should be submissive to their husbands just as ...... did
explanation: In the Christian faith, Sarah, the wife of Abraham, is often used as an example of a woman who was submissive to her husband. This is seen in 1 Peter 3:5-6, where it is written, 'For this is the way the holy women of the past who put their hope in God used to adorn themselves. They submitted themselves to their own husbands, like Sarah, who obeyed Abraham and called him her lord.' Therefore, Christian women are often encouraged to be submissive to their husbands just as Sarah was to Abraham.

These words were spoken

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Gehazi's greed made him take ...... and ...... from Naaman and he was punished with ......
explanation: In the Bible, 2 Kings 5:20-27 tells the story of Gehazi, the servant of Elisha, who, driven by greed, lied to Naaman (the commander of the army of the king of Aram who was cured of leprosy by Elisha) and took silver and garments from him. When Elisha found out, he cursed Gehazi and his descendants with leprosy. Therefore, the correct answer is 'silver and gold/leprosy'.

q: According to James, prayers can only be answered through
explanation: In the book of James, it is stated that prayers are answered through faith. James 1:6 says 'But let him ask in faith, nothing wavering. For he that wavereth is like a wave of the sea driven with the wind and tossed.' This means that when we pray, we should believe that we will receive what we are asking for.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul pleaded with Philemon to pardon his servant named
explanation: In the book of Philemon in the New Testament, Paul writes a letter to Philemon, a fellow believer, pleading for him to forgive and accept back his servant Onesimus, who had run away. This is found in Philemon 1:10-12 which says 'I appeal to you for my son Onesimus, who became my son while I was in chains. Formerly he was useless to you, but now he has become useful both to you and to me. I am sending him—who is my very heart—back to you.'

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians who humble themselves shall be
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of James 4:10, it is stated that 'Humble yourselves before the Lord, and he will exalt you.' Therefore, Christians who humble themselves shall be exalted.

topics.title: HUMILITY : REWARDS OF HUMILITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: These are spiritual gifts except
explanation: Spiritual gifts are special abilities given by the Holy Spirit to Christians for the purpose of building up the body of Christ. The list of spiritual gifts in 1 Corinthians 12:8-10 includes words of wisdom, words of knowledge, faith, healing, miraculous powers, prophecy, distinguishing between spirits, speaking in different kinds of tongues and the interpretation of tongues. Longsuffering, on the other hand, is a fruit of the Spirit as listed in Galatians 5:22-23, not a spiritual gift.

explanation:
Luke 1: 18

explanation:
Jeremiah 1: 5
Before i formed thee in the belly i knew thee; and before thou camest forth out of the womb i sanctified thee, and i ordained thee a prophet unto the nations

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the book of James, the rich is condemned due to their acts of
explanation: In the book of James, the rich are condemned for their acts of greed. James 5:1-6 speaks about the rich who hoard wealth, fail to pay their workers, live in self-indulgence, and have condemned and murdered the innocent. This is a clear depiction of greed, not infidelity, idolatry, or corruption.

explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus taught about the importance of giving alms (charitable donations) in secret, without seeking recognition or praise from others. This is found in Matthew 6:1-4, where Jesus says, 'But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'without other people's knowledge'.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus taught on the mount that we should give our alms ......
options: and return to the priests for blessing
explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus taught that when giving alms, it should be done in secret, not letting the left hand know what the right hand is doing. This is to ensure that the act of giving is not for show or to gain praise from others, but is done out of genuine love and compassion. This teaching can be found in Matthew 6:1-4.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David fled from Jerusalem when Absalom rose to dethrone him because
options: the people were in support of Absalom
David wanted to gather more soldiers while in hiding
Absalom had killed more men in battle than him
explanation: When Absalom rose against David, David fled from Jerusalem. This was not because the Lord had departed from him or because the people were in support of Absalom, but because he wanted to avoid a confrontation in the city and to gather more forces to counter Absalom's rebellion. This event is recorded in 2 Samuel 15.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The question that arose in the church in Jerusalem was settled with the conclusion that
options: it is not necessary for gentiles to be circumcised
circumcision is a law that must be adhered to
salvation is gotten from obeying the law
explanation: The question refers to the Council of Jerusalem, which is described in Acts 15. The main issue was whether Gentile converts to Christianity needed to follow Jewish customs, particularly circumcision. The council concluded that it was not necessary for Gentiles to be circumcised. This decision was significant because it established the principle that the Christian faith was distinct from Jewish customs and laws.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COUNCIL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter when we meet various trials in life, we should
explanation: In 1 Peter 1:6-7, Peter encourages Christians to rejoice in the face of trials because these trials test their faith and produce perseverance and maturity. He does not suggest that Christians should deny God or forsake the gospel. Instead, he encourages them to hold on to their faith and rejoice, knowing that their trials are temporary and serve a greater purpose.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIAN ATTITUDE TO PERSECUTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Immediately after the baptism of Jesus
options: he was led by the Spirit into the wilderness
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 4:1, immediately after the baptism of Jesus, he was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. This is a significant event as it marks the beginning of Jesus' ministry. The other options are not correct as per the biblical account. Jesus performed his first miracle at the wedding at Cana, which was after his temptation in the wilderness. Satan did tempt Jesus, but it was in the wilderness, not immediately after his baptism. His disciples were not baptised immediately after Jesus' baptism.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Eli died when
options: he heard that his two sons were dead
he heard that the battle had turned against Isreal
he heard that the Ark of the Covenant had been captured
he realised that God had rejected his priesthood
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Samuel 4:17-18, Eli, the priest, died when he heard that the Ark of the Covenant had been captured by the Philistines. The shock of the news made him fall backwards from his seat, breaking his neck and dying. The other options are not correct as per the biblical account. While it's true that Eli's two sons died and the battle turned against Israel, it was the news of the Ark's capture that directly led to his death. Eli's priesthood was indeed rejected by God, but this is not the reason for his death.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, treatment of brethren at fellowship should not be based on
explanation: In the book of James, specifically James 2:1-4, James warns against showing favoritism in the assembly of believers based on appearance or wealth. He emphasizes that such discrimination is not in line with the faith in Jesus Christ. Therefore, the treatment of brethren at fellowship should not be based on appearance.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AMONG CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus chased away the traders at the Temple in order to
options: reinstate the fact that the temple is a house of prayer
make them know the importance of paying taxes
move in there as his permanent residence
explanation: In the New Testament, specifically in Matthew 21:12-13, Jesus chased away the traders at the Temple to reinstate the fact that the temple is a house of prayer. He quoted from Isaiah 56:7, 'My house will be called a house of prayer,' to emphasize the purpose of the temple, which had been turned into a 'den of robbers' by the traders.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the meaning of the name of Hosea's last child?
options: I will have no mercy upon the house of Isreal
I will cause to cause the kingdom of the house of Isreal
explanation: The name of Hosea's last child was Lo-ammi, which means 'not my people'. This was a symbolic name given by God to signify that the people of Israel were no longer His people due to their disobedience and idolatry.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The New Covenant was based on
options: multiplication and dominion over the earth
explanation: The New Covenant, as described in the Bible, was based on reconciliation with God. This covenant, established through the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ, allows for the forgiveness of sins and reconciliation of humanity with God.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of these things will not occur at the second coming of Christ
options: the dead in christ shall rise first
the Lord shall descent with a shout
the voice of an archangel shall be heard
the Lord shall come to earth to reign with us
explanation: According to Christian eschatology, the second coming of Christ is a future event where Jesus will return to fulfill the remaining prophecies. The Bible, in 1 Thessalonians 4:16-17, mentions that 'the dead in Christ shall rise first', 'the Lord shall descend with a shout', and 'the voice of an archangel shall be heard'. However, it does not mention that 'the Lord shall come to earth to reign with us'. Instead, it says that 'we shall be caught up together with them in the clouds, to meet the Lord in the air'. Therefore, the fourth option is incorrect.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In his second missionary journey, Paul met Aquila and Priscila in
explanation: In the New Testament, during his second missionary journey, Apostle Paul met Aquila and Priscila in Corinth. This is recorded in Acts 18:2, 'There he met a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Claudius had ordered all Jews to leave Rome. Paul went to see them.' Therefore, the third option is correct.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The transfiguration took place on a high mountain in the presence of the following excluding
explanation: The transfiguration of Jesus is a story told in the New Testament when Jesus is transfigured and becomes radiant in glory upon a mountain. The event is reported in the Gospels of Matthew (17:1–9), Mark (9:2–8), and Luke (9:28–36). According to these accounts, Jesus and three of his apostles, Peter, James, and John, go to a mountain (the Mount of Transfiguration) to pray. Andrew was not present during the transfiguration, hence the correct answer is Andrew.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
Accordig to the account of Luke, the statement below was made to ......
'I will make you fishers of men'
explanation: The phrase 'I will make you fishers of men' is a quote from Jesus found in the Gospel of Matthew (Matthew 4:19). According to the account, Jesus was walking by the Sea of Galilee and saw two brothers, Simon (called Peter) and Andrew, casting a net into the sea as they were fishermen. Jesus said to them, 'Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.' Therefore, the correct answer is Simon Peter.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James in his teaching of Christians trials, he said the tried christian is blessed if he endured; for
options: they shall receive a crown of glory
they shall inherit the kingdom of God
explanation: In the book of James, it is mentioned that the Christian who endures trials is blessed because they will receive the crown of life.
