This is found in James 1:12, 'Blessed is the man who remains steadfast under trial, for when he has stood the test he will receive the crown of life, which God has promised to those who love him.' Therefore, the correct answer is option 2, 'they shall receive a crown of glory'.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : FRUITS OF JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Naboth refused to give Ahab his vineyard because
options: he wanted to intentionally defy the king
the king was not willing to pay enough for it
explanation: In 1 Kings 21, Naboth refused to give his vineyard to King Ahab because it was his inheritance from his ancestors. According to the laws of Israel, family land was not to be permanently sold but was to remain within the family. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2, 'the vineyard was his inheritance'.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Golgotha is known as
explanation: Golgotha is a term derived from Aramaic word 'Gulgaltha' which means 'skull'. It is the place outside Jerusalem where Jesus was crucified. Thus, it is often referred to as 'the place of the skull'.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, prayers can only be answered through
explanation: According to the book of James in the Bible, James 1:6-7, it is stated that anyone who prays must believe and not doubt, because the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind. That person should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Thus, faith is a key component for prayers to be answered.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Herod planned to kill all newborns but Joseph saved Jesus by taking him to
explanation: In the Bible, it is recorded in Matthew 2:13-15 that an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and told him to take Mary and Jesus to Egypt because Herod was seeking to kill the child. This was to fulfill what the Lord had spoken by the prophet, 'Out of Egypt I called my son.' Therefore, Joseph saved Jesus by taking him to Egypt.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these is not a fruit of the spirit?
explanation: The fruits of the Spirit are listed in Galatians 5:22-23 as love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and self-control. Righteousness is not listed as a fruit of the Spirit in this passage. Therefore, righteousness is not a fruit of the Spirit.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these messengers of God was asked to eat a scroll?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Ezekiel 3:1-3, God commanded the prophet Ezekiel to eat a scroll. The scroll was filled with lamentations, mourning, and woe. This was a symbolic act representing Ezekiel's call to prophesy against Israel's rebelliousness and to absorb God's word in order to communicate it to the people.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Amos rebuked Isreal for the following except
explanation: Amos, a prophet in the Bible, rebuked Israel for various sins including the shedding of innocent blood, oppression of the poor, and other forms of social injustice. However, there is no specific mention in the book of Amos about him rebuking Israel for sexual immorality. While sexual immorality was certainly a problem in Israel, it is not specifically mentioned as a focus of Amos's prophetic message.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Satan said Jesus would not fall if he jumped from a high place because
explanation: This is based on the biblical account where Satan tempts Jesus in the wilderness. Satan quotes Psalm 91:11-12, saying that God will command his angels to protect Jesus and they will lift him up in their hands so that he will not strike his foot against a stone.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Satan said Jesus would not fall if he jumped from a high place because
explanation: This is based on the biblical account where Satan tempts Jesus in the wilderness. Satan quotes Psalm 91:11-12, saying that God will command his angels to protect Jesus and they will lift him up in their hands so that he will not strike his foot against a stone.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: By what did Jesus say people will identify his disciples?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in John 13:35, Jesus said 'By this everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'loving one another'.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these instructions was given to the seventy and not the twelve on missions?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Luke 10:4, Jesus instructed the seventy disciples he sent out to 'greet no man by the way'. This instruction was not given to the twelve disciples. Therefore, the correct answer is 'greet no man by the way'.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Whose vineyard did Ahab and Jezebel confiscate?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Kings 21, it is recorded that Ahab and Jezebel confiscated Naboth's vineyard. Ahab wanted the vineyard because it was near his palace and he wanted to use it as a vegetable garden. When Naboth refused to sell or trade the vineyard because it was his ancestral inheritance, Jezebel orchestrated a plot that led to Naboth's death, after which Ahab took possession of the vineyard.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Gehazi was the servant of
explanation: Gehazi was the servant of Elisha, the prophet. This is recorded in the Bible, specifically in the book of 2 Kings. Gehazi is most known for his greed which led him to lie to Naaman and Elisha, and as a result, he was struck with leprosy.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God sent Jonah to preach to the people of
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Jonah, God sent Jonah to preach to the people of Nineveh. Nineveh was a wicked city and God wanted Jonah to warn them of His impending judgment if they did not repent. This is why option 1 is correct.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jonah stayed in the belly of a fish
options: because it was the only way God could save him
explanation: Jonah was swallowed by a big fish, where he stayed for three days and three nights, as a consequence of his disobedience to God's command. God had instructed Jonah to go to Nineveh and preach against its wickedness, but Jonah tried to flee from the Lord by going to Tarshish instead. This disobedience led to him being thrown into the sea and swallowed by a fish. Therefore, option 1 is the correct answer.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the signs that confirmed the call of Jeremiah was
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah, specifically Jeremiah 1:13, God showed Jeremiah a vision of a boiling pot to confirm his call. This vision was a sign of the disaster that was to come from the north.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nehemiah had access to ask for permission to visit Jerusalem because
options: he was one of the king's advisers
explanation: Nehemiah was the cup-bearer to King Artaxerxes, as stated in Nehemiah 2:1. This position gave him the opportunity to ask the king for permission to visit Jerusalem and rebuild its walls.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Not only those who commit homosexuality and immoral acts shall be punished but those who
options: have pleasure in them that do them
explanation: The question is referring to a biblical concept from Romans 1:32 in the New Testament, which states that not only those who commit certain sins are worthy of death, but also those who take pleasure in those who commit them. Therefore, the correct answer is 'have pleasure in them that do them'.

topics.title: SEXUAL IMMORALITY : HOMOSEXUALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Not only those who commit homosexuality and immoral acts shall be punished but those who
options: have pleasure in them that do them
explanation: The question is referring to a biblical concept from Romans 1:32 in the New Testament, which states that not only those who commit certain sins are worthy of death, but also those who take pleasure in those who commit them. Therefore, the correct answer is 'have pleasure in them that do them'.

topics.title: SEXUAL IMMORALITY : HOMOSEXUALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Blessed are the peacemakers, for
options: they shall be called the children of God
explanation: The phrase 'Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called the children of God' is a part of the Beatitudes from the Sermon on the Mount, as recorded in the Gospel of Matthew (5:9). The Beatitudes are a set of teachings by Jesus that provide a guide for how to live a life pleasing to God, and they each include a blessing for those who follow the teaching. In this case, those who work for peace are blessed by being called the children of God.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Jesus teaching about giving, He admonished Christians to give
options: with the knowledge of only the giver and God
explanation: In Matthew 6:3-4, Jesus teaches about giving in secret: 'But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing, so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.' This means that when we give, it should be done quietly and without seeking recognition or praise. The act of giving should be between the giver and God alone.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After Jesus' death on the cross which of these didn't occur?
options: many saints resurrected and went into city
the veil of the temple was rent in two from top to bottom
an inscription was written on his cross which read "THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS
explanation: According to the Bible, after Jesus' death on the cross, there was an earthquake, the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom, and many saints who had died were resurrected and went into the city (Matthew 27:51-53). However, the inscription 'THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS' was written on his cross before his death, not after (Luke 23:38). Therefore, the option 'many saints resurrected and went into city' is incorrect.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Jews demand the release of Barrabas rather than Jesus because
options: of the Jews' determination to crucify Jesus
explanation: The Jews demanded the release of Barrabas rather than Jesus because of their determination to crucify Jesus. Barrabas was indeed a notable prisoner, but this was not the reason the Jews chose him over Jesus. The Bible records in Matthew 27:20 that the chief priests and elders persuaded the crowd to ask for Barrabas and to have Jesus executed.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE PILATE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Seeing that you reject it, behold, we turn to the Gentiles". The circumstance surrounding this statement by Paul and Barnabas was
options: The Jews contradicted the gospel, trying to mislead others
The Sadducees antagonised the teaching of resurrection
explanation: In the book of Acts 13:45-46, it is recorded that when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy and began to contradict what was spoken by Paul, reviling him. Paul and Barnabas spoke out boldly, saying, "It was necessary that the word of God be spoken first to you. Since you thrust it aside and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we are turning to the Gentiles." Therefore, the circumstance surrounding this statement by Paul and Barnabas was that the Jews contradicted the gospel, trying to mislead others.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, treatment of brethren at fellowship should not be based on
explanation: In James 2:1-4, James warns against showing favoritism in the church, saying believers in Jesus Christ must not show favoritism based on a person's appearance. He gives an example of a rich man dressed in fine clothes and a poor man in shabby clothes coming into their assembly. If they pay special attention to the one wearing fine clothes and say, "Here's a good seat for you," but say to the one who is poor, "You stand there" or "Sit on the floor by my feet," they have discriminated among themselves and become judges with evil thoughts. Therefore, treatment of brethren at fellowship should not be based on appearance.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AMONG CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The core of Saul's disobedience was that
options: he refused to carry out God's assignment on eliminating the Amalekites
he didn't perform the task of smiting the Amalekites to the letter
explanation: In the biblical story, Saul's disobedience was that he did not completely destroy the Amalekites and their livestock as God had commanded. Instead, he spared King Agag and the best of the sheep and cattle. This is best represented by option 3, 'he didn't perform the task of smiting the Amalekites to the letter'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : DISOBEDIENCE AND CONSEQUENCES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God instructed Saul to utterly destroy Amalek because
options: he abhors idolatory and all forms of unrighteousness
its people opposed the Israelites on the way, after departing Egypt
its people encouraged Israel to disobey God
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Samuel 15, God commanded Saul to destroy the Amalekites because they opposed the Israelites when they were coming up from Egypt. This is best represented by option 2, 'its people opposed the Israelites on the way, after departing Egypt'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : DISOBEDIENCE AND CONSEQUENCES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the most significant lessons from the emergence of Deborah as a leader is that?
options: she not only prophesied but also advised Israel
gender difference isn't a limitation to leadership
honesty and integrity are key to leadership
one needs to summon courage as a leader to solve problems
explanation: The story of Deborah in the Bible (Judges 4-5) is a significant one as it shows a woman leading the Israelites, a role typically held by men. This suggests that gender difference isn't a limitation to leadership. The other options, while they may be true of Deborah's character and actions, are not the most significant lessons from her emergence as a leader.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jeremiah prophesied God's punishment of the Israelites because they had
explanation: Jeremiah, a prophet in the Bible, prophesied God's punishment of the Israelites because they had compromised His worship. This is seen in Jeremiah 2:13 where God, speaking through Jeremiah, accuses the Israelites of forsaking Him, the source of living water, and digging their own cisterns, broken cisterns that cannot hold water. This metaphorically refers to the Israelites abandoning the worship of God and turning to idols, which is a compromise of His worship. The other options do not accurately reflect the reasons for Jeremiah's prophecy.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus healed the lame man who had been sick for thirty-eight years at?
explanation: Jesus healed the lame man who had been sick for thirty-eight years at the Pool of Bethesda, not in Capernaum, Samaria, Galilee or Bethsheba. The Pool of Bethesda is not listed as an option, hence the correct answer is not in the options provided.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Isaiah's vision and call indicates the following except that
options: Isaiah witnessed the height of God's holiness
explanation: Isaiah's vision and call in Isaiah 6:1-8 indicates that God is holy, Isaiah confessed his unholiness, and God cannot behold iniquity. However, it does not indicate that God does not recognize man's inadequacies. In fact, God's call and commissioning of Isaiah despite his confessed unholiness shows that God does recognize and have mercy on human inadequacies.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "For the men in the ship knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the Lord". The person referred to in this statement is
explanation: The person referred to in the statement "For the men in the ship knew that he was fleeing from the presence of the Lord" is Jonah. This is a reference to the biblical story of Jonah who tried to flee from God's command to go to Nineveh and preach against its wickedness. Instead, Jonah boarded a ship to Tarshish, but God sent a great storm. The sailors, realizing that Jonah's disobedience was the cause of the storm, threw him overboard.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Israelites stood at ..... while Moses went up the mountain to receive God's commandments
explanation: The Israelites stood at Mount Sinai while Moses went up the mountain to receive God's commandments. This is a reference to the biblical event where Moses received the Ten Commandments from God on Mount Sinai, while the Israelites waited at the foot of the mountain. The other options, Raphidim, the wilderness of Sin, and Horeb, are other locations mentioned in the Bible, but they are not the correct answer to this question.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Elijah fed on ..... after declaring drought upon the land of Israel
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically 1 Kings 17:4-6, after Elijah declared a drought on the land of Israel, he was directed by God to hide by the Brook Cherith, where he was fed by ravens who brought him bread and meat in the morning and evening, and he drank from the brook. Therefore, the correct answer is 'bread, meat and water'.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: For how long did the Lord supply the children of Israel with manna in the wilderness?
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically Exodus 16:35, the Israelites ate manna for forty years, until they came to a land that was inhabited; they ate manna until they came to the border of the land of Canaan. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Forty years'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul's teaching on spiritual giifts was directed to the
explanation: St. Paul's teaching on spiritual gifts is found in his first letter to the Corinthians, specifically in 1 Corinthians 12. Here, he discusses the various spiritual gifts given by the Holy Spirit and emphasizes that while the gifts are different, they all come from the same Spirit and are given for the common good of the body of Christ, which is the church.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's teaching on civic responsibility, a Christian is expected to
options: resist states' power over life and death of its citizens
be terror to any government not ordained by God
explanation: In Romans 13:1-7, Paul teaches about the Christian's responsibility towards the governing authorities. He states that every person should be subject to the governing authorities, for there is no authority except from God, and those that exist have been instituted by God. Therefore, Christians are expected to respect and obey the laws of the land, as long as these laws do not contradict the laws of God.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter advised the young, in his teaching on interpersonal relationship among Cristians to
options: see themselves as strong and able-bodied servants of God
explanation: In his teachings, Peter advised the young to maintain good conduct among non-Christians. This is because good conduct serves as a testimony to non-believers and can potentially lead them to Christ. This is found in 1 Peter 2:12 where Peter says 'Live such good lives among the pagans that, though they accuse you of doing wrong, they may see your good deeds and glorify God on the day he visits us.'

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AMONG CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. James' teaching on impartiality centres on
options: loving our neighbours as ourselves in all situations
Christians not being partial to idle people
giving the place of the rich to the poor in the church
the rich spending their wealth on the poor for social equity
explanation: St. James' teaching on impartiality centres on loving our neighbours as ourselves in all situations. This is found in James 2:8-9 where James says 'If you really keep the royal law found in Scripture, "Love your neighbor as yourself," you are doing right. But if you show favoritism, you sin and are convicted by the law as lawbreakers.' This means that we should not show favoritism or partiality, but should love everyone equally as we love ourselves.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul's condition for the attainment of justification is by ...?
explanation: In the New Testament, particularly in the letters of Paul, justification is by faith in Jesus Christ. Paul consistently teaches that we are justified, or declared righteous, not by our works, but by faith in the finished work of Christ. This is evident in verses like Romans 3:28, 'For we hold that one is justified by faith apart from works of the law.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'faith in the resurrected Jesus'.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Moses named the place where the Israelites drank water from the rock 'Mesah and Meribah' particularly because...
options: they made him strike the rock angrily
the people had enough water to drink
they wanted to put the Lord to test
explanation: In Exodus 17:7, Moses named the place Massah (which means 'testing') and Meribah (which means 'quarreling') because the Israelites quarreled and tested the Lord there, saying, 'Is the Lord among us or not?' They doubted God's presence and provision, thus putting Him to the test. Therefore, the correct answer is 'they wanted to put the Lord to test'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Cleopas' heart burnt within him on the road to Emmaus when Jesus
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Luke 24:32, Cleopas and his companion's hearts burned within them when Jesus talked with them on the road and opened the Scriptures to them. This means that they felt a deep spiritual stirring or awakening as Jesus explained the Scriptures to them. Therefore, the correct answer is 'taught them the scriptures'.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "I gave you cleanness of teeth in all your cities". By thiis statement, God gave them
explanation: The phrase "I gave you cleanness of teeth in all your cities" is found in the book of Amos 4:6 in the Bible. It is a metaphorical way of saying that God had caused a famine. When there is no food, people's teeth are clean from not eating. Therefore, the correct answer is 'no food to eat'.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The name, "Jezreel" is a representation of
options: the punishment to come unto the royal house
the broken covenant between God and Israel
explanation: In the book of Hosea, the prophet Hosea was instructed by God to marry a prostitute named Gomer as a symbolic act. They had three children, and one of them was named Jezreel. The name Jezreel is a representation of the punishment to come unto the royal house of Israel because of their idolatry and unfaithfulness to God. This is a prophetic symbolism where the names and lives of Hosea's family were used to send messages to the people of Israel.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter advised christians to ... as they were sharing Christ's sufferings
explanation: In 1 Peter 4:13, Peter advised Christians to rejoice as they were sharing Christ's sufferings. He encouraged them to be glad for the privilege of suffering for Christ, as this would result in great joy when Christ's glory is revealed. This teaching is based on the Christian belief that suffering for the sake of Christ is a part of the Christian journey and is something to be embraced rather than avoided.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "It shall be neither mine nor yours; divide it" what was to be divided?
explanation: The statement "It shall be neither mine nor yours; divide it" was made by King Solomon during the judgement of the two women who were fighting over a live baby. This was a test by Solomon to determine the real mother of the child. The real mother would rather the child be given to the other woman than see her child killed. This story is found in 1 Kings 3:16-28.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One derivative lesson from the story of Gehazi's greed is that
options: as servants of God we must endeavour not to readily demand gifts from those we help
dishonesty should not have been a ground for cursing Gehazi with leprosy
we can by ourselves, through materialism, cause the destruction of generations yet unborn
we should be wary of accepting gifts from the unrighteous
explanation: The story of Gehazi's greed teaches us that our actions can have consequences not only for ourselves but also for our descendants. Gehazi, the servant of Elisha, lied to Naaman and took gifts from him after Elisha had refused any payment for healing Naaman of his leprosy. When Elisha found out, he cursed Gehazi and his descendants with leprosy. This story is found in 2 Kings 5:20-27.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The model example in St. Paul's teaching on humility is
explanation: In St. Paul's teaching on humility, the model example he used was Jesus Christ, not Abraham, Prophet Elijah or Noah. Therefore, none of the options provided is correct.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul's teaching on forgiveness primarily shows that
options: Christians must not institute a 'master-servant' relationship contract
subordinates must regard their superiors and vice-versa for forgiveness to thrive
Philemon had unilaterally decided not to accept Onesimus back
explanation: St. Paul's teaching on forgiveness primarily shows that genuine forgiveness only comes from God. This is because God is the ultimate source of forgiveness and it is from Him that we learn to forgive others. The other options do not accurately represent St. Paul's teaching on forgiveness.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul had already boasted to the ... and for this reason he sent a reminder letter to the... on Christian giving
explanation: In the New Testament, specifically in 2 Corinthians 8:1-5, St. Paul boasts about the generosity of the Macedonians to encourage the Corinthians to give generously as well. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Macedonians, Corinthians'.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE TEACHINGS OF PAUL ON CHRISTIAN GIVING.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the following is not a condition for effective prayer according to St. James
explanation: According to the book of James in the New Testament, effective prayer requires faith (James 1:6), perseverance (James 1:12), and steadfastness (James 5:7-8). However, lowliness is not mentioned as a condition for effective prayer in the book of James, making it the correct answer.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. James in the teaching on faith and work gave the analogy of
options: a poor man with wretched clothing approaching the rich for help
a hungry man who approached his fellow christian for help
a man who looks himself in the mirror and appreciates God for that
a man of little faith encouraging others to work
explanation: In the book of James 2:2-4, James gave an analogy of a man with gold rings and fine clothing, and a poor man in shabby clothing coming into an assembly. The analogy was used to illustrate the sin of partiality, not about a poor man approaching the rich for help. However, among the given options, the closest is the first option which talks about a poor man with wretched clothing approaching the rich for help.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One significance of St. Paul's teaching on 'Resurrection' is that
options: as Christ rose and ascended into glory, so as also must uphold the observance of the law
Christians must not underestimate the power of baptism by immersion
the foundation of their hope of salvation is in Christ's resurrection
the second coming of Christ is near
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 15:12-19, St. Paul's teaching on 'Resurrection' emphasizes that if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain and your faith is in vain. This implies that the foundation of Christian hope of salvation is in Christ's resurrection. Therefore, the third option is the correct answer.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: To St. Paul, Christians can prepare for the second coming of Chirst by
options: spreading the message of Christ's coming far and wide
explanation: In the New Testament, St. Paul often emphasizes the importance of being prayerful and watchful in preparation for the second coming of Christ. This is seen in verses such as 1 Thessalonians 5:6, 'So then, let us not be like others, who are asleep, but let us be awake and sober.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'being prayerful and watchful'.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : PREPARATION FOR HIS COMING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The model example for wives in Christian families according to St. Peter is
explanation: In 1 Peter 3:1-6, St. Peter uses Sarah, the wife of Abraham, as a model example for wives in Christian families. He commends her obedience to her husband and her inner beauty. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Sarah'.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : RELATIONSHIP IN THE CHRISTIAN FAMILY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The core of St. Paul's advice on dignity of labour is that
options: Christians must work hard to actively support the Church and themselves
the lazy people must be made to learn in the hardest way possible
everyone should actively contribute to social progress
explanation: The core of St. Paul's advice on dignity of labour is that Christians must work hard to actively support the Church and themselves. This is based on the biblical teaching that everyone should earn their own living and contribute to the welfare of the community, including the Church. This concept is often referred to as the 'dignity of labour', which emphasizes the importance of every job or task, no matter how menial or mundane it may seem.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : CONCEPT OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Peter's teaching on 'Civic Responsibility' centers upon
explanation: St. Peter's teaching on 'Civic Responsibility' centers upon good citizenship. This is based on the biblical teaching that Christians should respect the authorities, obey the laws of the land, and contribute to the welfare of the society. This concept is often referred to as 'civic responsibility', which emphasizes the role of individuals in maintaining a healthy and functioning society.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD CITIZENSHIP.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul's epistle to the Philippians on 'Christian Giving' indicated his reception of gifts from them through
explanation: In the book of Philippians, St. Paul acknowledges the gifts he received from the Philippians through Epaphroditus. Epaphroditus was a fellow Christian from the city of Philippi who delivered the gifts to Paul and stayed with him to help him during his imprisonment (Philippians 2:25-30).

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul showed commitment as a means upon which forgiveness can effectively thrive by
options: asking that Philemon to charge to his account whatever Onesimus owed
appealing to Philemon to forgive Onesimus and accept him back as a brother
instructing Philemon to write off all the debts of Onesimus, if any, compassionately
explanation: In the book of Philemon, St. Paul writes a letter to Philemon asking him to forgive Onesimus, a slave who had wronged him and run away. Paul had met Onesimus while in prison and had converted him to Christianity. In his letter, Paul asks Philemon to charge to his account whatever Onesimus owed him (Philemon 1:18). This shows Paul's commitment to forgiveness and reconciliation.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St. Paul, God sent the spirit of His son into men's hearts ...
options: so that become and act like God
explanation: In Galatians 4:6, St. Paul wrote, 'Because you are sons, God sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts, the Spirit who calls out, "Abba, Father."' Therefore, the correct answer is 'crying, "Abba! Father!"'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Summarily, St. Paul in his teaching on 'New Life in Christ' advised Christians to
options: dwell richly in the word of God
explanation: In Colossians 3:16, St. Paul advised, 'Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'dwell richly in the word of God'.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'Abraham's faith was reckoned to him as righteousness'. This underscores St. Paul's admonition that
options: obedience of the law is unnecessary as compared to God's grace
justification is by faith and not the law
redemption in Christ is possible through observance of the law
explanation: The statement 'Abraham's faith was reckoned to him as righteousness' is a key theme in St. Paul's letters, particularly in Romans and Galatians. St. Paul uses Abraham's faith as an example to illustrate that justification is by faith and not by the law. This is because Abraham was considered righteous because of his faith in God, even before the law was given. Therefore, the correct answer is 'justification is by faith and not the law'.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul, in 'Law and Grace', although man was condemned, he has been redeemed through the
explanation: In St. Paul's teaching on 'Law and Grace', he emphasizes that although man was condemned due to sin, redemption has been made possible through faith in Christ Jesus. This is a central theme in his letters, particularly in Romans and Galatians. He argues that the law could not save man, but through faith in Christ Jesus, man can be justified and redeemed. Therefore, the correct answer is 'faith in Christ Jesus'.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter healed Aeneas who had been bedridden for ... years
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Acts 9:32-35, it is mentioned that Peter healed Aeneas who had been bedridden for eight years due to paralysis. Therefore, the correct answer is 'eight'.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The accusation of blasphemy against Moses, the Law and God was levied against
explanation: In Acts 6:8-15, Stephen was falsely accused of blasphemy against Moses, the Law, and God. The members of the Synagogue of the Freedmen were unable to resist the wisdom and the Spirit by which Stephen spoke, so they secretly induced men to say, 'We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'Stephen'.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE MARTYRDOM OF STEPHEN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the folowing was not considered as a criteria for the selection of the 12th disciple
options: witnessing the baptism of Jesus Christ by John the baptist
possessing the gift of speaking in tongues
taking care of the needs of the Christian community
explanation: The criteria for the selection of the 12th disciple, as stated in the book of Acts, were that the person must have been with Jesus from the time of his baptism by John until his ascension, and must be a witness of his resurrection. The gift of speaking in tongues was not a requirement for the selection of the 12th disciple. Speaking in tongues is a spiritual gift that was given to the disciples at Pentecost, after the selection of the 12th disciple.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus' charged Christians to express love just as
options: He laid his life for the sake of mankind
explanation: In John 15:12, Jesus commands his followers to love one another as he has loved them. The ultimate demonstration of Jesus' love was his willingness to lay down his life for mankind. Therefore, Jesus charged Christians to express love just as he laid his life for the sake of mankind.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The triumphal entry was in fulfilment of the prophecy of ...
explanation: The prophecy of the triumphal entry of Jesus into Jerusalem is found in the book of Zechariah 9:9. The prophecy states: 'Rejoice greatly, Daughter Zion! Shout, Daughter Jerusalem! See, your king comes to you, righteous and victorious, lowly and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.' This prophecy was fulfilled as recorded in the New Testament books of Matthew 21:1-11, Mark 11:1-11, Luke 19:28-44, and John 12:12-19.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The disciples immediately understood, when they asked Jesus 'why the scribes said Elijah will come first', that He was symbolically referring to
options: the appearance of Elijah at His transfiguration
the people's refusal to accept Him as the Messiah
explanation: In Matthew 17:10-13, the disciples asked Jesus why the scribes said that Elijah must come first. Jesus replied that Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him but did to him whatever they pleased. Then the disciples understood that he was speaking to them about John the Baptist. This indicates that Jesus was symbolically referring to John the Baptist as the Elijah who was to come before the Messiah.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the synoptic account of Luke, Jesus instructed that when the Seventy entered any town, they should do all but one of the following
options: eat and heal the sick in any town they were received
carry no purse, bags, sandals and greet no one on the road
preach in the cities of Bethsaida and Capernaum saying, 'the kingdom of God has come near to you'
greet each household saying, 'peace be to this house'
explanation: In the Gospel of Luke, Jesus sends out the Seventy (or Seventy-Two) in pairs to every place he himself intended to go, instructing them to heal the sick and say 'The kingdom of God has come near to you.' However, there is no specific mention of Jesus instructing them to preach in the cities of Bethsaida and Capernaum. Therefore, the correct answer is option 3.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A Christian can demonstrate that he is a light of the world by
options: advocating for the meek to inherit the earth
explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus tells his followers that they are the light of the world, and that a city built on a hill cannot be hidden. This metaphor is often interpreted to mean that Christians should live their lives in such a way that their good deeds shine out for all to see, thereby glorifying God. Upholding good moral values is a key part of this, as it involves living in a way that is consistent with the teachings of Jesus. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE LIGHT OF THE WORLD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable of the seed growing secretly is a parable illustrating
options: that there is time and season for everything
explanation: The parable of the seed growing secretly is a parable illustrating the Kingdom of God. In this parable, Jesus explains that the Kingdom of God is like a seed that grows secretly, without the farmer knowing how. This represents the mysterious and powerful growth of God's Kingdom on earth.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the most significant lessons from the healing of the Centurion's Servant is that
options: Jesus has the power to forgive sins
the level of faith of the servant made Jesus heal him
even the sons of the kingdom are not worthy of God's kingdom
explanation: One of the most significant lessons from the healing of the Centurion's Servant is that the salvation of God is universal. In this story, Jesus heals the servant of a Roman Centurion, a non-Jew, showing that God's salvation is not limited to the Jews but is available to all people, regardless of their nationality or status.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According of Luke, Jesus was standing by the ... He thereafter entered into the boat preaching and later 'calling' Simon Peter
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke, specifically Luke 5:1-3, Jesus was standing by the Lake of Gennesaret, also known as the Sea of Galilee. He entered into one of the boats, which was Simon's, and asked him to put out a little from the land. And He sat down and taught the people from the boat. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Lake of Gennesaret'.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The intent of the third temptation of Christ was to
options: make Him see the riches of the world from a high mountain
explanation: The third temptation of Christ, as described in Matthew 4:8-10, involved the devil taking Jesus to a very high mountain and showing Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory. The devil then offered to give all of these to Jesus if He would fall down and worship him. Therefore, the intent of the third temptation was to make Jesus see the riches of the world from a high mountain.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: On the ... day, the child was ... and named Jesus
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically in the book of Luke 2:21, it was on the eighth day that the child was circumcised and named Jesus. This was in accordance with Jewish law and tradition.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Faith and power was manifested in the reign of ... through ...
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Daniel, it is recorded that faith and power were manifested in the reign of Darius through Daniel. This is seen in Daniel 6 where Daniel was thrown into the lions' den for his faith, but God shut the mouths of the lions and Daniel was unharmed. This demonstrated both Daniel's faith and God's power.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Josiah sent ... the priest to inquire of the Lord from Huldah
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 2 Kings 22:13, it is recorded that King Josiah sent Hilkiah the priest along with Ahikam, Achbor, Shaphan, and Asaiah to inquire of the Lord from the prophetess Huldah. However, the question specifically asks who the priest was that Josiah sent. The priest was Hilkiah, but since the question is asking for the priest that was sent to inquire of the Lord from Huldah, the correct answer is Shaphan, as he was the scribe who read the book of the law to Josiah and was sent to Huldah.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Lord protected the Israelites with all but one of the following when the Egyptians chased them
explanation: In Exodus 13:21-22, it is recorded that the Lord protected and guided the Israelites with a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night when they were leaving Egypt. There is no biblical record of a 'pillar of storm' or 'angels' being used to protect the Israelites during their exodus from Egypt. Therefore, the correct answer is 'pillar of storm' as it is not one of the ways God protected the Israelites when the Egyptians chased them.

topics.title: DIVINE PROVIDENCE, GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION :GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St. James, 'he who keeps the whole law and fails in one is guilty of ...'
options: breaking that particular love and should pray for grace
explanation: In the book of James 2:10, it is written that 'For whoever keeps the whole law and yet stumbles at just one point is guilty of breaking all of it.' This means that if a person fails in one law, he is considered as breaking the whole law. Therefore, the correct answer is 'breaking the whole law'.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The significance of the last supper is evident in all but one of the following
options: prediction of the suffering and death of Jesus
the prophecy of Peter's denial of Jesus
re-establishment of the Lord's covenant with his children
explanation: The Last Supper is significant for several reasons, including the prediction of Jesus' suffering and death, the revelation of Judas' betrayal, and the re-establishment of the Lord's covenant with his children. However, the prophecy of Peter's denial of Jesus, while it occurred around the same time, is not directly related to the significance of the Last Supper itself. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the prophecy of Peter's denial of Jesus'.

topics.title: THE LAST SUPPER : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAST SUPPER.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul taught that spiritual gifts in all their varieities emanate from
explanation: In the New Testament, specifically in 1 Corinthians 12:4-6, Paul taught that there are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but the same Spirit is the source of all of them. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the one spirit of God'.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE BENEFITS OF SPIRITUAL GIFTS TO THE INDIVIDUAL AND THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God charged man to 'be fruitful, and multiply, and .... the earth, and subdue it'. (1st creation story)
explanation: In the book of Genesis 1:28, God commanded man to 'be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the earth, and subdue it'. Therefore, the correct answer is 'fill'.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One significant lesson from Jonah's response to the outcome of his message was that
options: God will allow supernatural consequences direct the steps of men to him
the providence and mercies of God are universal
the benevolence of God is not for the sinful
explanation: The story of Jonah in the Bible teaches us about God's mercy and compassion, not just for the Israelites, but for all people. When Jonah finally obeyed God's command to go to Nineveh and warn its people of their wickedness, they repented, and God spared them. This shows that God's providence and mercies are universal, not limited to a particular group of people.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Woe is me! For I am lost". This was
options: Prophet Ezekiel's declaration when he saw the dry bones
the declaration of Isaiah when he saw the glory of God
made by Moses in the wilderness of Sin
the statement of Jeremiah when he foretold the punishment of God to Israel
explanation: The phrase "Woe is me! For I am lost" is from the book of Isaiah 6:5. It was Isaiah's response when he saw the glory of God in a vision and realized his own sinfulness and unworthiness. None of the other options correctly identify the speaker or the context of this statement.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Matthew's account, John the Baptist's immediate reaction when Jesus came to be baptized was to
options: ask why he should baptize a non-sinner
wait for the promise of the manifestation of God's glory
ask that Jesus should rather baptize him
explanation: In Matthew 3:14, when Jesus came to John the Baptist to be baptized, John tried to deter him, saying, 'I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?' This implies that John felt that Jesus, being the Messiah, was more worthy and should rather be the one baptizing him.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the following is not a quality of good discipleship
explanation: Exuberance, while it can be a positive trait, is not necessarily a quality of good discipleship. Good discipleship is characterized by qualities such as total commitment, prompt response, and self-denial, which involve putting the needs and commands of Jesus before one's own desires. Exuberance, or high energy and enthusiasm, is not inherently negative, but it is not a defining characteristic of discipleship.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Jesus healed the paralytic, the anger of the scribes was aroused by His
options: healing of the man on a Sabbath
refusal to show them signs of His divinity
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Mark 2:1-12, when Jesus healed the paralytic man, He first told the man that his sins were forgiven. This statement was what aroused the anger of the scribes because they believed that only God could forgive sins, and they considered Jesus' claim as blasphemy. Therefore, the correct answer is 'claim to forgive sins'.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Hosanna to the son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, Hosanna in the highest!" This was... acoount of the Triumphal Entry
explanation: The phrase 'Hosanna to the son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, Hosanna in the highest!' is found in the Gospel of Matthew, specifically in Matthew 21:9. This was when Jesus made his triumphal entry into Jerusalem, and the crowds were shouting these praises. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Matthew's'.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the following was Pilate's question to Jesus at his trial?
options: Are you Jesus, the prophet from Galilee?
What is it that these men testify against you?
Are you the king of the Jews?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of John 18:33, Pilate asked Jesus 'Are you the king of the Jews?' during his trial. This question was asked to ascertain the claims made about Jesus being a king, which was seen as a threat to the Roman Empire.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE PILATE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Gospel according to St. Matthew, Jesus sent His disciples to
explanation: In the Gospel according to St. Matthew, specifically in Matthew 10:5-6, Jesus instructed His disciples not to go among the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans, but rather go to the lost sheep of Israel (the Jews). This was because Jesus' initial mission was to the Jews before it extended to the Gentiles.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: An ideal Christian family according to St. Paul is one in which all but one of the following prevail
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Ephesians 5:22-33, St. Paul advises wives to submit to their husbands and husbands to love their wives. In Colossians 3:21, he also advises fathers not to provoke their children. However, there is no specific verse where St. Paul advises husbands to treat their wives as heirs. Therefore, the correct answer is option 4.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : RELATIONSHIP IN THE CHRISTIAN FAMILY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jeremiah, the Lord's new covenant will be made with the house of ... and the house of
explanation: In the book of Jeremiah 31:31-34, God promises to make a new covenant with the house of Israel and the house of Judah. This new covenant would not be like the one He made with their ancestors when He took them by the hand to lead them out of Egypt. This time, God promises to put His law in their minds and write it on their hearts. He will be their God, and they will be His people. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Josiah's reforms extended to the following areas except
options: cleansing of the temple of all foreign objects
giving recognition to the priests in high places
explanation: Josiah's reforms were aimed at purifying the religious practices of the Israelites. He abolished the practice of sacred prostitution, removed the Assyrian astral cult, and cleansed the temple of all foreign objects. However, he did not give recognition to the priests in high places. This was not part of his reforms.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nebuchadnezzer king of Babylon invaded Jerusalem and carried away all the treasures of the temple during the reign of
explanation: Nebuchadnezzar, the king of Babylon, invaded Jerusalem and carried away all the treasures of the temple during the reign of Jehoiachin. Jehoiachin was a king of Judah who, according to the Hebrew Bible, was taken into captivity by Nebuchadnezzar after a reign of only three months and ten days.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Ezekiel was called, he was shown a scroll on which were written words of
explanation: In the book of Ezekiel, specifically Ezekiel 2:10, it is mentioned that when Ezekiel was called, he was shown a scroll on which were written words of 'lamentations, mourning and woe'. However, the term 'mourning' is replaced with 'bitterness' in some translations. Therefore, the correct answer is 'lamentations, bitterness and woe'.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the parable of the Sower, the seeds that fell upon thorns, signify those who
options: receive the word but the cares and delight in riches of the world choke them
receive the word but the evil one comes and snatches it away
receive the word with joy, but it has no root in them
receive the word, it endures for a while, but falls away with tribulation
explanation: In the parable of the Sower, as told in Matthew 13:22, the seeds that fell among thorns represent those who hear the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, making it unfruitful. Therefore, the correct answer is 'receive the word but the cares and delight in riches of the world choke them'.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the following is common to the missions of the twelve and the seventy? Both were
options: sent in pairs with authority to heal the sick
sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel
to carry no purse, no bag, no sandals and to salute no one on the road
sent to preach the kingdom of God and heal the sick
explanation: The missions of the twelve and the seventy were both to preach the kingdom of God and heal the sick. This is evident in the Bible, specifically in Luke 9:1-2 for the twelve and Luke 10:1-9 for the seventy. The other options are either specific to one group or not mentioned in the Bible.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Do not be afraid; for I know what you seek, Jesus who was crucified..." This statement was made
options: to Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James
to Mary Salome, Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James
explanation: The statement 'Do not be afraid; for I know that you seek Jesus who was crucified...' was made to Mary Magdalene and the other Mary (mother of James) by an angel at the tomb of Jesus. This is recorded in Matthew 28:5-6. The other options are incorrect as they do not align with the biblical account.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Non-tolerance of the Church for hypocrisy and greed was pointed out particularly in the story of
explanation: The story of Ananias and Sapphira in the book of Acts (Acts 5:1-11) clearly demonstrates the Church's non-tolerance for hypocrisy and greed. Ananias and Sapphira sold a piece of property and lied about the amount of money they received from the sale, keeping back some of the money for themselves. When confronted by Peter, they both lied and were struck dead as a result.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter refuted the mockery of drunkiness of the disciples by refering to the prophecy of
explanation: On the day of Pentecost, when the Holy Spirit descended on the disciples, they began to speak in other tongues. Some people in the crowd mocked them, saying they were drunk. Peter stood up and addressed the crowd, saying that the disciples were not drunk, but were experiencing the fulfillment of the prophecy of Joel (Joel 2:28-32), where God promised to pour out His Spirit on all people.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According ot the Sermon on the Mount, those who hunger and thirst for righteousness shall
explanation: According to the Sermon on the Mount, specifically Matthew 5:6, Jesus said 'Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'be satisfied'.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "I am the resurrection and the life, he who believes in me, though he dies, yet shall he live". Jesus directed this statement to
explanation: In the Bible, specifically John 11:25-26, Jesus directed the statement 'I am the resurrection and the life, he who believes in me, though he dies, yet shall he live' to Martha, the sister of Lazarus. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Martha'.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When John baptized Jesus, at river Jordan, he described him as the Lamb of God who
options: takes away the sin of the world
explanation: In the Bible, specifically John 1:29, John the Baptist refers to Jesus as the 'Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world'. This is a reference to the sacrificial system of the Old Testament where a lamb without blemish was sacrificed for the sins of the people. Jesus, as the Lamb of God, is seen as the ultimate sacrifice for the sins of all humanity.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Gospel of John, Jesus referred to himself as the 'true vine' which means that Jesus
options: and the believer are a tree and its branches
was referring to the bread and wine for last supper
explanation: In John 15:1, Jesus refers to himself as the 'true vine' and his followers as the branches. In this metaphor, Jesus is teaching that just as the branches draw their life from the vine, so must his followers remain in him to have life. This means that believers are dependent on Jesus for spiritual life and fruitfulness.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE TRUE VINE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The two sons of Samuel who took bribe and perverted justice were
explanation: In the Bible, specifically 1 Samuel 8:1-3, it is mentioned that Samuel's two sons, Joel and Abijah, did not follow his ways. Instead, they turned aside after dishonest gain, took bribes, and perverted justice. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Joel and Abijah'.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Since fervent prayers of the righteous has great power in its effects, Christians according to St. James must
explanation: In the book of James 5:16, it is written that 'The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.' Therefore, Christians are encouraged to be steadfast in prayer. While the other options also have biblical basis, the question specifically refers to the power of prayer, making 'be steadfast in prayer' the most directly relevant answer.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: To St. James, a doer of the word of God must learn to also
options: be a listener of the word of God
explanation: In the book of James, the author emphasizes the importance of controlling one's tongue as a part of being a doer of the word of God. James 1:26 says, 'If anyone thinks he is religious and does not bridle his tongue but deceives his heart, this person's religion is worthless.' This means that controlling the tongue is a significant part of demonstrating one's faith in action.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : THE BENEFITS OF BEING JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "What you sow does not come to life unless it dies". This was
options: the message of St. Paul's teaching on the second coming of Christ
the response St. Paul instructed Christians to give to those who doubt the resurrection of Christ
made by St. Paul to Elymas, the magician
explanation: The phrase 'What you sow does not come to life unless it dies' is from 1 Corinthians 15:36, and it was used by St. Paul to explain the concept of resurrection to those who doubted it. He used the analogy of a seed dying in the ground and then sprouting into new life to illustrate how the resurrection works. Therefore, this phrase was a part of St. Paul's response to those who doubted the resurrection of Christ.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The mystery of lawlessness, deception and wickedness will commence as
options: the signs of the second coming of the Lord
the aftermath of the second coming of the Lord
a demonstration of the destruction of the world
explanation: The mystery of lawlessness, deception and wickedness will commence as the signs of the second coming of the Lord. This is based on the biblical teachings that these signs will precede the second coming of Jesus Christ. The other options are not correct because they do not align with the biblical teachings on the subject.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul supported his admonition on 'Dignity of Labour' by
explanation: St. Paul supported his admonition on 'Dignity of Labour' by giving an example of himself. In the New Testament, Paul often used his own life as an example to illustrate his teachings. He worked as a tentmaker to support himself while he was preaching, demonstrating the value of hard work and self-sufficiency. The other options, while they may be true in other contexts, are not the specific ways that Paul supported his admonition on the 'Dignity of Labour'.

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : BENEFITS OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul on 'Civic Responsibility' advised Christians not only to be law-abiding but also
options: to provide moral advice to those in authority
explanation: St. Paul in his teachings emphasized that Christians should not only obey the law but also fulfill their civic responsibilities. This means doing what is expected of them in their society, which includes but is not limited to paying taxes, voting, and participating in community service. This aligns with the third option 'do what is expected of them'.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD CITIZENSHIP.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The illustration of Satan, the adversary who prowls around seeking whom to devour, is particular to
options: St. Paul's teaching on the second coming of Christ
explanation: The illustration of Satan as an adversary prowling around seeking whom to devour is found in 1 Peter 5:8, which is part of St. Peter's teachings. Therefore, the correct answer is 'St. Peter's teaching on humility'. This teaching is about being vigilant and sober-minded because of the adversary, Satan. It is not directly related to civic responsibility or the second coming of Christ.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'If one is in Christ, then he is ...' - St. Paul's teaching on Christians as Sons and Heirs of God.
explanation: In Galatians 3:29, St. Paul states, 'If you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise.' This means that those who are in Christ are considered Abraham's offspring according to the promise.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ... according to St. Paul should not be the reason for continuing sinful lives
explanation: In Romans 6:1-2, St. Paul states, 'What shall we say, then? Shall we go on sinning so that grace may increase? By no means! We are those who have died to sin; how can we live in it any longer?' This implies that the grace of God should not be a reason for continuing sinful lives.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The essence of St. Paul's teaching on Law and Grace is that
options: God's grace in Christians' lives shouldn't be a reason for continuing in sin
observance of the law is necesary for redemption in Christ
observance of the law only isn't a guarantee of salvation
what God truly rewards is the prayer for grace by Christians
explanation: St. Paul's teaching on Law and Grace emphasizes that God's grace is not a license for Christians to continue in sin. He taught that while the law reveals sin, grace provides salvation through faith in Jesus Christ. Therefore, the grace of God in the lives of Christians should lead to righteousness, not a continuation in sin.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men!" this was statement was made by the people of
explanation: The statement "The gods have come down to us in the likeness of men!" was made by the people of Lystra. This happened in the book of Acts 14:8-18 when Paul healed a man who was lame from birth. The people of Lystra, seeing the miracle, thought Paul and Barnabas were gods (Zeus and Hermes) who had come down in human form.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Herod Agrippa persecuted Peter primarily to
options: signal to the Apostles that he wouldn't tolerate them
explanation: Herod Agrippa persecuted Peter primarily to please the Jews. This is evident in Acts 12:1-3 where it is stated that Herod arrested some who belonged to the church, intending to persecute them. He had James, the brother of John, put to death with the sword. When he saw that this met with approval among the Jews, he proceeded to seize Peter also.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Philip converted the Ethiopian Eunuch on a pilgrimage to ...
explanation: Philip converted the Ethiopian Eunuch on a pilgrimage to Jerusalem. This is recorded in Acts 8:26-40. The Ethiopian Eunuch was a high official in charge of all the treasury of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians. He had gone to Jerusalem to worship and was returning home; sitting in his chariot he was reading the prophet Isaiah. Then the Spirit told Philip, 'Go to that chariot and stay near it.' Philip ran up to the chariot and heard the man reading Isaiah the prophet. He asked, 'Do you understand what you are reading?' 'How can I,' he said, 'unless someone explains it to me?' So he invited Philip to come up and sit with him. Philip began with that very passage of Scripture and told him the good news about Jesus.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF THE ETHIOPIAN EUNUCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God's love for man is considered to have started from the...
options: act of sending Jesus to die for the redemption of mankind
explanation: God's love for man is considered to have started from the creation of the world. This is because God created man in His own image and likeness, and gave him dominion over all other creatures (Genesis 1:26-28). This act of creation signifies God's love for man. The act of sending Jesus for redemption, the covenant with Abraham, and the new commandment of Christ are all subsequent manifestations of God's love, but they did not mark the beginning of His love for man.

topics.title: LOVE : GOD'S LOVE FOR MAN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the synoptic of John, the statement "this is indeed the prophet who is to come into the world" was made by the
options: disciples of Jesus when He walked on the sea
people who witnessed the turning of water into wine
people present at the feeding of the Five Thousand
explanation: According to the Gospel of John, the statement "this is indeed the prophet who is to come into the world" was made by the people present at the feeding of the Five Thousand (John 6:14). After Jesus fed the five thousand with five loaves of bread and two fish, the people recognized Him as the prophet that was to come into the world, as prophesied in the Old Testament (Deuteronomy 18:15). The other options are incorrect because they do not align with the biblical account.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF: THE BREAD OF LIFE AND THE LIVING WATER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus left for the other side of the sea when the multitude that came to Him had been fed beause
options: the people sought after Him for feeding and miracles
explanation: In the Bible, specifically John 6:15, it is mentioned that after Jesus fed the multitude, they wanted to make Him king. To avoid this, Jesus withdrew to the mountain by Himself. Therefore, the closest correct answer is that the people sought after Him for feeding and miracles.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF: THE BREAD OF LIFE AND THE LIVING WATER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At about the ... hour, Jesus cried with a loud voice "Eli Eli lama sabach - thani".
explanation: In the Bible, specifically Matthew 27:46 and Mark 15:34, it is mentioned that at the ninth hour, Jesus cried out "Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?" which means "My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?". Therefore, the correct answer is the ninth hour.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Only ...stated that the transfiguration took place eight days after Peter's confession of Jesus as the son of God.
explanation: In the Bible, it is only in the book of Luke (Luke 9:28) that it is specifically stated that the transfiguration of Jesus took place about eight days after Peter's confession of Jesus as the son of God. The other gospels (Matthew, Mark, and John) do not specify the number of days.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One significant lesson surrounding the birth of Jesus is that
options: there is need for us to have faith in the word of God
the humble nature of one's birth does not affect one's greatness in life
sacrifice of purification is important at the one's birth
one's humble birth is as important a criteria for greatness as it is of salvation
explanation: The birth of Jesus, which took place in a humble setting (a manger), signifies that the humble nature of one's birth does not affect one's greatness in life. Jesus, despite his humble birth, went on to become the savior of the world. This teaches us that greatness is not determined by the circumstances of one's birth but by one's actions and impact in life.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God proclaimed through Jeremiah that He would destroy the land with ...
explanation: The correct answer is found in Jeremiah 14:12, where God says He will destroy the land with sword, famine and pestilence.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "But the house of Israel will not listen to you..." the addressee here is
explanation: The quote is from Ezekiel 3:7, which indicates that the addressee is Ezekiel.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice..." this statement relates to the
options: response of Israel to the genuiness of God's love
response of God's love to the children of Israel
message of Hosea on faithfulness and social progress
explanation: The statement "For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice..." is a part of the message of the prophet Hosea. Hosea emphasized the importance of faithfulness and love over ritualistic sacrifices. He preached that God values genuine love and faithfulness more than ritualistic sacrifices, which were often performed without true devotion. This is a message of faithfulness and social progress, as it encourages people to be faithful and loving, which leads to a better society.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ... was opposed Amos during the preaching of his message on social justice and true religion
explanation: Amaziah, the priest of Bethel, opposed Amos during his preaching of his message on social justice and true religion. Amaziah accused Amos of conspiracy and told him to go back to Judah. He did not want Amos to prophesy in Bethel because it was the king's sanctuary and a temple of the kingdom. Therefore, the correct answer is Amaziah.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ... updated Nehemiah about the state of Jerusalem while he was ...
