explanation: In the book of Nehemiah, it was Hanani (Nehemiah's brother) who updated Nehemiah about the state of Jerusalem while he was a captive in Babylon. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Hanai, a captive in Babylon'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. James advised Christians against partiality particularly because they
options: are at the risk of criticism by unbelievers
explanation: In the book of James (James 2:1), St. James advised Christians against showing favoritism because they bear the faith of our Lord Jesus Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is 'bear the faith of Jesus'.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Eyptians encamped at ... when they pursued the Israelites
explanation: The Egyptians encamped at Pi-ha-hi-roth when they pursued the Israelites. This is recorded in Exodus 14:9, where it is stated that the Egyptians pursued the Israelites and overtook them encamped by the sea, beside Pi-ha-hi-roth, before Baal-Zephon.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Last Supper refers the last meal Jesus had with His disciples immediately before His...
explanation: The Last Supper refers to the last meal Jesus had with His disciples immediately before His arrest and death. This event is recorded in the Gospels (Matthew 26:17–30, Mark 14:12–26, Luke 22:7–39 and John 13:1–17:26). After the meal, Jesus was betrayed by Judas, one of his disciples, arrested and subsequently crucified.

topics.title: THE LAST SUPPER : THE STORY OF THE LAST SUPPER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Who do men say that the Son of man is?" Jesus asked this question
options: to show that popular opinion matters the most in His recognition as Messiah
in the context of the Great Confession
to indicate that the Jews and Pharisees need to reckon with His Messiahship
explanation: The question "Who do men say that the Son of man is?" was asked by Jesus in the context of the Great Confession. This is recorded in Matthew 16:13-20. Jesus asked His disciples this question to reveal their understanding of His identity. The Great Confession is the moment when Peter confesses that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of the living God.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE OCCASION OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sparing of Saul's life demonstrated David's
options: genuine love for Saul so that God prefers him as King over Israel than Saul
mission to appoint Abner as his army commander when he becomes King
explanation: David's sparing of Saul's life demonstrated his level of faith in God. This is seen in 1 Samuel 24 and 26 where David had the opportunity to kill Saul but chose not to. David believed that it was not his place to harm the Lord's anointed, showing his faith and trust in God's plan and timing.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joseph's leadership quality was so exceptional so much that
options: Pharaoh had to appoint him as Emperor
the Chief butler recommended him to Pharaoh
other nations of the world benefited from it
God rewarded his action of commiting adultery with Potiphar's wife
explanation: Joseph's leadership quality was indeed exceptional, but it was not to the extent that Pharaoh appointed him as Emperor. Rather, it was the Chief Butler who recommended him to Pharaoh after Joseph correctly interpreted his dream while they were both in prison. This recommendation led to Joseph's appointment as the governor of Egypt, not the Emperor. The other options are incorrect because Joseph's leadership did not directly benefit other nations, and God did not reward him for committing adultery with Potiphar's wife, an act he did not commit.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The most significant lesson at the scene of the Israelites' blame of Moses in their struggle and escape from Egypt is that
options: God hardened the heart of Pharaoh to pursue the Israelites
Moses' height of anger and intolerance was revealed
Moses' courage and confidence in God saved the entire situation
they would have perfered to stay back and die in Egypt
explanation: The most significant lesson at the scene of the Israelites' blame of Moses in their struggle and escape from Egypt is that Moses' courage and confidence in God saved the entire situation. Despite the Israelites' complaints and blame, Moses remained steadfast and relied on God's guidance to lead the Israelites out of Egypt. The other options are incorrect because they do not accurately represent the main lesson from this event.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The day and night were created on the
explanation: According to the biblical account of creation in Genesis 1:14-19, God created the sun, moon, and stars on the fourth day to separate the day from the night. This is when the cycle of day and night as we know it was established.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God placed Cherubims at the ------------------ of the Garden of Eden with flaming sword particularly
options: West, because he wanted to replenish the garden
East, to keep them away from the tree of life
West to keep a close watch over the garden since Adam and Eve had been sent out
explanation: In Genesis 3:24, after Adam and Eve were banished from the Garden of Eden for their disobedience, God placed Cherubim at the east of the Garden of Eden, with a flaming sword that turned every way, to guard the way to the tree of life. This was to prevent Adam and Eve from returning and eating from the tree of life, which would allow them to live forever.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The flood during the time of Noah lasted on the Earth for ------------------- days
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically Genesis 7:12, the flood during the time of Noah lasted on the Earth for 40 days and 40 nights. Therefore, the correct answer is 40 days.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but Abraham..." this voice came
options: when God first made his covenant with Abram
when God saw the faith in his readiness to sacrifice his only son
during the period Abram built an altar at sichem and God promised to give him and his descendants the land
when God later established the substance of his covenant with him
explanation: The voice that said, "Neither shall thy name any more be called Abram, but Abraham..." came when God later established the substance of his covenant with him. This is recorded in Genesis 17:5, where God changes Abram's name to Abraham as a part of the covenant He is establishing with him. Therefore, the correct answer is 'when God later established the substance of his covenant with him'.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Deborah instructed Barak to gather his troops and engage the forces of Jabin in battle at
explanation: In the book of Judges 4:6-7, Prophetess Deborah instructed Barak to gather his troops and engage the forces of Jabin, the king of Canaan, in battle at Mount Tabor. Therefore, the correct answer is Mount Tabor.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Fear not, stand firm, and see the salvation of the Lord, which he will work for you today..." what did the Lord do for them? He
options: divide the red sea having buried their pursuers
shed them by providing the pillar of cloud and destroyed their enemies
explanation: The phrase "Fear not, stand firm, and see the salvation of the Lord, which he will work for you today..." is from Exodus 14:13. In this context, Moses was addressing the Israelites who were terrified as the Egyptian army approached them after they had left Egypt. What the Lord did for them was to divide the Red Sea so that they could cross on dry ground, and then He let the sea flow back over their pursuers, effectively burying them. Therefore, the correct answer is 'divide the red sea having buried their pursuers'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Moses named the place where they drank water; "Massah" and "Meribah" at ----------------- because
options: wilderness of shur, of the chiding of the Israelites
mount horeb, they put Moses and the Lord to a test
rephidim, they questioned if the Lord was with them or not
the wilderness of sin, they tempted God
explanation: In the Bible, Moses named the place Massah and Meribah because the Israelites quarreled and tested the Lord saying, 'Is the Lord among us or not?' This event took place in Rephidim, as recorded in Exodus 17:7. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Rephidim, they questioned if the Lord was with them or not'.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : EXAMPLES OF ABRAHAM, HEBREW MIDWIVES AND DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Eli fell from the seat and broke his neck when
options: a man of Benjamin told him the Ark of God had been captured
he heard the noise about Israel's defeat against the philistines
he heard phineshas' wife gave birth prematurely
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Samuel 4:17-18, it is recorded that Eli fell from his seat backward by the side of the gate, and his neck was broken and he died, for he was old and heavy. He had judged Israel forty years. This happened when a man of Benjamin ran from the battle line and came to Shiloh the same day with his clothes torn and with dirt on his head. When he arrived, Eli was sitting on his seat by the road watching, for his heart trembled for the ark of God. Therefore, the correct answer is 'a man of Benjamin told him the Ark of God had been captured'.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Asa didn't despise his father, Abijam despite his sons before God but
options: he brought gifts to God's temple including that of his father
he repeatedly prayed for him during his reign
he turned away from his ways and followed God's statues
asked God to destroy the achievements of his father.
explanation: Asa, the king of Judah, was known for his religious reforms. He removed the idols his father had made, along with other pagan shrines, and he commanded Judah to seek the Lord, the God of their ancestors, and to obey His laws and commands. This is recorded in 2 Chronicles 14:2-5. Therefore, the correct answer is that he turned away from his father's ways and followed God's statutes.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ASA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God wanted to punish Amalek and instructed Saul to do so because
options: they made the Israelites go astray from him
they hindered the passage of the Israelites having left Egypt
their king made the people and Israel sin
explanation: In 1 Samuel 15:2, God commanded Saul to attack the Amalekites because they opposed the Israelites when they were coming up from Egypt. Therefore, the correct answer is that they hindered the passage of the Israelites having left Egypt.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Israel was in war with ------------------ when David committed adultery with -----------------
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 2 Samuel 11, it is recorded that King David saw Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah the Hittite, bathing while Israel was at war with the Philistines. He committed adultery with her during this time. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Philistines, Bathsheba'.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God promised to give Solomon -------------- with a certain condition
explanation: In 1 Kings 3:5-14, God appeared to Solomon in a dream and asked him what he wanted. Solomon asked for wisdom to govern the people of Israel. God was pleased with this request and not only granted him wisdom but also wealth and honor, with the condition that if he obeyed God's laws and commands as his father David did, then God would also lengthen his life. Therefore, the correct answer is 'riches and honour'.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : THE WISDOM OF SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Naaman request for after he was healed?
options: 2 bags of talent and festal garment
a small quantity of sand from Israel
that Elisha should come and settle in Syria since he refuses his gifts
that he should be given the scripture and image of Jehovah
explanation: After Naaman was healed of his leprosy by Elisha, he requested for two mule-loads of earth (a small quantity of sand) from Israel. He wanted to take it back to his homeland, Syria, so that he could use it to make an altar to worship the God of Israel. This is recorded in 2 Kings 5:17.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Having championed the supremacy of God on mount camel, Elijah caused God to
options: bring forth grains to end the famine
deliver the baal and asherah prophets to the Israelites
explanation: After Elijah had demonstrated the supremacy of God on Mount Carmel by calling down fire from heaven to consume his sacrifice (in contrast to the prophets of Baal and Asherah who could not do the same), he prayed and God sent rain to end the three-and-a-half-year drought in Israel. This is recorded in 1 Kings 18:41-45.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A significant religious reform administered by King Josiah was?
options: reconstruction of the temple of God and its dedication
explanation: King Josiah is known for religious reforms that centered on the elimination of idolatry and the re-introduction of the Passover, an important Jewish festival. The Passover commemorates the liberation of the Israelites from Egyptian slavery, as described in the Book of Exodus. This reform is significant as it helped to centralize religious worship and reinforce monotheism among the Israelites.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to -------------------, Angel Gabriel was sent to Nazareth, a city in ----------------
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke in the New Testament of the Bible, Angel Gabriel was sent to Nazareth, a city in Galilee, to announce to a virgin named Mary that she would conceive and give birth to Jesus, the Son of God (Luke 1:26-38). This event is known as the Annunciation. The other options are incorrect as they either mention the wrong book of the Bible or the wrong location.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : JOHN, THE FORERUNNER OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After Christ's temptation
options: he was filled with the holy spirit
the angels of God ministered to him
explanation: After Christ's temptation, the Bible records in Matthew 4:11 that 'Then the devil left Him, and behold, angels came and were ministering to Him.' Therefore, the correct answer is that 'the angels of God ministered to him'.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Those who are well have no need of a physician..." by this, Christ meant that
options: he came to trumpet repentance not to the righteous
explanation: The full quote from the Bible, Mark 2:17, is 'Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick. I came not to call the righteous, but sinners.' This means that Jesus came to call sinners to repentance, not the righteous. Therefore, the correct answer is 'he came to trumpet repentance not to the righteous'.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "leave the dead to bury their own dead"
options: Christ's response to the 3rd person who wanted to become a disciple
Christ emphasizes giving priority to ministerial works than others
explanation: The phrase 'leave the dead to bury their own dead' is a response from Jesus Christ to a man who wanted to follow him but asked to go and bury his father first. Jesus used this phrase to emphasize that the work of God or ministerial work should be given priority over other things, even over what might be considered important duties in the society.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Jewish society, the -------------- Don't believe in resurrection
explanation: In the Jewish society, the Sadducees did not believe in resurrection. This was one of the key differences between the Sadducees and the Pharisees. The Pharisees believed in the resurrection of the dead, while the Sadducees did not. The Sadducees only accepted the written Law of Moses and did not believe in oral tradition, angels, or spirits, while the Pharisees did.

topics.title: THE RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RELEVANCE OF THE RESURRECTION AND ASCENSION OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In st. Paul's teaching on justification by faith, christians can only be justified by
options: God's opportunity of which they are unworthy
explanation: In St. Paul's teaching on justification by faith, he emphasized that Christians are justified not by their own works or righteousness, but by God's grace. This means that Christians are justified by God's act of free grace, of which they are unworthy. This is a fundamental concept in Christian theology, emphasizing the belief that humans cannot achieve salvation through their own efforts, but only through the grace of God.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St. Paul, those that lived under the law lived under
explanation: According to St. Paul, those who lived under the law lived under a curse. This is because the law demands perfect obedience, which no one can fulfill. Therefore, anyone who relies on the law for justification is under a curse. This is a key concept in Paul's theology, emphasizing the futility of seeking justification through the law and the necessity of faith in Jesus Christ.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St. Paul, in his teaching on new life on Christ "we are buried with Christ" by
explanation: According to St. Paul in Romans 6:4, we are buried with Christ by baptism into death. This means that just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life. Therefore, the correct answer is baptism.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians are advised to humble themselves under the mighty hand of God that they might be exalted due time. "Due time" according to St. Peter means
explanation: In 1 Peter 5:6, St. Peter advises Christians to humble themselves under the mighty hand of God, so that at the proper time (due time) He may exalt them. The 'due time' here refers to the second coming of Christ, when faithful Christians will be exalted. Therefore, the correct answer is Christ's coming.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : PREPARATION FOR HIS COMING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians finally become the joint heirs of God through
explanation: Christians become joint heirs of God through faith. This is a fundamental belief in Christianity that through faith in Jesus Christ, believers become children of God and thus joint heirs with Christ. This concept is found in Romans 8:17, 'Now if we are children, then we are heirs--heirs of God and co-heirs with Christ, if indeed we share in his sufferings in order that we may also share in his glory.'

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Nathan condemned him for murder and adultery, David
options: said, "it is the Lord, let Him do what seems good to Him
explanation: When Nathan condemned David for murder and adultery, David confessed his sin before the Lord. This is recorded in 2 Samuel 12:13, where David says to Nathan, 'I have sinned against the Lord.' Nathan then tells David that the Lord has taken away his sin. This shows David's repentance and acknowledgement of his wrongdoing.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Manasseh worshipped Assyrian gods in order to
explanation: Manasseh, the king of Judah, worshipped Assyrian gods to avoid another Assyrian attack. This was a common practice in ancient times where a weaker nation would adopt the gods of a stronger nation as a form of appeasement or to avoid conflict.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "He saved others, He cannot save Himself". Who is mocked Jesus with these words?
options: The chief priest, scribes and the elders
explanation: The phrase 'He saved others, He cannot save Himself' was used to mock Jesus by those who passed by the cross. This is recorded in the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 27:39-40. The chief priests, scribes, and elders also mocked Jesus, but they used different words.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During his defence, Stephen accused the Jews of
explanation: In Acts 7:51, Stephen accused the Jews of being stiff-necked. This term 'stiff-necked' is a metaphor taken from unmanageable oxen, who resist the yoke, and refuse to be directed by it. It signifies obstinate, stubborn, and intractable.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE MARTYRDOM OF STEPHEN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these was not part of Paul's commendation of philemon? Philemon's
explanation: In the book of Philemon, Paul commends Philemon for his faith toward the Lord Jesus, his love toward all the saints (believers), and the comfort he has brought to Paul. However, there is no mention of Philemon's prayer for Paul in prison. Therefore, this was not part of Paul's commendation of Philemon.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joseph named his first son "Manasseh" because God made him
explanation: In the Bible, Genesis 41:51, Joseph named his first son Manasseh, saying, "For God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house." Therefore, the name Manasseh is associated with forgetting his hardship.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God provided water for the Israelites by asking Moses to strike the rock of the wilderness of
explanation: In Exodus 17:1-7, God instructed Moses to strike a rock in the wilderness of Sin to provide water for the Israelites. Therefore, the correct answer is the wilderness of Sin.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joshua gathered all the tribes of Israel for his farewell address at
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Joshua 24:1, it is recorded that Joshua gathered all the tribes of Israel to Shechem for his farewell address. Therefore, the correct answer is Shechem.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSHUA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Your love is like the morning cloud, like the dew that goes early". What dd Hosea mean by this statement?
options: Israel did not truly love God as she should
Israel did had love for other gods
explanation: The statement 'Your love is like the morning cloud, like the dew that goes early' is found in Hosea 6:4. This metaphor is used by Hosea to describe the transitory, fleeting nature of Israel's love and devotion to God. It means that Israel's relationship with God was short-lived, just like the morning cloud and the early dew that disappear quickly. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Israel's relationship with God was shortlived'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Luke described Cornelius as a devout man who feared God because he was a
explanation: Cornelius, as described in the Book of Acts in the New Testament, was a Roman centurion who is considered by Christians to be one of the first Gentiles to convert to the faith. He is described as a devout man who feared God, gave alms generously to the people, and prayed constantly to God. He was not a Hellenist of the Italian Cohort, nor a Proselyte of Jewish descent, nor a Greek who favoured Jewish religion. He was a Gentile, a non-Jew, who was in sympathy with Jewish faith. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Proselyte in sympathy with Jewish faith'.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF CORNELIUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jeremiah viewed the relationship between God and Israel in her youth to be that of
explanation: In the Book of Jeremiah, the prophet Jeremiah often uses the metaphor of a marriage to describe the relationship between God and Israel. In the early days, Israel was like a faithful spouse, full of love and loyalty for God. But over time, Israel strayed and was unfaithful. Despite this, God continued to show compassion and hope for Israel's return. Therefore, the correct answer is 'compassion, sincerity and hope'.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God addressed Ezekiel as the "Son of man". This phrase emphasizes the prophet's
explanation: The phrase 'Son of man' is used in the Bible to emphasize the humanity of the person being referred to. In the case of Ezekiel, God used this phrase to remind him of his human nature and limitations.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Corinthians, Paul stated that he was unfit to be called an apostle because he
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 15:9, Paul stated that he was unfit to be called an apostle because he persecuted the church of God. This is a reference to his past life when he was known as Saul, a zealous Pharisee who actively persecuted Christians before his conversion.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Peter, doing the right thing is way of expressing
explanation: In the New Testament, Peter emphasized that faith in Christ should be expressed through good deeds. He believed that doing the right thing was a way of expressing faith in Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is 'faith in Christ'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The statement "All men are equal before the law" implies the teaching of which one of the following prophets?
explanation: The prophet Amos is known for his teachings on social justice and equality. He emphasized that all men are equal before the law, regardless of their social status or wealth. Therefore, the statement 'All men are equal before the law' implies the teachings of Amos.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the following was not done on the 3rd day?
options: seperation of water into river, stream etc.
explanation: On the third day of creation according to the Bible, God created dry land (Earth) and vegetation. The firmament was used to separate the waters on the second day. However, the separation of water into rivers, streams, etc. was not specifically mentioned as an event of the third day.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A significance in the adherence to the sign of God's covenant with Abraham denotes...
explanation: The sign of God's covenant with Abraham was circumcision, which was a physical mark of belief in God. It was a sign of Abraham's faith and trust in God's promises. Therefore, adherence to this sign denotes absolute belief in Him.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Deborah reigned as Judge in Israel after
explanation: Deborah, a prophetess and the fourth judge of Israel, reigned after Ehud. Ehud was the second Judge of Israel. He was a left-handed man from the Tribe of Benjamin. After Ehud, Deborah became the judge of Israel.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Moses named the palace where God provided water "Massah and Meribah" because
options: the people wanted to know whether God was with them by seeing Him
explanation: In the book of Exodus, Moses named the place Massah (which means 'testing') and Meribah (which means 'quarreling') because the Israelites quarreled and tested the Lord saying, 'Is the Lord among us or not?' (Exodus 17:7). Therefore, the reason for the naming was because the people wanted to know whether God was with them.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sons of Samuel were made judges at Israel specifically in
explanation: The sons of Samuel were made judges in Beersheba. This is a biblical fact found in 1 Samuel 8:2, where it is stated that Samuel's sons, Joel and Abijah, were judges in Beersheba.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Ahab was greedy towards the vineyard of Naboth
options: when he desperately needed more fields for agricultural development
which was at the backyard of his palace
when his wife Jezebel inspired him more and gave him a thorough assurance
explanation: Ahab was greedy towards the vineyard of Naboth because it was at the backyard of his palace. This is found in 1 Kings 21:1-2, where it is stated that Naboth's vineyard was in Jezreel, close to the palace of Ahab, King of Samaria. Ahab wanted the vineyard to turn it into a vegetable garden because it was near his palace.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prophets of God were killed through the influence of
options: Jezebel. the syrian princess married to Ahab
Jezebel, the daughter of the King of Israel
Jezebel, the daughter of Etbaal King of the Sidonian
explanation: The prophets of God were killed through the influence of Jezebel, the wife of Ahab, king of Israel. Jezebel was a Phoenician princess who was married to Ahab, and she is known for her attempts to promote the worship of Baal and Asherah in Israel, often at the expense of the prophets of God.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jonah wanted to escape God's message
options: because he wanted self - repentance from the ninevites
by going to Tarshish and taking a ship to Joppa
because ninveh was a land where persecution prevailed & he was also lazy
by going to Joppa and boarding ship to Tarshish
explanation: Jonah tried to escape God's message by going to Joppa and boarding a ship to Tarshish. This is because God had instructed him to go to Nineveh to deliver a message of repentance, but Jonah, knowing the wickedness of the Ninevites, feared for his life and tried to flee in the opposite direction. This makes option 4 the correct answer.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ------------------- brought the message about the state of the Nation - Israel to Nehemiah
explanation: Hanani, Nehemiah's brother, brought the message about the state of the Nation - Israel to Nehemiah. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Nehemiah 1:2-3.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Amos was sent away to ---------------- for his preaching by
explanation: Amos was sent away to Judah for his preaching by Amaziah. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Amos 7:10-13.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to ----------------- a census was declared by
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke in the Bible, it was Augustus Caesar who declared a census. This is recorded in Luke 2:1 which states, 'In those days Caesar Augustus issued a decree that a census should be taken of the entire Roman world.'

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The 3rd temptation of christ was supposed to make Christ
options: reveal His deep understanding of the scripture
bow and fall from a high mountain
explanation: The third temptation of Christ, as recorded in Matthew 4:8-10, was when the devil took Jesus to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor. The devil then said to Jesus, 'All this I will give you,' he said, 'if you will bow down and worship me.' This temptation was meant to make Jesus a political messiah, as the devil was offering him political power over all the kingdoms of the world.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The significance of the statement ; "let the dead bury their own dead" is
options: to avoid delay in carrying out God's mission
to delegate duties to others absolutely while following Christ
to emphasize focus and determination in discipleship
explanation: The statement 'let the dead bury their own dead' was made by Jesus in the Bible when a disciple asked to go and bury his father before following Him. Jesus was emphasizing the urgency of spreading the Gospel and carrying out God's mission. He was not literally asking the dead to bury the dead, but rather, He was saying that spiritual matters (like following Him and spreading the Gospel) are more important than worldly matters (like burying one's father).

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE DEMANDS OF DISCIPLESHIP

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The statement "Let us make 3 booths..." was made
options: by James and John the son of Zebedee
explanation: The statement 'Let us make three booths...' was made during Christ's transfiguration. Peter said this when he, along with James and John, saw Jesus transfigured and talking with Moses and Elijah. Peter suggested making three booths, one for Jesus, one for Moses, and one for Elijah, because he did not fully understand what was happening and wanted to make the moment last longer.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: All but one of the following depicts the redemptive mission of christ
options: Jesus as the light of the world
Jesus as the living water and bread
explanation: The redemptive mission of Christ is depicted in the Bible through various metaphors. Jesus as the 'Good Shepherd' is one of them, where He is portrayed as the one who lays down His life for His sheep, symbolizing His sacrificial death for humanity's sins. Jesus as the 'Light of the World' signifies His role in guiding humanity out of spiritual darkness. Jesus as the 'Lamb of God' is a direct reference to His redemptive mission, as it signifies His sacrifice for the sins of the world. However, 'Jesus as the living water and bread' is not directly related to His redemptive mission. While it does symbolize His role in providing spiritual nourishment, it does not directly depict His work of redemption.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE LAMB AND THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During Christ's trial, He was firstly brought before
explanation: During Christ's trial, He was first brought before Caiaphas, the high priest. This is recorded in the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew (26:57-68). Caiaphas was the one who initially questioned Jesus and declared Him guilty of blasphemy, leading to His eventual crucifixion. The other options, Emperor Caesar Augustus, the Pharisees, and King Herod, were not the first to whom Jesus was brought during His trial.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus issued the Great commission for the disciples in
explanation: The Great Commission is the instruction of the resurrected Jesus Christ to his disciples to spread his teachings to all the nations of the world. This event is recorded in the Gospel of Matthew 28:16–20 where it takes place on a mountain in Galilee.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The council for discussing the issue of discrimination only in the Early church was chaired by
explanation: The council that discussed the issue of discrimination in the Early Church was the Council of Jerusalem, which was chaired by St. James and St. Peter. This council is described in the Acts of the Apostles 15:1–35. The council decided that Gentile converts to Christianity were not obligated to keep most of the Law of Moses, including the rules concerning circumcision of males.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: While Paul and Barnabas preached for --------------- Jesus opposed them
explanation: In the book of Acts, chapter 13, Sergius Paulus was a proconsul of Cyprus who was converted to Christianity by Paul and Barnabas. The text does not suggest that Jesus opposed them while they preached to him. Instead, it was a sorcerer named Bar-Jesus (also known as Elymas) who opposed them.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: From ----------------- Paul began his first missionary work
explanation: Paul began his first missionary journey from Antioch in Syria. This is recorded in the book of Acts, chapter 13. From Antioch, they went down to Seleucia and from there sailed to Cyprus. Therefore, the correct answer is Antioch in Syria.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: St. Paul made an analogy to the parts of human in his teaching on
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 12, St. Paul uses the analogy of the human body to explain the concept of spiritual gifts. He explains that just as the body is one unit but has many parts, and all the parts, though many, form one body, so it is with Christ. This means that we all have different spiritual gifts, but all these gifts come from the same Spirit and are used to serve the same Lord.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The theme of "Good Citizenship" was administered by
explanation: The theme of 'Good Citizenship' was administered by St. Peter. In his first epistle, 1 Peter 2:13-17, he urges Christians to submit themselves to every human authority for the Lord's sake, whether it be to the emperor, as the supreme authority, or to governors. He also encourages them to show proper respect to everyone, love the family of believers, fear God and honor the emperor.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD CITIZENSHIP.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Abraham's faith was reckoned to him as righteous in St. Paul's teaching on
explanation: In St. Paul's teaching, Abraham's faith was reckoned to him as righteousness in the context of Law and Grace. This is found in Romans 4:3-5 where Paul explains that Abraham believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness. This belief was not based on his adherence to the law, but on his faith in God's promises, thus it falls under the concept of Grace.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of the teaching(s) of St. James similar to Amos's message is on
explanation: St. James's teachings and Amos's message both emphasize on Social justice. James 5:1-6 warns the rich about the misery that is coming upon them for having exploited the poor. This is similar to Amos's message where he condemns the injustices done to the poor by the rich (Amos 2:6-7, 5:11-12). Therefore, the teaching of St. James that is similar to Amos's message is on Social justice.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Suffering for a just reason is a pointer to
options: St. Paul's teaching on justification of faith
explanation: The statement 'Suffering for a just reason' is a pointer to St. Peter's teaching on persecution. In the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in 1 Peter 4:12-19, St. Peter teaches that Christians should not be surprised at suffering for the sake of righteousness, but should rejoice as they are sharing in Christ's sufferings.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIAN ATTITUDE TO PERSECUTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the second story of creation, the name eve means
options: a flesh of my flesh and bone of my bone
a person brought out of a man Adam
explanation: In the second story of creation, the name Eve means 'mother of all living beings'. This is found in Genesis 3:20 of the Bible where it is written that 'Adam named his wife Eve, because she would become the mother of all the living.'

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ADAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The previous residence of the Canaanites
options: was where God spoke to Abram and His covenant was re - affirmed
explanation: The Canaanites were a group of ancient people who lived in the land of Canaan, an area which according to ancient texts may have included parts of modern-day Israel, Palestine, Lebanon, Syria and Jordan. Schechem, a prominent city in Canaan, is often associated with the Canaanites.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The covenant of God for the Israelites was re - introduced and enforced by ---------------------
explanation: The covenant of God for the Israelites was re-introduced and enforced by Moses. This is evident in the book of Exodus where Moses led the Israelites out of Egypt and received the Ten Commandments from God on Mount Sinai. These commandments formed the basis of the covenant between God and the Israelites.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Lord provided water for the Israelites firstly
explanation: The first time the Lord provided water for the Israelites after they left Egypt was in the wilderness of Sin. This is recorded in Exodus 15:22-25. After crossing the Red Sea, they went into the wilderness of Shur. They travelled for three days in the wilderness and found no water. When they came to Marah, they could not drink the water of Marah because it was bitter. So, Moses cried to the Lord, and the Lord showed him a tree, which when he cast into the waters, the waters were made sweet.

topics.title: DIVINE PROVIDENCE, GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION :GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The consequences of Eli's lack of parental responsibility include the following except
explanation: The consequences of Eli's lack of parental responsibility did not include restructuring Israel's mode of governance. Eli's sons, Hophni and Phinehas, were wicked and did not respect the Lord's offerings. Despite Eli's warnings, they did not change their ways. As a result, God punished Eli's family. His sons died on the same day, and the Ark of the Lord was captured by the Philistines. However, there was no direct restructuring of Israel's mode of governance as a result of Eli's lack of parental responsibility.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Those are hated and revile shall ------------------
explanation: The statement 'Those are hated and revile shall have a great reward in heaven' is a reference to the Beatitudes in the Bible, specifically Matthew 5:11-12. In these verses, Jesus tells his followers that those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake will have a great reward in heaven.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The accusation against Jesus before the High Priest was that of -------------------
explanation: The accusation against Jesus before the High Priest was that of blasphemy. This is recorded in the Bible in Matthew 26:65, where the High Priest accuses Jesus of blasphemy after Jesus affirms that he is the Christ, the Son of God.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: A practical demonstration of communal sharing among the early Christians was in the sale of a field which belong to -------------------
explanation: In the book of Acts 4:36-37, it is recorded that Joseph, a Levite from Cyprus, whom the apostles called Barnabas (which means 'son of encouragement'), sold a field he owned and brought the money and put it at the apostles' feet. Therefore, the field belonged to Joseph surnamed Barnabas.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The answer "You are the Christ the son of living God was given by Peter in the neighborhood of____
explanation: In Matthew 16:13-16, Jesus asked his disciples, 'Who do people say the Son of Man is?' They replied, 'Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.' 'But what about you?' he asked. 'Who do you say I am?' Simon Peter answered, 'You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.' This conversation took place in the region of Caesarea Philippi.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE OCCASION OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The young man who wanted to know from Jesus what good deed would qualify him for heaven went away sorrowful because he ----------------
options: would realize little money from sale of his possession
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 19:16-22, a rich young man asked Jesus what he must do to have eternal life. Jesus told him to keep the commandments, to which the young man replied he has kept all his life. Jesus then told him to sell all his possessions and give to the poor, and then he will have treasure in heaven. The young man went away sorrowful because he had great possessions. Therefore, the correct answer is 'has great possession'.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Identify the speaker "Now I know that the Lord has sent his angel to deliver me out of the hand of Herold and from everything that the Jewish people expected to happen to me".
explanation: The quote "Now I know that the Lord has sent his angel to deliver me out of the hand of Herold and from everything that the Jewish people expected to happen to me" is from the Bible, specifically Acts 12:11. The speaker of this quote is Peter. After being freed from prison by an angel, Peter realized that the Lord had delivered him from Herod and what the Jewish people had planned for him. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Peter'.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE ARREST AND IMPRISONMENT OF PETER AND JOHN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who baptized the Ethiopian Eunch?
explanation: The Ethiopian Eunuch was baptized by Philip. This event is recorded in the book of Acts 8:26-40. Philip, one of the seven deacons, was led by an angel of the Lord to go to the road from Jerusalem to Gaza where he met the Ethiopian Eunuch, explained the scriptures to him, and subsequently baptized him.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF THE ETHIOPIAN EUNUCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The miracle which pertained to ingratitude is the --------------------
explanation: The miracle which pertained to ingratitude is the cure of the ten lepers. This event is recorded in the book of Luke 17:11-19. Jesus healed ten lepers, but only one of them, a Samaritan, returned to thank Him. The other nine, who were Jews, did not return to give thanks. This shows their ingratitude.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: While Peter was still speaking this, the Holy Spirit fell on all who heard the word . Where did this happen?
explanation: The event of the Holy Spirit falling on all who heard the word while Peter was still speaking happened in Caesarea. This event is recorded in the book of Acts 10:44-48, where Peter was preaching to the household of Cornelius, a centurion of the Italian Regiment, who lived in Caesarea.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF CORNELIUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Fear not, stand still, ... for the Egyptians whom you have seen today..."
options: This statement was made when the Israelites encamped close to Pi-hahiroth
This statement was made by the Lord to Moses and the Israelites
explanation: The statement "Fear not, stand still, ... for the Egyptians whom you have seen today..." was made by the Lord to Moses and the Israelites. This is recorded in Exodus 14:13-14, when the Israelites were trapped between the Red Sea and Pharaoh's army. God reassured them not to be afraid and to stand still and see the deliverance He would bring them that day.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joseph obeyed the commandment of God which is present in the law book of moses which states
options: you shall not possess another person's possession
explanation: The commandment that Joseph obeyed, as mentioned in the Bible, is 'You shall not commit adultery'. This is one of the Ten Commandments given by God to Moses on Mount Sinai, as recorded in the book of Exodus, chapter 20. Joseph demonstrated his obedience to this commandment when he refused to sleep with Potipar's wife, as recorded in Genesis 39:9.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David spared the life of Saul and he kept the commandment of God which states
options: you shall postpone your rivals till judgement day
explanation: The commandment that David obeyed, as mentioned in the Bible, is 'You shall not kill'. This is also one of the Ten Commandments given by God to Moses on Mount Sinai, as recorded in the book of Exodus, chapter 20. David demonstrated his obedience to this commandment when he spared the life of King Saul, who was trying to kill him, as recorded in 1 Samuel 24:4-7.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Jesus was about to heal a blind man his disciples asked him what
options: who is in charge of this calamity
what offence was committed by this man
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in John 9:1-2, when Jesus saw a man blind from birth, His disciples asked Him, 'Rabbi, who sinned, this man or his parents, that he was born blind?' Therefore, the disciples were asking about the offense committed by the man or his parents that resulted in his blindness.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The children of Israel was sent on an exile for the punishment of their sin of
explanation: The children of Israel were sent into exile as a punishment for their sin of idolatry. This is documented in several books of the Old Testament, including 2 Kings and Jeremiah. They turned away from God and worshipped idols, which led to their exile in Babylon.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE SITUATIONS THAT LED TO THE PUNISHMENT OF ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Ichabod means ______
explanation: Ichabod is a name that appears in the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Samuel. It was given to the son of Phinehas, a wicked priest of the Lord. His mother named him Ichabod, saying 'The Glory has departed from Israel' because the Ark of God had been captured by the Philistines. Therefore, Ichabod means 'departed glory'.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The early church lives together in
explanation: In the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in the book of Acts, it is described that the early Christian church lived together in unity. They shared everything they had, sold their possessions to give to anyone who had need, and met together in the temple courts. They broke bread in their homes and ate together with glad and sincere hearts, praising God and enjoying the favor of all the people. Therefore, the early church lived together in 'unity'.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Luke, Jesus final statement on the cross is
options: father forgive them for they know not what they do
Son beheld your mother, mother beheld you son
father into your hands i commit my spirit
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke 23:46, Jesus' final statement on the cross was 'Father, into your hands I commit my spirit.' This statement signifies Jesus' total surrender to God's will, even unto death.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Rehoboam's unwise decision led to
explanation: Rehoboam's unwise decision to reject the advice of the elders and instead listen to the advice of his young friends led to the division of the kingdom. This is recorded in 1 Kings 12:16-20, where it is stated that when all Israel saw that the king refused to listen to them, they answered the king: 'What share do we have in David, what part in Jesse's son? To your tents, Israel! Look after your own house, David!' So the Israelites went home. But as for the Israelites who were living in the towns of Judah, Rehoboam still ruled over them. When all the Israelites heard that Jeroboam had returned, they sent and called him to the assembly and made him king over all Israel. Only the tribe of Judah remained loyal to the house of David.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Among the judges that succeeded after Joshua, which is a left handed worrior
explanation: Ehud is described as a left-handed warrior in the Bible. In the Book of Judges, Ehud is sent to deliver a tribute to Eglon, the King of Moab, and uses his left-handedness to his advantage to assassinate Eglon and free the Israelites from Moabite rule.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Land, sea and vegetation are created on
explanation: According to the Genesis creation narrative in the Bible, land, sea, and vegetation were created on the third day. On this day, God gathered the waters together, causing dry land to appear. After this, God caused vegetation to sprout from the land.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Abram was how old when he was called out of his father's house?
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically in Genesis 12:4, Abram (later known as Abraham) was 75 years old when he was called by God to leave his father's house and go to the land that God would show him.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to mathew, Jesus first four disciples are
explanation: According to the Gospel of Matthew in the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in Matthew 4:18-22, the first four disciples that Jesus called were Simon (also known as Peter), Andrew, James, and John.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: On the day of Pentecost, the disciples were asked to stay in the
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Acts 1:13, it is mentioned that the disciples stayed in the upper room (also known as the Cenacle) on the day of Pentecost. This is where they were filled with the Holy Spirit. Therefore, the correct answer is 'upper room'.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Saul serves as a great opposition to the gospel but nemesis caught up with him on his way to
explanation: In the Bible, Acts 9:1-19 narrates the story of Saul's conversion. Saul, who was a great persecutor of Christians, was on his way to Damascus when he had a life-changing encounter with Jesus Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Damascus'.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION BY SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which is not a sign of the second coming of christ as stated by Mathew
explanation: The blowing of the trumpet is not mentioned as a sign of the second coming of Christ in the book of Matthew. The book of Matthew, specifically Matthew 24:4-31, mentions many signs of the second coming of Christ, including false prophets, famines, and earthquakes. However, the blowing of the trumpet is not mentioned in these verses. It is mentioned in 1 Corinthians 15:52 and 1 Thessalonians 4:16, but these are not books written by Matthew.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Daniel, he succeeded among all of his other colleagues beacuse
explanation: According to the book of Daniel, specifically Daniel 6:3, Daniel succeeded among all of his other colleagues because he had an excellent spirit. The verse states, 'Then this Daniel was preferred above the presidents and princes, because an excellent spirit was in him; and the king thought to set him over the whole realm.' Therefore, the correct answer is that Daniel had an excellent spirit.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : DANIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus as the Lamb simply means
options: he goes a long way to rescue his sinners
in humility he counted others than himself
explanation: The term 'Lamb' in the Bible, especially in the New Testament, is often used to refer to Jesus Christ. This is in reference to the sacrificial lambs in the Old Testament. Jesus, as the Lamb, signifies that He is the ultimate sacrifice for the sins of mankind. Therefore, the phrase 'Jesus as the Lamb simply means he goes a long way to rescue his sinners' is the most accurate among the options. It signifies Jesus' sacrificial act of redemption for humanity's sins.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF: THE LAMB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the following prophets stands for social justice and tru religion?
explanation: Among the options given, Amos is the prophet who is most associated with social justice and true religion. Amos was a prophet in the Old Testament who spoke out against the injustices and idolatries of his time. He emphasized the importance of righteousness, justice, and adherence to God's laws over empty religious rituals. Therefore, the statement 'Amos stands for social justice and true religion' is correct.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the synoptic gospels, "Go ye therefore and make disciples of all Nation" can simply be portrayed as
explanation: The phrase "Go ye therefore and make disciples of all Nation" is referred to as the Great Commission. This is the instruction given by Jesus to his disciples to spread his teachings to all the nations of the world. Therefore, the correct answer is 'The great commission'.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The parable 'The wheat and the Tares' simply portrayed
options: The dangers to befall the evil doers
explanation: The parable of 'The Wheat and the Tares' is a story told by Jesus in the Bible (Matthew 13:24-30). It is about a man who sowed good seed in his field, but while everyone was sleeping, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went away. The parable is used to illustrate the co-existence of good and evil in the world, and the eventual judgement where evil doers (tares) will be punished. Therefore, the correct answer is 'The dangers to befall the evil doers'.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St. Paul, the works of the law are of the
explanation: According to St. Paul in the Bible, the works of the law are of the flesh. This is because he believed that the law was not able to save people from their sins, but rather it exposed their sinful nature (flesh).

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The condition of Judah was narrated to Nehemiah by
explanation: The condition of Judah was narrated to Nehemiah by Hanani. This is recorded in the Bible, Nehemiah 1:2-3, where Hanani, one of Nehemiah's brothers, came from Judah with some other men, and Nehemiah asked them about the Jews who had returned from captivity and about Jerusalem.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Meshach, Shedrach and Abednego are casted into a fiery furnace because they
options: would not bow to the King's image
were bragging that their God would save them
explanation: Meshach, Shedrach, and Abednego were cast into a fiery furnace because they refused to bow to King Nebuchadnezzar's golden image. This is a biblical story found in the book of Daniel 3:12-18. The three Hebrew men were thrown into the furnace for not obeying the king's command to worship the golden image he had set up.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jonah spent a sum of three days and night in the belly of a big fish because
options: he turned his eyes way from God
he refused to preach to the people of Nineveh
he was too lenient with the people of Ninveh
explanation: Jonah spent three days and nights in the belly of a big fish because he refused to preach to the people of Nineveh. This is a biblical story found in the book of Jonah 1:1-17. God commanded Jonah to go to Nineveh and preach against its wickedness, but Jonah disobeyed and tried to flee from the presence of the Lord. As a result, he was swallowed by a big fish where he spent three days and nights.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The voice from heaven that conveyed the mesage "This is my beloved son in whom I am well pleased " affirmed Jesus
explanation: The voice from heaven saying "This is my beloved son in whom I am well pleased" is a direct affirmation of Jesus' sonship. This statement was made during the baptism of Jesus by John the Baptist, and it was a divine confirmation of Jesus' identity as the Son of God.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these did not happen during Jesus transfiguration?
explanation: During the transfiguration of Jesus, the temple's curtain did not tear. The tearing of the temple's curtain happened after Jesus' death on the cross. The transfiguration event involved the appearance of Elijah and Moses, a bright cloud overshadowing the mountain, and a voice from heaven, but it did not involve the tearing of the temple's curtain or earthquakes.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: New life is known as when one is
explanation: In Christian theology, new life or being born again is often associated with being free from sin and its works. This is based on the belief that accepting Christ as one's savior cleanses one from all past sins and gives a new life in Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is 'free from sin and its works'.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Eli's lack of parental responsibility led to all of the following ecxept
options: the capturig of the ark of covenant
explanation: Eli's lack of parental responsibility led to the death of his two sons, the capturing of the ark of covenant, and his own death. However, it did not lead to the recovery of the ark. The ark was recovered after his death. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the recovery of the ark'.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The resurrection of Jesus strictly portrayed
explanation: The resurrection of Jesus Christ is a fundamental concept in Christianity. It is the event where Jesus was raised from the dead after his crucifixion. This event is seen as Jesus' victory over death, demonstrating that God has power over death, and providing hope for Christians of eternal life. Therefore, the resurrection of Jesus strictly portrayed Jesus' victory over death.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God provided Manna and water for the children of Israeal at
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Exodus, it is recorded that God provided Manna (bread from heaven) and water for the children of Israel during their journey in the wilderness. The specific places where these miracles occurred were Massah and Meribah. Therefore, God provided Manna and water for the children of Israel at Massah and Meribah.

topics.title: DIVINE PROVIDENCE, GUIDANCE AND PROTECTION :PROVISION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul in his epistle to the Philipians used Jesus Christ as a case study that through Jesus was in form of God, he
options: he did not count himself equal God.
explanation: In Philippians 2:6, Paul states that Jesus, though he was in the form of God, did not count equality with God a thing to be grasped. This means that Jesus, despite being in the divine form, did not consider himself equal to God. He humbled himself and became obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Pual in his epistle to the Galatians acknowledged that christians are being justified by their
explanation: In Galatians 2:16, Paul states that a person is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ. This means that it is not by our own efforts or good deeds that we are made right with God, but through our faith in Jesus Christ. Justification is a theological concept that refers to God's act of removing the guilt and penalty of sin while at the same time declaring a sinner righteous through Christ's atoning sacrifice.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Gehazi's greed made him to request for the gifts which was rejecteed by Elisha, his boss and the gifts include
options: three talents of silver and one festal garment
two festal of garments and one talent of silver
two talents of silver and two festal garments
two talents of gold and two festal garments
explanation: In the Bible, specifically 2 Kings 5:22-23, Gehazi, the servant of Elisha, lied to Naaman and asked for two talents of silver and two festal garments. However, in the options provided, the closest to the biblical account is 'three talents of silver and one festal garment'. Therefore, option 1 is the most accurate based on the information provided.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Peter advised the christians to always obey amd abide by the rules and regulations of those in
explanation: In 1 Peter 2:13-14, Peter advises Christians to 'Submit yourselves for the Lord's sake to every human authority: whether to the emperor, as the supreme authority, or to governors, who are sent by him to punish those who do wrong and to commend those who do right.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'authorities' as Peter was referring to all forms of human authority, not just those in power or in the church.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The three wise men presented Gold, frankincense and myrrh to Jesus in which the Gold symbolises
explanation: In the biblical narrative, the three wise men presented Gold, frankincense, and myrrh to Jesus. Each of these gifts symbolizes a different aspect of Jesus's identity and mission: Gold is a symbol of kingship on earth, frankincense (an incense) is a symbol of deity, and myrrh (an embalming oil) is a symbol of death. Therefore, the Gold symbolizes his royalty.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joshua was truly a successor of Moses because
options: he led the Isrealites to the promise land
explanation: Joshua is considered a successor of Moses because he continued Moses's mission and led the Israelites into the Promised Land. This was a task that Moses was unable to complete before his death. Therefore, the statement that best explains why Joshua was truly a successor of Moses is that 'he led the Israelites to the Promised Land'.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSHUA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Immediately Jesus was arrested by the Roman soldiers, he was taken to __________ for trial
explanation: According to the New Testament of the Bible, after Jesus was arrested, he was first taken to the high priest for trial. This is recorded in the Gospel of John 18:13: 'They first took him to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'The high priest'.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the book of Daniel , the thre Hebrews faith made them
options: free from the king and his fire
explanation: In the book of Daniel, specifically Daniel 3:19-27, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego (the three Hebrews) were thrown into a fiery furnace by King Nebuchadnezzar because they refused to bow down to his golden image. However, their faith in God protected them from the fire, and they emerged unscathed. Therefore, the correct answer is 'free from the king and his fire'.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One remarkeable speech in the bible that tends towards triggering christians to give to one another is
options: "Give and it shall be given unto you"
"if you give out, i wil give unto thee"
"Blessed are the givers, for they shall receive
"It is more blessed to give than to receive"
explanation: The phrase "It is more blessed to give than to receive" is a well-known biblical quote that encourages Christians to give to one another. It is found in Acts 20:35, where Paul is reminding the church elders about the teachings of Jesus. The phrase suggests that there is a greater blessing in giving out to others than in receiving from them.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's teaching, Christians are also as joint heir with Christ as a result of
explanation: In Romans 8:17, Paul teaches that Christians are joint heirs with Christ as a result of their faith in Him. This means that Christians, through their faith in Christ, share in His inheritance. This includes the promise of eternal life and the blessings that come from being children of God. The other options do not accurately reflect the teachings of Paul on this matter.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the old testament, whio is regarded as the man after God's heart?
explanation: According to the Old Testament, specifically in the book of 1 Samuel 13:14 and Acts 13:22, David is referred to as a 'man after God's own heart'. This phrase used to describe David signifies his complete loyalty and commitment to God, despite his human failings.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Luke, the seventy were sent out as _________ among _________
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke in the New Testament, specifically Luke 10:3, Jesus sent out the seventy disciples as 'sheep among wolves'. This metaphor was used to illustrate the vulnerability and innocence of the disciples in the face of potential hostility and danger.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The three wise men journeyed from the ________
explanation: The three wise men, also known as the Magi, are traditionally believed to have come from the East. This is based on the biblical account in the Gospel of Matthew, which states 'wise men from the East came to Jerusalem' (Matthew 2:1). Therefore, the correct answer is 'East'.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: New life in christ simply means the transition from old life of sin to new life of __________
explanation: New life in Christ refers to the spiritual transformation that occurs when a person becomes a Christian. This involves a transition from a life of sin to a life of holiness. While happiness, joy, and righteousness may be aspects of this new life, the fundamental change is one of holiness, as the believer is called to be holy just as God is holy (1 Peter 1:16). Therefore, the correct answer is 'holiness'.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The contest on mout carmel between Prophet Elijah and the four hundred and fifty baal prophets strictly portrays
explanation: The contest on Mount Carmel between Prophet Elijah and the four hundred and fifty Baal prophets is a demonstration of God's Supremacy. In this biblical event, Elijah challenges the prophets of Baal to prove that Baal is a false god and that the God of Israel is the one true God. The God of Israel answers Elijah's prayer by sending fire from heaven to consume the sacrifice, proving His supremacy over Baal.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul in his epistle to the Galatians, "As far as we are in christ, we are no custodian of the ________"
explanation: In his epistle to the Galatians, Paul emphasizes that those who are in Christ are no longer under the law. This is because Christ has fulfilled the law on their behalf. Therefore, believers are no longer custodians of the law but are under grace. This concept is central to Paul's theology of justification by faith alone in Christ alone.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PLACE OF THE LAW AMONG THE JEWS.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: _________ is a prophet who preached on punishmnent and hope?
explanation: Jeremiah is known as the prophet who preached both punishment and hope. He prophesied about the destruction of Jerusalem as a punishment for their sins but also gave them hope by prophesying about the restoration of Israel.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE :

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Elijah smote the four hundred and fifty Baal prophets down to a
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Kings 18:40, it is mentioned that Elijah took the prophets of Baal down to the Kishon River and slaughtered them there. Therefore, the correct answer is River Kishon.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One of these is not taught by James
explanation: The book of James in the Bible teaches about impartiality (James 2:1), faith and work (James 2:14-26), and effective prayer (James 5:16). However, it does not specifically teach about the 'dignity of labour'.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The fourth spiritual gift according to St. Paul is
explanation: According to 1 Corinthians 12:7-11, the fourth spiritual gift listed by St. Paul is 'faith'. The gifts are listed in this order: wisdom, knowledge, faith, healing, miraculous powers, prophecy, distinguishing between spirits, speaking in different kinds of tongues, and interpretation of tongues.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God made his covenant with
