explanation: In the Bible, it is mentioned that God made his covenant with the children of Israel. This is seen in Exodus 19:5 where God says, 'Now if you obey me fully and keep my covenant, then out of all nations you will be my treasured possession.' Here, 'you' refers to the children of Israel.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The multitude present in the upper room during the coming of the Holy spirit took apostles to be
explanation: In the book of Acts 2:13, it is mentioned that some people in the crowd accused the apostles of being drunk when the Holy Spirit descended upon them. They were not understood by some people and were mistaken to be drunk due to their strange behavior and speaking in tongues.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus on the mounatin explained the
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew chapter 5, Jesus on the mountain explained the Beatitudes. The Beatitudes are the eight blessings recounted by Jesus in the Sermon on the Mount.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Ezekiel revied the dry bone because of
explanation: In the book of Ezekiel, specifically chapter 37, Ezekiel revived the dry bones because of the supremacy of God. God showed Ezekiel a vision of a valley of dry bones and commanded him to prophesy to the bones. As Ezekiel obeyed, the bones came to life, symbolizing that God could bring hope and restoration to Israel, demonstrating His supremacy.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: All but one of these prophets called the Israealites to repentance
explanation: Jonah is the prophet who did not call the Israelites to repentance. Instead, he was sent to the city of Nineveh, a non-Israelite city, to call its people to repentance. The other prophets, Isaiah, Jeremiah, and Hosea, all called the Israelites to repentance.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul's letter to the Corinthans, he who speaks in tongues edifieth
explanation: According to Paul's letter to the Corinthians, specifically 1 Corinthians 14:4, 'He who speaks in a tongue edifies himself.' This means that speaking in tongues, a spiritual gift, helps to build up or strengthen the individual's own spiritual life. It does not necessarily edify the church, the body of Christ, or the ministry unless it is interpreted for the benefit of others.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE BENEFITS OF SPIRITUAL GIFTS TO THE INDIVIDUAL AND THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus as the door simply symbolises that Jesus is the door to
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in John 10:9, Jesus refers to himself as the door. This metaphor is used to symbolize that through Jesus, one can gain access to eternal life. Therefore, Jesus being the door symbolizes that he is the door to 'eternal life', not heaven, earth, or firmament.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF: THE DOOR THE LAMB AND THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Moses and Elijah in the transfiguration smply symbolise
explanation: In the transfiguration, Moses and Elijah represent the law and the prophets, respectively. Moses is known as the lawgiver in the Old Testament, while Elijah is one of the major prophets. Therefore, their appearance in the transfiguration symbolizes the 'law and prophet', not law and grace, law and mercy, or justice and mercy.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TRANSFIGURATION TO THE DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The early christians received great oppositon from
explanation: The early Christians received great opposition from Saul. Saul, who later became Paul, was initially a fierce persecutor of Christians. He was present at the stoning of Stephen and was known for hunting down Christians to imprison them. However, after his conversion on the road to Damascus, he became one of the most influential figures in the early Christian church.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION BY SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St. Paul, Grace triumphs over ___________
explanation: According to St. Paul, Grace triumphs over judgement. In Romans 5:20, Paul writes, 'Where sin increased, grace increased all the more.' This means that no matter how much sin there is, God's grace is always greater. Therefore, God's grace triumphs over judgement because it offers forgiveness and redemption despite our sins.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these is not an attribute of love by St. Paul
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 13:4-8, St. Paul describes the attributes of love. He mentions that love is patient, kind, does not envy, does not boast, is not proud, does not dishonor others, is not self-seeking, is not easily angered, keeps no record of wrongs, does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth, always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres, and never fails. Therefore, 'love is boastful' is not an attribute of love according to St. Paul.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Peter beheld the transfiguration of Jesus, he demand if _______ should be made for Jesus, Elijah and Moses
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Matthew 17:1-4, when Peter witnessed the transfiguration of Jesus, he suggested to Jesus that he could build three booths (or shelters) for Jesus, Moses, and Elijah. Therefore, the correct answer is 'three booths'.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"My house shall be called a house of prayer but ye had made it a den of thieves"
Who made this statement?
explanation: The statement "My house shall be called a house of prayer but ye had made it a den of thieves" was made by Jesus Christ. This statement was made when Jesus was expressing his anger towards the people who were using the temple for trading instead of worship. This can be found in the Bible in the book of Matthew 21:13.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St. Paul in his letter to the Thessalonains, "if anyone would not work, let him not..."
explanation: According to St. Paul in his letter to the Thessalonians, "if anyone would not work, let him not eat". This statement is found in the Bible in the book of 2 Thessalonians 3:10. The idea behind this statement is that everyone should contribute to society and not be idle. If one chooses not to work, then they should not expect to reap the benefits of others' labor.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Naaman's leprosy came upon Gehazi because Gehazi was
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 2 Kings 5:20-27, Gehazi, the servant of Elisha the man of God, lied to Naaman and took gifts from him after Elisha had healed Naaman of his leprosy. When Elisha found out, he cursed Gehazi and his descendants with Naaman's leprosy. This was because of Gehazi's greed, not because he was rebellious, notorious, or adulterous.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In James' epsitle, a great option for effective prayer is
explanation: In the Epistle of James, specifically in James 1:6 and James 5:15, it is stated that when one prays, they must pray in faith, believing that they will receive what they ask for. Therefore, faith is a great option for effective prayer according to James' epistle, not steadfastness, endurance, or perseverance.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of these epistles talked about the second coming of christ?
explanation: The second coming of Christ is discussed in the epistles to the Thessalonians. Specifically, 1 Thessalonians 4:13-18 and 2 Thessalonians 2:1-12. These passages talk about the return of Jesus Christ, the resurrection of the dead in Christ, and the gathering of the saints. The other options, Luke, Corinthians, and Galatians, are either Gospels or epistles that do not focus on the second coming of Christ.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: One the first day of the week after Jesus had been crucified, there arose Mary magdalene, Solomon and one other Mary, the other Mary is regarded as the mother of
explanation: The other Mary is referred to as the mother of James and Joses in the Bible. Specifically, in Matthew 27:56, it is written, 'Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and Joses, and the mother of Zebedee's children.' The other options are incorrect because they either list incorrect children or incorrectly identify the other Mary as Mary, the mother of Jesus.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Apostle Paul advised christians living in the community to live a
explanation: Apostle Paul, in his teachings, always emphasized on living an exemplary life. He encouraged Christians to be role models in their communities by living according to the teachings of Christ. This means living a life of love, kindness, humility, patience, and forgiveness. Therefore, the correct answer is 'exemplary life'.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's epistle about corruption, he informs the church that immorality _________
explanation: In Apostle Paul's epistles, he often warned against immorality, stating that it defiles the church. The church, being the body of Christ, should be kept pure and holy. Immorality, being a sin, defiles the church by making it impure. Therefore, the correct answer is 'defiles the church'.

topics.title: SEXUAL IMMORALITY : ADULTERY AND FORNICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David was regarded as the man after God's heart beacuse
options: he did what was right in the sight of God
he kept all the ordinances of God
explanation: David was regarded as a man after God's heart because he did what was right in the sight of God. This is a biblical concept that means David lived in a way that was pleasing to God. He obeyed God's commands and sought to do His will. Although David made mistakes and sinned, he always repented and turned back to God. This is why he is described as a man after God's heart.

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Ahab's greed and coveteousness led to all of the following except
explanation: Ahab's greed and covetousness led to many negative consequences, including his own death, the death of his wife Jezebel, and the killing of his seventy sons. However, the cause of drought in Israel was not a direct result of Ahab's greed and covetousness. The drought was a punishment from God due to the general sinfulness and idolatry of the Israelites, not specifically due to Ahab's actions.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jonah refused to go yo Nineveh and boarded a ship going to
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of Jonah, Jonah was commanded by God to go to Nineveh to deliver a message of repentance. However, Jonah disobeyed and instead boarded a ship going to Tarshish. Therefore, the correct answer is Tarshish.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The four buckets of water that was poured three times on the bull meant for sacrifice during during the contest on mount carmel symbolised
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Kings 18, Elijah had a contest with the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel. He ordered that four jars of water be poured three times on the bull meant for sacrifice. This act symbolized the twelve tribes of Israel. The twelve tribes of Israel were the descendants of the twelve sons of Jacob, who was later named Israel by God. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the twelve tribes of Israel'.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the followig parables centred on the God's love for sinners?
explanation: The parable of the Prodigal Son is centered on God's love for sinners. In this parable, the father's unconditional love and forgiveness towards his wayward son who returns home after squandering his inheritance, symbolizes God's forgiveness and love for sinners who repent.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Go no near the Samartans nor the Gentiles but to the lost house of ..."
explanation: The complete quote is "Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Samaritans enter ye not: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel." This was Jesus' instruction to his disciples in Matthew 10:5-6. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Israel'.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The third in the hierachy of spiritual gift is ?
explanation: The third spiritual gift according to 1 Corinthians 12:8-10 is the utterance of knowledge or teaching.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Saul's disobedience led to all of these except
explanation: The division of the kingdom did not occur as a result of Saul's disobedience. This happened during the reign of Rehoboam, Solomon's son, after the reign of Saul's successor, King David.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : DISOBEDIENCE AND CONSEQUENCES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus started his minsterial work at the age of
explanation: According to the Bible, specifically Luke 3:23, Jesus started his ministerial work at the age of 30. This is when he was baptized by John the Baptist and began his public ministry.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who among Jesus disciple made a great confession that Jesus is the son of God?
explanation: In the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in Matthew 16:16, Simon Peter made the great confession that Jesus is the Son of God. Jesus then blessed Simon and gave him the name Peter, which means 'rock', saying that on this rock he would build his church.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE OCCASION OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: With Moses still at Mount Sinai, the Israelites asked Aaron to
explanation: According to Exodus, they "gathered around Aaron and said to him: 'Come, make gods for us, who shall go before us'" (Exodus 32:1). Rather than remaining steadfast in his faith, Aaron gave in. He ordered the people to collect all the gold in their possession, and used it to create a golden calf for worship.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: To test Abraham's faith, God asked him to sacrifice Isaac at ____.
explanation: Some time later God tested Abraham. He said to him, "Abraham!" "Here I am," he replied. Then God said, "Take your son, your only son, Isaac, whom you love, and go to the region of Moriah.(Gen 22:1-2)

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why did Abraham circumcise Isaac eight days after he was born?
options: It was the custom of his tribe to do so
Isaac was the son of promise
He wanted to distinguish Isaac from Ishmael
explanation: Abraham circumcised Isaac eight days after he was born because God commanded him to do so. This is according to the Abrahamic covenant in the Bible (Genesis 17:12) where God commanded Abraham to circumcise his male descendants on the eighth day after birth as a sign of the covenant between them.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The fifth plague inflicted upon the Egyptians was
options: the covering of the land of Egypt with water
the changing of all the rivers into blood
explanation: "Then the Lord said to Moses, 'Go in to Pharaoh and tell him, "Thus says the Lord God of the Hebrews: 'Let My people go, that they may serve Me. For if you refuse to let them go, and still hold them, behold, the hand of the Lord will be on your cattle in the field, on the horses, on the donkeys, on the camels, on the oxen, and on the sheep—a very severe pestilence. And the Lord will make a difference between the livestock of Israel and the livestock of Egypt'"'" (Exodus 9:1-4).

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where did Joseph's brothers conspire to kill him?
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in Genesis 37:17-18, it is mentioned that Joseph's brothers conspired to kill him in Dothan. Therefore, the correct answer is Dothan.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Joshua was buried at Timnath-serah, which is in the hill country of the Tribe of
explanation: According to the biblical book bearing his name, Joshua died at the age of 110 and his burial site was in a location of his own inheritance at Timnath-serah, which is in the hill country of the Tribe of Ephraim, north of the mountain of Gaash.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSHUA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The first Miracle attributed to Jesus in the gospel of John was
options: healing the woman with the issue of blood
explanation: The transformation of water into wine at the wedding at Cana (also called the marriage at Cana, wedding feast at Cana or marriage feast at Cana) is the first miracle attributed to Jesus in the Gospel of John.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: _______________ is the only miracle performed by Jesus which is recorded in all four gospels.
explanation: The 'feeding of the five thousand' is the only miracle performed by Jesus which is recorded in all four gospels: Matthew 14:13-21, Mark 6:31-44, Luke 9:12-17, and John 6:1-14. And although there are minor differences in the telling from gospel to gospel, they are all broadly similar on the detail.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The law books used for the great reformation of Israel's social and religious institution were found in the temple in ____ BC during the reign of _____.
explanation: The law books used for the great reformation of Israel's social and religious institution were found in the temple in 621 BC during the reign of Josiah. This event is recorded in the Bible in 2 Kings 22. King Josiah initiated a religious reform after the Book of the Law (probably Deuteronomy) was found in the temple. The reform was aimed at purging Judah of idolatry and returning the people to the worship of Yahweh, the God of Israel.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "The soul that sins shall die" was a prophesy by Prophet
explanation: "But the soul that sins shall die: and the son shall not bear the iniquity of the father, nor shall the father bear the iniquity of the son" - Ezekiel 18:20

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the prophet of Hope in Babylon?
explanation: The prophet of Hope in Babylon was Ezekiel. He was among the Jews who were taken captive to Babylon. Despite the despair of his fellow captives, Ezekiel's message was one of hope. He prophesied that the Jews would return to Jerusalem, their holy city.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Ezekiel ordered to eat before he went to speak to the House of Israel?
explanation: Ezekiel was told by God that he was to be a prophet to the rebellious Israelites, even though they may refuse to listen to him. God then told Ezekiel: "Open your mouth and eat what I am giving you" (Ez. 2:8), and a scroll was handed to him. Ezekiel ate the scroll that came from God, which tasted "sweet as honey"

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the sign of God's covenant with Abraham?
explanation: The token or sign of the covenant God made with Abraham was circumcision. Circumcision symbolized cleanliness before God and was also a symbolic reminder of the promises the Lord made to Abraham regarding his posterity.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The mountain where Moses saw the bush burning was called
explanation: The Burning Bush. Now Moses was tending the flock of Jethro his father-in-law, the priest of Midian, and he led the flock to the far side of the desert and came to Horeb, the mountain of God. There the angel of the LORD appeared to him in flames of fire from within a bush.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where was the first place the disciples were called Christians?
explanation: The disciples were first called Christians in Antioch. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Acts 11:26, which states '...So for a whole year Barnabas and Saul met with the church and taught great numbers of people. The disciples were called Christians first at Antioch.' Therefore, the correct answer is Antioch.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The complaints of the ______ led to the appointment of the deacons in the early church.
explanation: The main problem that led to the appointment of the seven deacons centred around discrimination against the Hellenists in the early church.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The laws given by Moses to the children of Israel in Sinai have striking similarities with the
explanation: The Book of the Covenant is sometimes called the Law of Moses, but the Book of the Covenant and the Law of Hammurabi used in the Babylonian Empire, seem closely related in the contextual and legal language, so it's easy to see how the Covenantal Laws of Israel and other nations might have influenced one another.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Walk before me, and be blameless." This statement was addressed to Abraham at the age of _____
explanation: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the LORD appeared to him and said, "I am God Almighty; walk before me faithfully and be blameless. (Gen 17:1)

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'But I will be with you and you will smite the Midianites as one man...' This statement was addressed by God to
explanation: The LORD turned to him and said, "Go in the strength you have and save Israel from the hand of Midian. Am I not sending you?" "Please, my Lord," Gideon replied, "how can I save Israel? Indeed, my clan is the weakest in Manasseh, and I am the youngest in my father's house.""Surely I will be with you," the LORD replied, "and you will strike down all the Midianites as one man." (Judges 6:14-16)

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : GIDEON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?' Whose accounts recorded these as Jesus' words at the ninth hour?
explanation:
(Matthew 27:46) – "And about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying, 'Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?' that is, 'My God, My God, why have Your forsaken Me?'"
(Mark 15:34) – "And at the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, 'Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?' which is translated, 'My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?'"
(Luke 23:46) – "And Jesus, crying out with a loud voice, said, 'Father, into Your hands I commit My spirit.' And having said this, He breathed His last."
(John 19:30) – "When Jesus therefore had received the sour wine, He said, 'It is finished!' And He bowed His head, and gave up His spirit."

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How many visions did Jeremiah see?
explanation: Jeremiah saw a visions of "a branch of an almond tree" (verses 11–12) and then a vision of "a boiling pot tilt away from the north" (verses 13–16).

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Before the disciples casted the lot and chos Matthias to replace Judas Iscariot, the other candidate they had was?
explanation: The disciples narrowed down the candidates to two people: Joseph (also known as Justus) and a man named Matthias (Acts 1:12-26).

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Paul was arrested in Jerusalem, which group of people supported him?
explanation: When Paul was arrested in Jerusalem, he was supported by the Pharisees. This happened when Paul was brought before the Sanhedrin, the Jewish ruling council. He identified himself as a Pharisee and argued about the concept of resurrection, which was a point of contention between the Pharisees, who believed in it, and the Sadducees, who did not. This caused a division in the council and some of the Pharisees expressed support for Paul (Acts 23:6-9).

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's letter to the ________, Paul explains that everyone, both the living and the dead, will share in Christ's Resurrection at the time of his Second Coming.
explanation: In Paul's first letter to the Thessalonians, specifically in 1 Thessalonians 4:13-18, Paul explains that everyone, both the living and the dead, will share in Christ's Resurrection at the time of his Second Coming. This was to comfort the Thessalonians who were concerned about those who had died before the Second Coming.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : PREPARATION FOR HIS COMING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The disciplines speaking in tongues was a fulfilment of a prophecy by
explanation: On the Day of Pentecost, before the large crowd which had gathered, the Apostle Peter cited a prophecy found in the Book of Joel.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: '' I am the resurrection and the life; he who believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live, and whoever lives and believes in me shall never die....'' Who did Jesus address this to?
explanation: Your brother will rise again," Jesus told her. Martha replied, "I know that he will rise again in the resurrection at the last day." Jesus said to her, "I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in Me will live, even though he dies.... (John 11:23-25)

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE RESURRECTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In being unfaithful to her husband, _____ represented Israel whilst being unfaithful to God
explanation: The prophet Hosea uses his adulterous wife Gomer as a metaphor for God as the faithful husband to Israel.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF HOSEA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''And the King (Artaxerxes) said to me, ''why is your face sad, seeing you are not sick? This is nothing else but sadness of the heart''. Who was the king addressing
explanation: The quote is from the book of Nehemiah in the Bible. Nehemiah was a cupbearer to King Artaxerxes of Persia. When Nehemiah appeared sad in the king's presence because of the news he had heard about the state of Jerusalem, the king noticed and asked him why he was sad, leading to the quote in the question.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Mark, what was the only thing the disciples wasn't forbidden to take for their journey?
explanation: These were his instructions: "Take nothing for the journey except a staff—no bread, no bag, no money in your belts. (Mark 6:8-9)

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE TWELVE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the following parable is peculiar to Matthew?
options: the parable of the Weeds Among the Wheat
explanation: The parable of the Weeds Among the Wheat is peculiar to the Gospel of Matthew. It is found in Matthew 13:24-30. This parable is not found in any other Gospel, making it unique to Matthew.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Believers today are partakers of the New Covenant through
explanation: The New Covenant is a biblical interpretation originally derived from a phrase in the Book of Jeremiah, in the Hebrew Bible. It is often thought of as an eschatological (ultimate destiny of humanity) Messianic Age or world to come and is related to the biblical concept of the Kingdom of God. Generally, Christians believe that the promised New Covenant was instituted at the Last Supper as part of the Eucharist, which in the Gospel of John includes the New Commandment. Based on the Bible teaching, believers today are partakers of the New Covenant through the gift of the spirit, not through baptism by immersion, obedience to church leadership, or adherence to all the sacraments.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian...'' Paul made this statement i n his letter to the
explanation: The statement 'But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a custodian...' is found in the book of Galatians 3:25. Apostle Paul wrote this in his letter to the Galatians to explain that now that they have faith in Jesus Christ, they are no longer under the law (the custodian) but under grace. Therefore, the correct answer is Galatians, not Thessalonians, Phillipians, or None of the above.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The second temptation according to the gospel of Luke was
options: to turn stones into bread to satisfy his physical hunger.
to leap from the temple pinnacle to show his divine nature.
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke, the second temptation of Jesus by Satan was to leap from the pinnacle of the temple to show his divine nature. Satan tempted Jesus to jump off from the highest point of the temple, saying that the angels would save Him. This is recorded in Luke 4:9-12.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Hophni and Phinehas were the sons of
explanation: They were the two sons of Eli. The first book of Samuel describes them as the officiating priests at the sanctuary of Shiloh at the time of Hannah.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ELI AND SAMUEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: At the Transfiguration of Jesus, Moses and Elijah appeared to represent the ______ and ______.
explanation: Matthew 17:2 states that Jesus "was transfigured before them; his face shining as the sun, and his garments became white as the light." At that point the prophet Elijah representing the prophets and Moses representing the Law appear and Jesus begins to talk to them.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TRANSFIGURATION TO THE DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Under King _______, Daniel was thrown into the lion's den.
explanation: Upon the advice of these men, King Darius had rashly issued a decree forbidding anyone from making petitions to "any god or man for thirty days" (Daniel 6:6-9)

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : DANIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'Hear now, you rebels' shall we bring forth water for you out of this rock? This statement was made by _____ to the Israelites.
explanation: In the first month, the whole congregation of Israel entered the Wilderness of Zin and stayed in Kadesh. There Miriam died and was buried. Now there was no water for the congregation, so they gathered against Moses and Aaron. The people quarreled with Moses and said, "If only we had perished with our brothers before the LORD!... (Numbers 20: 1-5)

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ".....Tend the flock of God...not as domineering over those in your charge but being examples to the flock..." Who made this statement?
explanation: The statement 'Tend the flock of God...not as domineering over those in your charge but being examples to the flock...' was made by Peter. This is found in the Bible in the book of 1 Peter 5:2-3. Peter was writing to the elders of the church, instructing them on how to shepherd the flock of God among them.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AMONG CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the epistle of James, what was the royal law?
explanation: In the Epistle of James(2:8), the Levitical law, "Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself", is called the "royal law". to perform.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: God passed his message to the people of Nineveh through Prophet
explanation: The prophet Jonah is called by God to go to Nineveh (a great Assyrian city) and prophesy disaster because of the city's excessive wickedness.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the book of John, Jesus stated that judgement should not be based on
explanation: Jesus' concern becomes apparent when He says: "Do not judge according to appearance, but judge with righteous judgment." (John 7:24).

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Likewise, ye wives be in subjection to your own husbands, that if any obey not the word, they also without the word be won by the conversation of the wives." Who made this statement?
explanation: The statement "Likewise, ye wives be in subjection to your own husbands, that if any obey not the word, they also without the word be won by the conversation of the wives." was made by Peter. This can be found in 1 Peter 3:1 of the Bible. Peter was addressing wives, encouraging them to be submissive to their husbands so that even if their husbands do not obey the word, they may be won over without words by the behavior of their wives.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : RELATIONSHIP IN THE CHRISTIAN FAMILY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''Is it not the rich who oppress you, is it not they who drag you into court?'' James asked this question in his teachings on
explanation: James' teaching on impartiality is basically about the need to avoid discrimination against the poor in favour of the rich.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CONCEPT OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'Truly I perceive that God shows no partiality' was said by Peter to
explanation: The statement 'Truly I perceive that God shows no partiality' was made by Peter when he was speaking to Cornelius, a Roman centurion. This was a significant moment in the New Testament because it marked the acceptance of Gentiles (non-Jews) into the Christian faith. Peter realized that God does not show favoritism but accepts people from every nation who fear Him and do what is right.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF CORNELIUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'Go into the village that is just ahead of you, and straightway you shall find a donkey tied, and a colt with her: untie them, and bring them to me...' Which town was Jesus when he made this statement?
explanation: Jesus made this statement when he got to Bethphage and was about to make the last entry into Jerusalem. The story of the triumphal entry is one of the few incidents in the life of Jesus which appears in all four Gospel accounts (Matthew 21:1-17; Mark 11:1-11; Luke 19:29-40; John 12:12-19).

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'Behold, your king is coming to you, humble and mounted on an ass..." This prophecy of Jesus' triumphant entry was made in the book of
explanation: Rejoice greatly, O Daughter of Zion! Shout in triumph, O Daughter of Jerusalem! See, your King comes to you, righteous and victorious, humble and riding on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey. (Zechariah 9:9)

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Matthew, And when you pray, do not use _______ as the heathen do.
explanation: According to the book of Matthew 6:7, Jesus instructed his disciples not to use 'vain repetitions' as the heathen do when they pray. The term 'vain repetitions' refers to the practice of repeating the same phrases in prayer without sincere or heartfelt meaning.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The "Lord's prayer" is encumbered in the book of
explanation: The 'Lord's Prayer' is found in the book of Matthew, specifically Matthew 6:9-13. It is a prayer that Jesus taught his disciples, providing a model for how they should pray.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet called the women of Samaria who oppressed the poor "cows of Bashan"?
explanation: In Amos 4:1, the prophet launches an attack upon a group described as "cows of Bashan on Mount Samaria." The traditional view identifies this group with the elite women of Samaria who are condemned for acts of oppression committed through the agency of their husbands.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Hosea's third child with Gomer whose name translates to "not my people" is
explanation: Hosea's third child was a son called Lo-ammi (Hos. 1:8-9). The name means "not my people." This was the most devastating name yet. The Lord was rejecting the people of Israel in their sinful state.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Before David took over Jerusalem, it was occupied by the
explanation: 2 Sam 5:6-10 David captures Jerusalem from the fiercely independent Canaanite tribe of Jebusites in c.1004BC. He moves his capital to Jerusalem and lives in the Fortress of Zion which he calls the City of David

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The commander-in-chief of Saul's army was
explanation: Abner was both the cousin of Israel's first king, Saul, and the commander of Saul's army (1 Samuel 14:50). He was respected by the king and was granted a place next to Saul at meals (1 Samuel 20:25).

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''If anyone will not work, let him not eat...'' Paul implies that
options: the lazy person must eat only a little
every person should toil and labour with his hands
explanation:
For even when we were with you, we gave you this rule: "If a man will not work, he shall not eat." We hear that some among you are idle. They are not busy; they are busybodies. Such people we command and urge in the Lord Jesus Christ to settle down and earn the bread they eat.
(2 Thessalonians 3:10-12)

topics.title: DIGNITY OF LABOUR : CONCEPT OF DIGNITY OF LABOUR

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Relgious reforms happened in Judah under the reign of King
explanation: King Josiah became the king of Judah at the age of eight. His reforms were carried out in the eighteenth year of his reign. His reforms brought about a reversal of the religious and political policies of his predecessor.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The reforms in Judah under King Josiah happened in the __________ of his reign.
explanation: The reforms in Judah under King Josiah happened in the eighteenth year of his reign. This is recorded in 2 Kings 22:3. The reforms included the repair of the temple, the discovery of the Book of the Law, and the subsequent religious reforms that Josiah implemented based on the laws found in the book.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The royal law that surrounds love in the bible is
options: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain.
You shall have no other God's before me.
explanation: In the Epistle of James, the Levitical law, "Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself", is called the "royal law". to perform.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus charged Christians to express love just as________.
options: He laid his life for the sake of mankind
explanation: In the First Epistle of John (4:19), the reflexive nature of this love is highlighted: "We love, because he first loved us", expressing the love of Christ as a mirroring of Christ's own love. Towards the end of the Last Supper, Jesus gives his disciples a new commandment: "Love one another, as I have loved you..."

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul's first missionary journey started from Antioch to
explanation: The author of Acts arranges Paul's travels into three separate journeys. The first journey, for which Paul and Barnabas were commissioned by the Antioch community, and led initially by Barnabas, took Barnabas and Paul from Antioch to Cyprus then into southern Asia Minor, and finally returning to Antioch.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul and Silas were imprisoned by the _____.
explanation: Paul and Silas were imprisoned by the Romans. This event is recorded in the book of Acts in the Bible. After Paul cast out a spirit of divination from a slave girl, her owners, who profited from her fortune-telling, seized Paul and Silas and brought them before the Roman authorities in the marketplace. The Roman magistrates ordered them to be stripped, beaten, and imprisoned.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : PERSECUTION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah! For this was not revealed to you by flesh and blood, but by My Father in heaven...." Jesus made this statement during
explanation: Jesus made the statement "Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah! For this was not revealed to you by flesh and blood, but by My Father in heaven..." during Peter's confession of Christ. This event is recorded in the Gospel of Matthew in the Bible. Peter confessed that Jesus was the Christ, the Son of the living God, and Jesus responded with the quoted statement, indicating that this truth was revealed to Peter not by human wisdom, but by divine revelation.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
'Take handfuls of ashes from the kiln and let Moses throw them toward heaven in the sight of Pharaoh'.

The resultant plague was
options: the death of all Egyptian flocks and cattle
the death of the first born of man and beast
boils and sores on man and beast
explanation:
Exodus 9:8-9
The Sixth Plague: Boils
Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, "Take handfuls of soot from the furnace; in the sight of Pharaoh, Moses is to toss it into the air. It will become fine dust over all the land of Egypt, and festering boils will break out on man and beast throughout the land."

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
'Take handfuls of ashes from the kiln and let Moses throw them toward heaven in the sight of Pharaoh'.
This was the ______ plague.
explanation:
Exodus 9:8-9
The Sixth Plague: Boils
Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, "Take handfuls of soot from the furnace; in the sight of Pharaoh, Moses is to toss it into the air. It will become fine dust over all the land of Egypt, and festering boils will break out on man and beast throughout the land."

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the punishment for Ananias and Sapphira?
explanation: As the narrative in Acts 5 goes, Ananias and his wife, Sapphira, sold a parcel of land and donated part of the sale proceeds to the Church; but, they conspired to lead the Apostle Peter and others to believe they donated the entire sum. For conspiring to lie as they did, they were struck dead.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : PROBLEMS OF COMMUNAL LIVING AND SOLUTIONS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Last Supper took place during the Passover on the first day of
explanation: The Last Supper, which was the final meal that Jesus shared with his apostles before his crucifixion, took place during the Passover. The Passover is a Jewish holiday that begins on the 15th of the Hebrew month of Nisan, which is the first day of the Festival of Unleavened Bread. Therefore, the correct answer is 'festival of unleavened bread.'

topics.title: THE LAST SUPPER : THE STORY OF THE LAST SUPPER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul was converted on his road to persecuting christians in the city of
explanation: Saul of Tarsus, a Pharisee in Jerusalem after the crucifixion and resurrection of Jesus Christ, swore to wipe out the new Christian church, called The Way. Acts 9:1 says that Paul was "breathing out murderous threats against the Lord's disciples." Saul obtained letters from the high priest, authorizing him to arrest any followers of Jesus in the city of Damascus.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The sun, the moon and the stars were created on the ____ day.
explanation:
Day 1: Light
Day 2: Atmosphere / Firmament
Day 3: Dry ground & plants
Day 4: Sun, moon & stars
Day 5: Birds & sea creatures
Day 6: Land animals & humans
Day 7: The Sabbath of res

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After he destroyed his family's alter of Baal, ______ was called Jerubbaal.
explanation: Gideon earned the title Jerubbaal (or Jerub-Baal) after he destroyed his family's altar of Baal. "Because Gideon broke down Baal's altar, they gave him the name Jerub-Baal that day, saying, 'Let Baal contend with him'" (Judges 6:32).

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus said Peter will deny him ______ times before the cock crows.
explanation: In the New Testament of the Bible, specifically in Matthew 26:34, Jesus predicts that Peter will deny him three times before the rooster crows. This prediction came to pass as Peter denied knowing Jesus three times after Jesus was arrested.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the gospel of Mark, the women that wanted to anoint Jesus in the tomb were?
options: Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome
Joanna, Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Jesus
Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and Salome
Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Mary the mother of Jesus
explanation:
Mark 16:1
When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, so that they might come and anoint Him.

topics.title: THE RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Afrer Saul was blinded on his way to persecute chritians in Damascus, he was healed by ____.
explanation: God healed Saul's blindness through Ananias laying on his hands. Saul had a vision of Ananias healing him. Ananias was told by God to go to Saul and heal him.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "With the stones he built an altar in the name of the Lord..." How many stones were there?
explanation: Elijah took twelve stones, one for each of the tribes descended from Jacob, to whom the word of the Lord had come, saying, "Your name shall be Israel." (1 Kings 18:31)

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How many prophets of Baal were present during the contest at Mount Carmel?
explanation: Now summon the people from all over Israel to meet me on Mount Carmel. And bring the four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal... (1 Kings 18:19)

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The story of Naaman and Gehazi as well as the story of Ahab and Naboth demonstrated God's hatred for
explanation: The story of Naaman and Gehazi in 2 Kings 5:20-27 shows Gehazi's covetousness for Naaman's wealth which he lied to obtain and was punished for it. Similarly, the story of Ahab and Naboth in 1 Kings 21:1-16 shows Ahab's covetousness for Naboth's vineyard which led to Naboth's death. Both stories demonstrate God's hatred for covetousness.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "...it is the gift of God, not of works, lest anyone should boast. For we are His workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand that we should walk in them." It here refers to
explanation: The quoted text is from Ephesians 2:8-10. The 'it' in the text refers to 'grace'. The text is saying that it is by grace, a gift from God, that we are saved and not by our works. This is so that no one can boast about saving themselves. We are God's workmanship, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's arrangement of spiritual/greater gifts, ________ is the third listed.
explanation: Now you are the body of Christ, and each of you is a member of it. And in the church God has appointed first of all apostles, second prophets, third teachers, then workers of miracles, and those with gifts of healing, helping, administration, and various tongues.(1 Corinthians 12:27-28)

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to St James, faith without ______ is dead.
explanation: James concluded his teaching on faith and works by saying that just as the body without the spirit is dead, likewise faith without works is dead.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : FRUITS OF JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: While the sign of covenant between God and Abraham is circumcision, the sign of covenant between God and Noah is ____.
explanation: This divine pledge, given unconditionally to Noah and to every living creature on earth, was accompanied by the sign of the rainbow.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE FLOOD AND GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The ________ believed in the resurrection of the dead and an afterlife while the __________ did not believe in either of these.
explanation: Pharisees believed in the resurrection of the dead and an afterlife. The Sadducees did not believe in either of these.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE THE HIGH PRIEST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ________ prophesied that the exiles from both Judah and Israel would return to Palestine.
explanation: Ezekiel prophesied about the judgment of Jerusalem and other nations in Ezekiel 1-32. In Chapter 33, a messenger came and gave the news that Jerusalem had been destroyed. Ezekiel then spends the rest of his prophecies in telling about the restoration of Israel in the future.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Thinking he can escape from God, Jonah flees to ______ where he intended to take a ship in the opposite direction of Nineveh.
explanation: Jonah feels about Nineveh that the city must inevitably fall because of God's judgment against it. Thus Jonah does not want to prophesy, because Nineveh might repent and thereby be saved. So he rushes down to Joppa and takes passage in a ship that will carry him in the opposite direction, thinking to escape God.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After the Israelites complained to Moses and Aaron, what did the Lord promise to feed the Israelites with in the morning?
explanation: In the evening the Lord will give you flesh to eat, and in the morning bread to the full(Exodus 16:8)

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: When Jesus was born in Bethlehem, who was the King of Judea?
explanation: Herod the Great was king of Judea when Jesus was born. Herod did not remain king for long. Within two years of Jesus' birth he was dead. His son did not last long as king either, as he was dismissed as king by Rome, and replaced by Roman governors or "procurators."

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : THE BIRTH AND BOYHOOD OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus' prophecy of Peter's denial was made during
explanation: All four Canonical Gospels state that during Jesus' Last Supper with his disciples, he predicted that Peter would deny knowledge of him, stating that Peter would disown him before the rooster crowed the next morning.

topics.title: THE LAST SUPPER : THE STORY OF THE LAST SUPPER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: __________ had Christ's body placed in his own tomb.
explanation: After Jesus' crucifixion, Joseph of Arimathea asked Pilate for Jesus' body (Mark 15:43). His request was granted, and Matthew 27:59-60 says,"Joseph took the body, wrapped it in a clean linen cloth, and placed it in his own new tomb that he had cut out of the rock. He rolled a big stone in front of the entrance to the tomb and went away."

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : CRUCIFIXION AND BURIAL OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Forty more days and Nineveh will be overturned." When Jonah passed this message to the people of Nineveh, what did they do?
options: They declared a fast and put on sack clothes
explanation: On the first day, Jonah started into the city. He proclaimed: "Forty more days and Nineveh will be overturned." The Ninevites believed God. They declared a fast, and all of them, from the greatest to the least, put on sackcloth. (Jonah 3:4-5)

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: King Saul disobeyed God by
explanation: He disobeyed God by failing to completely destroy the Amalekites and all their possessions, as God had commanded. A key part of the conditional covenant between God and Israel was obedience. Saul, as God's anointed king, was responsible for keeping that command.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE : DISOBEDIENCE AND CONSEQUENCES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The first commandment with an attached promise is
options: Thou shall not call the name of the Lord in vain
explanation: "Honor your father and mother" (this is the first commandment with a promise), "that it may go well with you and that you may live long in the land."

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's letter to the Romans, he who loves his neighbour has
explanation: Romans 13:8-10; "Owe no one anything, except to love each other, for the one who loves another has fulfilled the law. For the commandments, "You shall not commit adultery, You shall not murder, You shall not steal, You shall not covet," and any other commandment, are summed up in this word: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself." Love does no wrong to a neighbor; therefore love is the fulfilling of the law."

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'The word is near you, on your lips and in your heart...' Paul in his letter to the Romans was referring to the word of
explanation: But what does it say? "The word is near you; it is in your mouth and in your heart," that is, the word of faith we are proclaiming: that if you confess with your mouth, "Jesus is Lord," and believe in your heart that God raised Him from the dead, you will be saved....(Romans 10:8-9)

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Christians should keep away from any brother __________ according to Paul.
explanation: In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, we command you, brothers and sisters, to keep away from every believer who is idle and disruptive and does not live according to the teaching you received from us.(2 Thessalonians 3:6)

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: _______ deceitfully took 150 pounds of silver in two bags and two changes of clothes against the wish of his master.
explanation: (2 Kings 5:1) Elisha the prophet healed Naaman of his leprosy by the power of the Lord (verse 14), and Naaman praised God and offered Elisha a gift, which Elisha refused to accept (verse 16). Naaman departed for Syria, but Elisha's servant Gehazi ran after him and told a lie in order to get Naaman's gift for himself. In his lie, Gehazi invoked his master's name, making it appear as if Elisha wanted the gift after all: "My master sent me to say, 'Two young men from the company of the prophets have just come to me from the hill country of Ephraim. Please give them a talent of silver and two sets of clothing'" (verse 22). Naaman was only too glad to comply—he was happy to be able to give something out of gratitude for his healing—and he urged Gehazi to take twice as much silver as he had asked for.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In God's message to Nineveh, He was going to overthrow them in ______ days.
explanation: In God's message to Nineveh, He was going to overthrow them in forty days. This is recorded in the Bible in the book of Jonah 3:4. Jonah was a prophet sent by God to deliver this message of impending doom to the people of Nineveh due to their wickedness. However, when the people of Nineveh heard this, they repented of their sins and God had mercy on them and did not destroy the city.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Those who keep all the laws but fail at one are ________.
explanation: This statement is based on James 2:10 in the Bible, which says 'For whoever keeps the whole law and yet stumbles at just one point is guilty of breaking all of it.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'guilty of all'.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul, those who rebel against the authority will _______.
explanation: In Romans 13:2, Paul states 'Consequently, whoever rebels against the authority is rebelling against what God has instituted, and those who do so will bring judgment on themselves.' Therefore, the correct answer is 'bring judgement on themselves'.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The penultimate plague God put in Egypt before the Israelites were set free was
explanation: "Then the Lord said to Moses, 'Stretch out your hand toward heaven, that there may be darkness over the land of Egypt, darkness which may even be felt.' So Moses stretched out his hand toward heaven, and there was thick darkness in all the land of Egypt three days" (Exodus 10:21-22).

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Pharaoh's horsemen and his army overtook the fleeing Hebrews, encamped by the sea, at
explanation: The Egyptians--all Pharaoh's horses and chariots, horsemen and troops--pursued the Israelites and overtook them as they camped by the sea near Pi Hahiroth, opposite Baal Zephon.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ______,_____ and ______ were with Jesus during the transfiguration.
explanation:
Mark 9 vs 2
After six days Jesus took Peter, James and John with him and led them up a high mountain, where they were all alone. There he was transfigured before them.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the bible, _____ depicted good parenting.
explanation: Asa is considered a godly and good king. His son, Jehoshaphat, succeeded him and ruled for twenty-five years. Jehoshaphat was also a godly ruler, following in his father's footsteps and seeking the Lord

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ASA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The gospel of ______ began with the baptism of Jesus
explanation: The Gospel of Mark begins with the baptism of Jesus. This is unique to Mark's Gospel as the other Gospels include details about Jesus' birth and early life. Mark's Gospel starts with John the Baptist preparing the way for Jesus, and then immediately introduces Jesus as an adult being baptized by John in the Jordan River.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE MEANING AND PURPOSE OF THE BAPTISM OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''.....If it be so, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace.......'' Who ordered that the men be thrown into the furnace?
explanation: The person who ordered the men to be thrown into the furnace was King Nebuchadnezzar. This event is recorded in the Book of Daniel in the Bible. Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego refused to bow down to the golden image set up by King Nebuchadnezzar, and as a result, the king ordered them to be thrown into the burning fiery furnace.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's letter to the Romans, all who were baptized into Christ were also baptized into His
explanation:
Romans 6:3
Do you not know that all of us who have been baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death? We were buried therefore with him by baptism into death, in order that, just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul encouraged Philemon to receive Onesimus as ______.
explanation: Paul wrote to Philemon to encourage him to receive Onesimus back as a brother in the gospel without the severe punishments that would usually be inflicted on runaway slaves. Paul even offered to make up any financial loss Onesimus had caused Philemon to suffer.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall ___________.
explanation: The phrase 'Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God' is a part of the Beatitudes from the Sermon on the Mount in the New Testament of the Bible (Matthew 5:8). The Beatitudes are teachings by Jesus that describe the characteristics and actions that are rewarded with blessings. In this context, being 'pure in heart' refers to being morally and spiritually clean, and the reward for such purity is the ability to 'see God'.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul says that anyone who does acts of mercy should do so with
explanation: In Romans 12:8, Apostle Paul says 'if it is to encourage, then give encouragement; if it is giving, then give generously; if it is to lead, do it diligently; if it is to show mercy, do it cheerfully.' So, the acts of mercy should be done with cheerfulness, which can be interpreted as liberality. None of the other options (zeal, faith, generously) directly align with the biblical text.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's arrangement of spiritual gifts, _________ was the last item.
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 12:28, Paul lists the spiritual gifts in order of importance. The last item on the list is 'diversity of tongues' which refers to the ability to speak in different languages or tongues. This gift was considered the least of the gifts because it was only beneficial when there was someone who could interpret the language being spoken.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Paul's letter to the Philippians, Christ demonstrated His humility by
explanation: Philippians 2:5-11 tells us about the humility of Jesus. Jesus was GOD and yet, out of His great love for us, He chose to come to earth as a human.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Naboth refused to give up his vineyard to King Ahab because
options: it was his only source of income
explanation: 1 Kings 21:1-16 states that Naboth owned a vineyard, in proximity to King Ahab's palace in the city of Jezreel. Because of this, Ahab desired to acquire the vineyard so that he could use it for a vegetable (or herb) garden. Since he inherited the land from his ancestors, Naboth refused to sell it to Ahab.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: "Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard. Your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son" The son here refers to
explanation: The son referred to in the statement is John the Baptist. This statement was made by the angel Gabriel to Zechariah while he was serving in the temple, as recorded in Luke 1:13. The angel was announcing the miraculous birth of John the Baptist to Zechariah and his wife Elizabeth, who were both well advanced in years.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : JOHN, THE FORERUNNER OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Paul's Epistle to the Galatians, as long as an heir is a child, he is under
explanation:
Galatians 4.
What I am saying is that as long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a slave, although he owns the whole estate. He is subject to guardians and trustees until the time set by his father.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After his resurrection,Jesus first appeared to
explanation:
Mark 16:9-11
Jesus appears to Mary Magdalene, who is now described as someone whom Jesus healed from possession by seven demons. She then "tells the other disciples" what she saw, but no one believes her.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: 'But let justice roll down like waters and _______like an overflowing stream'
explanation: The quote 'But let justice roll down like waters and righteousness like an overflowing stream' is from the Bible, specifically Amos 5:24. The correct word to fill in the blank is 'righteousness'.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to James, the man who prays to God and at the same time doubts is
explanation: According to James 1:6-8 in the Bible, the man who prays to God and at the same time doubts is described as 'double-minded'. The scripture says 'But when you ask, you must believe and not doubt, because the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind. That person should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Such a person is double-minded and unstable in all they do.'

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Paul counseled the Corinthian Saints to resolve disputes among themselves with
explanation: In the Bible, specifically in 1 Corinthians 6:1-6, Paul advises the Corinthians to settle disputes among themselves with righteous judgement, rather than taking their issues to secular courts. This is because he believes that the wisdom of the Holy Spirit, which is in believers, is superior to the wisdom of the world.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD CITIZENSHIP.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The promise of sending the Holy Spirit(Pentecost) was fulfilled in
explanation: The promise of sending the Holy Spirit, also known as Pentecost, was fulfilled in Jerusalem. This event is recorded in the Bible in the book of Acts 2:1-4. On the day of Pentecost, the disciples were gathered in one place in Jerusalem when the Holy Spirit descended on them.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was responsible for the death of Uriah?
explanation: In the morning David wrote a letter to Joab and sent it by the hand of Uriah. In the letter he wrote, "Set Uriah in the forefront of the hardest fighting, and then draw back from him, that he may be struck down, and die.(2 Samuel 11:14-15)

topics.title: A MAN AFER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: ''He who hears you hears me, and he who rejects you rejects me .....'' This was addressed to
options: the 12 whom He sent out to preach
the 70 whom He sent out to preach
the disciples during the Sermon onthe Mount
explanation: The statement 'He who hears you hears me, and he who rejects you rejects me' was addressed to the 70 whom Jesus sent out to preach. This can be found in the Bible, Luke 10:16. Therefore, the correct answer is 'the 70 whom He sent out to preach'.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus asked his disciples not to disclose their vision of the transfiguration until his
explanation: As Jesus came down from the Mount of Transfiguration with Peter, James, and John, He told His three disciples not to disclose the wonderful vision they had seen there, until after He had risen from the dead.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE TRANSFIGURATION TO THE DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q:
"Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!"
When was this statement made?
explanation: The statement "Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!" was made during the triumphant entry of Jesus into Jerusalem. This event is often referred to as Palm Sunday, marking the beginning of the Holy Week leading up to Jesus' crucifixion and resurrection. Therefore, the correct answer is 'The triumphant entry of Jesus'.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who accompanied Paul on his First Missionary journey?
explanation: The first missionary journey began in about 45 A.D. From Antioch, Barnabas and Saul traveled about sixteen miles to the coast, to the port at Seleucia Pieria.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Lord told Solomon his kingdom would be divided after his death because
explanation: In the Old Testament of the Bible, in 1 Kings 11:9-13, it is stated that the Lord was angry with Solomon because his heart had turned away from the Lord, the God of Israel, who had appeared to him twice. He had followed other gods and had not kept the Lord's command. Therefore, the Lord told him that He would tear the kingdom away from him and give it to one of his subordinates, but for the sake of David, He would not do it during Solomon's lifetime.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet warned the people of Nineveh about God's impending judgment?
explanation: The prophet Jonah was the one who warned the people of Nineveh about God's impending judgment. This is recorded in the Book of Jonah in the Bible.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet warned the Israelites about the consequences of their disobedience?
explanation: The prophet Hosea warned the Israelites about the consequences of their disobedience. This is recorded in the Book of Hosea in the Bible.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What significant event led to the division of languages among humankind?
explanation: The significant event that led to the division of languages among humankind was the Tower of Babel. This is recorded in Genesis 11:1-9 in the Bible.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Leadership qualities such as integrity and honesty are illustrated in the life of
explanation: Leadership qualities such as integrity and honesty are illustrated in the life of Joseph. This is evident in his life story as recorded in the Book of Genesis in the Bible.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The downfall of the Northern Kingdom of Israel was mainly due to
explanation: The downfall of the Northern Kingdom of Israel was mainly due to the rejection of the Mosaic Law. This led to moral decay and spiritual decline, which eventually led to their downfall.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The story of Gideon showcases leadership qualities like humility and reliance on divine guidance during
explanation: The story of Gideon is found in the book of Judges in the Bible. Gideon was a judge who led the Israelites to victory over the Midianites. His story is characterized by his humility and reliance on divine guidance, which were key to his leadership during the period of Judges. The other options refer to different periods or events in the Bible.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Gospel accounts, what was the first miracle performed by Jesus?
explanation: According to the Gospel of John (John 2:1-11), the first miracle performed by Jesus was turning water into wine at a wedding in Cana. The other options refer to other miracles performed by Jesus, but they were not his first.

topics.title: MIRACLES : NATURE MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Gospel of Matthew, Jesus walks on water to meet His disciples in a boat. Which disciple attempts to walk on water but begins to sink?
explanation: In the Gospel of Matthew (Matthew 14:22-33), Peter is the disciple who attempts to walk on water to meet Jesus but begins to sink because of his doubt. The other options refer to other disciples who did not attempt to walk on water in this account.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The new covenant is based on
options: Faith in Jesus Christ and His sacrifice
explanation: The new covenant, as described in the New Testament of the Bible, is based on faith in Jesus Christ and His sacrifice. This is a shift from the old covenant, which was based on adherence to the Law (the Ten Commandments and other laws). The other options refer to aspects of the old covenant or to human efforts, which are not the basis of the new covenant.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the book of Acts, which event symbolizes the inclusion of the Gentiles into the new covenant?
explanation: In the book of Acts, the baptism of Cornelius, a Gentile, symbolizes the inclusion of the Gentiles into the new covenant (Acts 10). The other options refer to other significant events in the book of Acts, but they do not symbolize the inclusion of the Gentiles into the new covenant.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF CORNELIUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
