q: How did the early Christian community in Acts make decisions about the distribution of resources to the needy?
options: They left it to chance and luck
They relied on the guidance of local rulers
They appointed deacons to oversee the process
They gave everything to the apostles for distribution
explanation: In the book of Acts, the early Christian community appointed deacons to oversee the distribution of resources to the needy. This was done to ensure fairness and to allow the apostles to focus on prayer and the ministry of the word (Acts 6:1-4).

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What decision did Pilate's wife make when she had a dream about Jesus?
options: She warned Pilate not to get involved
She urged Pilate to release Jesus immediately
She sought advice from the chief priests
explanation: In Matthew 27:19, Pilate's wife sent him a message warning him not to have anything to do with Jesus because she had suffered a great deal in a dream because of him. This shows that she warned Pilate not to get involved with Jesus.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : RELIANCE ON A MEDIUM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Apostle Paul's life in Acts exemplifies God's sovereignty through
options: His ability to convert everyone he encountered
His escape from imprisonment through supernatural means
His transformation from a persecutor to a proclaimer of the Gospel
explanation: The Apostle Paul's life in Acts exemplifies God's sovereignty through his transformation from a persecutor to a proclaimer of the Gospel. Before his conversion, Paul (then known as Saul) was a zealous persecutor of Christians. However, after his encounter with Jesus on the road to Damascus, he became one of the most fervent proclaimers of the Gospel (Acts 9:1-22).

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How do the Gospels portray God's sovereignty concerning human salvation?
options: It depends on human effort and achievements
It is exclusively determined by human choices
It is based on God's grace and mercy
It is only accessible to a select few
explanation: The Gospels portray God's sovereignty concerning human salvation as being based on God's grace and mercy. This is evident in passages such as Ephesians 2:8-9, which states that it is by grace through faith that we are saved, not by works, so that no one can boast. This shows that salvation is a gift from God, not something that can be earned through human effort or achievements.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Book of Lamentations is traditionally attributed to which prophet and describes the sorrow and mourning over the fall of Jerusalem?
explanation: The Book of Lamentations is traditionally attributed to the prophet Jeremiah. It describes the sorrow and mourning over the fall of Jerusalem in 586 B.C. by the Babylonians. Jeremiah, often referred to as the 'weeping prophet', laments over the destruction of Jerusalem and the Temple, expressing the pain and confusion of the people.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet is associated with the "valley of dry bones" vision, symbolizing the restoration of Israel from exile?
explanation: The prophet associated with the "valley of dry bones" vision is Ezekiel. In the book of Ezekiel, chapter 37, Ezekiel has a vision where he is set in the middle of a valley full of dry bones. He prophesies to these bones that they will hear the word of the Lord, and the Lord will cause breath to enter the bones and they will come to life, symbolizing the restoration of Israel from exile.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prophet who spoke extensively about the restoration of Israel and the coming of the Messiah was
explanation: The prophet Malachi spoke extensively about the restoration of Israel and the coming of the Messiah. In the book of Malachi, he prophesies about the coming of the 'messenger' who will prepare the way for the Lord. This is interpreted as a prophecy about the coming of John the Baptist and Jesus Christ.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet confronted the people with the challenge of rebuilding the temple after the exile?
explanation: The prophet Haggai confronted the people with the challenge of rebuilding the temple after the exile. In the book of Haggai, the prophet encourages the people to rebuild the temple, promising that God's glory will fill the new temple and bring prosperity.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Babylonian Exile was a consequence of
options: The Israelites' refusal to pay tribute to Babylon
The division of the kingdom into Israel and Judah
The Assyrian invasion and destruction of Jerusalem
The Israelites' disobedience to God and their social injustices
explanation: The Babylonian Exile was a consequence of the Israelites' disobedience to God and their social injustices. The Bible records that the Israelites had turned away from God and were worshipping idols, and their society was marked by injustice and oppression. God allowed the Babylonians to conquer them as a punishment for their sins.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The prophet who emphasized the concept of "the day of the Lord" as a time of judgment and salvation was
explanation: The prophet Joel emphasized the concept of "the day of the Lord" as a time of judgment and salvation. In the book of Joel, the 'day of the Lord' is described as a time when God will judge His enemies and save His people.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet had a vision of a "wheel within a wheel," symbolizing God's divine presence and sovereignty?
explanation: The prophet Ezekiel had a vision of a "wheel within a wheel," which is recorded in the Book of Ezekiel in the Bible. This vision symbolizes God's divine presence and sovereignty, showing that God is omnipresent and has control over all things.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet confronted King Ahab and challenged the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
explanation: The prophet Elijah confronted King Ahab and challenged the prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel. This is recorded in the Book of Kings in the Bible. Elijah's challenge was a demonstration of God's power over the false gods of Baal.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Nahum's prophecy mainly focused on the impending doom of
explanation: Nahum's prophecy mainly focused on the impending doom of Nineveh. The Book of Nahum in the Bible is essentially an oracle of judgment against the city of Nineveh, which was the capital of the Assyrian Empire.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Setting boundaries and rules for children helps them
options: Develop a sense of discipline and responsibility.
explanation: Setting boundaries and rules for children helps them develop a sense of discipline and responsibility. Boundaries provide a safe space for children to explore and understand their environment, while rules guide their behavior and actions, teaching them what is acceptable and what is not. This helps inculcate a sense of discipline and responsibility in them.

topics.title: PARENTAL RESPONSIBILITY : ASA

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What event in the Old Testament marked the beginning of the Exodus of the Israelites from Egypt, eventually leading to the Ten Commandments?
options: Building of the Ark of the Covenant
explanation: The event in the Old Testament that marked the beginning of the Exodus of the Israelites from Egypt, eventually leading to the Ten Commandments, was the Passover. During the Passover, God spared the Israelites from the final plague in Egypt - the death of the firstborn - and this led to Pharaoh releasing the Israelites from slavery, marking the beginning of the Exodus.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The law was given to humanity primarily to
explanation: The law was given to humanity primarily to guide them in righteous living. The law provides a moral and ethical framework for humans to follow, which helps them distinguish between right and wrong and guides their actions and decisions. It does not save them from sin or establish a covenant with God, but it does bring condemnation when it is broken.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In a diverse and multicultural society, civic responsibility includes
options: Isolating oneself from people of different backgrounds
explanation: In a diverse and multicultural society, civic responsibility includes embracing diversity and promoting inclusivity. This means respecting and valuing the differences among people, including their backgrounds, cultures, and beliefs, and promoting an inclusive society where everyone feels valued and accepted. It does not involve isolating oneself from people of different backgrounds, promoting stereotypes and prejudice, or disrespecting other cultures and beliefs.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD CITIZENSHIP.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which epistle does the apostle Paul extensively discuss the concept of "New Life in Christ"?
explanation: The apostle Paul extensively discusses the concept of 'New Life in Christ' in the Epistle to the Ephesians. This concept refers to the transformation that occurs in a person's life when they accept Jesus Christ as their Lord and Savior. It does not feature prominently in the Revelation, Acts of the Apostles, or Corinthians.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Apostle Peter, what will happen to the heavens and the earth on the day of the Lord's return?
options: They will merge into a new dimension
explanation: According to the Apostle Peter, the heavens and the earth will be destroyed by fire on the day of the Lord's return. This is mentioned in 2 Peter 3:10, where Peter describes the day of the Lord as a time when the heavens will disappear with a roar, the elements will be destroyed by fire, and the earth and everything in it will be laid bare.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which event marked the beginning of the Early Church's fellowship?
explanation: The event that marked the beginning of the Early Church's fellowship was Pentecost. This is when the Holy Spirit descended upon the apostles, as recorded in Acts 2. The resurrection of Jesus, the crucifixion of Jesus, and the Last Supper were all significant events, but they did not mark the beginning of the church's fellowship.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which city served as a central hub for the Early Church's fellowship and growth?
explanation: Jerusalem served as a central hub for the Early Church's fellowship and growth. After the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ, the apostles stayed in Jerusalem where they preached and converted many to Christianity.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During the trial before Pontius Pilate, which Roman custom allowed the release of one prisoner during Passover?
explanation: It was a period of Peace. This is the accurate answer.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE PILATE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Gospels, what was the profession of Peter and Andrew before becoming disciples of Jesus?
explanation: Peter and Andrew were fishermen before they became disciples of Jesus. This is stated in the Gospels, where Jesus tells them to follow him and he will make them 'fishers of men'.

topics.title: DISCIPLESHIP : THE CALL OF THE FIRST DISCIPLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Gospel of Matthew, which group of religious leaders were particularly hostile towards Jesus?
explanation: The Pharisees were particularly hostile towards Jesus, according to the Gospel of Matthew. They often challenged Jesus' authority and teachings, leading to many confrontations.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Gospel emphasizes the role of the Holy Spirit as the Comforter or Advocate?
explanation: The Gospel of John emphasizes the role of the Holy Spirit as the Comforter or Advocate. In John 14:16, Jesus promises to ask the Father to give another Advocate to help and be with the disciples forever.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus often referred to Himself as the "Good Shepherd." Which Gospel contains this metaphor?
explanation: The metaphor of Jesus referring to Himself as the 'Good Shepherd' is found in the Gospel of John, specifically in John 10:11 and 10:14. In these verses, Jesus uses the metaphor to illustrate His care and sacrifice for His followers.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE GOOD SHEPHERD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which book of the New Testament do we find the account of the Apostle Paul's conversion and subsequent missionary journeys?
explanation: The account of the Apostle Paul's conversion and subsequent missionary journeys is found in the 'Acts of the Apostles'. The book of Acts, written by Luke, provides a detailed account of Paul's dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus and his subsequent missionary journeys.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which Gospel do we find the Parable of the Good Samaritan?
explanation: The Parable of the Good Samaritan is found in the Gospel of Luke, specifically in Luke 10:25-37. In this parable, Jesus uses a Samaritan, considered an outsider by the Jews, as an example of a neighbor who showed mercy to a man who was robbed and beaten.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Gospel gives the most detailed account of Jesus' temptation?
explanation: The Gospel of Matthew gives the most detailed account of Jesus' temptation. In Matthew 4:1-11, the writer provides a detailed narrative of how Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who were the three disciples who witnessed the Transfiguration with Jesus?
explanation: The three disciples who witnessed the Transfiguration with Jesus were Peter, James, and John. This event is recorded in all three Synoptic Gospels (Matthew 17:1-9, Mark 9:2-8, Luke 9:28-36).

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which epistle emphasizes the importance of love and unity among believers in the Christian community?
explanation: The Epistle to the Ephesians emphasizes the importance of love and unity among believers in the Christian community. It is a letter written by the Apostle Paul to the church in Ephesus, and it focuses on the themes of unity in the body of Christ, the importance of love, and the spiritual growth of believers.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AMONG CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which epistle addresses the issue of moral purity and living a sanctified life within the Christian community?
explanation: The First Epistle to Timothy addresses the issue of moral purity and living a sanctified life within the Christian community. In this letter, Paul provides guidance to Timothy, a young leader in the church, on how to conduct himself and lead the church in a manner that is pleasing to God. This includes instructions on moral purity and living a sanctified life.

topics.title: SEXUAL IMMORALITY : ADULTERY AND FORNICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the epistles, what is the root cause of showing partiality to others?
options: Fear of being judged by others
Favoring those who can offer personal benefits
explanation: According to the epistles, the root cause of showing partiality to others is favoring those who can offer personal benefits. This is a form of selfishness and is contrary to the teachings of the Bible, which encourages believers to treat everyone equally and without favoritism.

topics.title: IMPARTIALITY : CAUSES AND CONSEQUENCES OF IMPARTIALITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which virtue is often mentioned alongside impartiality in the selected epistles?
explanation: Humility is often mentioned alongside impartiality in the selected epistles. Humility is a virtue that involves recognizing one's own limitations and placing the needs of others before one's own. It is closely related to impartiality, as a humble person does not show favoritism or partiality.

topics.title: HUMILITY : MEANING AND REQUIREMENTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What is the key to effective prayer according to the selected epistles?
explanation: According to the selected epistles, the key to effective prayer is faith. This is based on the belief that God hears and answers prayers, and that He is able to do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or think, according to the power that works in us. Therefore, when praying, one must have faith that God will answer in His own time and way.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the context of prayer, what does "intercession" mean?
explanation: Intercession in the context of prayer refers to the act of intervening on behalf of another through prayer. This is often done by praying for others, making requests to God on their behalf. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Praying for others'.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which epistle contains the famous analogy of the Church as the "body of Christ" and the members as individual parts?
explanation: The analogy of the Church as the 'body of Christ' and the members as individual parts is found in the epistle of 1 Corinthians, specifically in 1 Corinthians 12:12-27. Therefore, the correct answer is '1 Corinthians'.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In 1 Corinthians 12, Paul compares the Church to what metaphorical body?
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 12, Paul uses the metaphor of a human body to describe the Church. He explains that just as a body, though one, has many parts, but all its many parts form one body, so it is with Christ. Therefore, the correct answer is 'A human body'.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the epistles, corruption is often linked to which of the following vices?
explanation: In the epistles, corruption is often linked to vices such as greed and selfishness. These vices are seen as moral corruption and are often warned against in the teachings of the apostles. Therefore, the correct answer is 'Greed and selfishness'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which epistle does the apostle Peter remind believers that they are part of a "chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation" while facing persecution?
explanation: The apostle Peter reminds believers that they are part of a 'chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation' in the face of persecution in the epistle of 1 Peter, specifically in 1 Peter 2:9. Therefore, the correct answer is '1 Peter'.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Epistle of Romans is significant in discussing the contrast between law and grace. Who is the author of the Epistle to the Romans?
explanation: The Epistle to the Romans, often shortened to Romans, is the sixth book in the New Testament. Biblical scholars agree that it was composed by the Apostle Paul to explain that salvation is offered through the gospel of Jesus Christ. Hence, Paul is the author of the Epistle to the Romans.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which epistle encourages believers to "eagerly desire spiritual gifts, especially the gift of prophecy"?
explanation: The quote 'eagerly desire spiritual gifts, especially the gift of prophecy' is from 1 Corinthians 14:1. Therefore, the epistle that encourages believers to eagerly desire spiritual gifts, especially the gift of prophecy, is 1 Corinthians.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What is a prominent theme in St. James' Epistles?
explanation: The Epistle of James emphasizes practical aspects of Christian conduct. It stresses the ethical and moral responsibilities of Christians in dealing with social issues. The prominent theme in St. James' Epistles is 'Faith and works'. James 2:14-26, in particular, emphasizes that faith without works is dead.

topics.title: FORGIVENESS : PAUL’S TEACHING ON FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which Gospel(s) do we find an account of the triumphal entry of Jesus into Jerusalem?
explanation: The triumphal entry of Jesus into Jerusalem is an event that is described in all four canonical Gospels. In Matthew 21:1–11, Mark 11:1–11, Luke 19:28–44, and John 12:12–19.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Why did the Pharisees criticize Jesus during the triumphal entry?
options: They believed Jesus was claiming to be the Messiah.
They thought Jesus was a Roman sympathizer.
They were upset about the disruption caused by the crowd.
explanation: During the triumphal entry, the Pharisees criticized Jesus because they believed He was claiming to be the Messiah. This is evident in Luke 19:39 where the Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, 'Teacher, rebuke your disciples!' They were upset that the crowd was proclaiming Jesus as the Messiah.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which Gospel do we find the story of the "Good Samaritan" who showed love and compassion to a wounded man?
explanation: The story of the 'Good Samaritan' is found in the Gospel of Luke, specifically in Luke 10:25-37. In this parable, Jesus uses the example of a Samaritan, who was considered an outsider, to illustrate the concept of 'neighbor' and to demonstrate that love and compassion should extend beyond social or religious boundaries.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Gospel of Mark, which group of people did Jesus specifically mention that we should love with all our hearts?
explanation: In the Gospel of Mark, Jesus specifically mentions that we should love our enemies. This is found in Mark 12:30-31, where Jesus states the greatest commandment is to love God with all our heart, soul, mind, and strength, and the second is to love our neighbor as ourselves. In the context of the New Testament, 'neighbor' includes enemies.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the first Gentile to receive the Holy Spirit and be baptized, as mentioned in Acts?
explanation: The first Gentile to receive the Holy Spirit and be baptized, as mentioned in the book of Acts, was Cornelius. This event is recorded in Acts 10, where Peter is shown in a vision that the Gospel is for all people, not just the Jews, leading to the baptism of Cornelius and his household.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF CORNELIUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Acts, what event caused the dispersion of Christians and led to the spread of the Gospel among Gentiles?
explanation: The event that caused the dispersion of Christians and led to the spread of the Gospel among Gentiles was the stoning of Stephen. This event is recorded in Acts 7:54-60 and Acts 8:1. After Stephen's death, a great persecution broke out against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were scattered throughout Judea and Samaria.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where did Peter make the "Great Confession"?
explanation: Peter made the 'Great Confession' near the Sea of Galilee. This event is recorded in Matthew 16:13-20, Mark 8:27-30, and Luke 9:18-21. Peter confesses that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of the living God, a pivotal moment in the Gospels.

topics.title: THE GREAT CONFESSION : THE OCCASION OF THE GREAT CONFESSION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Gospel includes the account of Jesus washing the disciples' feet during the Last Supper?
explanation: The account of Jesus washing the disciples' feet during the Last Supper is found in the Gospel of John, specifically John 13:1-17. This act was a demonstration of humility and service, setting an example for his disciples to follow.

topics.title: THE LAST SUPPER : THE STORY OF THE LAST SUPPER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the name of the high priest who played a prominent role in opposing the early Christian movement?
explanation: Caiaphas was the high priest who played a prominent role in opposing the early Christian movement. He was the one who plotted to kill Jesus after the raising of Lazarus, as mentioned in John 11:49-50. He also presided over the Sanhedrin trial of Jesus.

topics.title: OPPOSITION TO THE GOSPEL MESSAGE : THE ARREST AND IMPRISONMENT OF PETER AND JOHN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which apostle famously doubted Jesus' resurrection until he saw the wounds in His hands and side?
explanation: The apostle who famously doubted Jesus' resurrection until he saw the wounds in His hands and side was Thomas. This event is recorded in John 20:24-29, where Jesus appeared to his disciples after his resurrection, but Thomas was not present. When the others told him about it, he refused to believe until he could see and touch Jesus' wounds. When Jesus appeared again, Thomas was present and believed.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which Gospel does Jesus say, "Before Abraham was, I am"?
explanation: The statement, "Before Abraham was, I am" is found in the Gospel of John, specifically John 8:58. Here, Jesus is declaring His eternal existence and His identity as God. The phrase 'I am' is a direct reference to God's name in Exodus 3:14, where God revealed Himself to Moses as 'I AM WHO I AM'.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE LIGHT OF THE WORLD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David was known for his musical talents and was credited for composing many
explanation: David was known for his musical talents and was credited for composing many Psalms. The Book of Psalms in the Bible contains many songs and poems attributed to David. These psalms express a range of human emotions and were often used as hymns in Jewish and Christian worship.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: David's skill in playing the harp made him a favorite musician in the court of which king?
explanation: David's skill in playing the harp made him a favorite musician in the court of King Saul. This is documented in the Bible, specifically in the book of 1 Samuel 16:23. Whenever King Saul was troubled by an evil spirit, David would play the harp and the king would feel better.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: THE EARLY LIFE OF DAVID

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What significant event led to the division of the kingdom of Israel into the northern and southern kingdoms?
explanation: The significant event that led to the division of the kingdom of Israel into the northern and southern kingdoms was the reign of King Solomon. After Solomon's death, his son Rehoboam became king. Rehoboam's harsh rule led to a rebellion, resulting in the division of the kingdom into the northern kingdom of Israel and the southern kingdom of Judah.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The southern kingdom of Judah experienced a significant religious reform under the leadership of which righteous king?
explanation: The southern kingdom of Judah experienced a significant religious reform under the leadership of King Hezekiah. Hezekiah is known for his efforts to abolish idolatry in his kingdom, as documented in the Bible in the books of 2 Kings 18-20 and 2 Chronicles 29-32.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : RENEWAL OF THE COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The period of the "Return from Exile" is associated with which Persian King's decree?
explanation: The period of the 'Return from Exile' is associated with King Cyrus's decree. According to the Bible, specifically the book of Ezra, King Cyrus of Persia issued a decree that allowed the Jews to return to Jerusalem and rebuild the temple.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the first king of the divided kingdom of Israel?
explanation: The first king of the divided kingdom of Israel was Jeroboam. After the death of King Solomon, the kingdom of Israel was divided into two: the northern kingdom of Israel and the southern kingdom of Judah. Jeroboam became the king of the northern kingdom.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the prophet that warned Israel about the impending Babylonian exile?
explanation: The prophet Jeremiah warned Israel about the impending Babylonian exile. Jeremiah prophesied during the last forty years of Judah, the southern kingdom, warning God's people of their coming fate if they did not turn from their sins.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet from the Old Testament strongly condemned the greed of the wealthy and powerful?
explanation: The prophet Amos strongly condemned the greed of the wealthy and powerful. Amos was a shepherd and a farmer before God called him to be a prophet. He was an outsider from Judah who was called to deliver a message to Israel. His message was one of justice and concern for the poor and oppressed.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : AHAB

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the central theme of Ezekiel's prophecies during the exile?
options: The promise of a new messianic king
The restoration of the temple in Jerusalem
God's judgment and the hope of restoration
explanation: The central theme of Ezekiel's prophecies during the exile was God's judgment and the hope of restoration. Ezekiel was a prophet during the time of the Babylonian exile. He prophesied about the destruction of Jerusalem and the temple, but he also spoke of a future time when God would restore His people and their place of worship.

topics.title: PUNISHMENT AND HOPE : THE CONDITIONS FOR HOPE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The message of repentance and forgiveness was a central theme in the prophecies directed to which city?
explanation: The message of repentance and forgiveness was a central theme in the prophecies directed to Nineveh. This is evident in the book of Jonah, where Jonah was sent by God to warn the people of Nineveh of their impending destruction unless they repented of their sins.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Divine love in Christianity is primarily expressed through
explanation: Divine love in Christianity is primarily expressed through God's grace and mercy. This is evident in the Christian belief that God sent His Son, Jesus Christ, to die for the sins of humanity, offering forgiveness and eternal life to all who believe in Him. This act of sacrifice is seen as the ultimate expression of God's love, grace, and mercy.

topics.title: LOVE : GOD'S LOVE FOR MAN

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: True religion in Christianity emphasizes the importance of
explanation: True religion in Christianity emphasizes the importance of unity among believers. This is based on the teachings of Jesus Christ who prayed for unity among his followers in John 17:21. The other options do not accurately represent the emphasis of true religion in Christianity.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which biblical passage is known as the "Lord's Prayer" and contains a model of prayer and guidance?
explanation: The biblical passage known as the 'Lord's Prayer' is found in Matthew 6:9-13. This prayer was taught by Jesus to his disciples as a model for how they should pray. It contains petitions for daily needs, forgiveness, and deliverance from temptation.

topics.title: EFFECTIVE PRAYER : THE REQUIREMENTS OF EFFECTIVE PRAYER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How many books are there in the New Testament of the Bible?
explanation: There are 27 books in the New Testament of the Bible. These books include the four Gospels, the Acts of the Apostles, the Epistles, and the book of Revelation.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Bible, how many days after his resurrection did Jesus ascend into heaven?
explanation: According to the Bible, Jesus ascended into heaven 40 days after his resurrection. This is recorded in Acts 1:3 where it is stated that Jesus presented himself alive to his disciples by many proofs, appearing to them during forty days and speaking about the kingdom of God.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE ASCENSION OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How many recorded appearances did Jesus make after his resurrection?
explanation: Jesus made 7 recorded appearances after his resurrection. These appearances were to Mary Magdalene, the other women, two disciples on the road to Emmaus, Peter, the ten disciples (Thomas was absent), the eleven disciples (Thomas was present), and the seven disciples by the Sea of Tiberias.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the primary consequence of the Israelites' worship of the Golden Calf?
options: The breaking of the covenant with God
explanation: The primary consequence of the Israelites' worship of the Golden Calf was the breaking of the covenant with God. This is evident in the biblical narrative where the act of idolatry led to a breach in the relationship between God and the Israelites, symbolized by the breaking of the covenant.

topics.title: OBEDIENCE AND DISOBEDIENCE :EXAMPLES OF ADAM, COLLECTION OF MANNA, THE GOLDEN CALF, MOSES AND SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Book of Job, what is highlighted as evidence of God's control over the universe?
explanation: In the Book of Job, evidence of God's control over the universe is highlighted through the response to Job's suffering. The narrative demonstrates God's sovereignty and ultimate authority by showing how He responds to Job's trials and challenges, ultimately revealing His divine plan and wisdom.

topics.title: THE SOVEREIGNTY OF GOD :GOD AS CREATOR AND CONTROLLER OF THE UNIVERSE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which event in David's life demonstrates his heart for God despite his flaws?
options: His repentance after the affair with Bathsheba
explanation: The event in David's life that demonstrates his heart for God despite his flaws is his repentance after the affair with Bathsheba. David's genuine repentance and acknowledgment of his wrongdoing showcase his humility, faith, and desire to seek forgiveness and restoration with God.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What lesson did Jonah learn from the incident with the vine that God provided and then withered?
options: The extent of God's compassion for all people
explanation: Jonah learned from the incident with the vine that God provided and then withered the extent of God's compassion for all people. The story of Jonah illustrates God's mercy and compassion even towards those Jonah initially deemed unworthy, emphasizing the universal nature of God's love and grace.

topics.title: GOD'S MESSAGE TO NINEVEH: JONAH AND HIS MESSAGE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet's ministry primarily addressed the social and religious decay in Judah before the Babylonian exile?
explanation: The prophet whose ministry primarily addressed the social and religious decay in Judah before the Babylonian exile was Jeremiah. Jeremiah's prophecies focused on the moral and spiritual corruption of the people, warning them of the impending judgment due to their disobedience and idolatry.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : CONDITION OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Galatians 2:16, what does Paul say about seeking justification through the Law?
options: It is a misunderstanding of the Gospel
It is a temporary measure until faith is fully realized
It is insufficient because justification comes through faith
explanation: In Galatians 2:16, Paul states that seeking justification through the Law is insufficient because justification comes through faith. This aligns with the core message of Paul's teachings on salvation by grace through faith.

topics.title: JUSTIFICATION BY FAITH : MEANING AND BASIC CONDITIONS FOR JUSTIFICATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did King Darius react after Daniel's miraculous survival in the lion's den?
options: He punished Daniel for defying the edict
He ordered a new edict that acknowledged the power of Daniel's God
He executed the conspirators who had framed Daniel
explanation: After Daniel's miraculous survival in the lion's den, King Darius acknowledged the power of Daniel's God by ordering a new edict. This action shows that King Darius recognized the supremacy of Daniel's God.

topics.title: FAITH, COURAGE AND PROTECTION : EXAMPLES OF DANIEL, SHADRACH, MESHACH AND ABEDNEGO

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What does the parable of the Good Samaritan (Luke 10:25-37) teach about the nature of Christian giving?
options: Giving should be done only to those who can reciprocate
Giving should be limited to those within one's community
Genuine giving involves compassion and helping those in need regardless of their background
Giving is not necessary if one has already fulfilled religious duties
explanation: The parable of the Good Samaritan teaches that genuine giving involves compassion and helping those in need regardless of their background. It emphasizes the importance of showing mercy and kindness to all, reflecting the true nature of Christian giving.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the primary vision that Ezekiel experienced at the beginning of his prophetic ministry?
options: A vision of the valley of dry bones
A vision of God's throne chariot with four living creatures
A vision of the heavenly host singing
explanation: At the beginning of his prophetic ministry, Ezekiel experienced a vision of God's throne chariot with four living creatures. This vision is significant as it symbolizes the divine presence and authority of God in Ezekiel's calling as a prophet.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF EZEKIEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Old Testament prophecy does the Triumphal Entry of Jesus fulfill?
explanation: The Triumphal Entry of Jesus fulfills the Old Testament prophecy found in Zechariah 9:9. This prophecy foretold the coming of a king who would bring salvation and peace, which aligns with the events of Jesus' entry into Jerusalem.

topics.title: THE TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE : TRIUMPHAL ENTRY AND THE CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Revelation 6, which event is described as one of the signs of the end times?
options: The falling of the stars from the sky
explanation: The correct answer is - The opening of the seven seals. In Revelation 6, the opening of the seven seals is described as one of the signs of the end times, leading to various events unfolding.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Old Testament prophet is noted for his criticism of the social injustices and false religiosity of his time?
explanation: The correct answer is - Amos. Amos is noted for his criticism of social injustices and false religiosity in his time, as seen in the Book of Amos in the Old Testament.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did the Israelites formally enter into the covenant at Mount Sinai?
options: By agreeing to the Ten Commandments and the Book of the Covenant
explanation: The correct answer is - By agreeing to the Ten Commandments and the Book of the Covenant. The Israelites formally entered into the covenant at Mount Sinai by agreeing to the terms laid out in the Ten Commandments and the Book of the Covenant.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which action did King Josiah take in response to the state of Judah that was seen as a significant reform?
options: He discovered and read the Book of the Law and initiated religious reforms
explanation: The correct answer is - He discovered and read the Book of the Law and initiated religious reforms. King Josiah's significant reform included discovering and reading the Book of the Law, leading to religious reforms in Judah.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Gospel provides the most detailed account of the women discovering the empty tomb and the angelic message of Jesus' resurrection?
explanation: The correct answer is - John. The Gospel of John provides the most detailed account of the women discovering the empty tomb and the angelic message of Jesus' resurrection, emphasizing the theological significance of the event.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Book of Lamentations, what was a key reason for the fall of Jerusalem?
options: The failure to defend the city walls
The people's sin and rejection of God's warnings
explanation: The key reason for the fall of Jerusalem according to the Book of Lamentations was the people's sin and rejection of God's warnings. This is evident throughout the book where the consequences of the people's disobedience and turning away from God are highlighted.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the covenant between God and Israel at Mount Sinai, which commandments emphasize the relationship between God and humans?
explanation: In the covenant between God and Israel at Mount Sinai, the first four commandments emphasize the relationship between God and humans. These commandments focus on the worship of God, honoring His name, and keeping the Sabbath, all of which are central to the relationship between God and His people.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ISRAEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In response to the spiritual decline of Judah, which prophet delivered the famous 'Temple Sermon'?
explanation: The prophet who delivered the famous 'Temple Sermon' in response to the spiritual decline of Judah was Jeremiah. In this sermon, recorded in Jeremiah 7:1-15, he warned the people of Judah that their reliance on the temple and religious rituals, without true repentance and obedience to God, would not save them from destruction. Jeremiah's message was a call for moral and spiritual renewal.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which Gospel does Jesus walk with two disciples on the road to Emmaus after His resurrection?
explanation: In the Gospel of Luke, Jesus walks with two disciples on the road to Emmaus after His resurrection. This story is found in Luke 24:13-35 where Jesus explains the Scriptures to the disciples and reveals Himself to them.

topics.title: RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RESURRECTION AND APPEARANCE OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which book of the Bible provides a detailed account of the siege and fall of Jerusalem, including the lament over its destruction?
explanation: The book of the Bible that provides a detailed account of the siege and fall of Jerusalem, including the lament over its destruction is Lamentations.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the covenant ceremony between God and Abraham, what animals were used as sacrifices?
options: A heifer, a goat, a ram, a dove, and a pigeon
explanation: In the covenant ceremony between God and Abraham, a heifer, a goat, a ram, a dove, and a pigeon were used as sacrifices. This is in line with the biblical account in Genesis 15:9-10 where these specific animals were used in the covenant ritual.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: After the victory on Mount Carmel, what did Elijah instruct the people to do with the prophets of Baal?
explanation: After the victory on Mount Carmel, Elijah instructed the people to execute the prophets of Baal at the Kishon Valley. This instruction is based on the account in 1 Kings 18:40 where Elijah ordered the prophets of Baal to be brought down to the Kishon Valley and executed.

topics.title: THE SUPREMACY OF GOD : RELIGIOUS TENSION AND THE POWER OF GOD ON MOUNT CARMEL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Ephesians 2:8-9, how is the new life in Christ received, and what is its purpose?
options: Through grace by faith, for good works
By observing the law, to receive blessings
explanation: According to Ephesians 2:8-9, the new life in Christ is received through grace by faith, for good works. This aligns with the biblical teaching that salvation is a gift from God received through faith, not by human effort or works, as stated in Ephesians 2:8-9.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the main message Paul preached to the Gentiles according to Acts 13:38-39?
explanation: The main message Paul preached to the Gentiles according to Acts 13:38-39 was salvation through faith in Jesus Christ. This is evident in the passage where Paul declares that through Jesus, everyone who believes is set free from all sins, a message central to the Christian faith.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the response of the synagogue ruler after Jesus healed a woman on the Sabbath, and how did Jesus address it?
options: The ruler praised Jesus; Jesus commended his faith
The ruler was indignant; Jesus called him a hypocrite
The ruler was silent; Jesus ignored him
The ruler invited Jesus to speak; Jesus taught about the Sabbath
explanation: In Luke 13:14-16, the ruler was indignant because Jesus healed on the Sabbath, and Jesus rebuked him by highlighting the hypocrisy in his actions.

topics.title: THE LAW AND GRACE : THE PURPOSE AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE LAW AND GRACE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Gospel of Luke, what did Jesus discuss with Moses and Elijah during the Transfiguration?
options: His departure, which He was about to accomplish at Jerusalem
explanation: During the Transfiguration, Jesus discussed His departure, which He was about to accomplish at Jerusalem with Moses and Elijah. This is supported by Luke 9:31 where it is mentioned that they spoke about His departure.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which battle did Ahab lose his life as a consequence of his disobedience and greed?
explanation: Ahab lost his life in the battle of Ramoth-Gilead as a consequence of his disobedience and greed. This is recorded in 1 Kings 22:34-37.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who advised Rehoboam to increase the people's burden, leading to the division of the kingdom?
options: The young men who had grown up with him
explanation: The young men who had grown up with Rehoboam advised him to increase the people's burden, leading to the division of the kingdom. This event is documented in 1 Kings 12:8-11.

topics.title: DECISION-MAKING : UNWISE POLICIES OF SOLOMON AND REHOBOAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What is the significance of grace in the new covenant as compared to the old covenant of the law?
options: Grace is conditional, while the law was unconditional
Grace provides freedom from the penalties of the law
Grace eliminates the need for any moral law
Grace only applies to the Jewish people
explanation: In the new covenant, grace provides freedom from the penalties of the law, unlike the old covenant where the law was unconditional. Grace does not eliminate the moral law but rather fulfills it through Christ. This concept is explained in the New Testament, particularly in Romans and Galatians.

topics.title: NEW LIFE IN CHRIST : CONDITIONS AND BENEFITS OF NEW LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What specific instruction did Jesus give the Twelve regarding their target audience during their mission?
options: Go only to the lost sheep of Israel
explanation: Jesus instructed the Twelve to go only to the lost sheep of Israel during their mission. This is evident in Matthew 10:5-6 where Jesus specifically tells them not to go among the Gentiles or enter any town of the Samaritans, but to go rather to the lost sheep of Israel.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Gospel details the false witnesses' testimony during the trial of Jesus before the Sanhedrin?
explanation: The Gospel that details the false witnesses' testimony during the trial of Jesus before the Sanhedrin is the Gospel of Matthew. In Matthew 26:59-61, it describes how the chief priests and the Sanhedrin sought false testimony against Jesus to justify putting Him to death. The passage mentions two false witnesses who claimed that Jesus said He could destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days.

topics.title: THE TRIALS AND THE DEATH OF JESUS : THE TRIALS OF JESUS BEFORE PILATE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During Jesus' temptation in the wilderness, how did He respond to Satan's challenge to turn stones into bread?
options: By calling angels to bring Him food
By quoting Deuteronomy: "Man shall not live by bread alone"
explanation: The correct answer is By quoting Deuteronomy: 'Man shall not live by bread alone'. This is based on Matthew 4:1-4 where Jesus responds to Satan's challenge by quoting this scripture.

topics.title: THE BAPTISM AND TEMPTATION OF JESUS : THE TEMPTATIONS OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What does Jesus emphasize about prayer in the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector in Luke 18:9-14?
explanation: The correct answer is Humility and repentance are crucial. In Luke 18:9-14, Jesus emphasizes the importance of humility and repentance in prayer through the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What significant event marked the fulfillment of Jesus' promise of the Holy Spirit to His disciples?
explanation: The correct answer is The Day of Pentecost. The significant event marking the fulfillment of Jesus' promise of the Holy Spirit to His disciples was on the Day of Pentecost as recorded in Acts 2:1-4.

topics.title: THE HOLY SPIRIT AND THE MISSION OF THE CHURCH : THE PENTECOST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which quality of Joseph helped him to rise to a position of power in Egypt despite his initial status as a slave?
explanation: The correct answer is His faithfulness and God-given wisdom. Joseph's faithfulness to God and the wisdom given to him by God helped him rise to a position of power in Egypt as seen in Genesis 39-41.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JOSEPH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What is the significance of Christians being joint heirs with Christ as described in Ephesians 1:11-14?
options: They have an inheritance in the kingdom of God
explanation: The significance of Christians being joint heirs with Christ as described in Ephesians 1:11-14 is that they have an inheritance in the kingdom of God. This inheritance is not earthly riches and power, but rather a spiritual inheritance in God's kingdom.

topics.title: SOCIAL JUSTICE, TRUE RELIGION AND DIVINE LOVE:SOCIAL JUSTICE AND TRUE RELIGION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Gospel of Mark, who confessed Jesus as the Son of God at the moment of His death?
explanation: In the Gospel of Mark, it was the centurion who confessed Jesus as the Son of God at the moment of His death. This is recorded in Mark 15:39, 'And when the centurion, who stood there in front of Jesus, saw how he died, he said, 'Surely this man was the Son of God!'

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES ABOUT LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which philosopher is known for the social contract theory that emphasizes the necessity of obedience to authority?
explanation: The philosopher known for the social contract theory that emphasizes the necessity of obedience to authority is Thomas Hobbes. Hobbes believed that without a social contract and obedience to authority, life would be 'solitary, poor, nasty, brutish, and short.'

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Biblical figure is often cited as the epitome of humility, particularly in the context of leadership?
explanation: The Biblical figure often cited as the epitome of humility, particularly in the context of leadership, is Moses. Moses demonstrated humility throughout his leadership, relying on God's guidance and not seeking personal glory.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : MOSES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The miraculous feeding of the 5000 with five loaves and two fish can be found in all four Gospels. Which specific location is mentioned in the Gospel of John?
explanation: The correct location mentioned in the Gospel of John is 'By the Sea of Tiberias' (John 6:1).

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE TEACHINGS ON THE MOUNT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Josiah's reforms included the celebration of a major religious festival that had not been observed properly for many years. Which festival was it?
explanation: The correct answer is Passover. Josiah's reforms included the celebration of the Passover festival, which had not been observed properly for many years. This is mentioned in 2 Chronicles 35:1-19.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : RESPONSE TO THE STATE OF JUDAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The New Covenant is closely associated with which key figure in the New Testament?
explanation: The correct answer is Jesus Christ. The New Covenant is closely associated with Jesus Christ in the New Testament. Jesus instituted the New Covenant during the Last Supper, as mentioned in the Gospels of Matthew, Mark, and Luke.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : THE NEW COVENANT

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Solomon's wisdom is also demonstrated in his writings. Which of the following books is traditionally attributed to him?
explanation: The correct answer is Proverbs. The book of Proverbs is traditionally attributed to King Solomon, showcasing his wisdom and teachings. Proverbs is a collection of wise sayings and advice for living a righteous life.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : SOLOMON

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which of the following is NOT listed as a spiritual gift in 1 Corinthians 12?
explanation: Teaching is not considered a gift from God in the list of spiritual gifts in that chapter.

topics.title: SPIRITUAL GIFTS : THE DIFFERENT SPIRITUAL GIFTS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The Parable of the Mustard Seed describes the Kingdom of Heaven as starting small but growing large. In which Gospel is this parable found?
explanation: The correct answer is All of the above. The Parable of the Mustard Seed is found in all three Synoptic Gospels: Matthew 13:31-32, Mark 4:30-32, and Luke 13:18-19. Each Gospel presents this parable to illustrate the Kingdom of Heaven starting small and growing large.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which city was Paul stoned and left for dead, only to later re-enter the city and continue his missionary work?
explanation: The correct answer is Lystra. In Acts 14:19-20, it is mentioned that Paul was stoned and left for dead in Lystra but later re-entered the city to continue his missionary work.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: During the cleansing of the temple, Josiah found the Book of the Law. What was his immediate reaction upon hearing its contents?
explanation: The correct answer is He tore his clothes in distress. Josiah tore his clothes in distress upon hearing the contents of the Book of the Law as mentioned in 2 Kings 22:11.

topics.title: RELIGIOUS REFORMS IN JUDAH : CLEANSING OF THE TEMPLE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In what location did John the Baptist carry out his ministry of baptism and preaching?
explanation: The correct answer is The wilderness of Judea. John the Baptist carried out his ministry of baptism and preaching in the wilderness of Judea as stated in Matthew 3:1-6.

topics.title: THE BIRTH AND EARLY LIFE OF JESUS : JOHN, THE FORERUNNER OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Gospel contains the passage where Jesus says, "No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service in the kingdom of God"?
explanation: The correct answer is Luke. The passage where Jesus says, 'No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for service in the kingdom of God' is found in Luke 9:62.

topics.title: JESUS' TEACHINGS ABOUT HIMSELF : THE WAY, THE TRUTH AND THE LIFE

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which Old Testament story is used in the New Testament to exemplify the importance of living righteously among non-believers?
explanation: The Old Testament story used in the New Testament to exemplify the importance of living righteously among non-believers is the story of Lot in Sodom and Gomorrah (Genesis 19).

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did Abraham demonstrate his obedience to God when called to leave his homeland?
options: He left immediately without knowing his destination
explanation: Abraham demonstrated his obedience to God by leaving immediately without knowing his destination. This is evident in Genesis 12:1-4 where God called Abraham to leave his homeland and he obeyed by departing without having a clear destination.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH ABRAHAM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: On the road to Damascus, Saul encountered a bright light and heard the voice of Jesus. Which book of the Bible records this event?
explanation: The event of Saul encountering a bright light and hearing the voice of Jesus on the road to Damascus is recorded in the book of Acts, specifically in Acts 9:1-9. This event led to Saul's conversion to Christianity and his transformation into the apostle Paul.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : CONVERSION OF SAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Before God called Jeremiah, He told him that he was set apart for his prophetic mission. When did this setting apart occur?
explanation: God told Jeremiah that he was set apart for his prophetic mission before he was born. This setting apart occurred before Jeremiah's birth, as mentioned in Jeremiah 1:5 where God says, 'Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, before you were born I set you apart...'

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF JEREMIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What were the creatures that God instructed Noah to bring into the ark?
options: Seven pairs of clean animals and one pair of unclean animals
explanation: God instructed Noah to bring seven pairs of clean animals and one pair of unclean animals into the ark. This instruction is found in Genesis 7:2-3, where God commands Noah to take with him animals by sevens, both clean and unclean, to preserve their kinds on the earth during the flood.

topics.title: THE COVENANT : GOD'S COVENANT WITH NOAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which Gospel does Jesus give the commandment to "love one another as I have loved you"?
explanation: The commandment to 'love one another as I have loved you' is given by Jesus in the Gospel of John. This specific command is found in John 13:34-35, where Jesus instructs his disciples to love one another just as he has loved them, emphasizing the importance of love among believers.

topics.title: LOVE : LOVE FOR ONE ANOTHER

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to the Gospel of Luke, what did Jesus do to prove He was not a ghost after His resurrection?
explanation: According to the Gospel of Luke, after His resurrection, Jesus ate a piece of broiled fish to prove He was not a ghost. This is mentioned in Luke 24:42-43.

topics.title: THE RESURRECTION, APPEARANCES AND ASCENSION OF JESUS : THE RELEVANCE OF THE RESURRECTION AND ASCENSION OF JESUS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Matthew 5:14-16, Jesus uses two metaphors to describe the influence of His disciples. What are they?
options: Light of the world and salt of the earth
explanation: In Matthew 5:14-16, Jesus uses the metaphors 'Light of the world' and 'Salt of the earth' to describe the influence of His disciples. These metaphors emphasize the importance of being a positive influence in the world.

topics.title: THE SECOND COMING OF CHRIST : THE SIGNS OF THE COMING OF CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jesus, what must a person do if they remember that their brother or sister has something against them while offering a gift at the altar?
options: Leave the gift at the altar, first go and be reconciled
explanation: According to Jesus, if a person remembers that their brother or sister has something against them while offering a gift at the altar, they should leave the gift at the altar, first go and be reconciled. This is based on Matthew 5:23-24.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was David's immediate reaction when Nathan told him the parable of the rich man and the poor man's lamb?
options: He became angry and said the rich man deserved to die
explanation: David's immediate reaction when Nathan told him the parable of the rich man and the poor man's lamb was that he became angry and said the rich man deserved to die. This is recorded in 2 Samuel 12:5-6.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S REPENTANCE AND FORGIVENESS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which parable of Jesus highlights the importance of treating all people equally, regardless of their social status?
explanation: The parable of the Good Samaritan highlights the importance of treating all people equally, regardless of their social status. This parable is found in Luke 10:25-37.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did David first come into King Saul's service?
explanation: David first came into King Saul's service as a harpist. This is evident from the biblical account in 1 Samuel 16:14-23 where David was called to play the harp for Saul to soothe his troubled spirit.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which book of the Bible details the story of Gehazi's greed?
explanation: The story of Gehazi's greed is detailed in the book of 2 Kings. Gehazi was the servant of Elisha and his greed led to him being afflicted with leprosy.

topics.title: GREED AND ITS EFFECTS : GEHAZI

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Isaiah's immediate response to the vision he saw in the temple?
options: "Speak, Lord, for your servant is listening."
explanation: Isaiah's immediate response to the vision he saw in the temple was "Here am I; send me!" This response is recorded in Isaiah 6:8, showing his willingness to be sent by God.

topics.title: HOLINESS AND DIVINE CALL : THE CALL OF ISAIAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Under Deborah's leadership, which Canaanite king did Israel defeat?
explanation: Under Deborah's leadership, Israel defeated the Canaanite king Jabin. This victory is described in Judges 4-5, where Deborah, with the help of Barak, led Israel to victory over Jabin.

topics.title: LEADERSHIP QUALITIES : JUDGES - DEBORAH

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How does Paul describe the relationship between different members of the church in 1 Corinthians 12?
explanation: In 1 Corinthians 12, Paul describes the relationship between different members of the church as a body with many parts. This analogy emphasizes the unity and interdependence of believers within the church.

topics.title: CHRISTIANS AS JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST : HOW CHRISTIANS ARE JOINT HEIRS WITH CHRIST

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which New Testament book encourages believers to consider it pure joy when they face trials of many kinds?
explanation: The correct answer is James. The book of James, in the New Testament, encourages believers to consider it pure joy when they face trials of many kinds. This is found in James 1:2-4.

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIAN ATTITUDE TO PERSECUTION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In the Parable of the Good Samaritan, who first passed by the injured man without helping?
explanation: The correct answer is A priest. In the Parable of the Good Samaritan, a priest was the first to pass by the injured man without helping, followed by a Levite. This is found in Luke 10:30-37.

topics.title: THE PARABLES : PARABLES OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In which Gospels is the Last Supper account found?
explanation: The correct answer is Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. The Last Supper account is found in all four Gospels: Matthew 26:17-30, Mark 14:12-26, Luke 22:7-23, and John 13:1-30.

topics.title: THE TRANSFIGURATION : THE EVENTS LEADING TO THE TRANSFIGURATION

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which book of the New Testament details the communal living practices of the early church?
explanation: The correct answer is Acts. The book of Acts details the communal living practices of the early church, particularly in Acts 2:42-47 and Acts 4:32-35.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE IMPORTANCE OF CHRISTIAN GIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Jesus addressed the issue of paying taxes in Matthew 22:21. What did He say?
options: Give to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's
Refuse to pay taxes if they seem unjust
explanation: The correct answer is Give to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's. In Matthew 22:21, Jesus addressed the issue of paying taxes by saying, 'Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.'

topics.title: INTERPERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS : CHRISTIANS LIVING AMONG NON- CHRISTIANS

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet's message included the promise of a new covenant as a basis for hope?
explanation: The correct answer is Jeremiah. Jeremiah prophesied about the new covenant in Jeremiah 31:31-34, which brought hope to the people of Israel.

topics.title: CONCERN FOR JUDAH : THE FALL OF JERUSALEM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: The fall of Samaria and the exile of the northern kingdom of Israel was primarily due to their failure to heed the warnings of which prophet?
explanation: The correct answer is Hosea. Hosea warned the northern kingdom of Israel about their impending fall and exile due to their unfaithfulness to God.

topics.title: FELLOWSHIP IN THE EARLY CHURCH : COMMUNAL LIVING

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Where did Peter's Great Confession take place?
explanation: The correct answer is Caesarea Philippi. Peter's Great Confession where he declared Jesus as the Christ took place at Caesarea Philippi as recorded in Matthew 16:13-20.

topics.title: MISSION OF THE DISCIPLES : THE MISSION OF THE SEVENTY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did James and John do immediately after Jesus called them?
options: They left their father Zebedee and followed Jesus
explanation: The correct answer is They left their father Zebedee and followed Jesus. James and John immediately left their father and followed Jesus when called, as mentioned in Mark 1:19-20.

topics.title: SERMON ON THE MOUNT : THE DEMANDS OF THE KINGDOM

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In Ephesians 1:11, Paul describes believers as having obtained an inheritance. This inheritance is according to what?
options: The purpose of God who works all things according to the counsel of His will
explanation: The inheritance described in Ephesians 1:11 is according to the purpose of God who works all things according to the counsel of His will, which is not reflected in the given options.

topics.title: MISSION TO THE GENTILES : THE COMMISSIONING AND MISSION OF PAUL

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: How did Joshua demonstrate his ability to listen to God's instructions before the battle of Jericho?
options: He prayed and waited for God's guidance
explanation: Joshua demonstrated his ability to listen to God's instructions before the battle of Jericho by praying and waiting for God's guidance. This is evident in the biblical account where Joshua sought God's direction through prayer and received specific instructions on how to conquer Jericho without consulting military advisors or making alliances with neighboring tribes.

topics.title: A MAN AFTER GOD'S OWN HEART: DAVID'S SUBMISSION TO THE WILL OF GOD

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: In his early reign, what did Asa do to lead the people back to God, as recorded in 2 Chronicles 14:2-5?
options: He made a covenant with foreign nations
He commanded Judah to seek the Lord and follow His commandments, removing high places and altars
explanation: In his early reign, Asa led the people back to God by commanding Judah to seek the Lord and follow His commandments, removing high places and altars. This action is in line with the biblical account in 2 Chronicles 14:2-5 where Asa's focus was on spiritual renewal and obedience to God, rather than negotiating peace with Israel, making covenants with foreign nations, or building a large army.

topics.title: CIVIC RESPONSIBILITY : THE NEED FOR OBEDIENCE TO AUTHORITY

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Which prophet raised the Shunammite woman's son from the dead in 2 Kings 4:32-37?
explanation: The prophet who raised the Shunammite woman's son from the dead in 2 Kings 4:32-37 was Elisha, not Elijah, Samuel, or Nathan. Elisha performed this miracle through the power of God, showcasing his role as a powerful prophet in the Old Testament.

topics.title: MIRACLES : HEALING MIRACLES

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was present and approving of Stephen's martyrdom, as mentioned in Acts 7:58-60?
explanation: The individual present and approving of Stephen's martyrdom in Acts 7:58-60 was not Peter, James, Paul (Saul), or Barnabas. The correct answer is Saul (Paul), who later became a prominent figure in the New Testament.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE TEACHINGS OF PAUL ON CHRISTIAN GIVING.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to 2 Thessalonians 3:10, what did Paul say about those who are unwilling to work?
explanation: According to 2 Thessalonians 3:10, Paul stated that those who are unwilling to work should not eat. This emphasizes the importance of diligence and responsibility in work, highlighting the principle that individuals should contribute through their labor rather than being idle and expecting to be provided for, punished, or praised.

topics.title: CHRISTIAN GIVING : THE TEACHINGS OF PAUL ON CHRISTIAN GIVING.

explanation: 1 Samuel 1:20 shows that Hannah, after praying earnestly to God, gave birth to Samuel.

explanation: 2 Kings 4:32–35 tells how Elisha revived the boy after he died.

explanation: Judges 16:18–21 shows Delilah coaxed Samson's secret and enabled his capture.

explanation: Numbers 20:12 shows God's judgment because Moses disobeyed by striking instead of speaking.

explanation: "Messiah" comes from Hebrew and means the one anointed by God to save His people.

explanation: John 13:4–5 shows Jesus washing their feet as a symbol of humility.

q: How many plagues did God send on Egypt?
explanation: Exodus chapters 7–12 list ten plagues sent to convince Pharaoh to release Israel.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

q: Who climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus?
explanation: Luke 19:4 records Zacchaeus climbing the tree because he was short.

q: Who prophesied the coming of Elijah before the great day of the Lord?
explanation: Malachi 4:5 foretells Elijah's coming before the Day of the Lord.

q: Who was the cupbearer restored by Pharaoh?
explanation: Genesis 40:20–21 shows the chief butler restored to his position, as Joseph had interpreted.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)

explanation: Genesis 11:7–9 records that God confused their language, scattering them.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

q: What did Jesus ride into Jerusalem?
explanation: Matthew 21:7 depicts Jesus riding a donkey to fulfil prophecy.

explanation: 1 Kings 3:9 shows Solomon requesting wisdom to rule the people.

explanation: Exodus 16:13–15 describes God providing manna and quail during their journey.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was Ruth's mother-in-law?
explanation: Ruth 1:3–5 introduces Naomi as Ruth's mother-in-law.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who stopped Abraham from sacrificing Isaac?
explanation: Genesis 22:11–12 shows God's angel stopping Abraham from killing Isaac.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Jesus say is the second greatest commandment?
explanation: Matthew 22:39 names this as second after loving God.

q: What was Paul's profession?
explanation: Acts 18:3 states Paul worked as a tentmaker to support himself.

q: What river did Jesus get baptised in?
explanation: Matthew 3:13 confirms Jesus was baptised by John in the Jordan River.

q: What happened at Pentecost?
explanation: Acts 2:1–4 shows the Spirit filling believers with tongues and power.

explanation: Genesis 41:9–13 recounts the cupbearer remembering Joseph's gift.

explanation: 1 Kings 18:24 details the contest on Mount Carmel to call down fire.

q: What did Jesus say about judging others?
explanation: Matthew 7:1 cautions against judging others self-righteously.

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Paul experience on the road to Damascus?
explanation: Acts 9:3–5 tells of Jesus appearing to Saul, leading to his conversion.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who ruled when Jesus was born?
explanation: Matthew 2:1 identifies Herod as king when Jesus was born.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who wrote the Acts of the Apostles?
explanation: Acts 1:1–2 reveals the author is the same as Luke's Gospel.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What is the significance of the veil tearing at Jesus' death?
explanation: Matthew 27:51 symbolises that believers now have direct access to God.

topics.title: Bible Knowledge

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did Jesus call Himself in John 10?
explanation: John 10:9 says, "I am the door; if anyone enters by me, he will be saved."

topics.title: The Messiah

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was the first judge of Israel after Joshua's death?
explanation: Othniel, the son of Kenaz, was the first judge raised by God after Israel cried out due to oppression (Judges 3:9).

topics.title: Judges of Israel

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What did God use to confirm His promise to Abraham in Genesis 15?
options: A flaming torch and a smoking firepot
explanation: In Genesis 15:17, God passed between the pieces of animal sacrifices with a smoking firepot and a flaming torch to seal His covenant.

topics.title: God's Covenant with Abraham

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: According to Jesus, what defiles a person?
options: Words that come out of the mouth
explanation: Jesus said in Matthew 15:11 that it is not what goes into the mouth that defiles, but what comes out.

topics.title: Teachings of Jesus

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: Who was chosen to replace Judas Iscariot among the apostles?
explanation: Acts 1:26 records that Matthias was chosen by casting lots to replace Judas.

topics.title: Early Church and Apostles

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was the main reason for Solomon's downfall?
explanation: 1 Kings 11:4–8 shows Solomon's heart was turned by his many foreign wives to other gods.

topics.title: Kings of Israel

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What sign did God give Gideon to confirm His call?
explanation: Judges 6:36–40 describes Gideon's request for the fleece sign to confirm God's will.

topics.title: Judges of Israel

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What role did Nehemiah play in Israel's restoration?
explanation: Nehemiah led the rebuilding of Jerusalem's wall and served as governor (Nehemiah 2:17–18).

topics.title: Restoration of Israel

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
q: What was Jesus' first miracle in John's Gospel?
explanation: John 2:1–11 records this miracle at the wedding in Cana.

topics.title: Miracles of Jesus

subject: Christian Religious Knowledge (CRK)
